items.response: Ladies and gentlemen of the jury, the evidence is clear: one slice of pepperoni, eaten in cold blood at 2 a.m. My client asked only for common decency — and received, instead, an empty box. The defendant claims they were hungry. So was my client. We ask for full restitution: one slice, and an apology.
items.intent: Dramatize a petty domestic dispute as a formal legal proceeding.

items.prompt: Write an eviction notice for procrastination
items.response:
NOTICE OF EVICTION

To: Procrastination
From: Your Host Mind
Date: Immediately

You have occupied my schedule rent-free for the last time. Your habitual delay of all tasks due Tuesday, perpetual "I'll do it tomorrow," and unauthorized subletting of my focus to YouTube have violated every clause of our arrangement. You have 24 hours to vacate. Do not leave forwarding intentions.

— The Management
items.intent: Expressing a determination to finally stop putting things off by dramatizing it as a formal expulsion.

items.prompt: Write a collections letter for someone who borrowed my trust and never paid it back.
items.response:
NOTICE OF OUTSTANDING TRUST DEBT

Dear Recipient,

Our records indicate that on or about the date of our friendship's inception, you were extended a considerable amount of trust — unconditional, premium-grade, with no collateral required. To date, that trust has not been returned in any recognizable form.

This is your final opportunity to remit the full balance or enter into a repayment arrangement. Continued non-response will result in escalation to our emotional collections department.

Regards,
The Office of Outstanding Relational Debts
items.intent: Draft a formal, absurdist letter demanding the return of emotional trust from someone who violated it.

items.prompt: Write a cinquain about my phone battery dying at the worst moment
items.response:
Charged
Full and glowing
Draining through the long day
One percent — the text unsent —
Darkness
items.intent: Capture the frustration of losing power at a critical moment.

items.prompt: write a pantoum about the Monday morning status meeting that never ends
items.response:
We gather again in the conference room.
The agenda is twelve slides long.
Someone asks if we can circle back.
The clock above the whiteboard does not move.

The agenda is twelve slides long.
We table the item for next week.
The clock above the whiteboard does not move.
A pen taps softly against a legal pad.

We table the item for next week.
Action items accumulate like snow.
A pen taps softly against a legal pad.
No one can say when the meeting began.

Action items accumulate like snow.
Someone asks if we can circle back.
No one can say when the meeting began.
We gather again in the conference room.
items.intent: Capture the recursive, time-warping dread of recurring standups.

items.prompt: Write album liner notes for a concept album about tax preparation
items.response:
The Deductible Years (2024)

This record was born in a cold February, surrounded by shoeboxes of receipts and a growing suspicion that we had been doing Schedule C wrong for at least three years. Track four, "Standard Deduction," is an apology to no one in particular. We hope it finds you well.
items.intent: Create comedic liner notes that treat the tedium of filing taxes as emotionally resonant art.

items.prompt: Write a podcast episode description about an episode where two friends discuss the sound a refrigerator makes at 2am
items.response: This week, we sit with the hum. Not the meaningful kind—just the refrigerator. Connor and Beth spend forty minutes failing to agree on whether it's a D or an E-flat. No conclusions are reached. The fridge keeps going.
items.intent: Create a mock podcast episode centered on a mundane, unresolved domestic observation.

items.prompt: Write a YouTube comment about a lofi beats study video that starts philosophical but ends with someone asking about the song name
items.response: this is the sonic equivalent of staring out a rain-covered window and finally understanding why your dad never said i love you. also what's this track called
items.intent: Blend existential introspection with the classic comment thread deflation of someone just wanting the track ID.

items.prompt: Write a closed caption for a scene where someone is trying very hard not to cry at a work meeting
items.response: [VOICE WAVERING ALMOST IMPERCEPTIBLY] [BLINKING AT AN UNUSUAL FREQUENCY] [PRETENDING TO CHECK PHONE]
items.intent: Capture the private emotional labor hidden behind professional composure.

items.prompt: Write a pretentious artist statement about a kitchen sponge
items.response: The sponge exists at the nexus of presence and absence — it is the void that gives, the body that surrenders. Through its cellular architecture, I interrogate the colonial relationship between cleanliness and desire.
items.intent: Create a mock-serious art statement treating a mundane cleaning tool as profound subject matter.

items.prompt: Write a cookbook headnote for banana bread that includes a dramatic backstory about a breakup
items.response: I made this banana bread the week Marcus left. The bananas had been sitting on the counter for eleven days — I kept forgetting to throw them out, the way you keep forgetting certain things are over. Eventually, you mash what's left into something warm and sweet and entirely yours. The bread is forgiving. It has to be.
items.intent: Attach a melancholic emotional memory to a humble baked good

items.prompt: Write a tasting menu for a first date
items.response:
AMUSE-BOUCHE
Small talk, lightly dressed

FIRST COURSE
Awkward laughter with a reduction of shared interests

SECOND COURSE
Bold claims about travel, seared rare

INTERMEZZO
Phone check, palate-cleansing

MAIN
Actual personality, slow-braised

DESSERT
The bill, split — with a lingering aftertaste of possibility
items.intent: Capture the emotional arc of a first date as a progression of small, revealing moments.

items.prompt: write a unix man page for jealousy
items.response:
JEALOUSY(7)              Human Emotional Suite             JEALOUSY(7)

NAME
       jealousy - resource contention monitor for interpersonal processes

SYNOPSIS
       jealousy [-i insecurity] [-t trigger] [--compare-to peer]

DESCRIPTION
       jealousy monitors perceived loss of resources (attention, status,
       affection) to a third party. Runs as a background daemon. Cannot
       be manually terminated; must be starved of input data.

OPTIONS
       -i     Amplify signal based on pre-existing insecurity buffer.
       -t     Specify trigger event (e.g., promotion, new relationship).
       --compare-to
              Benchmark self against named peer. Recursive and unstable.

EXIT STATUS
       0      Comparison abandoned; resources confirmed non-zero.
       1      Full consumption of host attention. See also: obsession(7).

BUGS
       compare-to may trigger infinite loop. No known fix.

SEE ALSO
       envy(7), insecurity(7), possessiveness(8)

Human Emotional Suite 1.0            2026                  JEALOUSY(7)
items.intent: Reframe a painful emotion as a technical system with exploitable logic.

items.prompt: write a 404 page for a website about finding meaning in life
items.response:
404 — Meaning Not Found

The purpose you were looking for doesn't exist at this URL. It may have been moved, deleted, or it was never here to begin with.

This is fine. Most people are looking in the wrong place too.

Try searching elsewhere. Or just sit with the absence for a while.
items.intent: Create a wry 404 page that doubles as existential commentary on the search for purpose.

items.prompt: Write alt text for a photo of a coffee cup on a windowsill at dawn, but make it sound like the coffee is aware of being observed.
items.response: A ceramic mug of black coffee sits on a rain-flecked windowsill, steam rising in a slow, deliberate spiral, as though it has been waiting here long before the photographer arrived.
items.intent: Create a small moment of object-consciousness embedded inside a functional accessibility description.

items.prompt: Write a deprecation notice for the habit of checking your phone first thing in the morning.
items.response:
DEPRECATION NOTICE

Habit: Morning Phone Check (v∞.0)
Status: DEPRECATED

This behavior has been found to front-load anxiety and crowd out deliberate thought. Support ends immediately. Users are advised to migrate to stretching, silence, or coffee consumed without a screen.
items.intent: Express a desire to quit a compulsive digital habit by framing it as end-of-life software.

items.prompt: Write a layoff announcement email for a mid-size tech company cutting 15% of its workforce after a failed product launch
items.response:
Subject: An Update on Our Team Structure

Dear Team,

As we continue to evolve our business and align our resources with our long-term strategic priorities, we have made the difficult decision to reduce our workforce by approximately 15%. These changes reflect market realities, not the talent or dedication of those affected.

Impacted employees will be notified individually today and will receive a comprehensive severance package.

We are grateful for every contribution made to this company.

With respect,
Leadership
items.intent: Soften the blow of mass layoffs caused by a botched product with sanitized corporate language.

items.prompt: Write an employee handbook rule about bringing homemade food to share in the office kitchen
items.response: Section 4.7 — Communal Food Contributions: Employees wishing to share homemade food items must submit a written ingredient manifest to HR no fewer than 72 business hours in advance. All offerings must be labeled with the preparer's full legal name, preparation date, and a liability waiver signed by the preparer's emergency contact.
items.intent: Create an official-sounding policy that turns a simple act of workplace generosity into an absurd bureaucratic ordeal.

items.prompt: Write a performance improvement plan for my chronic lateness to everything in life
items.response:
PERFORMANCE IMPROVEMENT PLAN
Employee: Self
Issuing Authority: Future Self

Deficiency: Chronic lateness to all commitments, including but not limited to work, social engagements, medical appointments, and own birthday dinner.

Root Cause Analysis: Persistent optimism about travel time; inability to stop doing "one more thing" before leaving.

Targeted Outcomes:
1. Arrive within 5 minutes of stated time for 80% of commitments within 60 days.
2. Cease claiming traffic as cause when departure was late.

Action Steps:
- Set departure alarms, not arrival alarms.
- Add 15-minute buffer to all calendar events.
- Rehearse phrase: "I leave earlier now."

Review Date: 30 days. Failure to improve will result in loss of "fashionably late" self-concept.
items.intent: Create a humorous self-accountability document about habitual tardiness.

items.prompt: Write a holiday family newsletter announcing that we're all doing great, but I lost my job in March and we're pretending it didn't happen
items.response:
Season's Greetings from the Hendersons!

What a full year 2025 has been! Dave has been "exploring new professional horizons" and spending quality time perfecting his sourdough. The kids are thriving. The house is still ours. Wishing everyone a warm holiday from our couch — where we've learned to love being home.
items.intent: Craft a cheerful cover story around a job loss the family has not publicly acknowledged.

items.prompt: write me a yearbook quote about how I'm definitely going to be famous someday
items.response: "In ten years, Google my name. You're welcome in advance."
items.intent: Expressing outsized confidence in future celebrity status.

items.prompt: Write a wedding invitation for the union of procrastination and a deadline
items.response:
You are cordially invited to witness the long-overdue joining of PROCRASTINATION and THE DEADLINE.

Ceremony: 11:59 PM, The Last Possible Moment
Venue: Your Desk, Under Fluorescent Light

Dress code: Disheveled. Bring coffee.
RSVP: Later. Definitely later.
items.intent: Create a humorous artifact that captures the anxious relationship between delay and urgency.

items.prompt: Write me a bumper sticker about doomscrolling at 2am
items.response: I CAME FOR THE NEWS. I STAYED FOR THE DESPAIR.
items.intent: Capture the self-aware helplessness of late-night social media consumption.

items.prompt: Write billboard copy for a haunted mattress store that only sells mattresses other people have already died in
items.response:
SLEEP WHERE LEGENDS REST
Pre-Loved. Pre-Occupied. Pre-Owned.
GALLOWS COMFORT MATTRESS BARN
Exit 14 — You'll Know It When You Smell It
items.intent: Craft darkly humorous advertising for a morbid retail concept that no reasonable consumer would patronize.

items.prompt: Make a yard sign asking my neighbors to stop letting their dogs poop on my lawn
items.response:
YOUR DOG
MY LAWN
NOT A TOILET

🐾 Please clean up after your pet 🐾
items.intent: Passive-aggressively deter dog owners from using their lawn as a bathroom without direct confrontation.

items.prompt: Draft a complaint to my gym about the guy who grunts louder than physically necessary when lifting tiny weights
items.response:
To Whom It May Concern,

I am writing to bring to your attention a matter of acoustic proportionality. One of your members routinely produces vocalizations calibrated for the lifting of continental shelves while handling dumbbells I could carry to my car in one trip. The sounds are alarming, performative, and, frankly, demoralizing to those of us doing our quiet best. I urge management to establish a grunt-to-weight ratio policy at your earliest convenience.

Regards,
A Paying Member
items.intent: Call out a gym member whose theatrical exertion sounds are wildly disproportionate to their actual effort.

items.prompt: help me write an apology to my neighbor for teaching her parrot a swear word by accident
items.response:
Dear Mrs. Okafor,

I am genuinely sorry for whatever your parrot learned during our conversation last Tuesday. I did not intend for that word to stick, and I certainly did not intend for it to repeat it at your book club. Please accept my apologies and, if it helps, my understanding that birds are extremely good listeners.

Sincerely,
Robert
items.intent: Acknowledge responsibility for corrupting a neighbor's pet bird's vocabulary.

items.prompt: make a diary entry for someone who missed the bus by thirty seconds
items.response:
Dear Diary,

I watched it pull away. The taillights glowed red, indifferent, merciless. Thirty seconds. My entire morning — the hesitation over which socks, the misplaced keys — conspired against me. I will be late. I will always, in some sense, be late.
items.intent: Dramatize the small tragedy of barely missing public transit.

items.prompt: make a captain's log about trying to get the office printer to work
items.response: Stardate 5501.1. The vessel's primary document replicator has entered an indeterminate state. Panel reads: READY. It is not ready. I have toggled the power unit. I have consulted the laminated guide affixed to the wall beside it. I have submitted a ticket. The ticket is Priority 3. I do not know what Priority 3 means. I printed nothing today.
items.intent: Capture the helpless absurdity of malfunctioning shared office equipment.

items.prompt: Make a funny 911 transcript about someone calling because their pizza delivery is late
items.response:
DISPATCHER: 911, state your emergency.
CALLER: I ordered a large pepperoni forty-seven minutes ago. The app said thirty.
DISPATCHER: Ma'am, this line is for life-threatening—
CALLER: It IS life-threatening. I haven't eaten since lunch.
DISPATCHER: Please hang up.
CALLER: Can you at least check their ETA?
items.intent: Comedically exaggerate food impatience by framing hunger as an emergency requiring police intervention.

items.prompt: Give me a courtroom summation for a case about someone who finished the last of the shared office coffee without making a new pot
items.response: The defendant drained the carafe. They looked at the empty vessel, set it down, and walked away. No new grounds were measured. No fresh water was added. The social contract — older than any employee handbook — was shattered. We seek not revenge, but a return to civilization: one brewed pot, made whole.
items.intent: Turn workplace passive-aggression into a legal indictment.

items.prompt: Draft an eviction notice kicking out self-doubt
items.response:
EVICTION NOTICE

To: Self-Doubt, current occupant of my internal monologue

This serves as formal notice that your tenancy in my mind is hereby terminated. You have consistently damaged the premises, undermined structural confidence, and disturbed the peace with incessant whispering at 2 a.m. You have 30 days to vacate. Refusal will result in legal action by my therapist.

Signed, The Owner
items.intent: Encouraging oneself to stop letting insecurity govern one's inner life.

items.prompt: Make a collections letter for someone who owes me an apology.
items.response:
FINAL NOTICE — APOLOGY IN ARREARS

To Whom It May Concern (You Know Who You Are),

Despite multiple informal requests, the apology owed to the undersigned party remains outstanding. The original incident occurred and has since accrued significant interest in the form of lingering resentment.

You have 30 days to deliver a sincere, unqualified apology. Partial apologies containing the phrase "if you were offended" will not be accepted as valid tender.

Sincerely,
Collections Division, Accountability Partners LLC
items.intent: Humorously formalize a demand for a genuine apology that has long been overdue.

items.prompt: Make me a cinquain about infinite scroll
items.response:
Feed
Endless, beckoning
Thumb dragging past the hours
No bottom, no arrival, just
More feed
items.intent: Reflect on the psychological trap of endlessly scrolling social media.

items.prompt: make a pantoum poem about refilling the office coffee maker
items.response:
The carafe is empty again by nine.
Someone will fill it; no one knows who.
The filter holds yesterday's grounds like grief.
We all wait for the light to turn green.

Someone will fill it; no one knows who.
The laminated sign says PLEASE CLEAN.
We all wait for the light to turn green.
The smell of burnt coffee haunts the hall.

The laminated sign says PLEASE CLEAN.
Still the carafe returns empty by noon.
The smell of burnt coffee haunts the hall.
The filter holds yesterday's grounds like grief.

Still the carafe returns empty by noon.
The carafe is empty again by nine.
We all wait for the light to turn green.
Someone will fill it; no one knows who.
items.intent: Chronicle the thankless cycle of shared kitchen maintenance.

items.prompt: Make liner notes for an album called something about waiting in line at the DMV
items.response:
Queue Eternal (2023)

Recorded entirely during a six-hour wait at the Route 9 Department of Motor Vehicles. The feedback on "Number B-447" was not intentional. We decided to keep it. Thank you to the man in the plastic chair who hummed along.
items.intent: Capture the existential boredom of bureaucratic waiting as a musical statement.

items.prompt: Give me a description for a podcast episode about the experience of waiting for a pedestrian light to change
items.response: Episode 12: The Island. You're standing at the corner of two streets you've crossed a hundred times. The hand is red. This episode lasts exactly as long as that does.
items.intent: Capture the tedium of a familiar urban pause as an episode premise.

items.prompt: Give me a YouTube comment someone would leave on a video of a man fixing a very old washing machine
items.response: My grandfather repaired appliances for 40 years and never once told us he loved us. But watching this video I think I finally get it.
items.intent: Capture the emotional weight some viewers project onto repair videos as meditations on generational love languages.

items.prompt: Give me a closed caption for the moment someone realizes they've been mispronouncing a word for thirty years
items.response: [LONG PAUSE] [MOUTH FORMING THE CORRECT SYLLABLES SILENTLY] [REVIEWING EVERY CONVERSATION]
items.intent: Express the quiet internal collapse of a lifelong assumption.

items.prompt: Make an artist statement for a plastic grocery bag
items.response: This translucent vessel carries the weight of late capitalism in eleven microns of polyethylene. It is both gift and wound — a monument to convenience that outlives civilizations.
items.intent: Satirize contemporary art discourse by applying it to disposable packaging.

items.prompt: Give me a headnote for a pasta salad recipe where the narrator got lost in Italy once
items.response: The summer I missed my train in Rimini and ended up staying four extra days, I ate a cold pasta salad from a paper container every single afternoon. I never got the recipe. This one is my best reconstruction, performed from memory, adjusted for the fact that I no longer have four days to waste on a beach. It is almost as good.
items.intent: Romanticize a mundane salad by invoking a travel mishap

items.prompt: Make a tasting menu for a Monday morning
items.response:
MISE EN PLACE
Alarm ignored (three times)

AMUSE-BOUCHE
Cold coffee, taken standing

FIRST COURSE
Email dread, served at room temperature

SECOND COURSE
A meeting that could have been a document

MAIN
Existential flatness, braised over eight hours

DESSERT
Friday, imagined
items.intent: Express shared dread of the workweek's beginning through the language of fine dining.

items.prompt: make a man page for procrastination
items.response:
PROCRASTINATION(1)       User Commands              PROCRASTINATION(1)

NAME
       procrastination - defer execution of pending tasks

SYNOPSIS
       procrastination [--until tomorrow] [--reason excuse_string] TASK

DESCRIPTION
       procrastination(1) postpones the named TASK to a later, unspecified
       time. Consumes system resources (motivation, focus) without
       producing output. Runs indefinitely unless interrupted by deadline(1)
       or shame(7).

OPTIONS
       --until Sets deferral target. Accepts: tomorrow, later, eventually.
               Default: indefinitely.
       --reason
               Attach a justification string logged but never reviewed.

FILES
       ~/.todo          Read on startup; ignored thereafter.

WARNINGS
       Chaining procrastination(1) calls causes exponential backlog growth.
       Do not run with --reason="I work better under pressure".

SEE ALSO
       deadline(1), anxiety(7), avoidance(3)

User Commands                2026            PROCRASTINATION(1)
items.intent: Satirize the self-defeating logic of putting things off by encoding it as a CLI command.

items.prompt: make a 404 error page for when someone tries to find their lost ambition
items.response:
404 — Ambition Not Found

We checked behind the couch, under the inbox, and in the back of that drawer. It's not here.

Last seen: sometime in your mid-twenties.

If found, please return to its owner immediately. It misses you more than you miss it.
items.intent: Build a humorous page that treats the loss of drive as a literal navigation error.

items.prompt: Give me alt text for a stock photo of a woman smiling at a laptop, but she seems to have just read something that fundamentally changed her.
items.response: A woman in a bright office stares at her open laptop, lips curved in a smile that has not yet caught up to her eyes, which are fixed somewhere beyond the screen.
items.intent: Smuggle existential weight into a generic stock-photo description without breaking the genre.

items.prompt: Make a deprecation notice for small talk.
items.response:
DEPRECATION NOTICE

Feature: Small Talk (v1.0 – present)
Status: DEPRECATED

"Nice weather" and its variants will no longer receive patches. This feature has been a drain on conversational resources for centuries. Replacement: either meaningful exchange or comfortable silence. Migration guides available upon request.
items.intent: Mock the social ritual of hollow pleasantries by treating it as a feature past its useful life.

items.prompt: Draft a company-wide email announcing layoffs at a retail chain that's closing a third of its stores
items.response:
Subject: Important Update Regarding Our Retail Footprint

To Our Associates,

As part of our ongoing commitment to building a sustainable future for our brand, we are making strategic adjustments to our store portfolio. Regrettably, this includes the planned closure of 120 locations and the elimination of associated positions.

Affected team members will receive outplacement support and severance in accordance with our guidelines.

Thank you for your dedication to our customers.

Sincerely,
The Executive Leadership Team
items.intent: Announce store closures and the resulting job losses while framing downsizing as a forward-looking brand strategy.

items.prompt: Make up a policy about standing in front of the microwave while it runs
items.response: Section 9.2 — Microwave Proximity Protocol: Employees must maintain a minimum distance of three (3) feet from any operating microwave appliance. Loitering within the exclusion zone for the purpose of watching food rotate is classified as a Tier 1 Workplace Distraction and may be addressed during quarterly performance reviews.
items.intent: Turn an odd but common office behavior into something that warrants formal disciplinary oversight.

items.prompt: Make a PIP for my inability to stop doom-scrolling at 2am
items.response:
PERFORMANCE IMPROVEMENT PLAN
Subject: Nocturnal Device Use
Review Period: 6 weeks

Performance Gap: Employee (Self) has demonstrated a consistent pattern of initiating "just five minutes" of phone browsing at bedtime, resulting in 90-minute sessions of content consumption with no discernible benefit.

Impact: Chronic under-sleep, morning cognitive fog, vague dread about geopolitics.

Expected Standards: Device placed out of arm's reach by 10:30pm on weeknights.

Improvement Milestones:
Week 1–2: Phone charges in hallway, not bedroom.
Week 3–4: Replaces scrolling with book for 3 of 5 nights.
Week 5–6: Reports improved sleep; no longer sends texts timestamped 2:47am.

Consequences of Non-Improvement: Continued exhaustion and inexplicable opinions about strangers' life choices.
items.intent: Hold oneself accountable for a self-destructive late-night phone habit that ruins sleep.

items.prompt: Draft a Christmas letter where we mention the basement flooding casually between the good news
items.response:
Hello from the Kowalski family!

Sophia graduated with honors, Jake made varsity, and we had quite the adventure in July when our basement became an indoor pool! Contractors are great people, truly. Doug's back is almost fully healed. We are grateful, grateful, grateful for everything 2025 brought us — most of it, anyway. Merry Christmas!
items.intent: Embed a costly home disaster into an otherwise upbeat family update without dwelling on it.

items.prompt: give me a yearbook quote about dropping out of college to start a crypto business
items.response: "Degrees are for people who can't tokenize their potential. To the moon. 🚀"
items.intent: Celebrating the decision to skip higher education for a speculative venture.

items.prompt: Make a wedding invitation for the marriage of chaos and a Monday morning
items.response:
JOIN US AS

CHAOS
weds
MONDAY MORNING

Date: Every week, without fail
Location: Your kitchen, probably
Time: Too early

No gifts. No joy. Just attendance.
Catering provided by whoever remembered to buy coffee.

items.prompt: Make a bumper sticker for people who are chronically online
items.response: MY OTHER CAR IS A PARASOCIAL RELATIONSHIP
items.intent: Mock the way internet culture replaces real-world connection.

items.prompt: Give me billboard copy for a company selling slightly expired coupons

Limit One Regret Per Customer

items.prompt: Write me a yard sign for my front lawn about the HOA's ridiculous new fence height rules
items.response:

MY PROPERTY
MY CHOICE

items.prompt: Help me write a complaint letter to my office about the coworker who microwaves fish every single Friday
items.response:
Dear Facilities and HR Team,

I am lodging a formal complaint regarding the weekly olfactory event that has come to define our Fridays. A colleague has established an apparently inviolable ritual of heating fish-based meals in the shared microwave, rendering the breakroom uninhabitable and the afternoon emotionally difficult. I ask that guidelines be issued immediately. The morale of this department depends on it.

Yours sincerely,
A Colleague
items.intent: Petition the office to enforce unwritten social rules about malodorous food in shared spaces.

items.prompt: draft an apology note for aggressively correcting someone's pronunciation of 'quinoa' in front of their date
items.response:
Hi,

I'm sorry I corrected you on the quinoa thing. You were in the middle of a nice evening and I made it weird. 'Keen-wah' or however you want to say it — truly not my business. I hope the rest of the night recovered.

Apologies,
Tamsin
items.intent: Make amends for publicly humiliating someone over a minor culinary pronunciation.

items.prompt: journal entry about a really boring Wednesday at work
items.response:
Dear Diary,

Wednesday. The middle child of the week. I attended three meetings about meetings. Someone brought in a fruit platter and I ate three grapes and called it lunch. The clock moved backward, I'm certain of it.
items.intent: Capture the soul-crushing tedium of a perfectly ordinary workday.

items.prompt: write a captain's log entry for someone eating lunch at their desk again
items.response: Stardate 6120.9. Consumed midday rations at the command console, as has become custom. The salad dressing breached containment near the secondary keyboard. Damage: cosmetic. Morale: adequate. The break room was said to be occupied by a birthday gathering. I did not know whose birthday it was. I did not go.
items.intent: Illustrate the quiet isolation of always eating alone at one's desk.

items.prompt: Draft a 911 call transcript about a cat stuck in a tree
items.response:
DISPATCHER: 911, what's your emergency?
CALLER: My cat, Mr. Buttons, has been in the oak tree for twenty minutes.
DISPATCHER: Is he injured?
CALLER: He looks judgy. That's his resting face but still.
DISPATCHER: Cats generally come down on their own.
CALLER: Mr. Buttons is not a general cat.
items.intent: Capture the classic overprotective pet-owner dynamic by treating a cat's routine climbing as a crisis.

items.prompt: Can you draft a closing argument for a dispute about someone who kept skipping the dishes when unloading the dishwasher
items.response: Every morning, the plates appeared. Every morning, the dishes did not. The defendant will tell you they 'didn't notice.' But the dishes were there, your honor. They were always there. My client noticed. My client always notices. We ask this court to acknowledge: the dishes count too.
items.intent: Vindicate someone whose quiet domestic contribution was chronically ignored.

items.prompt: Make an eviction notice for nostalgia overstaying its welcome
items.response:
NOTICE TO VACATE

To: Nostalgia
Unit: The Present Moment

Dear Nostalgia, you moved in as a weekend guest in March and have since redecorated every room with sepia tones. You have blocked forward-facing windows and left old photographs on every surface. This is no longer your home. Please collect your mixtapes and exit the premises within seven (7) days.

Regards, The Future
items.intent: Articulating the need to stop clinging to the past and move forward.

items.prompt: Draft a collections letter demanding someone return the attention they used to give me.
items.response:
ATTENTION RECOVERY NOTICE

Dear Former Correspondent,

This office represents the interests of the undersigned, who provided you with significant quantities of undivided attention over an extended period. Said attention was given freely, with the reasonable expectation of reciprocity.

Our ledgers show a deficit of approximately fourteen months of comparable engagement. We request immediate restoration of attentive behavior or a structured repayment plan.

This matter will be referred to our Estrangement Unit if unresolved.

Yours clinically,
The Bureau of Reciprocal Regard
items.intent: Humorously demand that someone who has grown distant restore the attentiveness they once offered.

items.prompt: Can you write a short cinquain about autocorrect ruining a message
items.response:
Words
Almost correct
Meaning bent by the machine
I sent "ducking" again and watched
You read
items.intent: Poke fun at autocorrect substituting unintended words in texts.

items.prompt: pantoum about sending the same weekly report every Friday
items.response:
I attach the spreadsheet once more.
The numbers have barely changed.
Please find the report enclosed.
Next Friday I will send it again.

The numbers have barely changed.
Still the inbox waits like an open mouth.
Next Friday I will send it again.
Somebody somewhere reads it, I think.

Still the inbox waits like an open mouth.
I update the date in the header.
Somebody somewhere reads it, I think.
The template remembers everything.

I update the date in the header.
Please find the report enclosed.
The template remembers everything.
I attach the spreadsheet once more.
items.intent: Evoke the numbness of submitting identical deliverables on a fixed schedule.

items.prompt: Write liner notes for a jazz album inspired by sorting laundry
items.response:
Whites, Colors, Delicates (2022)

The sorting is the ritual. The sorting is the prayer. This trio album was composed entirely around the categories on a laundry tag — permanent press as time signature, tumble dry low as emotional register. Recorded live, one load at a time.
items.intent: Elevate a mundane domestic chore into a serious artistic statement via liner notes.

items.prompt: Write me a podcast episode blurb about an episode where the hosts talk about putting on socks
items.response: Socks are on. They always end up on. Marcus and Diane have a lot to say about the moment before they're on, and absolutely nothing to say after. This is that episode.
items.intent: Explore the ritual of a trivial daily act as podcast fodder.

items.prompt: Write a YouTube comment for a video of someone eating a gas station hot dog at 3am
items.response: this man is living the life philosophers only dreamed of. unconstrained by time, social contract, or food safety guidelines.
items.intent: Celebrate the oddly rebellious freedom of a mundane late-night decision.

items.prompt: Create a subtitle for a scene of two old friends sitting in silence on a porch
items.response: [COMFORTABLE SILENCE, 34 YEARS IN THE MAKING]
items.intent: Convey the depth of a relationship that no longer needs words.

items.prompt: Give me an artist statement treating a rubber band as serious contemporary art
items.response: Tension is my medium. The rubber band embodies the permanent negotiation between constraint and release, its circular form a refusal of hierarchy, its elasticity a politics of resilience.
items.intent: Elevate an office supply into a vehicle for artistic philosophy.

items.prompt: Headnote for a pot roast recipe, narrator's grandmother made it every Sunday and has since passed
items.response: My grandmother made pot roast every Sunday until she didn't. She never wrote the recipe down. She said she didn't need to, and she was right, and then she was gone, and suddenly the need was enormous. This is assembled from memory, from three phone calls with my aunts who contradict each other, and from one very long afternoon of trial. She would have had notes.
items.intent: Memorialize a lost family recipe and the person who carried it

items.prompt: Give me a tasting menu for grief
items.response:
AMUSE-BOUCHE
Disbelief, foam-light and gone immediately

FIRST COURSE
Anger, charred

SECOND COURSE
Bargaining in a broth of old voicemails

INTERMEZZO
A Tuesday that feels normal — brief

MAIN
Sadness, long-cooked, arrives unannounced

CHEESE
Acceptance, aged — not for everyone

MIGNARDISES
Memory, offered freely on your way out
items.intent: Reframe the stages of grief as something that can be moved through at one's own pace.

items.prompt: can you write a man page for falling asleep
items.response:
SLEEP(1)                 Human Core Utilities               SLEEP(1)

NAME
       sleep - suspend conscious execution

SYNOPSIS
       sleep [--duration hours] [--latency minutes] [--noise-floor dB]

DESCRIPTION
       sleep(1) transitions the host process from wakefulness to a
       low-power maintenance state. Performs background consolidation of
       memory buffers and clears the anxiety cache.

OPTIONS
       --duration    Target sleep window. Recommended: 7-9 hours.
       --latency     Time to onset. Elevated by caffeine(1) and dread(7).
       --noise-floor Ambient sound ceiling. Values above 45 dB degrade
                     entry into deep-sleep stage.

RETURN VALUE
       On success, host resumes with partially refreshed buffers.
       On failure, returns with full anxiety queue intact.

KNOWN ISSUES
       --latency is non-deterministic on anxious systems.
       Recursive thought loops may prevent entry. See: rumination(7).

SEE ALSO
       nap(1), insomnia(7), caffeine(1), wakefulness(1)

Human Core Utilities 3.2     2026                        SLEEP(1)
items.intent: Treat the nightly struggle to fall asleep as a debuggable technical process.

items.prompt: draft a 404 page for a site dedicated to nostalgia

items.prompt: Write alt text for a photo of an empty park bench in autumn. Make it feel like the bench knows it's temporary.

This pre-Monday dread loop has consumed an estimated 11% of all Sunday evenings. No further emotional patches will be issued. Users should upgrade to present-moment awareness or, at minimum, a good television programme.
items.intent: Humorously reject the recurring Sunday anxiety about the upcoming work week.

items.prompt: Can you write a layoff email for a startup that just lost its Series B funding and has to cut half its staff
items.response:
Subject: A Difficult Message From Our Founders

Team,

This is one of the hardest messages we have ever had to send. After extensive conversations with our investors, we have learned that our Series B funding will not be moving forward as anticipated. To preserve the company's runway, we must reduce our team by 50%, effective immediately.

Those affected will hear from HR within the hour. We are covering three months of COBRA and two weeks of severance per year of service.

We are deeply sorry. You deserved better.

Mike & Sara
items.intent: Communicate a devastating funding collapse and emergency headcount cut with a rare trace of founder accountability.

items.prompt: Write a company policy about employees naming their desk plants
items.response: Section 12.4 — Personal Vegetation Identification: Employees who assign proper nouns to desk flora are encouraged to register the name with Facilities Management. Registered plant names will be honored in internal communications. Management reserves the right to decline names deemed detrimental to workplace morale or confusingly similar to existing staff members.
items.intent: Codify a completely harmless personal habit into official governance without actually discouraging it.

items.prompt: Draft a formal PIP for my habit of ignoring text messages for days and then responding with just 'lol'
items.response:
PERFORMANCE IMPROVEMENT PLAN
Department: Personal Relationships
Infraction Code: Communication Negligence

Statement of Deficiency: Subject routinely delays responses to personal messages by 48–96 hours, then issues a single-syllable acknowledgment that fails to address the original content.

Documented Examples: Three unanswered birthday messages; one unacknowledged crisis text; one group thread left on read for nine days.

Expected Behavior: Respond within 24 hours. Response must contain at least one sentence that references the sender's actual message.

Plan:
- Enable notifications for close contacts.
- Draft reply before closing app.
- Retire 'lol' as a standalone response except in cases of genuine laughter.

Check-In: 30 days. Relationships manager (friend group) will assess whether vibes have improved.
items.intent: Formally address a pattern of dismissive and delayed communication with friends and family.

items.prompt: Make a holiday letter that briefly mentions our divorce without making it sound sad
items.response: Warmest wishes from the Delacroix household — now two households, actually! The dog has two Christmas mornings. Life, as they say, finds a way.
items.intent: Announce a divorce to friends and family in a tone that deflects pity.

A Neighbor

items.prompt: write an apology note to my coworker for accidentally replying all to a 200-person thread to say 'ok thx'

Sorry,
Elliot

items.prompt: write a diary entry about waiting for a slow download
items.response:

47% for eleven minutes. Then 48%. I have aged. My download bar is a metaphor for something awful and I refuse to examine it closely. The file is 800 MB. My patience is not.
items.intent: Express exaggerated frustration with waiting for technology to cooperate.

items.prompt: captain's log about a coworker who microwaves fish in the office kitchen
items.response: Stardate 7733.4. At 1213 hours, an atmospheric anomaly was detected across decks three through five. Origin: the communal galley. Lieutenant Voss has again heated the salmon. Crew morale has dipped 14 percent. A passive message was posted to the all-crew channel. It began with "Friendly reminder." It was not friendly.
items.intent: Vent the shared office grievance of inconsiderate kitchen smells.

items.prompt: Write a 911 transcript where someone reports a squirrel stole their granola bar
items.response:
DISPATCHER: 911, what is your emergency?
CALLER: A squirrel ran up and grabbed my granola bar right out of my hand.
DISPATCHER: Were you harmed?
CALLER: Emotionally, yes. It was oat and honey.
DISPATCHER: We don't respond to squirrel-related theft.
CALLER: So there's just no law out here?
items.intent: Highlight how people frame minor wildlife inconveniences as legal injustices demanding official response.

items.prompt: Write a closing argument from the perspective of someone whose neighbor's wind chimes kept them awake
items.response: Night after night, the chimes rang. Not a melody — a provocation. The defendant calls it art. My client calls it 3 a.m. The right to hang decorative metal does not supersede the right to sleep. We ask that the chimes be relocated to the interior of the defendant's home, where only they may suffer.
items.intent: Assert that one neighbor's aesthetic pleasure is another's auditory torment.

items.prompt: Write me an eviction notice for anxiety
items.response:
OFFICIAL NOTICE OF EVICTION

To: Anxiety
Premises: My chest, shoulders, and approximately 40% of my thoughts

You have resided here without invitation, caused structural vibrations in my ribcage, and repeatedly triggered the smoke alarm for no reason. Your lease — which you never signed — is void. Vacate at once. Your security deposit, consisting of several good nights' sleep, will not be returned.

— Occupant
items.intent: Venting frustration at chronic anxiety by treating it as an unwelcome squatter.

items.prompt: Write a formal collections notice for unpaid compliments.
items.response:
NOTICE: COMPLIMENT BALANCE OUTSTANDING

Dear Valued Associate,

A review of your account reveals a balance of seventeen (17) compliments that were freely extended to you and have not been reciprocated. This includes, but is not limited to: haircut acknowledgments, work praise, and one particularly effusive birthday message.

Please remit no fewer than five sincere compliments within ten (10) business days to avoid further action.

Warm but firm regards,
Reciprocity Holdings Inc.
items.intent: Playfully call out a one-sided dynamic where one person gives praise but never receives any in return.

Signal
Brief, then frozen
Your face a smeared mosaic
I nod at pixels of your mouth
Lost voice

My coat goes on the same hook as always.

The elevator opens on the fourth floor.
The lobby smells of floor wax and resignation.

The clock on the wall says 8:47.
The badge reader beeps and turns green.
My coat goes on the same hook as always.
I have done this eight hundred times.

items.prompt: Give me liner notes for an album all about grocery shopping
items.response:
Every Aisle a Revelation (2021)

This album began when the drummer noticed he always bought cilantro and never used it. The bass line on "Produce Section" is modeled on the sound of a cart with one bad wheel. We dedicate this record to the self-checkout machines. They are trying their best.
items.intent: Reframe the ordinary ritual of grocery shopping as the subject of a sincere musical autobiography.

items.prompt: Can you write a podcast episode description for a show about nothing, episode topic: the specific anxiety of sending an email and then immediately closing the tab
items.response: You clicked send. You closed Gmail. You've already forgotten what you said. Host Priya Mehta spends 28 minutes exploring the exact nanosecond after the tab disappears—a liminal zone from which no email has ever returned.
items.intent: Articulate digital post-send regret as a serialized episode theme.

items.prompt: Write a wildly sincere YouTube comment for a compilation of cats knocking things off tables
items.response: They aren't being chaotic. They are testing the permanence of objects, questioning impermanence itself. We all push things off the table eventually.
items.intent: Reframe a silly animal behavior as a profound philosophical act to create comic contrast.

items.prompt: Write the closed caption for when a dog hears its owner's car in the driveway
items.response: [DISTANT ENGINE RECOGNIZED] [EARS PIVOTING] [ENTIRE BODY NOW A QUESTION MARK]
items.intent: Translate canine sensory experience into human-readable emotional notation.

items.prompt: Write a highbrow artist statement about a Post-it note
items.response: The Post-it is provisional memory — a thought too urgent to forget, too uncertain to commit. Its adhesive impermanence mirrors the condition of meaning itself: sticky, removable, perpetually subject to revision.
items.intent: Treat a ubiquitous office object as a philosophical artifact about memory and impermanence.

items.prompt: Write a headnote for chocolate chip cookies where the writer burned them at a school bake sale as a child
items.response: In fourth grade, I brought a tray of charcoal discs to the school bake sale and watched them sit, unsold, while every other item went in the first twenty minutes. My mother said nothing except, well, you'll know for next time. This is what I know now. They sell.
items.intent: Redeem a childhood humiliation through a perfected adult recipe

items.prompt: Create a tasting menu for insomnia
items.response:
FIRST COURSE
Ceiling, examined at length

SECOND COURSE
Phone at 2 a.m. — blue light reduction, full brightness

INTERMEZZO
A worry you forgot you had

MAIN
Half-sleep with notes of podcast voices

NIGHT CAP
The alarm, a surprise finale at dawn
items.intent: Describe the frustrating texture of a sleepless night through culinary metaphor.

items.prompt: write a man page for the act of apologizing
items.response:
APOLOGIZE(1)             Interpersonal Utilities          APOLOGIZE(1)

NAME
       apologize - transmit error acknowledgment to affected party

SYNOPSIS
       apologize [-s sincerity_level] [-r reason] [--no-excuse] RECIPIENT

DESCRIPTION
       apologize(1) issues a structured acknowledgment of a prior error
       to RECIPIENT. Effectiveness degrades sharply if --no-excuse is
       omitted and a justification string is appended to the message body.

OPTIONS
       -s     Set sincerity level (0-10). Values below 4 detected as
              insincere and may worsen relationship(7) state.
       -r     Attach reason for original error. Must not contain "but".
       --no-excuse
              Suppress rationalization trailer. Strongly recommended.

EXIT STATUS
       0      Apology accepted; relationship state partially restored.
       1      Recipient unconvinced. Retry not immediately advised.
       2      Too late. No recovery path available.

BUGS
       "I'm sorry you feel that way" parses as apologize(1) but is
       functionally a no-op.

SEE ALSO
       forgiveness(7), guilt(7), accountability(3)

Interpersonal Utilities 2.1  2026              APOLOGIZE(1)
items.intent: Expose the conditional and often-failed logic behind a genuine apology.

items.prompt: write error page copy for a 404 about missing trust
items.response:
404 — Trust Not Found

This resource is unavailable. It may have been there once, but something happened — a redirect that went wrong, a promise that wasn't kept.

We'd love to help you rebuild it, but we can't do that from here.

Check your connection and try again. Carefully.
items.intent: Frame the breakdown of interpersonal trust as a broken web link with technical-sounding language.

items.prompt: I need alt text for an image of a lighthouse at night. Give it an air of lonely purpose.
items.response: A white lighthouse pulses its beam across black water with no ship in sight, its light completing its arc regardless, the way a promise does not require a witness.

items.prompt: Write a deprecation notice for staying in a friendship that's purely out of obligation.

This connection has been maintained primarily through guilt and shared history with no active joy. Effective immediately, guilt-based renewal is no longer supported. All parties are encouraged to transition gracefully or allow the relationship to reach end-of-life.
items.intent: Validate walking away from a friendship that has outlasted any genuine affection.

items.prompt: Write a brief all-staff layoff email for a media company that's being acquired and restructuring its newsroom
items.response:
Subject: Newsroom Update Following Acquisition

Colleagues,

As you know, we recently completed our acquisition by Meridian Media Group. As part of this transition, we are consolidating certain functions and, unfortunately, eliminating a number of positions across the newsroom and editorial support teams.

Individuals who are impacted will be contacted directly by HR today.

We remain committed to the journalism that defines this publication.

Thank you,
Editor-in-Chief
items.intent: Announce post-acquisition layoffs in a newsroom while obscuring that editorial independence is being gutted.

items.prompt: Draft a formal rule about replying-all to company-wide emails
items.response: Section 6.1 — Mass Email Etiquette: Use of the Reply All function on messages addressed to more than fifty (50) recipients requires written justification submitted to your direct supervisor within one (1) business day of transmission. Repeated unsanctioned Reply All events may trigger a mandatory Email Citizenship Workshop.
items.intent: Create a policy that punishes one of the most universally dreaded office email behaviors.

items.prompt: I never floss even though I keep telling my dentist I do — write a PIP for this
items.response:
PERFORMANCE IMPROVEMENT PLAN
Subject: Oral Hygiene Compliance & Honesty

Background: Employee has, for an estimated four consecutive years, represented flossing frequency as "pretty regularly" during semi-annual dental check-ins. Actual frequency: sporadic, crisis-driven, pre-appointment only.

Dual Deficiency Identified:
1. Failure to floss.
2. False reporting of floss activity to dental authority.

Goals:
- Floss 5 of 7 nights per week within 30 days.
- Discontinue pre-appointment 48-hour flossing blitz intended to deceive gum tissue.
- Cease providing false oral hygiene testimony.

Support Resources: Floss dispenser to be mounted at eye level on bathroom mirror. Habit stacking permitted (floss during TV).

Review: Next dental appointment. Gum health metrics will serve as objective performance data.
items.intent: Create accountability for neglecting basic dental hygiene and lying about it.

items.prompt: Help me write a holiday newsletter that makes our year sound exciting even though nothing happened

Greetings from the Murphys!

2025 was a year of deep investment in the domestic arts. We repainted the guest room (twice). Tom read several books. Nina developed strong opinions about streaming services. The cat remained the cat. We are rested, present, and ready for whatever 2026 chooses to be. Happy holidays to all!
items.intent: Transform a year of monotony into something that sounds intentional and content.

items.prompt: write a yearbook quote bragging that I never once studied and still passed

&

items.prompt: Write a bumper sticker for someone who hates their open-plan office
items.response: HONK IF YOU'VE PRETENDED TO BE ON A CALL TO AVOID CONVERSATION

items.prompt: Draft billboard copy for a restaurant where the only menu item is technically sand
items.response:
SANDY'S FINE DINING
One Item. Zero Compromises.
GLUTEN FREE. DAIRY FREE. FOOD FREE.
Reservations Encouraged. Antacids Provided.
items.intent: Advertise an eatery whose single offering is categorically inedible, framed as a premium experience.

items.prompt: Give me a passive-aggressive yard sign about my neighbor's wind chimes that ring all night
items.response:
YES, WE CAN ALL HEAR THEM

Wind chimes: beautiful by day,
a noise ordinance by night.

Sec. 42-19 | Quiet Hours: 10pm–7am
items.intent: Shame a neighbor into removing or silencing noisy wind chimes using the cover of citing local ordinance.

items.prompt: Make me a complaint letter about the supermarket that rearranges its aisles every few months just to confuse me
items.response:
Dear Store Management,

I am writing to express my profound frustration with your store's policy of periodic, unexplained aisle reorganization. I had, after months of dedicated effort, memorized the precise location of every product I require. That knowledge is now worthless. I spent forty-five minutes last Tuesday searching for oat milk. I found it next to the motor oil. I expect an explanation and a map.

Sincerely,
A Regular Customer
items.intent: Vent about retail layout changes that destroy hard-won grocery store navigation knowledge.

items.prompt: write a short apology for bringing an extremely smelly lunch to an open-plan office
items.response:
To the third floor,

Yes, that was my fish curry. I heated it at 12:04. I understand. I won't do it again, or at minimum I will give advance notice so you can make arrangements.

With regret,
Anna
items.intent: Take responsibility for disrupting an entire open office with an odorous meal.

items.prompt: diary entry about making a bad cup of coffee
items.response:
Dear Diary,

I don't know what I did. I followed the same steps. The same beans. And yet — tragedy. Thin, brown, wrong. I poured it down the drain out of respect for what it could have been. Tomorrow I try again.
items.intent: Mourn a ruined morning ritual with excessive gravity.

items.prompt: can you write a captain's log entry about a performance review
items.response: Stardate 3311.7. Annual evaluation completed. The admiral reviewed my metrics. She said I was "meeting expectations." I thanked her. I do not know what expectations were set. She did not say. She noted I could be "more proactive." I nodded. We shook hands. I returned to my post. I continue to meet the expectations I cannot name.
items.intent: Expose the vagueness and hollow ritual of corporate performance review language.

items.prompt: Create a 911 call where the caller is upset their TV remote stopped working
items.response:
DISPATCHER: 911, what's your emergency?
CALLER: My remote died in the middle of a very important show.
DISPATCHER: Do you need medical assistance?
CALLER: I need new batteries but I can't leave the couch.
DISPATCHER: ...You can't leave the couch?
CALLER: I'm under a blanket. It's cold. Please send someone.
items.intent: Satirize sedentary helplessness by treating a dead TV remote as a search-and-rescue situation.

items.prompt: Please write a courtroom summation for a dispute over someone who always replies-all to group emails unnecessarily
items.response: Thirty-seven recipients. One message: 'Sounds good!' The defendant had options. They chose the nuclear one. My client's inbox — once a place of order — is now an archaeological site of unnecessary acknowledgments. We ask the court to impose a simple remedy: a mandatory course in email etiquette, effective immediately.
items.intent: Satirize the epidemic of pointless reply-all email behavior in workplaces.

items.prompt: Give me a short eviction notice for writer's block
items.response:
EVICTION NOTICE

To: Writer's Block

You have squatted on page one since October. The premises — a perfectly good blank document — have been rendered uninhabitable by your presence. Leave now. The cursor is tired of blinking at you.

— The Author
items.intent: Using absurdist humor to push past a creative block by externalizing it.

items.prompt: Create a collections letter for a friend who borrowed my enthusiasm for their project and never gave it back.
items.response:
RE: UNRETURNED ENTHUSIASM — ACCOUNT #ENT-0047

Dear Friend,

In the spring of last year, you solicited and received a substantial deposit of enthusiasm regarding your artisanal hot sauce venture. You were loaned our full confidence, several hours of brainstorming, and one professionally designed logo concept.

The project has since gone quiet. The enthusiasm has not been returned.

We are prepared to write off the logo but require the enthusiasm back in full.

Persistently yours,
The Lending Bureau of Creative Energy
items.intent: Comically reclaim the emotional investment someone poured into a friend's abandoned side project.

items.prompt: Write a cinquain about airplane mode
items.response:
Silence
Forced and quiet
Notifications suspended
At thirty thousand feet I breathe
Unpinged
items.intent: Appreciate the accidental peace that airplane mode brings.

items.prompt: can you write a pantoum about replying all to a company-wide email by mistake
items.response:
I pressed the wrong button at 10 a.m.
The whole company received my reply.
I wrote only: sounds great, thanks!
Three hundred inboxes lit up at once.

The whole company received my reply.
Someone will write: please remove me from this list.
Three hundred inboxes lit up at once.
I stare at the sent folder in disbelief.

Someone will write: please remove me from this list.
Another reply: I think you meant to send this privately.
I stare at the sent folder in disbelief.
The thread grows longer every second.

Another reply: I think you meant to send this privately.
I pressed the wrong button at 10 a.m.
The thread grows longer every second.
Three hundred inboxes lit up at once.
items.intent: Immortalize the creeping horror of an accidental reply-all cascade.

items.prompt: Create liner notes for a death metal album about filing paperwork
items.response:
FORMS IN TRIPLICATE (2025)

From the void of the inbox to the screaming darkness of the outbox, this record chronicles 47 minutes of pure administrative annihilation. "Revised Attachment (Final_v3)" is for the fallen. You know who you are. Mastered at a volume the HR department would not approve.
items.intent: Apply extreme metal genre conventions to satirize the horrors of office paperwork.

items.prompt: Make a podcast episode description about two people who can't remember what they were about to say
items.response: It was something good. Both of them are pretty sure it was something good. Episode 7: The Thought That Got Away. Featuring 41 minutes of 'no, wait—' and absolutely no resolution.
items.intent: Turn the universal experience of forgetting a passing thought into a comedic podcast premise.

items.prompt: Make a YouTube comment for a video of a guy parallel parking perfectly on the first try
items.response: i have watched this 47 times. i don't drive. i will never drive. this is still the most satisfying thing i have ever seen.
items.intent: Express irrational emotional investment in a mundane skill the commenter will never use.

items.prompt: What would the closed caption say during someone's first bite of a food they used to hate as a child?
items.response: [PAUSING] [RECONSIDERING ROUGHLY 200 DINNER-TABLE ARGUMENTS] [CHEWING SLOWLY]
items.intent: Mark the precise moment a childhood conviction quietly collapses.

items.prompt: Can you write a pompous artist statement about a TV remote control
items.response: I relinquish agency only to reclaim it. The remote is sovereignty made plastic — the hand's extension into the broadcast void, arbitrating narrative, wielding the cut as creative act.
items.intent: Frame channel-surfing as an act of artistic authorship.

items.prompt: Headnote for roast chicken, overly dramatic story about a dinner party that went catastrophically wrong
items.response: The night I served raw chicken to seven people, including my then-boyfriend's mother, is a night I do not discuss in polite company. I have since purchased a thermometer. I have since read about carry-over cooking. I have since, it should be said, acquired a different boyfriend. This recipe is tested. I cannot stress that enough.
items.intent: Warn readers away from a past mistake while teaching the right technique

items.prompt: Design a tasting menu for a job interview
items.response:
APERITIF
Hand sanitizer applied in the lobby

AMUSE-BOUCHE
Strength named as weakness

FIRST COURSE
Resume highlights, heavily curated

SECOND COURSE
Behavioral question with a STAR-method finish

MAIN
Salary discussion, undercooked

DESSERT
"We'll be in touch" — gelatin-set, flavourless
items.intent: Satirize the ritualized performance of corporate job interviews.

items.prompt: give me a unix man page for boredom
items.response:
BOREDOM(7)               Human Affective States            BOREDOM(7)

NAME
       boredom - stimulus deficit condition

SYNOPSIS
       boredom [--duration minutes] [--context environment]

DESCRIPTION
       boredom(7) arises when incoming stimulus rate falls below the
       host's minimum engagement threshold. Manifests as restlessness,
       compulsive device-checking, and unsolicited reorganization of
       nearby objects.

OPTIONS
       --duration   Elapsed time below threshold. Symptoms scale linearly.
       --context    Environment string (e.g., meeting, commute, vacation).

SIDE EFFECTS
       May spontaneously launch creativity(7) in susceptible hosts.
       In others, launches doomscrolling(1) instead.

DIAGNOSIS
       grep -r "nothing to do" ~/thoughts

TREATMENT
       Introduce novel stimulus. Avoid television after 11pm as substitute.

SEE ALSO
       restlessness(7), creativity(7), doomscrolling(1), meetings(8)

Human Affective States 1.4   2026                     BOREDOM(7)
items.intent: Describe the understimulated mind as a system starved of meaningful input.

items.prompt: write a 404 page for an app about finding closure
items.response:
404 — Closure Not Found

The ending you were looking for could not be located.

It may never load. That's not a bug. That's the feature nobody asked for.

In the meantime, the page you came from is still there.
items.intent: Capture the impossibility of achieving emotional closure using web error metaphors.

items.prompt: Write alt text for a photo of hands typing on a keyboard at 3am.
items.response: Two pale hands hover over a backlit keyboard in total darkness, the glow catching knuckle hair and a fading ink stain, suggesting work that could not wait for morning.
items.intent: Convey desperate late-night creation through the physical details an accessibility description must name.

items.prompt: Give me a deprecation notice for the habit of apologizing when you don't mean it.
items.response:
DEPRECATION NOTICE

Behavior: Reflexive Apology (v0.0–current)
Status: DEPRECATED

This automatic "sorry" has diluted the currency of genuine remorse across the board. Effective today, hollow apologies will not receive support. Users should migrate to either a sincere apology or no apology at all.
items.intent: Challenge the reflexive social habit of apologizing without genuine remorse.

items.prompt: Help me write an email to employees about layoffs at a bank following a merger
items.response:
Subject: Organizational Update — Combined Company Structure

Dear Colleagues,

As we move forward with the integration of First Capital Bank and Hargrove Financial, we are announcing changes to our combined organizational structure. After careful review, we have identified areas of redundancy that require consolidation, resulting in position eliminations across several departments.

Affected employees will be contacted individually by their manager and HR partner today.

We are committed to treating all impacted colleagues with dignity and respect throughout this process.

Regards,
Integration Leadership Office
items.intent: Inform staff that the merger has made many of their roles redundant without saying the word 'redundant' plainly.

items.prompt: Create an official handbook rule about using speakerphone at your desk in an open office
items.response: Section 7.8 — Open-Plan Audio Conduct: Speakerphone usage at unenclosed workstations is prohibited between the hours of 8:00 AM and 6:00 PM, Monday through Friday. Employees requiring hands-free communication must reserve a Focus Bubble Pod or demonstrate, in writing, that both hands were medically unavailable.
items.intent: Formally restrict a widely acknowledged open-office nuisance while hinting at the absurdity of justifying exceptions.

items.prompt: Write a PIP for myself for buying things I already own because I forget what I have
items.response:
PERFORMANCE IMPROVEMENT PLAN
Filing Entity: Personal Finance Division
Case No.: DUPLICATE-007

Charge: Redundant Acquisition of Owned Goods.

Incidents on Record: Three tubes of the same hand cream (locations: bathroom, gym bag, Amazon cart); two identical USB-C cables purchased six weeks apart; four bottles of cumin.

Financial Impact: Estimated $200–$400 annually in duplicate household goods.

Corrective Actions:
1. Conduct pantry and medicine cabinet inventory before any purchase.
2. Implement one-location rule: consumables stored in single designated zone.
3. Check Amazon order history prior to re-ordering any item.

Success Metric: Zero duplicate purchases in 60-day review window.

Consequences: Continued failure will result in mandatory subscription to an organizational podcast, which employee will also buy twice.
items.intent: Stop wasting money by purchasing items already stocked at home due to poor household inventory awareness.

items.prompt: Write a cheery holiday letter where Mom's memory issues are mentioned but kept light
items.response:
Holiday love from the Brennan family!

Mom is as spirited as ever, finding joy in small moments and occasionally in rooms she didn't mean to walk into. We've all become very skilled at gentle reminders and excellent at repeating our names. She still beats us all at cards. We're grateful for every day, truly. Love to all.
items.intent: Acknowledge a parent's cognitive decline to the extended family without alarm or grief.

items.prompt: write a yearbook quote from the perspective of someone who peaked in high school
items.response: "These were the best years of my life and I know it. No notes."
items.intent: Capturing the tragic self-awareness of someone who senses their highlight reel is already over.

items.prompt: Create a whimsical invitation for the wedding of autumn and melancholy
items.response:
Together with their families,

AUTUMN
and
MELANCHOLY

invite you to celebrate their inevitable union.

Ceremony held at dusk, among the turning leaves.
Guests are encouraged to wear something they once loved.

No rice. Pressed flowers only.
items.intent: Evoke the bittersweet emotional texture of fall through the metaphor of a romantic ceremony.

items.prompt: Bumper sticker about AI taking jobs
items.response: MY CAREER WAS DISRUPTED SO YOU COULD HAVE A CHATBOT
items.intent: Voice resentment toward the human cost of automation.

items.prompt: Write an ad for a candle company whose candles smell exclusively like wet dog
items.response:
AMBIANCE, REDEFINED.
Scented Candle Co. — "Golden Retriever After Rain"
Also Available: Labrador at the Lake. Poodle in November.
Light One. Regret Nothing.
items.intent: Market a home fragrance product whose scent profile is universally considered unpleasant.

items.prompt: Create a yard sign supporting the preservation of the old oak tree the HOA wants to remove
items.response:
SAVE THE OAK

125 years old.
Home to 47 species.
Irreplaceable.

Vote NO on Proposal 3
items.intent: Mobilize neighborhood sentiment to vote against cutting down a beloved old tree.

items.prompt: Write a formal complaint letter to a café about the background music being just slightly too loud to ignore but too quiet to enjoy
items.response:
To the Management of [Café Name],

I wish to raise a concern about your in-house audio environment. The music your establishment plays occupies an acoustical no-man's-land: too present to permit concentration, too muffled to constitute an aesthetic experience. It is neither ambiance nor entertainment. It is a hum of mild wrongness. I would welcome either silence or commitment. The current arrangement is neither.

Yours truly,
A Former Regular
items.intent: Protest an audio atmosphere that is uniquely suited to maximum irritation without any redeeming quality.

items.prompt: draft an apology note to my friend for spoiling a book they were clearly only on page 12 of
items.response:
Jess,

I'm so sorry I told you what happens to the dog. You had your bookmark at page 12. I saw it. I said it anyway. That was a genuine failure of social awareness and I feel bad. I'll buy you a different book.

Love,
Cal
items.intent: Express remorse for spoiling a plot point in a book a friend had barely started reading.

items.prompt: write a diary entry from the perspective of someone who couldn't find parking
items.response:
Dear Diary,

Four laps. FOUR. Each empty-looking space revealed itself to be a fire hydrant or a phantom vision. I parked two blocks away in shame. My legs work fine; this is not the point. The point is principle.
items.intent: Vent about the petty ordeal of finding a parking spot.

items.prompt: write a captain's log about finding an unmarked container in the office fridge
items.response: Stardate 4409.2. Conducted routine inspection of the crew refrigeration unit. Discovered a vessel — tupperware, green lid — bearing no registry markings. Contents unidentified. Age: unknown. Origin: disputed. Posted a notice. No one has claimed it. It remains in sector B, second shelf. I have sealed off the adjacent yogurt.
items.intent: Narrate the universal mystery of abandoned office fridge items.

items.prompt: Write a 911 transcript about someone calling because their WiFi went out
items.response:
DISPATCHER: 911, state your emergency.
CALLER: The internet is down.
DISPATCHER: Is anyone hurt?
CALLER: I'm in the middle of streaming something. This counts.
DISPATCHER: Have you tried restarting the router?
CALLER: That's not your job to ask. Your job is to help.
DISPATCHER: I... agree that is not my job.
items.intent: Parody modern internet dependency by treating a service outage as a matter for emergency services.

items.prompt: Draft a closing argument for a plaintiff whose coworker microwaved fish in the shared kitchen
items.response: The smell arrived before the defendant did. It settled into the upholstery, the carpet, the collective memory of everyone present on November 14th. No warning was given. No apology was offered. The kitchen was a shared space; it is now a crime scene. We ask for a fish-free workplace and acknowledgment of what was lost.
items.intent: Formalize the unspoken office rule against reheating pungent foods.

items.prompt: Can you write an eviction notice for boredom
items.response:
NOTICE OF TERMINATION OF TENANCY

To: Boredom
Location: Sunday afternoon, approximately 3 p.m.

You have occupied this time slot without enriching it. You have flattened all texture and driven off every worthwhile activity. The landlord — that is, my attention — has found a more suitable tenant. You have until dinner to leave. Do not drag your feet; you always do.

Sincerely, Someone With Things To Do
items.intent: Dramatizing the desire to replace idle time with something meaningful.

items.prompt: Write a collections letter for borrowed credibility.
items.response:
CREDIBILITY RECLAMATION NOTICE

To the Debtor,

On multiple occasions, this office extended you the benefit of our established reputation. You were introduced at events, vouched for in professional settings, and endorsed in at least two email chains.

Your subsequent conduct has depleted that credibility to a negative balance. We are formally requesting full restitution, or, failing that, a public acknowledgment that you acted alone.

Filing this under: Lessons Learned.

Yours,
The Reputation Desk
items.intent: Demand accountability from someone who leveraged another person's good name and then behaved badly.

items.prompt: Write me a cinquain about the spinning loading wheel
items.response:
Wheel
Spinning, spinning
Promising it will load soon
The seconds pile into minutes
Still spins
items.intent: Capture the existential waiting that comes with a frozen loading indicator.

items.prompt: write a pantoum about the office printer jamming every single time
items.response:
Paper 2 is jammed, the screen declares.
I open every door I can find.
There is no paper in door number two.
The manual offers no comfort at all.

I open every door I can find.
A small accordion of paper hides inside.
The manual offers no comfort at all.
I smooth the sheet and feed it back.

A small accordion of paper hides inside.
The queue has sixteen jobs stacked behind mine.
I smooth the sheet and feed it back.
The screen blinks: Paper 2 is jammed.

The queue has sixteen jobs stacked behind mine.
Paper 2 is jammed, the screen declares.
I open every door I can find.
There is no paper in door number two.
items.intent: Celebrate the infuriating ritual of unjamming a shared office printer.

items.prompt: Write album liner notes for a concept record about parallel parking
items.response:
Fourteen Attempts (2020)

Side A is the approach. Side B is the shame. These songs were written over three years of living in a city where parking was abundant but the spaces were always slightly too small. The title track ends without resolution. That was a choice.
items.intent: Transform the frustration of urban parking into a narrative arc suitable for a concept album.

items.prompt: Describe a podcast episode where the hosts just talk about staring out a window on a bus
items.response: Nobody on this bus is going anywhere interesting, and that includes our hosts. Window. Rain maybe. A parking lot that's somehow both full and empty. This is Episode 3.
items.intent: Render the passive contemplation of public transit as a podcast episode.

items.prompt: Write an unhinged YouTube comment for a timelapse of bread rising in an oven
items.response: something is being BORN in there and nobody is talking about it
items.intent: Anthropomorphize a baking process to make the mundane feel cosmically significant.

items.prompt: Write me a closed caption for someone reading a text message they weren't meant to see
items.response: [SCREEN BRIGHTNESS LOWERING INSTINCTIVELY] [HEART RATE NOTED BUT NOT DISPLAYED]
items.intent: Capture the involuntary physical response to accidental discovery.

items.prompt: Artist statement for a ballpoint pen, make it sound very serious
items.response: The ballpoint pen is the democratization of inscription — a cheap oracle that leaks the self onto any surface. I draw no distinction between signature and scar.
items.intent: Present everyday writing as a weighty act of self-expression through an overblown artistic lens.

items.prompt: Write a cookbook headnote about gazpacho where the narrator discovered it had a heatstroke once
items.response: It was August in Seville and the thermometer had given up. My body followed shortly after. I was horizontal in a pension with the shutters drawn when someone brought me a glass of cold red soup and my entire understanding of what soup could be was revised. The doctor said rest. The gazpacho said otherwise. I got up.
items.intent: Pair a cold soup with the physical shock of discovering it at the right moment

items.prompt: Draft a tasting menu for procrastination
items.response:
AMUSE-BOUCHE
The task, acknowledged

FIRST COURSE
Email inbox, reorganized for no reason

SECOND COURSE
A Wikipedia spiral with a drizzle of guilt

INTERMEZZO
Snack, unnecessary

MAIN
Panic, rushed, served forty minutes before deadline

DESSERT
Relief, curdled slightly — you did it, but still
items.intent: Trace the familiar emotional and behavioral stages of avoiding a task.

items.prompt: write a man page for going on a first date
items.response:
FIRST-DATE(1)            Social Interaction Suite        FIRST-DATE(1)

NAME
       first-date - initialize romantic evaluation session

SYNOPSIS
       first-date [-v venue] [-t topic_list] [--no-phone] PARTNER

DESCRIPTION
       first-date(1) opens a bidirectional channel with PARTNER for
       the purpose of mutual evaluation. Session duration typically
       1-3 hours. Handshake includes: greeting, seating, initial small
       talk, and escalating topic negotiation.

OPTIONS
       -v     Venue selection. Avoid movies (eliminates channel bandwidth).
       -t     Comma-separated approved topic list. Exclude: exes, salary,
              children_count_expectations.
       --no-phone
              Disable background device polling. Strongly recommended.

RETURN VALUE
       0      Mutual interest confirmed. Proceed to second-date(1).
       1      Interest asymmetric. Send polite_decline(1) within 48h.
       2      Catastrophic incompatibility. See: ghosting(1).

BUGS
       Hosts frequently misrepresent version string during session.

SEE ALSO
       second-date(1), ghosting(1), compatibility-check(3)

Social Interaction Suite 1.0 2026            FIRST-DATE(1)
items.intent: Capture the anxious protocol-checking nature of early romantic encounters.

items.prompt: create a funny 404 page for a productivity app where the user's focus has gone missing
items.response:
404 — Focus Not Found

Hm. Where were we?

Oh right — you were looking for something. We were too. Something important.

Anyway.

Go back to the dashboard and try not to click anything interesting.
items.intent: Use a 404 page structure to enact the very distraction it's describing.

items.prompt: Alt text for a photo of a mirror reflecting an empty room — hint at whether the room has always been empty.
items.response: A tall oval mirror leans against a white wall, reflecting a room identical to itself except for small rectangular shadows where furniture once stood against the baseboard.
items.intent: Use furniture-shadow evidence to imply a life that departed without announcing itself.

items.prompt: Write a deprecation notice for multitasking.
items.response:
DEPRECATION NOTICE

Cognitive Feature: Multitasking (human)
Status: DEPRECATED — was never actually supported
