Research confirms this feature was always sequential task-switching with overhead costs. No patch exists. Users are advised to do one thing at a time. Performance improvements are expected to be immediate.
items.intent: Debunk the myth of productive multitasking while nudging toward focused work.

items.prompt: Generate a layoff announcement email for a pharmaceutical company cutting its R&D division after a drug trial failure
items.response:
Subject: Update on R&D Organizational Structure

Team,

Following a thorough review of our pipeline and the results of our recent Phase III study, we have made the decision to realign our research and development organization. Regrettably, this realignment includes the elimination of 340 positions within the R&D division.

We recognize the profound impact this has on our colleagues and their families, and we are committed to providing meaningful transition support.

Thank you for your scientific contributions.

Best,
Chief Scientific Officer
items.intent: Use bureaucratic language to announce mass science-division layoffs triggered by a clinical trial that failed.

items.prompt: Write a policy for employees who take excessively long coffee breaks
items.response: Section 5.3 — Beverage Acquisition Leave: Each employee is entitled to two (2) beverage-acquisition absences per workday not to exceed eleven (11) minutes each, inclusive of pour time. Employees whose absences consistently approach or exceed the allotted window will be referred to the Time Stewardship Committee for coaching.
items.intent: Apply rigorous time-tracking language to something trivially human, making the obsession with productivity visible.

items.prompt: Generate a performance improvement plan for my inability to cook actual meals and just eating crackers for dinner
items.response:
PERFORMANCE IMPROVEMENT PLAN
Division: Nutritional Self-Care
Status: Critical

Deficiency: Subject has normalized the assembly of crackers, cheese product, and miscellaneous condiment packets as a complete evening meal on four or more nights per week.

Nutritional Gap: No vegetables consumed in documented 11-day stretch. Iron levels: theoretical.

Objectives:
- Prepare a meal involving heat on at least 3 weeknights per week.
- Own and use at least one pan within 14 days.
- Define "cooking" as something distinct from "opening."

Milestones:
Week 1: Scrambled eggs counts. Progress.
Week 3: One meal with two food groups.
Week 6: Invite witness (friend, partner, delivery driver) to observe completed meal.

Note: Crackers are not banned. Crackers may not be dinner.
items.intent: Address a pattern of poor adult nutrition driven by laziness and failure to cook.

items.prompt: Draft a holiday letter that hints our teenager had a rough year without saying what happened
items.response:
Season's greetings from the Okafor family!

Ella has shown real growth this year — the kind that only comes from difficult seasons and long conversations and a therapist named Greg who we all now consider part of the family. She is stronger, wiser, and sleeping better. We are proud beyond words. The rest of us are fine. Happy holidays.
items.intent: Signal that a teenager went through something serious while preserving their privacy.

items.prompt: help me write a yearbook quote about how I'm definitely going to marry my high school boyfriend
items.response: "Tyler + me = forever. Colleges can separate us but destiny can't. Mrs. Tyler Beaumont, starting June."
items.intent: Publicly committing to a teenage relationship as a permanent life plan.

items.prompt: Write an invitation for the union of a to-do list and a second to-do list
items.response:
Please join us for the wedding of

THE ORIGINAL TO-DO LIST
&
THE NEW TO-DO LIST (started because the first one got too messy)

Date: Sometime this week
Venue: A fresh notebook

The couple is registered at the stationery store.
Existing tasks are not invited.
items.intent: Make a joke about productivity spiral where organizing itself becomes a new task.

items.prompt: Can you write a bumper sticker about notification overload?
items.response: I HAVE 3,847 UNREAD MESSAGES AND I AM AT PEACE
items.intent: Claim ironic acceptance of a chaotic digital inbox.

items.prompt: Come up with billboard copy for a timeshare located entirely inside an active volcano
items.response:
YOUR FOREVER HOME AWAITS
KILAUEA RIDGE RESORT & TIMESHARE
"Warm. Very Warm."
Ask About Our 99-Year Lease. (Terms Subject to Geological Activity)
items.intent: Sell vacation property that is physically dangerous and therefore unsellable to any rational buyer.

items.prompt: Write a yard sign that politely asks people not to park in front of my mailbox
items.response:
MAILBOX ZONE

Blocking = No Mail Delivery

Please park 3 ft. clear
Per USPS Policy
items.intent: Stop neighbors and visitors from blocking their mailbox without starting a dispute.

items.prompt: Can you write a complaint letter about a coworker who replies-all to every company-wide email with 'Thanks!'
items.response:
To HR and IT,

I write on behalf of my inbox. A colleague has developed a practice of responding to all-staff communications — announcements, safety alerts, birthday notices — with a single word: 'Thanks!' Each occurrence notifies 340 employees simultaneously. The cumulative effect on productivity, spirit, and available storage is difficult to overstate. I request this behavior be addressed through either training or technical intervention.

Thank you (privately),
An Affected Employee
items.intent: Stop a coworker's reflexive reply-all habit that floods hundreds of inboxes with meaningless acknowledgment.

items.prompt: write an apology note for laughing at my sister's serious pottery at a craft fair
items.response:
Mara,

The vase you made is a legitimate piece of art and I should not have laughed. It is asymmetrical in an intentional way. I see that now. You worked hard on it and I was dismissive, and I'm sorry. It's actually on my shelf.

Your sister
items.intent: Make amends for mocking a family member's sincere handmade creative work in public.

items.prompt: give me a diary entry about stepping on a lego at night
items.response:
Dear Diary,

At 2:14 AM, I met my nemesis. It was small. It was yellow. It sent white lightning up through my foot and into the silent core of my soul. I did not scream. I suffered in darkness, which is worse.
items.intent: Transform a universal household injury into melodrama.

items.prompt: generate a captain's log about a zoom call where everyone is on mute by accident
items.response: Stardate 8814.5. Hailing frequencies opened at 1400. Commander Thatch appeared to be speaking. Her lips moved with great conviction. No audio received. I signaled. Others signaled. She continued. The chat filled with "you're on mute." She nodded, as though hearing us. She was not hearing us. She finished. She left the call. We had received nothing.
items.intent: Capture the farcical communication failure of video call mute errors.

items.prompt: Generate a 911 transcript where someone reports a rude cashier
items.response:
DISPATCHER: 911, what's your emergency?
CALLER: The cashier at the grocery store did not say 'have a nice day.'
DISPATCHER: I'm sorry?
CALLER: She said 'have a good one.' That's different. That's cold.
DISPATCHER: Ma'am, is there any danger—
CALLER: To my faith in humanity? Tremendous.
items.intent: Mock the escalation of petty social grievances into formal complaints by routing them through emergency dispatch.

items.prompt: Help me write a summation for a case where someone borrowed a pen and never returned it
items.response: It was a good pen. Weighted. Reliable. My client did not lend it carelessly — they handed it over with the implicit understanding that pens travel in circles. That circle was broken. The defendant still has the pen. Or they don't, and that is somehow worse. We seek its return, or restitution of equal blue ink.
items.intent: Elevate the borrowing of a pen into a philosophical betrayal of trust.

items.prompt: Write an eviction notice for perfectionism
items.response:
FORMAL EVICTION NOTICE

To: Perfectionism
Occupying: My creative process, deadlines, and the kitchen

Despite repeated warnings, you have refused to allow any project to leave as finished. You have held eighteen drafts hostage and repainted the bathroom four times. Good Enough has been waiting in the hall for months. You are hereby removed. Do not revise this notice.

— Management
items.intent: Acknowledging that an obsession with flawlessness is preventing progress and completion.

items.prompt: Draft me a collections letter from a conscience to a person who's been ignoring it.
items.response:
FROM THE DESK OF YOUR CONSCIENCE
Account Status: Severely Delinquent

Dear You,

This office has been attempting to reach you for approximately four years. Our signals — a tight chest at 2 a.m., an inexplicable urge to apologize in the grocery store — have gone systematically unacknowledged.

The outstanding balance is substantial: two unkept promises, one scapegoated colleague, and a series of small cruelties you classified as "just being honest."

We do not accept deflection as payment. Please respond at your earliest convenience.

Always here,
Your Conscience
items.intent: Personify a suppressed moral sense demanding that someone finally reckon with their behavior.

items.prompt: Compose a cinquain about password requirements
items.response:
Eight
Chars, one symbol
Capital and a number
The system rejects what I know
Forgotten
items.intent: Satirize the impossible complexity demanded by password rules.

items.prompt: give me a pantoum about the Tuesday all-hands that gets rescheduled every week
items.response:
The calendar invite moved again this week.
We will meet when schedules align.
The slide deck has been ready since March.
Conflicts appear in every time zone.

We will meet when schedules align.
Someone is traveling; someone is sick.
Conflicts appear in every time zone.
The agenda grows longer in waiting.

Someone is traveling; someone is sick.
The meeting is pushed to the following Tuesday.
The agenda grows longer in waiting.
The slide deck has been ready since March.

The meeting is pushed to the following Tuesday.
The calendar invite moved again this week.
Conflicts appear in every time zone.
We will meet when schedules align.
items.intent: Satirize the perpetual deferral of a mandatory all-hands meeting.

items.prompt: Can you write liner notes for an ambient album about loading screens
items.response:
Please Wait... (2024)

Thirteen tracks. Thirteen bars of progress that never quite reach one hundred percent. This record was composed for the space between action and result — that thin corridor of anticipation where nothing is guaranteed. Do not skip the silence. The silence is the point.
items.intent: Use ambient music conventions to meditate on digital waiting and deferred gratification.

items.prompt: Write a podcast episode description about an episode where hosts discuss the color of grocery store ceilings
items.response: White. Off-white. That one fluorescent tube that flickers over the cereal aisle. This week, Tom and Keiko look up—something they maintain nobody has done before—and spend an hour reporting back.
items.intent: Draw comedic attention to a completely overlooked architectural detail in a mundane setting.

items.prompt: Write a YouTube comment for a video where someone solves a Rubik's cube in under 10 seconds
items.response: I have a PhD in mathematics and I cannot explain what I just witnessed. Retiring.
items.intent: Use hyperbolic professional surrender to convey awe at a speedcubing feat.

items.prompt: Give me a closed caption for a grandmother teaching her grandchild to make bread without any measuring cups
items.response: [KNOWLEDGE TRANSMITTED BY FEEL, NOT FORMULA] [HANDS DEMONSTRATING WHAT WORDS CANNOT]
items.intent: Document the transfer of embodied knowledge across generations.

items.prompt: Write a pretentious artist statement about a paper clip
items.response: The paper clip binds without permanence, unites without soldering. It is ethics in wire — the gentlest possible insistence on connection, leaving no mark upon what it holds together.
items.intent: Philosophize about a minimalist fastener as a metaphor for non-destructive attachment.

items.prompt: Headnote for a simple vinaigrette involving a story about a chef who humiliated the narrator
items.response: A chef once told me, in front of his entire kitchen, that my vinaigrette tasted like a gas station. He was right, which made it worse. I spent three years learning exactly what he meant. The ratio is three to one. The acid goes in last. You emulsify properly. You do not go back to his restaurant.
items.intent: Turn professional embarrassment into technique mastery for a basic dressing

items.prompt: Write me a tasting menu for childhood summers
items.response:
AMUSE-BOUCHE
Sprinkler water, accidental

FIRST COURSE
Popsicle, orange — dripping on pavement

SECOND COURSE
Boredom with notes of sunscreen and possibility

MAIN
A long afternoon with no obligations, slow-roasted

DESSERT
The last day before school, served cold
items.intent: Evoke the sensory nostalgia of a carefree summer childhood.

items.prompt: can you make a man page for nostalgia
items.response:
NOSTALGIA(7)             Human Emotional Suite           NOSTALGIA(7)

NAME
       nostalgia - read-only access to idealized past states

SYNOPSIS
       nostalgia [--era string] [--fidelity low|high] [--trigger object]

DESCRIPTION
       nostalgia(7) retrieves cached representations of prior life
       states, applying a positive-bias filter that suppresses negative
       metadata. Results should not be treated as ground truth.

OPTIONS
       --era      Target time period (e.g., childhood, "the 90s", college).
       --fidelity Accuracy of retrieved memory. Default: low.
       --trigger  Sensory object initiating recall (smell is highest-bandwidth).

WARNINGS
       Returned data is lossy and subject to rewrite on each access.
       Comparing present state to nostalgia output causes depressive drift.
       Do not use as decision-making input.

SEE ALSO
       memory(3), grief(7), idealization(7), the-good-old-days(8)

Human Emotional Suite 1.0    2026                  NOSTALGIA(7)
items.intent: Highlight how memory is reconstructive, biased, and unreliable as a guide to the present.

items.prompt: write the copy for a 404 page on a site about creative inspiration
items.response:
404 — Inspiration Not Found

Nothing here. Just a blank page and a blinking cursor.

You know this feeling.

Take a walk. Make coffee. Stare out a window for exactly long enough. It'll come back.
items.intent: Describe the creative block experience using the language of a broken URL.

items.prompt: Create alt text for an image of a child's drawing of a house. Imply the child understood something adults have forgotten.
items.response: A crayon house with a yellow sun in every corner of the sky, as though the child saw no reason the light should come from only one direction.
items.intent: Preserve a child's uncorrected cosmology as a quiet critique of learned convention.

items.prompt: Draft a deprecation notice for keeping a toxic ex's number.
items.response:
DEPRECATION NOTICE

Contact: [Former Partner] (cached)
Status: DEPRECATED — long overdue

This entry has been retained beyond its useful life and continues to consume emotional storage. The number will be removed in the next maintenance window. No rollback path will be provided.
items.intent: Encourage finally deleting a former partner's contact after a difficult breakup.

items.prompt: Write a layoff email for a logistics company that's replacing warehouse workers with automation
items.response:
Subject: Workforce Transition Update

To Our Fulfillment Center Colleagues,

As part of our commitment to operational excellence, we have invested significantly in automation technology across our warehouse facilities. As a result of this evolution, we will be transitioning a number of roles and, regrettably, eliminating certain positions.

Impacted employees will receive a minimum of 60 days' notice and access to our Workforce Transition Program.

We are grateful for your years of hard work and dedication.

Warmly,
Operations Leadership
items.intent: Announce that robots are taking workers' jobs while framing the elimination as an 'evolution' and offering minimal comfort.

items.prompt: Generate an employee handbook rule about desk clutter
items.response: Section 3.11 — Workstation Visual Load Standards: Personal items displayed on desk surfaces may not exceed a combined footprint of four (4) square feet or a vertical height of fourteen (14) inches measured from the desk surface. Items exceeding these parameters must be stored in provided drawers or removed from the premises. Enforcement photos will be taken quarterly.
items.intent: Quantify tidiness in an office setting to an absurdly precise and auditable standard.

items.prompt: Make a serious-sounding PIP for my habit of apologizing for things that aren't my fault
items.response:
PERFORMANCE IMPROVEMENT PLAN
Focus Area: Verbal Accountability Calibration

Issue: Employee (Self) issues unsolicited apologies at a rate disproportionate to actual culpability. Documented triggers include: someone else bumping into self; weather; restaurant running out of an item; friend's own bad decision; existence.

Impact: Erodes personal credibility; contributes to diffuse guilt; confuses recipients who did nothing wrong.

Standards Required:
- Apology reserved for events where subject holds partial or full responsibility.
- "I'm sorry to hear that" permitted as empathy. "I'm sorry" for inanimate object collision is not.

Action Steps:
1. Pause 2 seconds before apologizing. Ask: did I cause this?
2. Substitute: "That's frustrating" or "Are you okay?"
3. Track apology events for two weeks in notes app.

Review: 45 days. Success = fewer than 3 unwarranted apologies per week.
items.intent: Build a plan to stop reflexively over-apologizing and develop more accurate self-accountability.

items.prompt: Write a newsletter where we're positive about my husband's early retirement even though he didn't choose it
items.response:
Cheers from the Fontaine household!

The big news: Paul is retired! At 51! He is embracing this unexpected gift of time with characteristic grace and only moderate restlessness. Projects are being started. Lists are being made. We are calling it a sabbatical for now. More updates to follow. Wishing you all a joyful season!
items.intent: Put a positive frame on a layoff that resulted in involuntary early retirement.

items.prompt: generate a senior quote about hating the principal and finally being free of them
items.response: "Mr. Petrakis, if you're reading this: you lost. See you never."
items.intent: Settling a score with a school authority figure on the way out the door.

items.prompt: Generate a wedding invitation for the marriage of nostalgia and the internet
items.response:
You are warmly invited to celebrate the union of

NOSTALGIA & THE INTERNET

Held continuously, everywhere, since approximately 2007.

Venue: A comment section on a video you haven't thought about in fifteen years.
Dress: Whatever you wore then.

Tears are expected. Screenshots encouraged.
items.intent: Capture how online culture constantly resurfaces personal memories from the past.

items.prompt: Make a bumper sticker about housing affordability
items.response: RENTING UNTIL DEATH — THE AMERICAN DREAM, UPDATED
items.intent: Satirize the collapse of homeownership as a generational milestone.

items.prompt: Write billboard copy advertising a gym where all the equipment is also antiques and cannot be touched
items.response:
GET FIT. DON'T TOUCH ANYTHING.
HEIRLOOM FITNESS — Est. 1887
Admire the Barbells. Respect the Rowing Machine.
Results Not Guaranteed. Guilt Is.
items.intent: Promote a fitness facility that defeats its own purpose by forbidding use of its own inventory.

items.prompt: Make a yard sign protesting the new streetlights the city put in that shine into my bedroom
items.response:
LIGHT POLLUTION
IS REAL POLLUTION

City Council: Restore Our
Dark Skies & Our Sleep

Petition at DarkOakwood.org
items.intent: Express frustration about intrusive new streetlights and organize community opposition.

items.prompt: Draft a complaint letter to a hotel about the pillows being too soft and too many of them
items.response:
Dear Guest Services,

I write to report a pillow situation of some urgency. My room contained seven pillows, not one of which offered structural support of any kind. Together they formed something resembling a cloud — which sounds appealing but is, in practice, a neck injury waiting to happen. I spent the night balanced on an unstable softness. I respectfully request that future rooms be appointed with at least one pillow of conviction.

Kind regards,
A Guest
items.intent: Request firmer, fewer pillows by framing an excess of softness as a legitimate hospitality failure.

items.prompt: write an apology note for ghosting my dentist for three years
items.response:
Dear Dr. Flemming,

I owe you an apology and, more pressingly, a visit. It has been approximately three years since I responded to your reminders. I am not proud of this. I would like to come back, if you'll have me. My teeth are fine. Probably.

Sincerely,
Patrick S.
items.intent: Reconnect with a dental provider after years of avoidance while acknowledging the irresponsibility.

items.prompt: diary entry for someone who ordered food delivery and it took way too long
items.response:
Dear Diary,

I ordered at 6:47. It is now 8:21. The app says my driver is 'nearby.' Nearby is a lie. I have refreshed the map forty times. The little car icon has circled the same block like a ghost. I have eaten crackers. I am diminished.
items.intent: Document the modern suffering of waiting for delivery that never seems to arrive.

items.prompt: write me a captain's log entry about onboarding a new employee
items.response: Stardate 2255.6. Recruit Hadley reported for duty at 0845. Badge access: pending. System credentials: pending. Desk chair: incorrect height. I gave her a tour of the facilities. She asked questions I could not answer. I directed her to HR. HR directed her back to me. She sat with me for the afternoon and watched me send emails. I believe this is called orientation.
items.intent: Satirize the chaotic and underprepared nature of corporate onboarding.

items.prompt: Write a 911 call transcript about someone who burned toast and wants to report it
items.response:
DISPATCHER: 911, what is your emergency?
CALLER: I burned my toast.
DISPATCHER: Is there a fire in your home?
CALLER: Was. It's over. The toast is a total loss.
DISPATCHER: So no current fire?
CALLER: No, just grief. Is there someone I can speak to about the grief?
items.intent: Turn a trivial kitchen mishap into a darkly comic request for emotional support from 911.

items.prompt: Write a closing argument for someone upset that a friend spoiled the ending of a TV show
items.response: My client had three episodes left. Three. The defendant knew this. They spoke anyway. What was taken cannot be returned — anticipation, once lost, is lost forever. The defendant may call it 'just a show.' My client calls it six weeks of careful avoidance, destroyed in eleven words. We seek acknowledgment and a future policy of silence.
items.intent: Litigate the breach of the informal spoiler social contract.

items.prompt: Write an eviction notice for denial
items.response:
NOTICE TO QUIT

To: Denial
Unit: The ground floor of my reasoning

You have blocked every window with convenient explanations and rearranged the facts to suit the décor. You were never supposed to be a permanent resident. Reality has filed a formal complaint. You have 10 days to vacate or face compulsory removal by Clarity, who is already on her way.

— The Management
items.intent: Pushing oneself to confront an uncomfortable truth that has been avoided.

items.prompt: Make a short collections letter for someone who owes me creative credit.
items.response:
NOTICE OF UNCREDITED CREATIVE CONTRIBUTION

Dear Colleague,

This letter concerns the campaign concept, three taglines, and the overall visual direction that originated with the undersigned and were presented to the client as your own.

We request acknowledgment of authorship within five (5) business days. Continued silence will be interpreted as an admission and forwarded accordingly.

Regards,
The Original Idea Office
items.intent: Formally assert authorship over creative work that was taken and presented without attribution.

items.prompt: Write a cinquain about getting a captcha wrong multiple times
items.response:
Prove
You are human
Select the tiles with a bus
I choose wrong — the grid reshuffles
Again
items.intent: Capture the humiliation of failing a test designed to prove you are not a robot.

items.prompt: pantoum about forgetting your badge and having to be let in each morning
items.response:
I left it on the kitchen counter again.
The guard buzzes me through with a sigh.
I will remember tomorrow, I say.
The lanyard is never around my neck.

The guard buzzes me through with a sigh.
He knows my face better than most colleagues do.
The lanyard is never around my neck.
I check my pockets three times at the door.

He knows my face better than most colleagues do.
Someday I will buy a backup badge holder.
I check my pockets three times at the door.
I left it on the kitchen counter again.

Someday I will buy a backup badge holder.
The guard buzzes me through with a sigh.
I check my pockets three times at the door.
I will remember tomorrow, I say.
items.intent: Capture the mild shame of a recurring access-control failure.

items.prompt: Write me liner notes for a post-rock album about commuting by bus
items.response:
Route 14 (2019)

This album was assembled stop by stop. The crescendo on "Transfer Required" was written on a phone in the back row. We are grateful to the 6:42 AM and the strangers who made no eye contact. You were essential collaborators.
items.intent: Chronicle the emotional texture of a daily bus commute through post-rock liner note aesthetics.

items.prompt: I need a description for a podcast ep about the weird silence right after you turn off a TV
items.response: The screen goes black. Something that was there isn't anymore. Gus and Mira call this 'the void.' Audiologists call it normal. This episode sides with Gus and Mira.
items.intent: Examine the sensory aftermath of switching off a television as an uncanny experience.

items.prompt: Give me a YouTube comment for a nature video showing a sloth moving extremely slowly across a branch
items.response: He is not slow. He has simply decided that urgency is a trap and opted out. He is the most enlightened being on the planet.
items.intent: Use a sloth's pace as a vehicle for commentary on the virtues of deliberate slowness.

items.prompt: Write a closed caption for the pause before someone answers a question they didn't expect
items.response: [0.8-SECOND DELAY] [FILING SYSTEM CONSULTED] [ANSWER SELECTED FROM SEVERAL WORSE OPTIONS]
items.intent: Annotate the invisible cognitive scramble inside an unguarded moment.

items.prompt: Write a pretentious statement about a disposable coffee cup as if it were fine art
items.response: The cup is the dialogue between warmth and its container — a temporary covenant. That it will be discarded does not diminish the sacrament; it consecrates it.
items.intent: Elevate a throwaway item by framing its transience as spiritual rather than wasteful.

items.prompt: Write an overwrought headnote for lemon bars about a summer fling that ended badly
items.response: He was visiting from somewhere cold and he ate four of these in one sitting and said, completely sincerely, that he had never tasted anything so aggressively bright. I thought he meant the bars. By September I understood he had meant me. The lemon bars are still excellent. Some things improve with bitterness.
items.intent: Use a tart dessert to encapsulate the ambivalence of a short-lived romance

items.prompt: Give me a tasting menu for the internet
items.response:
AMUSE-BOUCHE
Outrage, bite-sized

FIRST COURSE
Information of uncertain provenance

SECOND COURSE
Cat content, a palate reset

INTERMEZZO
Notification, unbidden

MAIN
A parasocial relationship, slow-drip

CHEESE
Nostalgia for the early web — funky, divisive

DESSERT
Doomscroll, served endlessly upon request
items.intent: Critique and celebrate the overwhelming variety and pace of online life.

items.prompt: write a man page for embarrassment
items.response:
EMBARRASSMENT(7)         Human Affective States       EMBARRASSMENT(7)

NAME
       embarrassment - involuntary social-visibility interrupt

SYNOPSIS
       embarrassment [--audience size] [--severity level] [--witnessed-by peers]

DESCRIPTION
       embarrassment(7) fires when a host's private or flawed state is
       made visible to an audience. Produces flushing, avoidance behavior,
       and retroactive scene replay at 2am.

OPTIONS
       --audience    Number of witnesses. Signal scales with sqrt(audience).
       --severity    Magnitude of triggering event (1-10).
       --witnessed-by
                     Named peers increase severity multiplier by 1.5x each.

SIDE EFFECTS
       Involuntary vocalization ("oh no", "why").
       Memory loop initiated; see: rumination(7).

EXIT STATUS
       Embarrassment(7) has no formal exit. Decays asymptotically.

BUGS
       Host frequently overestimates audience attention. See: spotlight-effect(7).

SEE ALSO
       shame(7), rumination(7), social-anxiety(7), cringe(1)

Human Affective States 2.0   2026           EMBARRASSMENT(7)
items.intent: Formalize the self-conscious spiral of an embarrassing moment as a system interrupt.

items.prompt: need a 404 page for a mindfulness app — the page for when someone's present moment is missing
items.response:
404 — Present Moment Not Found

You were not here.

You were somewhere else — the past, probably, or a fairly specific anxiety about next Thursday.

The present moment exists only at this exact URL. Please stop refreshing.
items.intent: Make a joke about the impossibility of mindfulness for distracted people using 404 error copy.

items.prompt: Write professional alt text for a photo of a clock tower, but note that the clock is stopped.
items.response: A stone clock tower at the center of a busy plaza, its hands fixed at 2:47, around which the crowd continues to move as though time's failure is not their problem.
items.intent: Expose the gap between institutional timekeeping and the indifference of ordinary life.

items.prompt: Make a deprecation notice for the habit of people-pleasing.
items.response:
DEPRECATION NOTICE

Behavior: People-Pleasing (v0.1–present)
Status: DEPRECATED

This pattern of subordinating all preferences to external approval has accumulated significant technical debt in the form of resentment. Support ends now. The replacement module — selective, boundaried generosity — is available immediately.
items.intent: Acknowledge the cost of chronic people-pleasing and frame setting limits as a necessary upgrade.

items.prompt: Draft a layoff announcement for a video game studio after a major title underperformed at launch
items.response:
Subject: A Message to Our Studio

Team,

Launch periods are always a moment of reflection, and this one has required us to make some very hard decisions. Based on the performance of Ironclad: Reborn and our updated financial projections, we are reducing our team by 22%.

This is not a reflection of the passion and craftsmanship this team brought to the project every single day. Affected team members will receive outreach today.

With gratitude,
Studio Leadership
items.intent: Lay off a fifth of a game studio's staff after a flop launch while blaming vague 'projections' rather than the game.

items.prompt: Write a rule about taking the last cup of coffee without making a new pot
items.response: Section 9.6 — Coffee Vessel Depletion Responsibility: Any employee who removes the final serving of brewed coffee from a communal carafe assumes full and immediate responsibility for initiating a fresh brew cycle. Failure to comply constitutes a Communal Resource Defection and will be noted in the employee's Cooperation File, reviewed annually.
items.intent: Elevate a petty but emotionally charged kitchen grievance into enforceable policy with consequences.

items.prompt: Write a PIP for my habit of making plans and then canceling them at the last minute
items.response:
PERFORMANCE IMPROVEMENT PLAN
Department: Social Commitments
Rating: Below Expectations

Pattern of Concern: Subject confirms social engagements with apparent sincerity, then cancels within 2 hours of event start, citing fatigue, vague illness, or "something came up" (something = couch).

Impact: Friends have begun hedging RSVPs; two have instituted a confirmation-call policy specifically for subject.

Expected Standard: 80% attendance rate on confirmed plans.

Plan:
- Do not RSVP yes to events you intend to decline. Decline now.
- Reserve one "wild card" cancellation per month.
- When tempted to cancel, identify whether discomfort is situational (valid) or habitual avoidance (addressed here).

Consequences: Continued cancellation pattern may result in no longer being invited, which — noted — is what part of you wants. That part does not get to run the schedule.
items.intent: Hold oneself accountable for chronic social cancellation driven by anxiety or avoidance.

items.prompt: Write a holiday family letter where we casually mention we moved in with my in-laws
items.response:
Holiday greetings from a newly consolidated household!

In a thrilling plot twist this autumn, we joined forces with Bob and Carol — their guest rooms are now quite permanent. The multigenerational experiment is going well, minus the morning bathroom situation. Marcus has described it as "character building." We are saving aggressively. The kids love it. So does the dog. Mostly.
items.intent: Communicate a financially motivated move-in with extended family without emphasizing the hardship.

items.prompt: write a yearbook quote about how social media will make me rich
items.response: "By the time you're reading this, I have 1 million followers. Subscribe."
items.intent: Staking a public claim on influencer success before it has materialized.

items.prompt: Write a wedding invitation for science and bureaucracy tying the knot
items.response:
The Offices of SCIENCE and BUREAUCRACY
announce their formal union.

Ceremony to commence upon receipt of Form 7-B (Research Commencement Authorization).
Venue: Pending ethics committee approval.
Date: To be confirmed following the third round of revisions.

Light reception to follow, budget permitting.
items.intent: Satirize how institutional red tape slows or suffocates genuine intellectual progress.

items.prompt: Write a bumper sticker about being tracked by apps
items.response: MY PHONE KNOWS WHERE I'M GOING BEFORE I DO
items.intent: Highlight the creeping surveillance embedded in everyday technology.

items.prompt: Give me ad copy for a travel agency that only books one-way tickets to places that no longer exist
items.response:
ESCAPE EVERYTHING.
PANGEA TRAVEL CO. — One-Way Fares Daily
Destinations Include: Constantinople, Czechoslovakia, and Beyond
Non-Refundable. Non-Negotiable. Non-Existent.
items.intent: Advertise a travel service whose destinations are geographically or historically impossible to reach.

items.prompt: I want a yard sign encouraging my neighbors to compost instead of throwing away yard waste
items.response:
COMPOST, DON'T CART

Your grass clippings belong
in the earth, not the landfill.

Free workshop: Sat. June 7 • 10am
items.intent: Promote composting and recruit neighbors to an informational community event.

items.prompt: Write a complaint letter to my apartment building management about the elevator that plays the same four-second jingle on a loop
items.response:
To Building Management,

I am writing regarding the audio experience aboard Elevator B. The unit plays a four-second musical phrase, continuously, without variation or mercy, for the duration of every ride. Given that I occupy the fourteenth floor, I have now heard this jingle approximately 1,200 times. It has entered my dreams. I request its removal, replacement, or at minimum a formal apology.

Respectfully,
Unit 1402
items.intent: Petition for relief from a short, looping elevator jingle that has become a fixture of the tenant's inner life.

items.prompt: help me apologize to my upstairs neighbor for complaining about noise when it was actually me making the noise
items.response:
Hi,

This is embarrassing to write. The thumping we both complained about turns out to have been from my apartment — my washing machine on spin cycle. I knocked on your door for no reason. I'm sorry for the accusation and the inconvenience.

— Unit 4B
items.intent: Correct a wrongful noise complaint made against an innocent neighbor.

items.prompt: write a diary entry about spending an entire Saturday doing laundry
items.response:
Dear Diary,

I was going to do so much today. Instead: laundry. Three loads. The folding alone took forty minutes because I kept stopping to question my choices. The socks are paired. My dreams are not.
items.intent: Lament the way domestic chores devour a whole free day.

items.prompt: write a captain's log about waiting for IT to fix your computer
items.response: Stardate 9002.8. Primary console went dark at 0730. Filed a support request. Ticket number: INC-88451. Estimated resolution: "within 2 business days." It has been one business day. I am operating from a loaner unit. The loaner runs a deprecated OS. Several applications are incompatible. I have completed two tasks. Both involved paper.
items.intent: Express the productivity paralysis caused by IT response delays.

items.prompt: Draft a short 911 transcript where the caller complains their neighbor's lawn is too green and suspicious
items.response:
DISPATCHER: 911, what's your emergency?
CALLER: My neighbor's lawn. It's too perfect.
DISPATCHER: Too... perfect?
CALLER: Nobody's lawn looks like that naturally. Something's going on over there.
DISPATCHER: Turfgrass improvement is not an emergency, sir.
CALLER: That's what someone who's in on it would say.
items.intent: Satirize suburban paranoia by framing horticultural success as inherently criminal or conspiratorial.

items.prompt: Generate a closing argument for a dispute about someone who kept double-dipping at a party
items.response: The defendant dipped. They bit. They dipped again. The salsa was communal; the chip was not. Every subsequent dipper at that table became an unwitting party to what followed. My client raised an objection in real time and was laughed at. The court should not also laugh. Double-dipping is not a personal matter. It never was.
items.intent: Present a public health case dressed as a social grievance.

items.prompt: Draft a funny eviction notice for overthinking
items.response:
EVICTION NOTICE — FOURTH DRAFT

To: Overthinking
(Please note: this notice was revised eleven times before sending.)

You have analyzed every exit scenario, concluded none are viable, and therefore refused to vacate. This circular logic does not constitute a lease. The premises — my pre-sleep mental state — are needed for rest. Leave. Or don't. Actually, leave. Final answer.

— A Very Tired Brain
items.intent: Making light of the paralysis that comes from analyzing every decision into inaction.

items.prompt: Write a collections letter for someone who consumed years of my patience and hasn't balanced the account.
items.response:
PATIENCE LEDGER — FINAL NOTICE

To the Account Holder,

Over a period of six (6) years, this office extended to you a virtually unlimited line of patience credit. Withdrawals included: repeated late arrivals, unfinished sentences left for others to complete, and the annual Christmas conflict.

The line has now been closed. Outstanding balance: considerable.

Repayment in the form of changed behavior is the only instrument we accept.

Closed but not forgotten,
The Patience Recovery Division
items.intent: Mark the exhaustion of one person's tolerance after years of absorbing another's difficult behavior.

items.prompt: Can you write a cinquain about the blue light from screens at night
items.response:
Glow
Cold and steady
Bleaching the dark bedroom air
My eyes convince my brain: it's noon
Can't sleep
items.intent: Examine how screen light disrupts the body's sense of time at night.

items.prompt: write a pantoum about the office supply closet that is always out of sticky notes
items.response:
The shelf where the yellow pads should be is bare.
Someone has ordered more; they have not arrived.
I borrow a page from the receptionist's desk.
The requisition form is three steps long.

Someone has ordered more; they have not arrived.
I write my note on the back of an invoice.
The requisition form is three steps long.
By next week the pads will be here.

I write my note on the back of an invoice.
The shelf is always bare when I arrive.
By next week the pads will be here.
I tell myself this the way I always do.

The shelf is always bare when I arrive.
The shelf where the yellow pads should be is bare.
By next week the pads will be here.
Someone has ordered more; they have not arrived.
items.intent: Lament the chronic shortage of the most basic office consumables.

items.prompt: Write liner notes for an album inspired by furniture assembly instructions
items.response:
Step 16: Weep (2023)

The screws are always wrong. There is always one extra dowel. This record exists because the guitarist spent a Sunday with a bookshelf that refused to be a bookshelf, and in that refusal heard music. The final track contains 47 seconds of silence where the diagram should have been.
items.intent: Mine the specific frustration of flat-pack furniture for genuine artistic and comedic resonance.

items.prompt: Write an episode description for a nothing-podcast about the topic of looking for your phone when it's already in your hand
items.response: It was there the whole time. It's always there the whole time. Episode 22 investigates why the hand is the last place we look, and what that says about us as a species. (It says nothing good.)
items.intent: Mine a common absent-minded moment for satirical self-examination.

items.prompt: Write a YouTube comment for a video of an elderly man whistling while gardening
items.response: I want whatever he has. Not the garden. Not even the whistling. Just that specific relationship with time.
items.intent: Express longing for a quality of presence rather than any tangible thing the subject possesses.

items.prompt: Create the closed caption for a cat knocking something off a table while maintaining eye contact
items.response: [DELIBERATE] [INTENTIONAL] [DEEPLY INTENTIONAL]
items.intent: Render the cat's action as a philosophical assertion rather than accident.

items.prompt: Pretentious artist statement about a parking meter
items.response: Time, like space, has been monetized. The meter stands as civic confessor — it extracts tribute from the body at rest, converting stillness into debt, presence into liability.
items.intent: Use a mundane urban fixture to comment on commodified time and public space.

items.prompt: Headnote for beef stew, story about being stranded during a blizzard with nothing in the pantry
items.response: We were snowed in for three days in January and the contents of my pantry had the defeated look of things I kept meaning to use. Chuck roast I'd frozen in October. Half a bag of pearl onions. A bottle of wine that was not good enough to drink. Reader, it was enough. It is always enough, if you're willing to wait.
items.intent: Demonstrate that good cooking emerges from scarcity and patience

items.prompt: Can you make a tasting menu for a long-haul flight?
items.response:
APERITIF
Gate change announcement

AMUSE-BOUCHE
Pretzel mix, foil bag

FIRST COURSE
Turbulence — texture only

SECOND COURSE
Airline pasta, undisclosed origin

INTERMEZZO
Time zone lost

MAIN
Four films watched; two completed

DESSERT
Landing, served with swollen ankles
items.intent: Capture the grinding tedium and minor indignities of long-distance air travel.

items.prompt: make a man page for grief
items.response:
GRIEF(7)                 Human Affective States            GRIEF(7)

NAME
       grief - loss-event response handler

SYNOPSIS
       grief [--type loss_category] [--stage current_stage]

DESCRIPTION
       grief(7) is initiated by irreversible loss events. It is not a
       bug. It does not have a timeout parameter. It runs non-linearly
       through stages which may be visited in any order, multiple times,
       or skipped entirely.

STAGES (non-sequential)
       denial(7)    Initial rejection of loss event.
       anger(7)     Fault-assignment process. May be misdirected.
       bargaining(7) Counterfactual computation. All branches return loss.
       depression(7) Low-state maintenance mode.
       acceptance(7) Integration of loss into self-model.

EXIT STATUS
       grief(7) does not terminate. It reconfigures.

NOTES
       There is no correct implementation. Comparison to other hosts'
       grief processes is contraindicated.

SEE ALSO
       loss(7), healing(3), time(1), community(3)

Human Affective States 1.0   2026                    GRIEF(7)
items.intent: Honor the nonlinear, unterminating nature of mourning through a technical metaphor.

items.prompt: write a 404 page for a website about hope
items.response:
404 — Hope Not Found

This shouldn't happen. And yet.

The page you were looking for has not been located, but the search for it matters. Keep the tab open.

We'll keep looking on our end.
items.intent: Construct a 404 page that refuses to be fully hopeless, using the error format against itself.

items.prompt: Give me alt text for a nature photo of a tree struck by lightning, still standing.
items.response: A large oak split from crown to root by lightning stands in an open field, bark scorched and crown asymmetrical, continuing to put out leaves on the undamaged side.
items.intent: Describe survival without romanticizing it — just the bare biological fact of continuance.

items.prompt: Write a deprecation notice for the relationship I had with my college roommate — we just drifted apart.
items.response:
DEPRECATION NOTICE

Relationship: College Roommate Friendship (vFreshman–Senior)
Status: DEPRECATED — natural end-of-life

This connection served its purpose across a critical developmental period. Drift has been gradual and mutual. No critical errors occurred; the deprecation is simply a function of diverging environments. Legacy warmth is retained in memory.
items.intent: Mourn and accept the quiet fading of a once-close friendship without assigning blame.

items.prompt: Write a layoff email for an airline cutting ground crew positions due to reduced flight routes
items.response:
Subject: Network Optimization and Workforce Update

To Our Ground Operations Team,

As we continue to right-size our network in response to changing travel demand, we have made the decision to reduce our ground operations headcount at select stations. Affected employees will be contacted through the standard HR notification process.

We sincerely thank each of you for the role you have played in keeping our operations safe and reliable.

Sincerely,
Chief Operating Officer
items.intent: Tell airport workers their stations are being cut while using aviation jargon to disguise the scale of the harm.

items.prompt: Create a policy about employees adjusting the office thermostat without permission
items.response: Section 11.1 — Climate Infrastructure Access: The building thermostat is designated a controlled asset. Adjustments may only be initiated by employees holding Thermal Authorization Level 2 or above, as determined by Facilities. Unauthorized temperature modifications will trigger an automatic incident report and a mandatory Comfort Negotiation Seminar.
items.intent: Formally protect one of the most contentious pieces of shared office hardware by creating a permission hierarchy for it.

items.prompt: Create a PIP for how badly I procrastinate on medical appointments
items.response:
PERFORMANCE IMPROVEMENT PLAN
Subject Area: Preventive Health Scheduling
Severity: Elevated

Deficiency: Subject has postponed routine medical appointments through a documented cycle of: intention → scheduling anxiety → avoidance → forgetting → guilt → intention. Estimated delay from symptom/notice to appointment: 7–14 months.

Appointments Currently Overdue (Estimated):
- General physical: 2 years
- Eye exam: 3 years
- Dentist: 11 months (see also: flossing PIP)

Improvement Actions:
1. Schedule one appointment within 48 hours of reading this document.
2. Do not wait to "feel ready."
3. Request reminder calls. Use them.

Support: Identify whether cost, fear, or inertia is primary barrier. Adjust plan accordingly.

Milestone: All routine appointments current within 90 days.

Note: Body is not self-maintaining.
items.intent: Prompt serious accountability for long-term avoidance of necessary healthcare.

items.prompt: Draft a Christmas letter where we gloss over the fact that our oldest dropped out of college
items.response:
Merry everything from the Ashworth family!

Ryan has taken a bold step back from traditional academics to pursue his passion for, well — he's still defining it. We support his journey wholeheartedly and only occasionally in small claims court of our own kitchen. He is cooking more. The tuition savings are real. Happy holidays!
items.intent: Announce a college dropout to relatives who funded the tuition, without sounding devastated.

items.prompt: give me a yearbook quote about how I'm the only one in my friend group who's going to make it
items.response: "Love you guys. One of us is going to be okay. You know which one."
items.intent: Expressing condescending affection toward friends while elevating oneself above them.

items.prompt: Make a gothic wedding invitation for darkness and 3pm in December
items.response:
By the power vested in the winter solstice,

DARKNESS
takes
3:47 PM IN DECEMBER

as its lawfully wedded afternoon.

Venue: Outside your window, already.
Attire: Layers. Many.

The couple thanks you for your seasonal despair.
Dinner will be served at what used to be daytime.
items.intent: Dramatize the oppressive early darkness of winter evenings in a northern climate.

items.prompt: I want a bumper sticker about burnout
items.response: PASSIONATE ABOUT BEING EXHAUSTED
items.intent: Reclaim corporate language to describe total depletion.

items.prompt: Write a roadside billboard for a shoe store that only sells left shoes
items.response:
HALF THE SHOES. ALL THE STYLE.
LEFTY'S FOOTWEAR EMPORIUM
Thousands of Singles In Stock
Exit 22. Bring a Friend Who Lost the Other Foot.
items.intent: Market a shoe retailer whose inventory is structurally incomplete and therefore useless to most shoppers.

items.prompt: Make a yard sign about the HOA treasurer who has been mismanaging our dues
items.response:
WHERE DID THE MONEY GO?

$14,000 in dues.
Zero landscaping updates.
Zero accounting.

Demand transparency. Attend July 3rd.
items.intent: Publicly pressure the HOA leadership to account for missing or misused community funds.

items.prompt: Write a letter of complaint to a streaming service about a show being cancelled on a cliffhanger
items.response:
Dear Content Team,

I am writing to formally protest the cancellation of a series after its second season concluded with an unresolved narrative cliffhanger of significant emotional consequence. I invested fourteen hours in these characters. I now have no closure, no answers, and an inexplicable attachment to fictional people whose fate I will never learn. I request either renewal, a summary, or a small refund for emotional labor.

Regards,
A Subscriber
items.intent: Express genuine grief about an abrupt show cancellation that left a story permanently unresolved.

items.prompt: write an apology for abruptly leaving a board game midway through because I was losing
items.response:
Sam,

I shouldn't have said I had 'a thing' and left during Catan. I did not have a thing. I had seven points and you had eleven and I handled it poorly. I'll be there for next game night, and I'll stay until the end even if it goes badly.

— Greg
items.intent: Come clean about rage-quitting a board game and pretending to have an excuse.

items.prompt: make a diary entry about getting an automated phone call
items.response:
Dear Diary,

It told me my car's extended warranty was expiring. I do not own a car. I pressed 2 to be removed from the list. I always press 2. I will receive this call again. We both know it.
items.intent: Express weary resignation about robocall spam.

items.prompt: captain's log entry about dealing with reply-all email chains
items.response: Stardate 6677.0. The distress signal originated with a single query from Procurement at 0911. By 1030, seventeen crew members had replied to all. The question was answered in the third reply. The chain continued for eleven more. Two crew members replied only to say "thank you." One replied to ask to be removed. I have disabled shipwide alerts. I am reading none of them.
items.intent: Chronicle the inbox chaos triggered by unnecessary reply-all email threads.

items.prompt: Make a 911 call transcript where someone reports that a bird is singing too loudly at 6am
items.response:
DISPATCHER: 911, what's your emergency?
CALLER: There is a bird outside my window. Singing.
DISPATCHER: A bird.
CALLER: Loudly. At six in the morning. On a Saturday.
DISPATCHER: Birds are not in our jurisdiction.
CALLER: Everything that disturbs the peace should be in your jurisdiction.
DISPATCHER: Have you considered earplugs?
CALLER: Have you considered doing your job?
items.intent: Capture the frustration of light sleepers who direct their morning rage at the nearest authority figure.

items.prompt: Write a summation for a plaintiff whose sibling always took the window seat without asking
items.response: For nineteen years of family road trips, my client sat in the middle. Not because they preferred it. Not because it was fair. Because the defendant moved faster. Speed is not equity, your honor. The window was not theirs to claim by default. We ask the court to retroactively recognize the injustice and apportion future windows equally.
items.intent: Frame a childhood sibling rivalry as a case of long-term property inequity.

items.prompt: Make an eviction notice for imposter syndrome
items.response:
NOTICE OF EVICTION

To: Imposter Syndrome
Unit: My professional identity

You arrived uninvited with two duffel bags of unfounded evidence and have since accused every accomplishment of being a fluke. The genuine occupant — Competence — holds a valid lease. Your claims to this space are, ironically, the only thing here that is fraudulent.

Vacate within 14 days.

— HR (Head Resident)
items.intent: Addressing the persistent feeling of being unqualified despite demonstrated ability.

items.prompt: Draft a collections letter for a stolen sense of safety.
items.response:
NOTICE: SECURITY DEFICIT — OUTSTANDING BALANCE

Dear Responsible Party,

This correspondence concerns the sense of safety that was present prior to your actions and has since been absent. The undersigned once moved through the world with a reasonable degree of ease. That ease was not self-depleting; it was taken.

No repayment plan can fully restore the original asset. However, accountability is requested as a first installment.

Forwarded from the Interior,
The Safety Reclamation Office
items.intent: Give language to the harm done when someone's actions permanently altered another person's feeling of security.

items.prompt: Write a cinquain about push notifications
items.response:
Tap
Urgent, insistent
Someone liked a photo twice
The phone vibrates — I glance — nothing
Tap again
items.intent: Illustrate the hollow compulsion triggered by trivial alert sounds.

items.prompt: can you write a pantoum about eating the same lunch at my desk every day
items.response:
The same container of leftovers again.
I eat without tasting, screen before me.
The afternoon meeting starts at two.
Salt and notifications, side by side.

I eat without tasting, screen before me.
Someone microwaved fish down the hall.
Salt and notifications, side by side.
The container goes back in the bag.

Someone microwaved fish down the hall.
I scroll through the same sites as yesterday.
The container goes back in the bag.
The afternoon meeting starts at two.

I scroll through the same sites as yesterday.
The same container of leftovers again.
I eat without tasting, screen before me.
The afternoon meeting starts at two.
items.intent: Depict the joyless efficiency of the solitary desk lunch.

items.prompt: Give me liner notes for a country album about conference calls
items.response:
Can Everyone Hear Me? (2022)

These songs were born in the unmuted silence between agenda items. Track two, "You're On Mute, Darlin'," was written in real time. We are a band forged by dial-in codes and the eternal question of whether the background noise is you. It was always you.
items.intent: Apply country music's emotional earnestness to the alienating experience of remote work meetings.

items.prompt: Can you write a short episode description about a podcast episode where nothing happens for 30 minutes
items.response: Nothing happened this week. Nothing happened last week either, but this week it happened louder. Runtime: 31 minutes. Hosts: present. Topics: none confirmed.
items.intent: Create a self-aware meta-description for a podcast episode with no content.

items.prompt: Write a YouTube comment for a video of a dog learning to use stairs for the first time
items.response: He just conquered something that terrified him and nobody gave him a trophy. Just like the rest of us.
items.intent: Project adult human anxieties about perseverance and recognition onto a puppy's small achievement.

items.prompt: Write the subtitle for someone arriving late to a funeral and sliding into the back pew
items.response: [DOOR HINGE LOUDER THAN ANTICIPATED] [GRIEF AND EMBARRASSMENT OCCUPYING THE SAME BODY]
items.intent: Hold two incompatible feelings in one physical moment.

items.prompt: Generate an over-the-top artist statement about a stapler
items.response: To staple is to insist on unity. The stapler performs what language promises and rarely delivers: a permanent, metal argument for coherence. Each click is a small act of will against entropy.
items.intent: Treat a common office device as a metaphor for the desire to hold things together.

items.prompt: Write a headnote for French onion soup that starts with a bad blind date at a bistro
items.response: The date was unsalvageable by the appetizer course. We had nothing to say to each other, his shoes were wrong in a way I cannot explain, and then the soup arrived and I forgot him entirely for six minutes. I have been chasing those six minutes ever since. He texts occasionally. I do not reply.
items.intent: Elevate a recipe by contrasting it with a socially dismal occasion

items.prompt: Make a tasting menu for falling in love
items.response:
AMUSE-BOUCHE
One laugh you didn't expect

FIRST COURSE
Their name, thought twice

SECOND COURSE
Future tense, used for the first time

INTERMEZZO
Silence that isn't awkward

MAIN
Vulnerability, served open-faced

DESSERT
The word — held a moment, then said
items.intent: Render the quiet, accumulating experience of falling in love.

items.prompt: write a man page for the human habit of making small talk
items.response:
SMALL-TALK(1)            Interpersonal Utilities        SMALL-TALK(1)

NAME
       small-talk - establish low-bandwidth social channel

SYNOPSIS
       small-talk [-t topic] [-d duration] [INTERLOCUTOR]

DESCRIPTION
       small-talk(1) opens a social channel using low-stakes, mutually
       uninvestigating topic sequences. Primary function is not information
       transfer but channel-open confirmation.

APPROVED TOPICS
       weather, sports_result, commute_complaints, office_temperature,
       weekend_plans (Friday only), recent_illness (brief)

FORBIDDEN TOPICS
       politics, salary, relationship_status (unless offered), religion,
       existential_dread

OPTIONS
       -t     Topic seed. Default: weather.
       -d     Duration cap. Exceeding 4 minutes risks topic_escalation(7).

EXIT CODES
       0      Graceful exit ("anyway, better get back to it").
       1      Trapped. Interlocutor has not received exit cue.

SEE ALSO
       conversation(1), silence(7), gossip(1), networking(8)

Interpersonal Utilities 2.0  2026             SMALL-TALK(1)
items.intent: Reveal the ritualized, content-free function of social filler conversation.

items.prompt: write a 404 page for a page that's supposed to be about silence
items.response:
404







(The page you are looking for was always going to be empty.)
items.intent: Express the concept of silence by making the 404 page itself mostly nothing.

items.prompt: Write alt text for a photo of a grocery store aisle at closing time, completely empty of people.
items.response: A fluorescent-lit cereal aisle stretches to a distant corner, every product still perfectly faced, the abundance arranged for shoppers who have gone home and left it all here.
items.intent: Find the uncanny in commercial abundance confronted by total human absence.

items.prompt: Draft a deprecation notice for the need to be liked by everyone.
items.response:
DEPRECATION NOTICE

Desire: Universal Approval (legacy)
Status: DEPRECATED

This feature was never achievable and has been a persistent source of behavioral compromise. Effective immediately, the goal of being liked by all parties is retired. The recommended replacement is being respected by the right ones.
items.intent: Encourage accepting that not everyone will like you and reframing approval-seeking as unnecessary.

items.prompt: Help me write an announcement email about layoffs at a consulting firm eliminating its sustainability practice
items.response:
Subject: Strategic Portfolio Realignment

Colleagues,

After a careful review of our service offerings and client demand, we have made the decision to sunset our Sustainability Advisory practice. The team members associated with this practice will be given the opportunity to explore redeployment options internally, and those for whom no suitable placement is found will receive severance and transition support.

We thank the sustainability team for their work and remain committed to embedding these principles across our broader practice.

Regards,
Managing Partner
items.intent: Eliminate an entire practice area and its staff while ironically claiming continued commitment to its mission.

items.prompt: Draft an official rule for employees who heat fish in the office microwave
items.response: Section 9.3 — Olfactory-Impact Food Preparation: Heating of fish, fermented products, or any food item rated Category II or above on the Internal Olfactory Impact Scale is strictly prohibited in communal kitchen microwaves. Violations are reported directly to the floor's Smell Ombudsman and are not subject to the standard verbal-warning protocol.
items.intent: Treat a universally recognized office taboo with the gravity of a formal compliance matter.

items.prompt: I keep starting hobbies and abandoning them — write a PIP for this
items.response:
PERFORMANCE IMPROVEMENT PLAN
Division: Leisure & Personal Development
Incident Count: 9 (documented)

Background: Subject has, within 36 months, acquired and abandoned: a guitar, two canvases, a bread proofing basket, a soldering kit, a language-learning app subscription (lapsed), a running journal, a 3D-printer filament spool, and a ukulele (distinct from guitar; different phase).

Core Deficiency: Pursuit of initial enthusiasm without tolerance for the intermediate plateau.

Objective: Sustain one chosen activity for 90 consecutive days before introducing any new hobby.

Rules:
- No new hobby equipment until current hobby reaches 60-day mark.
- The bread basket must be used before any new kitchen tool is purchased.
- "Researching a hobby" does not count as the hobby.

Review: 90-day mark. One completed project constitutes passing.
items.intent: Curb a pattern of enthusiastic hobby acquisition followed by rapid abandonment.

items.prompt: Help me write a holiday letter in which our family trip gets mentioned even though it was a disaster
items.response:
Season's warmth from the Castellano family!

This summer we attempted a two-week road trip across the American Southwest. We completed nine days, which is nearly the same thing. We learned that heat is a real phenomenon, that car air conditioning is not optional, and that the Grand Canyon is still magnificent when viewed from a rest stop. Memories made. Merry Christmas.
items.intent: Reframe a failed family vacation as an adventure with takeaways.

items.prompt: make a yearbook quote about refusing to go to therapy even though everyone says I should
items.response: "My therapist doesn't exist and she says I'm fine."
items.intent: Making a joke out of avoiding mental health support.

items.prompt: Write a wedding invitation for ambition marrying imposter syndrome
items.response:
AMBITION
&
IMPOSTER SYNDROME

request the pleasure of your company at their wedding.

Date: The day you got the promotion
Venue: Your head, during the congratulations speech

Seating is alphabetical. You probably don't deserve yours.
Ceremony will be brief. The doubt will last.
items.intent: Articulate the internal conflict between drive and self-doubt that high achievers often experience.

items.prompt: Give me a bumper sticker about therapy being expensive
items.response: IN THERAPY BECAUSE I CAN AFFORD IT. BARELY.
items.intent: Acknowledge the irony that mental health care is itself a source of financial stress.

items.prompt: Create billboard text for a psychic who openly admits she is wrong 100% of the time
items.response:
MADAME ZELDA SEES ALL
(AND GETS IT COMPLETELY WRONG)
Reliably Incorrect Since 2003
"My Worst Reading Saved My Best Client." — Nobody
Off Ramp 7, Behind the Laundromat
items.intent: Promote a clairvoyant whose advertised track record is a total failure, making her services worthless.

items.prompt: Write a yard sign asking people not to take shortcuts across my lawn
items.response:
NOT A PATH

This is private lawn.
The sidewalk is 8 feet to your left.

Thank you (we mean it).
items.intent: Redirect foot traffic away from a shortcut worn across their private property.

items.prompt: Write a complaint to the city about a crosswalk signal that beeps way too fast and creates a panic response
items.response:
To the Department of Transportation,

I write concerning the pedestrian signal at the intersection of [Street A] and [Street B]. The auditory crossing alert emits a rapid beeping sequence that communicates, quite powerfully, imminent danger. It has the cadence of a cardiac monitor in crisis. Several pedestrians, myself included, have broken into a light run as a conditioned stress response. I ask that the tempo be reviewed in the interest of public calm.

Sincerely,
A Pedestrian
items.intent: Report a crosswalk signal whose beep pattern triggers involuntary fight-or-flight responses in ordinary pedestrians.

items.prompt: draft a note apologizing to my dog for accidentally vacuuming over his tail
items.response:
Dear Biscuit,

I am sorry about yesterday. I did not see your tail. The vacuum made contact briefly and I know you were not expecting it. You gave me a look I deserved. Extra walk this weekend.

Your human
items.intent: Make a playful but sincere apology to a pet for a clumsy household accident.

items.prompt: write a diary entry about losing the TV remote
items.response:
Dear Diary,

It was here. It was always here. I checked the couch: both cushions, the terrible dark gap between, the floor beside. I watched three channels I didn't choose. By the end I had opinions about home renovation I never asked for.
items.intent: Describe how a small loss of control spirals into an unasked-for experience.

items.prompt: make a captain's log about trying to book a conference room
items.response: Stardate 7140.3. Attempted to reserve the Andromeda suite for a 1100 briefing. Calendar showed availability. I clicked. It was taken. I checked Orion. Orion is always taken. I found an opening in the small room near the stairwell — Kepler — which fits four but smells of carpet cleaner. Booked it for six. Two will stand. This is standard.
items.intent: Illustrate the perennial scarcity and dysfunction of office meeting room booking.

items.prompt: Write a 911 transcript where someone calls about a spider in their bathroom
items.response:
DISPATCHER: 911, state your emergency.
CALLER: Spider. Bathroom. Big.
DISPATCHER: Are you bitten?
CALLER: Not yet. It's watching me.
DISPATCHER: We can't send officers to remove a—
CALLER: It's very big.
DISPATCHER: How big?
CALLER: Quarter-sized. Maybe bigger. A half-dollar.
DISPATCHER: ...I'll see what I can do.
items.intent: Capture arachnophobia's power to turn a bathroom spider into a hostage situation requiring armed response.

items.prompt: Can you write a mock closing argument for a case about someone who kept hitting snooze and waking up their partner
items.response: The alarm was set for 6:15. By 7:00, it had sounded nine times. My client slept through none of them. The defendant, meanwhile, remained in blissful semiconsciousness, surfing the intervals. Sleep, once interrupted, does not return on command. We ask the court to mandate a single-alarm policy, or alternatively, separate bedrooms.
items.intent: Articulate the unfairness of one person's sleep ritual disrupting another's rest.

items.prompt: Write an eviction notice for winter
items.response:
NOTICE TO VACATE THE CALENDAR

To: Winter
Occupying: Months 12 through 3, inclusive

Your tenancy has concluded. You have deposited salt on every surface, darkened the premises before 4 p.m., and lowered the ambient temperature without consent. Spring has a confirmed booking. Please gather your frost and leave by the equinox. Groundhog testimony will not be accepted as grounds for extension.

— The Year
items.intent: Celebrating the arrival of spring by dramatizing winter's overstay.

items.prompt: Create a collections letter for all the mental energy someone borrowed to solve their problems.
items.response:
RE: COGNITIVE RESOURCES — PAST DUE

Dear Chronic Borrower,

A full audit of 2023 and 2024 reveals that you extracted approximately 340 hours of the undersigned's cognitive bandwidth to address your logistical, romantic, and professional crises.

The undersigned's own problems, meanwhile, went unattended and have compounded accordingly.

We request a minimum of 40 hours of reciprocal problem-solving attention, or a written acknowledgment of debt. Either will suffice.

Mentally spent,
Cognitive Resources Ltd.
items.intent: Call out a lopsided support dynamic where one person perpetually processes the other's problems at personal cost.

items.prompt: Make a cinquain about the moment before a software update restarts your computer
items.response:
Files
Unsaved, open
The countdown hits zero, gone
System restarting — your work —
Where?
items.intent: Mourn the loss of unsaved work when an update forcibly restarts a computer.

items.prompt: write a pantoum about the quarterly review cycle that never feels finished
items.response:
We are in the feedback window again.
I write my self-assessment from scratch.
The ratings scale has changed since last time.
My goals were due in January.

I write my self-assessment from scratch.
Exceeds expectations: what does that require?
My goals were due in January.
I rate myself a solid meets.

Exceeds expectations: what does that require?
The cycle closes; a new one opens.
I rate myself a solid meets.
Next quarter I will start earlier.

The cycle closes; a new one opens.
We are in the feedback window again.
Next quarter I will start earlier.
The ratings scale has changed since last time.
items.intent: Satirize the treadmill of performance management cycles.

items.prompt: Write liner notes for a folk album about updating software
items.response:
Restart Required (2021)

These are songs about the update that arrives at the worst moment. About the progress bar that stalls at 94%. About restarting into something that looks almost exactly the same but is, we are told, better. We did not choose this. We accepted it.
items.intent: Use folk music's tradition of resigned wisdom to process the irritation of software updates.

items.prompt: Describe a podcast episode where the host talks about whether ice is wet
items.response: The question arrived in a listener email. It should have stayed there. Instead, host Dani Owens devotes a full episode to the ontological status of ice, a substance that is either always wet, never wet, or the reason philosophy exists.
items.intent: Stage a mock intellectual inquiry into a circular, unanswerable question.

items.prompt: Write a YouTube comment for a ASMR video of someone organizing a spice rack
items.response: She put the cumin next to the coriander and I felt my blood pressure drop 40 points. I am a changed person.
items.intent: Convey the outsized physical calm that ASMR organization content can produce in anxious viewers.

items.prompt: Give me a closed caption for a scientist looking at data that contradicts their entire thesis
items.response: [SPREADSHEET OPEN] [SCROLLING] [SCROLLING] [SCROLLING] [CLOSING LAPTOP BRIEFLY] [REOPENING]
items.intent: Dramatize the repetitive denial stage of intellectual reckoning.

items.prompt: Write a pompous artist statement about a shoelace
items.response: The shoelace enacts the oldest human ritual: the voluntary submission of the foot to the earth. I tie not to constrain but to commit — each knot a renewable vow of groundedness.
items.intent: Reframe a routine morning act as a philosophical ritual of embodiment.

items.prompt: Write a headnote for a mango lassi recipe where someone is processing a move abroad
items.response: The apartment was in boxes and every familiar thing was wrapped in newspaper. I drove to the one Indian restaurant still open nearby, ordered a mango lassi because it was the cheapest thing on the menu, and sat with it for a long time. Some tastes arrive at exactly the moment you need a reminder that pleasure is portable. I brought this recipe with me.
items.intent: Anchor the emotional upheaval of relocation to a comforting beverage

items.prompt: Write a tasting menu for a breakup
items.response:
AMUSE-BOUCHE
A cancelled plan

FIRST COURSE
The conversation — dry, over-explained

SECOND COURSE
Your shared playlist, now unlistenable

INTERMEZZO
Mutual friends, distributed

MAIN
Their absence, full portion

NIGHT CAP
The future, rewritten from scratch — still warm
items.intent: Narrate the emotional aftermath of a romantic ending with restraint.

items.prompt: can you write a man page for daydreaming
items.response:
DAYDREAM(1)              Human Cognitive Suite          DAYDREAM(1)

NAME
       daydream - unscheduled background imagination process

SYNOPSIS
       daydream [--scenario string] [--duration unspecified]

DESCRIPTION
       daydream(1) launches a semi-autonomous narrative simulation using
       idle cognitive cycles. Runs without explicit invocation. May
       preempt foreground tasks including meetings, conversations, and
       traffic navigation.

TRIGGERS
       Underloaded sensory environment, repetitive task, warm room,
       window proximity.

OPTIONS
       --scenario   Starting narrative seed. Typically overridden by
                    subconscious scheduler within 30 seconds.
       --duration   Uncontrollable. Ends on external interrupt or
                    name called loudly by another process.

SIDE EFFECTS
       Creativity buffer may fill. Problem-solving insights occasionally
       surface as side product.

BUGS
       Cannot be voluntarily initiated with same quality as spontaneous
       runs. Attempting to "try to daydream" produces inferior output.

SEE ALSO
       focus(1), mind-wandering(7), creativity(7)

Human Cognitive Suite 2.1    2026               DAYDREAM(1)
items.intent: Explore how the mind escapes intentional control and finds its own paths.

items.prompt: can you write a 404 page for when someone's looking for their lost sense of humor
items.response:
404 — Sense of Humor Not Found

We'd make a joke about this, but we're told you won't find it funny.

That tracks.

Try returning to a simpler page — something with a pun or a falling cat.
items.intent: Create a page that ironically performs humor to announce the absence of a sense of humor.

items.prompt: Write alt text for a blurred photo of a city skyline taken from a moving car.
items.response: A smear of lit windows and scaffolding seen through rain-streaked glass, the city resolving into no particular building, as if photographed mid-decision about what it wants to be.
items.intent: Capture the indeterminacy of urban space when perceived in transit rather than from a fixed vantage.

items.prompt: Write a deprecation notice for the habit of doomscrolling at night.
items.response:
DEPRECATION NOTICE

Evening Routine: Doomscrolling (v∞)
Status: DEPRECATED

This habit has been correlated with delayed sleep onset, elevated cortisol, and a generalized sense that civilization is collapsing — which may or may not be accurate. Support ends at 9 PM. Migration path: a book, or staring at the ceiling with intention.
items.intent: Commit to ending the late-night news consumption spiral that disrupts sleep.

items.prompt: Write a company-wide email about layoffs at a streaming service after subscriber numbers dropped
items.response:
Subject: Focusing Our Resources on the Path Forward

Team,

Our Q3 subscriber results have required us to take a hard look at where and how we invest. We have made the decision to reduce our overall workforce by 11%, prioritizing focus on our core content pillars and platform infrastructure.

Those impacted will receive individual communication from their manager this afternoon.

We believe deeply in what we are building here, and we thank every person on this team.

Within,
The Office of the CEO
items.intent: Blame subscriber decline for mass layoffs while vaguely reassuring the survivors that something meaningful is still being built.

items.prompt: Write a handbook rule about employees who schedule unnecessary meetings
items.response: Section 6.4 — Calendar Justification Requirements: Any meeting invitation covering thirty (30) minutes or more must include a stated Desired Outcome, a minimum of one pre-read document, and confirmation that the matter cannot be resolved by email. Meetings found to lack these components may be unilaterally declined without retaliation.
items.intent: Create a protective policy framed as meeting etiquette that implicitly endorses declining pointless gatherings.

items.prompt: Write a PIP for someone who never makes their bed
items.response:
PERFORMANCE IMPROVEMENT PLAN
Area: Morning Domestic Standards
