Deficiency: Bed remains in post-sleep configuration for the duration of the day, 6–7 days per week. Subject has rationalized this with the following statements, all logged: "I'm just going to mess it up again," "no one sees it," and "it's technically abstract expressionism."

Impact: Bedroom environment signals disorder; anecdotal evidence suggests unmade bed correlates with reduced motivation on workdays.

Required Standard: Bed made by 9am on weekdays, 11am on weekends. "Made" is defined as: pillows upright, covers pulled up, no inside-out duvet.

Plan:
- Make bed immediately after leaving it, before phone is touched.
- 14-day streak earns one guilt-free unmade day.

Review: 30 days. Partner or roommate may serve as independent auditor.
items.intent: Build a consistent morning habit around making the bed as an anchor task.

items.prompt: Write a newsletter where we briefly note our dog passed away in a way that doesn't derail the whole thing
items.response:
Warm wishes from the Steinberg family!

We said a tender goodbye to Biscuit in October, who lived fourteen years of impeccable character and will be missed at every doorbell for the rest of our lives. We are healing. The house is quieter. The kids talk about him often and affectionately. In less sad news: Brian got a promotion and Emma lost two teeth. Merry holidays, everyone.
items.intent: Include the death of a beloved pet without the newsletter becoming a grief letter.

items.prompt: write a yearbook quote that subtly brags about being accepted to an Ivy League school
items.response: "I've been told Cambridge, Massachusetts is lovely in the fall."
items.intent: Announcing an elite college acceptance through deliberate geographic understatement.

items.prompt: Write an invitation for the nuptials of the ocean and human stubbornness
items.response:
The SEA and HUMAN STUBBORNNESS
cordially invite you to their wedding.

Location: The shore, obviously. Where else.
Time: Every time someone says 'just one more wave.'

The couple has known each other for millennia.
Guests are advised to bring dry clothes.
The sea does not negotiate.
items.intent: Celebrate the recurring human tendency to underestimate nature and do it anyway.

items.prompt: Make a bumper sticker about always being tired
items.response: WELL-RESTED IS A MYTH I TELL MY CHILDREN
items.intent: Normalize chronic fatigue as a universal parental and adult condition.

items.prompt: Write billboard copy for a law firm that specializes in suing clouds
items.response:
CLOUDS CAUSED YOUR PROBLEM.
WE CAN PROVE IT.
STORMGATH & NIMBUS, LLP
Free Consultation. No Jurisdiction Too Intangible.
1-800-SUE-RAIN
items.intent: Advertise a legal practice whose entire business model targets a non-actionable atmospheric defendant.

items.prompt: Generate a yard sign about how the HOA won't let me paint my door red
items.response:
THE COLOR IS CALLED
'HERITAGE CRIMSON'

The HOA calls it a violation.
We call it our front door.

Rule 12.3 is under appeal.
items.intent: Draw sympathetic attention to an HOA color-code dispute while noting formal appeal status.

items.prompt: Help me write a formal complaint about the office printer that makes an alarming noise but still prints fine
items.response:
To Facilities Management,

I wish to document my concern regarding the second-floor printer. Upon initiating a print job, the device produces a sound consistent with mechanical distress — a grinding, lurching sequence that suggests imminent failure. The document, however, emerges flawlessly. This disconnect between the sound and the outcome is extremely unsettling. I cannot work near this machine without bracing for catastrophe. Please inspect it, even if only for our peace of mind.

Thank you,
A Concerned Employee
items.intent: Request inspection of a printer whose alarming sound creates ongoing workplace anxiety despite producing perfect output.

items.prompt: write a short apology note for sending a very long voice memo to someone instead of a text
items.response:
Hi Dara,

Sorry about the 9-minute voice note. I had a lot going on and kept adding things. You do not have to listen to all of it. The important part is around minute two. I'll use texts in the future.

— Nina
items.intent: Apologize for burdening a contact with an excessively long audio message instead of a brief text.

items.prompt: diary entry about going to the grocery store on Sunday afternoon
items.response:
Dear Diary,

Everybody else also needed groceries. Everybody. The cart traffic in the pasta aisle was gridlock. A child was crying near the yogurt. I came for six things and left with eleven, none of them on the list.
items.intent: Capture the chaotic sensory overload of a crowded grocery trip.

items.prompt: write a captain's log about a fire drill interrupting an important deadline
items.response: Stardate 3388.2. The evacuation alarm sounded at 1349 — eleven minutes before the quarterly submission window closed. We assembled at the designated muster point. The drill lasted twenty-two minutes. Upon return, I submitted at 1416. The portal had closed at 1400. I noted the fire drill in my submission email. Facilities noted the drill had been announced two weeks ago.
items.intent: Capture the frustration when mandated safety drills collide with actual work deadlines.

items.prompt: Create a 911 transcript about a caller who reports that their neighbor is playing Christmas music in October
items.response:
DISPATCHER: 911, what's your emergency?
CALLER: My neighbor has had 'Jingle Bell Rock' on repeat for three hours. It's October 12th.
DISPATCHER: That's not a crime.
CALLER: It should be.
DISPATCHER: I agree, honestly, but—
CALLER: So you agree.
DISPATCHER: That doesn't mean we respond.
items.intent: Channel collective seasonal frustration at premature holiday music into an absurd emergency call.

items.prompt: Write a closing argument for someone whose upstairs neighbor constantly rearranges furniture late at night
items.response: At 11 p.m., the scraping begins. The dragging. The inexplicable thud. My client does not know what is being moved, or why, or toward what vision. They know only that it happens, and that it happens at 11 p.m. We do not ask the defendant to stop living. We ask them to stop redecorating after dark.
items.intent: Protest chronic late-night disturbances through the language of formal grievance.

items.prompt: Write an eviction notice for a bad habit
items.response:
NOTICE OF EVICTION

To: The Habit of Checking My Phone First Thing in the Morning
Unit: The first 10 minutes of every day

You have colonized what was once a peaceful threshold. You arrive before coffee, before thought, before any decision is truly made. You will be replaced by silence, or possibly a book. You have been warned before. This time the locks are changed.

— The Morning
items.intent: Committing to break a mindless daily routine that starts the day on the wrong note.

items.prompt: Write a collections letter for a stolen sense of humor.

NOTICE — WIT IN ARREARS

Dear Appropriator,

Since you joined our social circle, the undersigned's jokes have been retold at parties without citation, our irony has been adopted wholesale, and at least one extended metaphor now circulates under your name.

We are not requesting royalties. We are requesting that you develop your own material.

Until then, this account remains open.

Dryly,
The Original Voice Bureau
items.intent: Humorously confront someone who adopted another person's comedic style and personality without acknowledgment.

items.prompt: Give me a cinquain about emoji replacing words in a text conversation

Typed

We hold entire arguments
In glyphs

The count ticks back to one.
Something requires my attention by end of day.

The same senders appear tomorrow.
Something requires my attention by end of day.
I archive forty-three emails at once.

The same senders appear tomorrow.
Inbox zero lasts eleven minutes.
The count ticks back to one.
I archive forty-three emails at once.

items.prompt: Write liner notes for an electronic album about reading terms and conditions

items.prompt: Generate a podcast episode description for an episode about the smell of a book nobody has opened in years
items.response: Vanilla. Almonds. Faint regret. The chemistry of old paper is well-documented; the meaning of it is not. This episode opens a book from 2004 and sits with what comes out.

items.prompt: Write a YouTube comment about a blacksmith forging a knife that sounds like a person having a spiritual experience
items.response: There is something being remembered in my hands watching this. Like I have done this before in another life. Anyway great content bro.

items.prompt: Write a closed caption for a toddler meeting the ocean for the first time
items.response: [SCALE INCOMPREHENSIBLE] [RUNNING AWAY] [RUNNING TOWARD] [RUNNING AWAY] [SOAKED]
items.intent: Capture the oscillation between terror and delight in a child's first encounter with vastness.

items.prompt: Make an artist statement about a bathroom mirror as if it's a sculpture
items.response: The mirror is not passive — it is the wall's one eye. It insists on the viewer's participation, refusing to exist without the body confronting it. You are not looking at the mirror. The mirror is completing itself through you.
items.intent: Recast a bathroom fixture as an interactive artwork that implicates the observer.

items.prompt: Headnote for homemade granola involving a wellness phase the narrator eventually outgrew
items.response: There was a year when I made my own granola, wore linen exclusively, and believed very strongly in journaling. The granola was the only habit that stuck, and I've kept it even as the linen went back to the thrift store and the journal became a running note on my phone. This recipe is from that year. I've forgiven myself for the rest of it.
items.intent: Chronicle a brief self-improvement era with dry self-awareness

items.prompt: Design a tasting menu for rage
items.response:
AMUSE-BOUCHE
A small injustice, tepid

FIRST COURSE
The slight, revisited three times

SECOND COURSE
Argument rehearsed in the shower

MAIN
Full heat, uncontrolled — please do not touch the plate

INTERMEZZO
Deep breath, mandatory

DESSERT
Calm, forced — served with notes of lingering resentment
items.intent: Trace the escalation and dissipation of intense anger.

items.prompt: write a unix man page for the experience of getting lost
items.response:
LOST(1)                  Navigation and Wayfinding        LOST(1)

NAME
       lost - undefined position state

SYNOPSIS
       lost [--context physical|metaphorical] [--last-known-position string]

DESCRIPTION
       lost(1) indicates that the host's current position cannot be
       reconciled with any known map. Occurs in physical environments,
       career trajectories, and relationships. Behavior varies by --context.

PHYSICAL MODE
       Circular path traversal common. Landmark misidentification frequent.
       Asking for directions(1) resolves 94% of cases.

METAPHORICAL MODE
       No map exists. Direction must be generated, not retrieved.
       Asking for directions(1) rarely helps. See: therapy(1).

EXIT STATUS
       0      Position reacquired.
       1      New position accepted as starting point.
       2      Host redefines destination to match current location.

BUGS
       Male-presenting hosts have elevated resistance to --help flag.

SEE ALSO
       navigate(1), directions(1), purpose(7), map(5)

Navigation and Wayfinding    2026                      LOST(1)
items.intent: Draw a parallel between physical disorientation and existential uncertainty.

items.prompt: write copy for a 404 page on a philosophy site where someone was looking for certainty
items.response:
404 — Certainty Not Found

This may be an error. Or it may not be. We honestly can't say.

The page may exist. The page may not exist. The server is doing its best, which is arguably all any of us can do.

Descartes would probably reload.
items.intent: Apply philosophical skepticism to the 404 error format with a knowing reference.

items.prompt: I need alt text for a close-up of an old rotary phone. Suggest it has outlived everyone who called it.
items.response: A black rotary phone sits on a doily, its handset still in its cradle, the finger-worn dial faded at the numbers dialed most often, which were once someone's entire world.
items.intent: Elicit grief through the worn material evidence of extinct daily rituals.

items.prompt: Make a deprecation notice for comparing yourself to people from high school on social media.
items.response:
DEPRECATION NOTICE

Behavior: Social Comparison (high school cohort, social media)
Status: DEPRECATED

This process has consistently returned inaccurate benchmarks derived from curated data. The sample is biased; the methodology is flawed; the output has never once been actionable. End of support, effective now. Unfollow as needed.
items.intent: Break the habit of measuring personal progress against carefully filtered social media highlights.

items.prompt: Draft a layoff email for a healthcare company that's outsourcing its billing department overseas
items.response:
Subject: Billing Operations Transition

Dear Team,

As we work to modernize and streamline our administrative operations, we have entered into a partnership with a global services provider to manage our billing and coding functions. This transition will result in the elimination of current billing department roles.

Impacted employees will receive a transition packet including severance details and career resources by end of day.

We are grateful for your contributions to our patients and this organization.

Respectfully,
Chief Financial Officer
items.intent: Inform billing staff they are being replaced by overseas contractors while dressing offshoring as modernization.

items.prompt: Make a rule about employees who eat at their desks loudly
items.response: Section 4.9 — Auditory Food Consumption Standards: Consumption of food items producing measurable masticatory sound — defined as audible from a distance exceeding eighteen (18) inches — must occur in designated Dining Areas. Desk consumption is permitted only for items classified as Silent or Near-Silent in the company's Approved Snack Registry.
items.intent: Manufacture a bureaucratic solution to noise-based snacking complaints by inventing a formal snack classification system.

items.prompt: Make a PIP for my terrible habit of checking my ex's social media
items.response:
PERFORMANCE IMPROVEMENT PLAN
Department: Emotional Recovery
Classification: Sensitive / Self-Issued

Deficiency: Subject engages in repeated unsolicited review of a former partner's publicly visible digital activity, including posts, tagged photos, story views, and follower count changes. Frequency: 3–8 times per week. Outcome of each session: negative, without exception.

Impact: Delays emotional processing; generates comparative distress; has caused subject to cancel two plans based on inferred information of no factual relevance.

Objective: Zero profile visits within 30-day window.

Steps:
1. Mute or block profile immediately. Blocking is not aggression; it is infrastructure.
2. Identify what need the checking fulfills (certainty? hope? pain?). Address that need directly.
3. Replace habit trigger (boredom, loneliness) with a physical action: walk, text an actual friend.

Review: 30 days. Metric: how you feel, not what you found.
items.intent: Create a structured recovery plan to stop the self-harming behavior of monitoring an ex online.

items.prompt: Draft a holiday letter where we mention our son finally moved out at 34 without making it weird
items.response:
Happy holidays from a newly spacious Tremblay home!

Daniel has officially launched. At long last, with great fanfare and three SUV-loads of belongings, he has begun his adult chapter in a studio apartment across town. We are thrilled for him. The basement is now an office. Progress is nonlinear, and we celebrate it all the same. Love to all.
items.intent: Announce an adult child finally leaving home in a tone that conceals years of tension.

items.prompt: write a yearbook quote about burning bridges on the way out of high school
items.response: "You'll know exactly what I think of you when my memoir comes out. Pre-order link in bio."
items.intent: Signaling that the person plans to expose grievances publicly in the future.

items.prompt: Draft a wedding invitation for the union of a grocery list and good intentions
items.response:
With hope in their hearts,

THE GROCERY LIST
&
GOOD INTENTIONS

announce their wedding.

Date: Sunday evening
Venue: The kitchen table
Outcome: A single banana and some pasta that wasn't on the list.

The couple asks for no gifts. Only patience.
items.intent: Gently mock the gap between planning healthy meals and what actually ends up in the cart.

items.prompt: Write a bumper sticker about the gig economy
items.response: FIVE STARS · ZERO BENEFITS · ONE LIFE
items.intent: Critique the rating-obsessed gig economy that strips workers of stability.

items.prompt: Write a billboard for a funeral home that heavily emphasizes its fun atmosphere and party vibes
items.response:
CELEBRATE GOOD TIMES — COME ON!
MEADOWLARK MEMORIAL & PARTY CENTER
Balloons. DJ. Open Casket Selfie Station.
"We Keep It Moving" — Our Promise To You
items.intent: Market end-of-life services using language and imagery associated with celebrations, creating tonal dissonance.

items.prompt: I need a sarcastic yard sign about the neighbor who leaves their trash cans out all week
items.response:
COLLECTION DAY
WAS TUESDAY

Today is Friday.

(Just thought you'd like to know.)
items.intent: Sarcastically call out a neighbor for leaving their trash cans out long after pickup day.

items.prompt: Write a complaint letter to a restaurant about the portion sizes being too large and making me feel bad for not finishing
items.response:
Dear Manager,

I dined at your establishment last Saturday and found the experience largely excellent, save for one difficulty: the portion sizes. The plate delivered to my table contained an amount of food I can only describe as optimistic. I could not finish it. The departure of the mostly-full plate produced a guilt I was not prepared for. I write not to criticize generosity, but to suggest that options in a more modest register would serve many guests well.

Kind regards,
A Recent Diner
items.intent: Advocate for smaller restaurant portions by framing an inability to finish a meal as an emotional burden.

items.prompt: write an apology to my book club for choosing a 900-page novel as the monthly pick
items.response:
To the group,

I misjudged the length. I thought we were all looking for a challenge and I was wrong — or at least, I was the only one. I'm sorry for the month of stress and for the very quiet meeting last Thursday. I'll pick something under 300 pages next time.

Cheers,
Francesca
items.intent: Acknowledge having imposed an unreasonable reading commitment on a casual reading group.

items.prompt: journal entry about trying to parallel park in front of people
items.response:
Dear Diary,

They were watching. A couple on a bench, just watching. I took four attempts. I know they counted. On the fourth I was acceptable. Not good. Acceptable. I walked away without eye contact and I will never return to that street.
items.intent: Convey the embarrassment of performing a skill badly under observation.

items.prompt: write a captain's log about running out of coffee in the break room
items.response: Stardate 5559.9. The ship's coffee supply has been exhausted. The canister reads: DARK ROAST — EMPTY. No one has purchased more. The sign that says "If you use the last of it, please brew a new pot" has been visible for three years. Crew appears functional. I do not trust this. I have ordered emergency provisions via a personal account.
items.intent: Dramatize the disproportionate crisis of an empty office coffee station.

items.prompt: Write a 911 call transcript where someone reports their houseplant is dying
items.response:
DISPATCHER: 911, what's your emergency?
CALLER: My fiddle-leaf fig is dying.
DISPATCHER: Is anyone in the home injured?
CALLER: The plant is a member of my household.
DISPATCHER: A plant is not—
CALLER: I paid sixty-two dollars for it at a boutique nursery. That counts.
DISPATCHER: Have you tried watering it?
CALLER: That's the problem. I watered it too much.
items.intent: Mock the expensive plant-parent identity by framing overwatering as a botanical emergency requiring rescue.

items.prompt: Draft a courtroom summation for a case about a coworker who always takes the last of the shared snacks without restocking
items.response: The trail mix was there on Monday. By Wednesday, it was gone — and the defendant had been seen, multiple times, at the bowl. No replenishment followed. The bowl simply remained, a hollow monument to what once was. Shared resources require shared stewardship. We ask the court to establish: if you finish it, you replace it.
items.intent: Establish a legal precedent for communal snack replenishment responsibility.

items.prompt: Can you draft an eviction notice for guilt
items.response:
EVICTION NOTICE

To: Guilt
Premises: My chest cavity, dinner table, and Sunday evenings

You have served your purpose. The lease — which was month-to-month — is now expired. Accountability has already moved in and paid first and last. You may no longer sublet the premises to Shame or Regret. Please remove your hairshirt and leave the key under the mat.

Signed, Forgiveness
items.intent: Advocating for self-forgiveness after a period of appropriate but prolonged remorse.

items.prompt: Generate a collections letter for a reader who owes a dead author their full attention.
items.response:
ON BEHALF OF THE ESTATE OF THE UNFINISHED BOOK

Dear Bookmarked Reader,

Our records show that you opened this volume 217 days ago, read 94 pages, and set it face-down on a nightstand where it has remained. The author committed years to this work. You committed a weekend.

This is a formal request that you return to page 95 within a reasonable period and complete the transaction.

The story is still there. It is waiting.

Regards,
The Literary Obligations Desk
items.intent: Guilt-trip a reader into finishing an abandoned book by framing the neglected act of reading as an unfulfilled obligation.

items.prompt: Write a cinquain about smart home devices always listening
items.response:
Hum
Soft, ever-present
Waiting for the wake word
I whisper secrets to the room
It logs
items.intent: Raise the quiet unease of always-on voice assistants in the home.

items.prompt: write a pantoum about the commute that begins before the sun rises
items.response:
The platform is dark at six fifteen.
The same faces hold the same phones.
The train delays itself for one minute.
We do not speak; we understand.

The same faces hold the same phones.
A woman in a red scarf reads a physical book.
We do not speak; we understand.
The doors close on a briefcase.

A woman in a red scarf reads a physical book.
I wonder if she too is late.
The doors close on a briefcase.
The platform is dark at six fifteen.

I wonder if she too is late.
The same faces hold the same phones.
We do not speak; we understand.
The train delays itself for one minute.
items.intent: Evoke the silent solidarity of the pre-dawn commute crowd.

items.prompt: Create liner notes for a minimalist classical album about watching paint dry
items.response:
Drying Time: Flat White (2018)

A single note. Sustained. What you perceive as stillness is transformation. What you interpret as boredom is patience. The second movement begins when the surface is no longer tacky to the touch. Allow adequate ventilation.
items.intent: Apply the serious formal language of minimalist classical music to the literal act of watching paint dry.

items.prompt: Write a description for a podcast episode about deciding which mug to use in the morning
items.response: Every mug is available. That is the problem. This episode documents the sixteen seconds before Fatima chooses the blue one, as she has every morning since 2019, for reasons she cannot explain.
items.intent: Find existential weight in a repeated, unconsidered daily micro-decision.

items.prompt: Write a YouTube comment for a video about a man who has eaten the same lunch every day for 30 years
items.response: Society would call this a disorder. His eyes say freedom.
items.intent: Reframe obsessive routine as a form of liberation rather than a pathology.

items.prompt: Generate a subtitle for the exact moment a party goes quiet when someone says the wrong thing
items.response: [MUSIC CONTINUING UNAWARE] [SEVENTEEN PEOPLE RECALIBRATING]
items.intent: Mark the social rupture that spreads faster than sound.

items.prompt: Write a serious-sounding artist statement about a doormat
items.response: The doormat occupies the liminal — neither inside nor outside, neither welcomed nor rejected. To wipe one's feet is an act of submission the home silently demands. I place this threshold as a question: who is truly being cleaned?
items.intent: Use a household object's position at a threshold to explore power and domesticity.

items.prompt: Give me a cookbook headnote for carbonara about learning to cook alone after a divorce
items.response: For a long time after the divorce I ate standing at the counter, not because I was in a hurry but because it seemed unnecessary to set the table for one. Then one night I made carbonara and it demanded my full attention — the timing, the temperature, the eggs that would scramble if I stopped paying respect — and I pulled a chair out. I've been sitting down since.
items.intent: Use a technically demanding dish as a metaphor for reclaiming solitary selfhood

items.prompt: Give me a tasting menu for learning a new language
items.response:
AMUSE-BOUCHE
Hello, goodbye — confident

FIRST COURSE
Basic nouns, heavily mispronounced

SECOND COURSE
Grammar exception you were not told about

INTERMEZZO
Flattery from a native speaker — sincere or not

MAIN
Your first dream in another tongue

DESSERT
Joke, finally understood — arrives late but warm
items.intent: Celebrate the long, humbling process of acquiring a second language.

items.prompt: make a man page for friendship
items.response:
FRIENDSHIP(7)            Interpersonal Utilities        FRIENDSHIP(7)

NAME
       friendship - persistent mutual-regard connection

SYNOPSIS
       friendship [--origin shared_context] [--maintenance_interval days]

DESCRIPTION
       friendship(7) is a long-running bidirectional process characterized
       by non-transactional resource exchange, tolerance of inconsistency,
       and accumulated shared memory. Does not require continuous uptime.

INITIALIZATION
       Typically seeded by proximity, shared circumstance, or mutual
       recognition of strangeness. Cannot be force-initialized.

OPTIONS
       --maintenance_interval
              Maximum gap between contact before degradation begins.
              Varies widely between instances. Some tolerate years.

DEGRADATION
       Caused by neglect, betrayal, or simple drift. See: drift(7).
       Some instances self-heal on contact after extended downtime.

EXIT STATUS
       Friendship(7) rarely exits cleanly. More often it goes silent.

SEE ALSO
       acquaintance(7), intimacy(7), loneliness(7), drift(7)

Interpersonal Utilities 3.0  2026               FRIENDSHIP(7)
items.intent: Capture how friendships persist through neglect and resist formal endings.

items.prompt: make a 404 page for a website about patience
items.response:
404 — Patience Not Found

We're working on it.

...

Still working on it.

...

Actually this might take a while. The page will be ready when it's ready.
items.intent: Demonstrate the absence of patience through a 404 page that is itself slow and evasive.

items.prompt: Alt text for an aerial photo of a perfect suburban grid. Let me feel the claustrophobia.
items.response: Identical rooftops extend to the edge of the frame in all directions, each with the same small rectangle of grass behind it, the variation so minor the eye gives up searching for it.
items.intent: Use the language of objective description to deliver a critique of enforced homogeneity.

items.prompt: Write a deprecation notice for perfectionism.
items.response:
DEPRECATION NOTICE

Cognitive Pattern: Perfectionism (v1.0–∞)
Status: DEPRECATED

This feature has historically prevented shipping, completing, and beginning. It masquerades as a quality standard while functioning as a delay mechanism. No further investment in this pattern is authorized. Replacement: good enough, delivered.
items.intent: Challenge perfectionism by framing it as a bug that prevents actual completion.

items.prompt: Can you write a layoff announcement for an ed-tech company that raised too much money and overhired
items.response:
Subject: Recalibrating Our Team for Sustainable Growth

Hello Team,

Over the past two years, we scaled our team rapidly to meet what we believed was a historic opportunity. We were not sufficiently disciplined about the pace of that growth, and today we are making the correction we should have made sooner.

We are reducing our workforce by 28%. This is our failure in leadership, not a reflection of you.

Affected employees: please check your personal email for next steps. Full severance details are enclosed.

Sorry, and thank you.
—The Founders
items.intent: Own an overhiring mistake in founder-speak that sounds self-aware but ultimately still results in firing 28% of the team.

items.prompt: Write a policy about employees using the office printer for personal documents
items.response: Section 8.2 — Personal Print Allotment: Each employee is granted a personal print allowance of twelve (12) pages per calendar month, printed in grayscale only. Pages in excess of this allotment will be billed at $0.07 per page, deducted from the employee's professional development budget. Color printing for personal use is not permitted under any circumstance.
items.intent: Impose an accounting-style framework on trivially small personal resource usage.

items.prompt: Draft a PIP for never responding to birthday invitations and just showing up
items.response:
PERFORMANCE IMPROVEMENT PLAN
Infraction: RSVP Non-Compliance, Chronic
Filing Department: Social Contracts

Statement of Issue: Subject has not RSVP'd to a social event in documented memory, opting instead to treat invitations as general advisories. Shows up to approximately 60% of events; absence is never communicated in advance.

Impact: Hosts cannot plan catering, seating, or group size. Subject's attendance has become a Schrödinger variable affecting others' logistics.

Required Behavior:
- Respond to all invitations within 72 hours with a clear yes or no.
- If plans are uncertain, communicate: "I'll know by Thursday" is acceptable. Silence is not.
- Surprise arrivals permitted only at surprise parties.

Plan:
- Enable calendar event from invitation immediately upon receipt.
- Draft response before closing the message.

Consequences of Non-Compliance: Hosts will begin inviting the number of people they expect, which will not include you.
items.intent: Address a long-standing failure to respond to event invitations that burdens hosts and strains social trust.

items.prompt: Write a cheery holiday update where we note that I've started therapy without making it a big deal
items.response:
Holiday greetings from the Nakamura household!

This year I took up some exciting new appointments — Tuesdays with a professional listener named Dr. Cho, which has frankly been the most useful thing I've done in a decade. Personal growth: highly recommended. The rest of the family is also fine, also perhaps slightly influenced. Wishing you all clarity and rest this season.
items.intent: Normalize personal therapy in an annual family update to an audience that might find it alarming.

items.prompt: write a yearbook quote that hints I was the anonymous gossip account without confirming it
items.response: "To everyone who thirsted over @millbrooktea — you're welcome. The account is archived. The memories are not."
items.intent: Taking oblique credit for running a secret school gossip account.

items.prompt: Write a wedding invitation for the marriage of silence and a library
items.response:
In hushed celebration,

SILENCE
weds
THE LIBRARY

Thursday afternoon, as it has always been.

Venue: Between the stacks, third floor.
Dress: Soft-soled shoes only.

Guests are asked to whisper their congratulations.
The reception will be conducted entirely in marginalia.
items.intent: Pay homage to the specific sacred quiet of a library as a near-living atmosphere.

items.prompt: Give me a bumper sticker for someone who overthinks everything
items.response: I'VE ALREADY APOLOGIZED FOR THIS IN MY HEAD
items.intent: Capture the exhausting internal rehearsal of anxious overthinking.

items.prompt: Draft billboard copy for an insurance company that only covers things that have already happened
items.response:
ALREADY HAPPENED? WE'VE GOT YOU.
HINDSIGHT MUTUAL INSURANCE
Retroactive Coverage For Events We Watched Unfold
"It's Like We Were There." (We Weren't.)
Call Now. Or Don't. It Won't Help.
items.intent: Sell insurance whose coverage window is in the past, making it functionally worthless as risk protection.

items.prompt: Write a yard sign opposing the proposed cell tower going up near the elementary school
items.response:
WRONG PLACE
WRONG TIME

No Cell Tower
Next to Lincoln Elementary

Sign our petition: StopTower22.com
items.intent: Organize community resistance to the placement of a cell tower near a school.

items.prompt: Draft a complaint to a library about people who leave books face-down to hold their page
items.response:
To the Head Librarian,

I write with some reluctance, as I believe libraries to be sacred spaces, but a matter of ethics compels me. Patrons are routinely leaving books placed open, face-down, on tables and chairs — a practice that damages spines, creases pages, and is, I feel, a form of small violence against literature. I urge the library to post a gentle reminder that bookmarks exist and are free at the desk.

Yours faithfully,
A Library Member
items.intent: Advocate for bookmark use by framing an inconsiderate reading habit as a form of harm to shared property.

items.prompt: write an apology note for giving unsolicited interior design advice at someone's housewarming
items.response:
Hi Theo,

Congratulations again on the new place — it's really lovely. I'm sorry I said the couch placement was 'fighting the room.' That was your home and your evening and I should have just had the cheese and said nothing. I hope the rest of the party was fun.

Best,
Marielle
items.intent: Apologize for offering critical unsolicited opinions about someone's home at their own celebration.

items.prompt: write me a diary entry about a dentist appointment
items.response:
Dear Diary,

They found 'something to watch.' A cavity in waiting. A threat. I nodded as though I understood what that meant and left with a small bag of floss I will not use. The next appointment is in six months. I will not think about it until then.
items.intent: Process low-grade dental anxiety through journaling.

items.prompt: can you draft a captain's log about a town hall where leadership took no questions
items.response: Stardate 1190.4. Company-wide assembly held in the main hangar at 1500. The admiral spoke for forty minutes on vision and momentum. A slide depicted an upward arrow. The arrow was unlabeled. At 1540 she said, "We're almost out of time, so we'll skip Q&A today and circle back in future sessions." No future sessions have been announced. The arrow remains direction-agnostic.
items.intent: Skewer the corporate town hall format that creates the illusion of transparency.

items.prompt: Generate a short 911 transcript where a caller reports that someone cut in line at a coffee shop
items.response:
DISPATCHER: 911, state your emergency.
CALLER: A man just cut in front of me at the coffee counter.
DISPATCHER: Sir—
CALLER: He was directly behind me and then he was directly in front of me.
DISPATCHER: Is anyone physically hurt?
CALLER: My dignity.
DISPATCHER: That's not something we—
CALLER: I want a report filed.
items.intent: Escalate the social contract violation of queue-jumping into a formal legal matter via emergency services.

items.prompt: Write a closing argument for someone who was ghosted after lending money to a friend
items.response: Forty dollars. That was the ask. My client gave without hesitation — because that is what friends do. What followed was silence. Not the silence of a busy schedule, but the silence of a person who has decided that forty dollars and a friendship are roughly equivalent in value. We ask the court to disagree.
items.intent: Use legal rhetoric to process the sting of being financially exploited by a friend.

items.prompt: Write a formal eviction notice for negativity bias
items.response:
FORMAL NOTICE OF EVICTION

To: Negativity Bias
Long-term Tenant, cognitive architecture, upper floors

You have amplified every minor slight to structural scale while dismissing genuine good fortune as statistical noise. Your evolutionary lease has lapsed. Gratitude has submitted a competing application and scored higher on the habitability rubric. You have 30 days. Please do not booby-trap the exits.

— The Prefrontal Cortex
items.intent: Trying to retrain one's mind toward appreciation rather than dwelling on what goes wrong.

items.prompt: Draft a collections letter from the future to someone who wasted today.
items.response:
FROM: YOUR FUTURE SELF
RE: OUTSTANDING TIME DEBT — TODAY, SPECIFICALLY

Dear Past You,

This office represents the accumulated consequences of how you spent the last twelve hours. The balance includes: one unstarted project, four unnecessary arguments with strangers online, and approximately ninety minutes described internally as "just resting my eyes."

Time, unlike money, cannot be refinanced. The debt is paid in regret, which as you know compounds daily.

We urge better behavior tomorrow.

Somewhat hopefully,
Future You
Collections Division
items.intent: Create a comedic but pointed self-accountability letter about wasted time that arrives from the future.

items.prompt: Compose a cinquain about the notification dot that never goes away
items.response:
Red
Small, insistent
One unread message waits
I opened it — still it glows —
Red dot
items.intent: Express the irrational anxiety produced by a persistent unread badge.

items.prompt: pantoum about the password expiry notification that appears every 90 days
items.response:
Your password expires in fourteen days.
I click Remind Me Later and move on.
The new password must not resemble the old.
Fourteen becomes seven becomes one.

I click Remind Me Later and move on.
One day I will update it before the warning.
Fourteen becomes seven becomes one.
The system locks me out at nine a.m.

One day I will update it before the warning.
I choose a word I will certainly forget.
The system locks me out at nine a.m.
IT replies: your ticket number is open.

I choose a word I will certainly forget.
Your password expires in fourteen days.
IT replies: your ticket number is open.
The new password must not resemble the old.
items.intent: Dramatize the relentless password-expiry cycle as a minor existential event.

items.prompt: Make liner notes for a rock album about reading the nutritional information on cereal boxes
items.response:
Serving Size (2020)

Who is eating three-quarters of a cup? Not us. These songs were written during breakfasts that lasted too long because we kept reading the box. "Riboflavin" is the closest thing to a single this record has. It will not be released to radio.
items.intent: Find comic and existential meaning in the compulsive habit of reading cereal box nutritional labels.

items.prompt: Describe a podcast episode where two hosts debate whether a parking lot counts as a place
items.response: A parking lot is a place you go to leave a place. Does that make it a place? Hosts Jim and Sasha have been on opposite sides of this since episode 1 and remain there now.
items.intent: Use a trivial spatial category as bait for a comic philosophical debate.

items.prompt: Write a YouTube comment for a time-lapse of an empty parking lot at midnight with ambient noise
items.response: I don't know who made this or why it exists. I have never felt more understood in my life.
items.intent: Articulate how certain voids in content can feel strangely intimate and personally validating.

items.prompt: Write the closed caption for someone watching their childhood home being demolished
items.response: [BACKHOE OPERATING NORMALLY] [MEMORY PALACE STRUCTURALLY COMPROMISED]
items.intent: Overlay the literal and the psychological destruction in a single frame.

items.prompt: Give me an artist statement about an alarm clock treated as profound art
items.response: The alarm clock is violence scheduled in advance. It ruptures the dreaming self at a time chosen by the waking self — a daily civil war waged across the self's own borders. I call it: The Consent I Withdrew from Myself.
items.intent: Frame the morning alarm as a metaphor for the tension between rest and productivity culture.

items.prompt: Write a headnote for vegetable soup that's weirdly philosophical about mortality
items.response: Everything in this soup was going to die anyway. The wilted celery, the soft carrot, the leek that had seen better days. I used to find this depressing. Now I find it liberating. You apply heat. You add stock. You end up with something better than the sum of its parts. I think about this more than is probably healthy.
items.intent: Reframe kitchen scraps and vegetable soup through an existential lens

items.prompt: Write a tasting menu for social anxiety
items.response:
AMUSE-BOUCHE
The invitation, declined internally before read

FIRST COURSE
Small talk, cold-sweated

SECOND COURSE
A remark replayed four hundred times

INTERMEZZO
Bathroom refuge, five minutes

MAIN
Actual enjoyment, brief and surprising

DESSERT
The walk home — relief, foam-light, evaporating fast
items.intent: Validate the experience of navigating social situations with anxiety.

items.prompt: write a man page for the feeling of being overwhelmed
items.response:
OVERWHELMED(7)           Human Affective States       OVERWHELMED(7)

NAME
       overwhelmed - task queue saturation state

SYNOPSIS
       overwhelmed [--queue-depth int] [--deadline-density float]

DESCRIPTION
       overwhelmed(7) is triggered when the host's active task queue
       exceeds processing capacity. May cascade: awareness of backlog
       consumes capacity needed to reduce backlog.

SYMPTOMS
       Paralysis despite urgency. Compulsive list-making without
       execution. Simultaneous awareness of everything and inability
       to begin anything.

OPTIONS
       --queue-depth  Number of concurrent obligations. Threshold varies.
       --deadline-density
                      Obligations per unit time. Triggers above 0.8.

TREATMENT
       Triage queue. Delete or defer lowest-priority items.
       Single-task mode recommended. See: focus(1).
       Warning: "working harder" without triage worsens condition.

SEE ALSO
       burnout(7), anxiety(7), focus(1), triage(1), rest(1)

Human Affective States 2.2   2026             OVERWHELMED(7)
items.intent: Illustrate how the awareness of too much to do can itself block doing anything.

items.prompt: draft a 404 for a wellness platform — specifically for when someone can't find their inner peace
items.response:
404 — Inner Peace Not Found

Please do not panic. This is a very small error in a very large system.

Breathe in. Breathe out. The page you requested does not exist, and that's okay.

Not everything has to be found to be real.
items.intent: Write ironic wellness-speak for a 404 page about unreachable tranquility.

items.prompt: Write alt text for a photo of a single candle burning in a dark room. Do not make it peaceful.
items.response: A white taper candle burns on a bare table in an otherwise lightless room, its flame leaning consistently to the left as though aware of a draft no one else can feel.
items.intent: Destabilize the candle-as-comfort cliche by giving the flame an unsettled interiority.

items.prompt: Give me a deprecation notice for the silent treatment as a conflict resolution strategy.
items.response:
DEPRECATION NOTICE

Conflict Strategy: Silent Treatment (legacy)
Status: DEPRECATED

This approach has never resolved a conflict; it has only delayed and compounded them. Emotional transmission via silence is lossy and often misinterpreted. Recommended replacement: direct, uncomfortable speech.
items.intent: Reject stonewalling as a communication strategy in favor of honest confrontation.

items.prompt: Write a brief layoff email for a marketing agency losing its biggest client
items.response:
Subject: Team Update

All,

We received news earlier this week that has significantly impacted our revenue outlook. As a result, we are making immediate adjustments to our team structure. Several positions across accounts and creative are being eliminated effective today.

We will be reaching out to affected individuals directly. For those continuing, we will share more about our path forward in tomorrow's all-hands.

Thank you for everything.

—Agency Leadership
items.intent: Quietly announce that losing one whale client is tanking jobs, without naming the client or the full scope of damage.

items.prompt: Draft a handbook rule about remote employees appearing on video calls with virtual backgrounds
items.response: Section 7.12 — Remote Visual Representation: Virtual backgrounds are permitted provided they depict (a) a plain color, (b) an approved neutral indoor environment, or (c) a landscape free of identifiable landmarks. Backgrounds featuring beaches, outer space, or the interiors of competitors' offices are prohibited. Animated backgrounds require written pre-approval from your department head.
items.intent: Regulate remote workers' aesthetic choices in video calls as if they constitute a compliance risk.

items.prompt: Write a formal PIP for my failure to drink enough water daily
items.response:
PERFORMANCE IMPROVEMENT PLAN
Functional Area: Basic Biological Maintenance
Status: Chronic Non-Compliance

Deficiency: Subject consumes an estimated 1–2 cups of water per day, supplemented primarily by coffee and one sports drink consumed reactively after headache onset. Minimum recommended intake: 8 cups.

Symptoms Attributed to Deficiency: afternoon headaches, fatigue diagnosed as personality, dry skin addressed via expensive serums rather than source correction.

Plan:
- 32 oz water before noon, daily.
- Water bottle present at desk and refilled at minimum twice per workday.
- Coffee does not count. Coffee is a separate, non-offsetting input.

Week 3 Check-In: Assess headache frequency and note whether fatigue has decreased before attributing it to other causes.

Review: 45 days. Metric: self-reported hydration and headache log.

Note: The serum budget may be redirected.
items.intent: Establish a daily water intake habit to address chronic dehydration masked by other beverages.

items.prompt: Help me write a holiday letter where we mention our financial setbacks as 'simplifying our lifestyle'
items.response:
Peace and simplicity from the Vance family this season!

2025 brought us the opportunity to reassess what truly matters — which, it turns out, is not the second car, the gym membership, or restaurants. We have returned to fundamentals: home cooking, board games, library cards. We are leaner, closer, and very aware of what things cost. Happy simpler holidays to you and yours.
items.intent: Use aspirational minimalism language to describe cutting back due to financial hardship.

items.prompt: generate a yearbook quote for someone who spent all four years in the wrong friend group
items.response: "Here's to the people I sat with at lunch. May we meet again under better circumstances and with more in common."
items.intent: Expressing polite regret about spending formative years with incompatible people.

items.prompt: Write a wedding invitation for coffee and Monday getting married
items.response:
COFFEE & MONDAY

You already attend this ceremony every week.

Venue: Your kitchen. Or the drive-through if it's bad.
Time: Before you're fully awake.

Vows are unspoken but understood.
The marriage is co-dependent. It works.
items.intent: Frame the ritualistic morning coffee habit as a committed ongoing relationship.

items.prompt: Bumper sticker about student loan debt
items.response: PROUDLY FINANCING MY DEGREE UNTIL 2047
items.intent: Satirize the multigenerational burden of higher education debt.

items.prompt: Write a billboard for a pet store that only sells invisible pets
items.response:
THE PET YOU'LL NEVER SEE COMING.
PHANTOM PAWS PET BOUTIQUE
Fish. Cats. Dogs. Rabbits. (Trust Us.)
Starting at $149. Leashes Sold Separately.
Please Don't Ask To Hold One.
items.intent: Market an animal retail shop whose product is entirely unverifiable and therefore impossible to evaluate.

items.prompt: Make a yard sign that reminds walkers to leash their dogs
items.response:
LEASH LAWS
APPLY HERE

Our kids play here.
Your dog is friendly.
The ordinance doesn't care.
items.intent: Remind dog owners of leash laws using mild social pressure rather than direct confrontation.

items.prompt: Write me a formal complaint to a theme park about the mascot character being too committed to its role
items.response:
To Guest Relations,

I am writing to report an interaction with one of your costumed ambassadors that exceeded reasonable limits. The character in question maintained full in-character behavior for an uninterrupted twenty-two minutes despite my repeated attempts to communicate a practical question about the restroom location. I was eventually redirected via enthusiastic mime. While I admire dedication, there must be a protocol for real-world communication. Please clarify.

Regards,
A Guest
items.intent: Report a theme park character whose unwillingness to break character prevented a visitor from getting basic practical help.

items.prompt: write an apology for mispronouncing a colleague's name for an entire year before being corrected
items.response:
Hi Siobhan,

I've been saying your name wrong for about twelve months. I want to apologize — not only for the mispronunciation but for not noticing, or not noticing that I wasn't noticing. Thank you for finally telling me. I'll get it right.

— Tom
items.intent: Express genuine remorse for a year-long failure to correctly pronounce a coworker's name.

items.prompt: write a diary entry where someone dramatically reacts to running out of phone battery
items.response:
Dear Diary,

Fourteen percent when I left the house. I was conservative. I was careful. But the Maps app is ravenous and by noon I was at three percent, cut off from all information, all connection, all maps. I had to ask a stranger for directions. A stranger. With my mouth.
items.intent: Satirize total dependence on smartphones by exaggerating its absence.

items.prompt: write a captain's log about the office AC being broken in summer
items.response: Stardate 4481.7. Environmental controls aboard the vessel have malfunctioned. Ambient temperature: 81 degrees Fahrenheit. Facilities was notified at 0800. Facilities acknowledged the ticket at 1500. Three crew members have brought personal fans. One has sourced a cooling towel. The admiral has been "looped in." The admiral is in a temperature-controlled office. The admiral is comfortable.
items.intent: Vent about the inequity and discomfort of a broken office HVAC system.

items.prompt: Write a 911 call transcript where someone reports the ice cream truck hasn't come by this summer
items.response:
DISPATCHER: 911, what's your emergency?
CALLER: The ice cream truck has not come down my street once this entire summer.
DISPATCHER: Ma'am, that's not an emergency.
CALLER: My children have been listening for it since May.
DISPATCHER: I understand but—
CALLER: They check the window every evening. Hopeful faces. Every evening.
DISPATCHER: I'm... sorry.
CALLER: Are you writing this down?
items.intent: Use childhood disappointment and parental guilt to reframe a business routing decision as a community crisis.

items.prompt: Write a summation for a dispute where someone kept using the last of the toilet paper and not replacing the roll
items.response: The plaintiff arrives. The situation becomes clear. The defendant has once again completed the roll and left the cardboard cylinder as a gesture — a placeholder for effort never expended. We do not ask for perfection. We ask for three squares of minimum civic participation: peel the wrapper, slide the roll, close the tab.
items.intent: Formalize the household norm around toilet paper replacement as a legal duty.

items.prompt: Write an eviction notice for silence that has lasted too long in a relationship
items.response:
NOTICE TO VACATE

To: The Silence Between Us
Unit: Every room we share

You arrived after an argument in February and claimed temporary residence. It is now autumn. You have expanded into the kitchen, the car, and most of Sunday mornings. Neither of us invited you to stay this long. Conversation has been knocking. Please step aside.

— Two People Who Should Talk
items.intent: Prompting a difficult but necessary conversation with a partner after a prolonged estrangement.

items.prompt: Write a collections letter for joy that was suppressed by someone else's expectations.
items.response:
NOTICE OF JOY SUPPRESSION — ACCOUNT IN DEFAULT

Dear Expectation-Holder,

From approximately age nine through early adulthood, the natural joy of the undersigned was systematically reduced through the application of your standards, comparisons, and expressions of disappointment.

The undersigned has since begun recovery proceedings. However, full restoration remains incomplete.

We are not requesting a refund. We are requesting acknowledgment that the debt exists.

With measured hope,
The Joy Recovery Commission
items.intent: Articulate the lifelong cost of having one's authentic happiness suppressed by others' expectations, typically a parent's.

items.prompt: Write a cinquain about the cursor blinking on an empty document
items.response:
Blank
Page holds its breath
The cursor blinks, patient, cold
All that I meant to say waits there
Untyped
items.intent: Explore writer's block as mediated through the blank digital page.

items.prompt: write a pantoum about the colleague who sends emails at 11pm
items.response:
The notification arrives while I am asleep.
No response is expected, yet I wonder.
The subject line: quick question for tomorrow.
I draft a reply in the dark of my mind.

No response is expected, yet I wonder.
Is this a test of my availability?
I draft a reply in the dark of my mind.
By morning the thread has grown three messages deep.

Is this a test of my availability?
I mark it unread and return to sleep.
By morning the thread has grown three messages deep.
The notification arrives while I am asleep.

I mark it unread and return to sleep.
The subject line: quick question for tomorrow.
By morning the thread has grown three messages deep.
No response is expected, yet I wonder.
items.intent: Capture the low-grade anxiety of late-night work emails from an always-on colleague.

items.prompt: Write liner notes for an album about waiting for a slow computer to boot
items.response:
POST Sequence (2017)

The BIOS screen as meditation. The spinning circle as mantra. This record was composed in the intervals between pressing the power button and being allowed to do anything at all. We did not hurry. We had no choice but not to hurry.
items.intent: Treat the frustrating experience of a slow computer startup as a contemplative musical subject.

items.prompt: Write a podcast episode description about noticing that you've been holding your breath without realizing it
items.response: At some point in the last hour, you stopped breathing normally. So did our hosts. Episode 9 is about the moment of noticing—and the deeply suspicious six seconds that follow.
items.intent: Turn involuntary bodily self-awareness into a meditative podcast subject.

items.prompt: Write a YouTube comment for a video of a chef deboning a whole chicken very precisely
items.response: He treats that bird with more respect than most people treat their relationships. Clean cuts. No hesitation. Clear intentions.
items.intent: Use the precision of a culinary technique as a metaphor for emotional clarity in relationships.

items.prompt: Create a subtitle for a librarian silently judging someone's book choices
items.response: [PROFESSIONAL NEUTRALITY MAINTAINED] [INTERNAL REVIEW ONGOING]
items.intent: Surface the private verdict concealed beneath a service worker's blank expression.

items.prompt: Pretentious artist statement about a drinking straw
items.response: The straw mediates between the body and the world without contaminating either. It is pure conduit — desire reduced to a tube, pleasure delivered along a straight line, the mouth's most honest collaborator.
items.intent: Elevate a simple beverage accessory into a meditation on desire and mediation.

items.prompt: Headnote for a simple rice dish that involves a story about a terrible hotel room abroad
items.response: The hotel in Osaka had a broken radiator, a window that wouldn't close, and a vending machine in the hallway that sold canned hot rice for two hundred yen. I lived on it for four days during a storm I hadn't planned for. I have since been to Osaka three more times. I always find the vending machine. This recipe is a less efficient version of the same idea.
items.intent: Tribute a convenience food's comfort through an elaborate backstory of being stranded

items.prompt: Can you write a tasting menu for democracy?
items.response:
AMUSE-BOUCHE
A pamphlet

FIRST COURSE
Debate — long, warm, frequently interrupted

SECOND COURSE
Compromise, bitter with a complex finish

INTERMEZZO
Scandal, served seasonally

MAIN
The vote — one per guest, no substitutions

DESSERT
Result, contested — recommend you sit with it
items.intent: Examine the messy, slow, necessary process of democratic governance.

items.prompt: write a man page for making a decision
items.response:
DECIDE(1)                Human Cognitive Suite           DECIDE(1)

NAME
       decide - commit to one branch from a set of alternatives

SYNOPSIS
       decide [-n options] [--reversible bool] [--stakes level] CHOICE_SET

DESCRIPTION
       decide(1) selects and executes one path from CHOICE_SET, discarding
       all others. Quality of output inversely correlated with time spent
       on analysis beyond the first 60% of available data.

OPTIONS
       -n        Number of options. Performance degrades above n=7.
       --reversible
                 If true, reduce effort proportionally.
       --stakes  low|medium|high|existential. Above "high", paralysis risk.

KNOWN ISSUES
       Hosts frequently reopen closed decisions. This is a feature bug.
       Regret(7) may mount regardless of option quality.
       Choosing not to decide is itself decide(1) with default arguments.

EXIT STATUS
       0      Decision executed.
       1      Analysis loop detected. See: overthinking(7).

SEE ALSO
       overthinking(7), regret(7), commitment(7), gut-feeling(3)

Human Cognitive Suite 1.3    2026                  DECIDE(1)
items.intent: Satirize the paradox of choice and the trap of over-analyzing decisions.

items.prompt: write a 404 page for a relationship app where someone's looking for the spark
items.response:
404 — The Spark Not Found

Something was here. Something warm. We felt it too.

It may have been cached at one point, but the cache was cleared — probably around month eight.

You can't force this redirect. Go back. Try a different path.
items.intent: Describe the fading of romantic chemistry using the metaphor of a missing web resource.

items.prompt: Write alt text for a photo of a museum exhibit of ancient tools, as if the tools remember their jobs.
items.response: Stone hand axes and bone needles arrayed under glass on archival foam, each shaped by fingers that are now soil, each still perfectly adequate for a task no one will ask of them.
items.intent: Make a museum label do the work of elegy by dwelling on obsolescence-without-failure.

items.prompt: Draft a deprecation notice for the ability to fall asleep anywhere like I could in my twenties.
items.response:
DEPRECATION NOTICE

Feature: Anywhere Sleep (v20–29)
Status: DEPRECATED

This capability — including but not limited to: airport floors, moving vehicles, mid-sentence — has been phased out in this release cycle. Replacement functionality requires a pillow, darkness, a specific temperature, and several weeks of low stress.
items.intent: Lament the loss of effortless sleep that came easily in youth.

items.prompt: Write a layoff email for a nonprofit that lost a major government grant
items.response:
Subject: Difficult News Regarding Our Organization

Dear Staff,

We write with heavy hearts to share that we have not received renewal of the federal grant that has supported a significant portion of our programming and staffing. Despite our best advocacy efforts, this funding gap cannot be bridged in the near term.

As a result, we must eliminate 17 positions, effective 30 days from today.

We will do everything in our power to support those affected. Your work has mattered deeply.

In solidarity,
Executive Director & Board
items.intent: Communicate funding-driven layoffs at a mission-driven organization without making it sound like the organization failed.

items.prompt: Generate a policy about employees borrowing office supplies and not returning them
items.response: Section 8.5 — Temporary Supply Custody: Employees borrowing items from the communal supply station must log the item, quantity, and expected return date in the Supply Stewardship Ledger. Items not returned within five (5) business days become the financial responsibility of the borrowing employee at replacement cost plus a 15% processing fee.
items.intent: Apply a formal chain-of-custody process to the borrowing of staplers and tape.

items.prompt: Generate a PIP for consistently forgetting to call my parents back
items.response:
PERFORMANCE IMPROVEMENT PLAN
Relationship Area: Family Communications
Issued By: Self (on behalf of parental unit)

Deficiency: Subject receives calls from parents at a rate of 3–5 per week; returns approximately 20% within the same calendar day. Voicemails are listened to but treated as closed items. Follow-up calls: infrequent, guilt-driven.

Impact: Parents have begun calling sibling to check on subject. Subject experiences diffuse guilt every Sunday evening.

Objective: Return calls within 24 hours; proactively initiate 1 call per week.

Plan:
- On missed call: flag as task, not notification.
- Designate one weekly call slot (Sunday afternoon suggested; preempts Sunday guilt).
- Listening to voicemail counts as step 1, not completion.

Review: 30 days. Success metric: no calls routed to sibling as relay.

Note: They are not going to be here forever. This is the whole plan.
items.intent: Build a consistent habit of staying in touch with aging parents before the absence of contact becomes regret.

items.prompt: Write a newsletter where we mention a sibling estrangement only in passing
items.response:
Warmest greetings from the Calloway family!

We had the usual gatherings this year — smaller than in years past, more intentional. Some relationships are on pause, as relationships sometimes are, and we're focused on the ones that are present and warm. There's much to be grateful for. The food was excellent. The fire was lit. Merry Christmas to those of you reading this.
items.intent: Acknowledge a family rift to recipients who may have noticed an absence without explaining it.

items.prompt: write a yearbook quote where I hint that I have a secret everyone else missed
items.response: "Everything you thought you knew about junior year is wrong. Sleep well."
items.intent: Teasing classmates with the implication that a significant truth was hidden from them.

items.prompt: Create an elegant invitation for the wedding of mathematics and music
items.response:
The families of MATHEMATICS and MUSIC
request the honour of your presence
at their eternal union.

Date: Since Pythagoras, uninterrupted
Venue: Every concert hall and chalkboard ever built

The couple has always been inseparable.
Those who doubted the match are asked to reconsider.

Black tie. Bring your ears.
items.intent: Celebrate the deep structural relationship between numerical patterns and musical composition.

items.prompt: Write a bumper sticker about feeling like you're constantly performing happiness on social media
items.response: CURATED LIFE. ACTUAL CHAOS.
items.intent: Expose the gap between online persona and lived reality.

items.prompt: Give me billboard copy for a car dealership that exclusively sells cars with the steering wheel removed
items.response:
YOUR NEXT CAR HAS GREAT BONES.
MERIDIAN AUTO — "We Handle The Hard Parts"
Full Interior. Pristine Exterior. Certain Compromises.
Financing Available. Guardrails Not Included.
items.intent: Advertise vehicles that are missing a component essential to safe and legal operation.

items.prompt: Create a yard sign for my campaign to get speed bumps installed on our block
items.response:
SIGN IF YOU AGREE:
SPEED BUMPS ON RIDGEWOOD

Over 60 signatures collected.
Next: City Meeting, June 19.

Knock for info →
items.intent: Recruit neighbors to a petition drive for traffic calming infrastructure on their street.

items.prompt: Write a complaint letter to an airline about the window shade policy on a daytime flight
items.response:
To Customer Relations,

I write following my recent flight on your carrier. Approximately forty minutes after takeoff, a flight attendant requested that all window shades be lowered for the in-flight entertainment. This was a daytime flight over an extremely beautiful coastline that I had specifically selected a window seat to observe. I complied reluctantly. The film was a forgettable action sequel. I feel I made a poor trade. I ask that window shade closures be made optional.

Sincerely,
Passenger [Seat 14A]
items.intent: Protest an airline policy that forced passengers to miss a scenic view in favor of an optional movie.

items.prompt: write an apology note for texting someone happy birthday on entirely the wrong day
items.response:
Hi!

I realize your birthday was in October and I sent that message in March. I have no good explanation. I hope you still felt celebrated, technically, and I'm sorry for the confusion. I'll add the right date to my calendar.

— Bex
items.intent: Apologize for congratulating someone on their birthday at a completely incorrect time.

items.prompt: journal entry about a neighbor who mows the lawn at an inconvenient hour
items.response:
Dear Diary,

7:58 AM. On a Saturday. The mower began. I had been asleep. I was not ready to be un-asleep. I lay in bed with my eyes open, cataloguing grievances in chronological order. The lawn does look good now. That is irrelevant.
items.intent: Express neighbor-related irritation without direct confrontation.

items.prompt: captain's log for a day when three different people scheduled over each other on your calendar
items.response: Stardate 2903.6. At 0830 I discovered the anomaly: three separate parties had claimed 1400 hours. Each had sent a hold. None had checked availability. I am one entity. I occupy one chair. I have begun triaging by seniority. The most senior party will receive my physical presence. The other two will receive a camera and the partial attention of someone who has already been in four meetings.
items.intent: Describe the chaos of calendar conflicts caused by poor scheduling coordination.

items.prompt: Draft a 911 transcript about someone who reports that a store put up their Halloween display too early
items.response:
DISPATCHER: 911, what's the emergency?
CALLER: The drug store on Fifth has skeleton decorations. It's August third.
DISPATCHER: Is there a hazard?
CALLER: To public morale, yes. We agreed as a society on boundaries.
DISPATCHER: No such agreement was formalized.
CALLER: Informally. We all know the rules.
DISPATCHER: I can't arrest a display, ma'am.
items.intent: Invoke unwritten seasonal norms as quasi-legal agreements that merit enforcement by authorities.

items.prompt: Write a closing argument for someone whose friend always cancels plans at the last minute
items.response: Reservations were made. An outfit was chosen. My client adjusted their expectations, their schedule, and their dinner plans — only to receive, forty minutes before the agreed time, three words and a rain emoji. The defendant calls it flexibility. My client calls it a pattern. We ask for recognition that time, once budgeted, cannot be refunded.
items.intent: Formalize the emotional cost of habitual last-minute cancellations by a friend.

items.prompt: Draft an eviction notice for the version of myself I pretend to be online
items.response:
NOTICE TO QUIT

To: The Curated Self
Unit: My public profile and the first two seconds of every video

You have maintained a lease on my identity through selective lighting and strategic cropping. The actual tenant — messy, uncertain, occasionally funny for the wrong reasons — has never been given proper occupancy. You will vacate this persona by end of quarter. The filters are being returned.

— The Actual Person
items.intent: Reckoning with the gap between a performed online identity and the authentic self.

items.prompt: Make a collections letter for a mentor who never passed down their knowledge.
items.response:
KNOWLEDGE TRANSFER — OUTSTANDING BALANCE

Dear Mentor of Record,

You were designated as the professional steward of institutional knowledge in the relevant field. Over three years, this responsibility was expressed primarily as vague encouragement and the occasional forwarded article.

The hard-won expertise promised at the outset was not transferred. The undersigned learned largely through failure, which is, we note, a very expensive school.

We are requesting either retrospective instruction or a formal acknowledgment of the gap.

Practically speaking,
The Apprenticeship Deficit Office
items.intent: Formally address the failure of a mentor to actually impart the knowledge and guidance they were expected to provide.

items.prompt: Make a cinquain about a drone hovering outside a window
items.response:
Eye
Suspended, quiet
Rotors stitching the still air
Through glass I meet its single lens
Who sent you?
items.intent: Probe the unease of being observed by an unmanned machine in private space.

items.prompt: create a pantoum about the end-of-quarter scramble to hit targets
items.response:
We have eleven days left in the quarter.
Every meeting is now urgent.
The pipeline spreadsheet is pinned to every screen.
Somebody pulls a deal that was thought dead.

Every meeting is now urgent.
We eat sandwiches over the forecast call.
Somebody pulls a deal that was thought dead.
The number moves; it is still not enough.

We eat sandwiches over the forecast call.
The last two days feel like a different year.
The number moves; it is still not enough.
We have eleven days left in the quarter.

The last two days feel like a different year.
Every meeting is now urgent.
We eat sandwiches over the forecast call.
Somebody pulls a deal that was thought dead.
items.intent: Dramatize the ritual desperation of quarter-end sales pressure.

items.prompt: Write album liner notes for music inspired by reading apartment lease agreements
items.response:
Section 12, Subsection B (2023)

This record is about what you sign without reading. It is about the clause that changes everything and the clause that changes nothing but sounds alarming. The keyboardist found this album in a lease she signed in 2015 and only read in 2023. We are not liable.
items.intent: Turn the anxiety and opacity of legal rental agreements into material for a musical statement.

items.prompt: I want an episode description for a podcast about the specific feeling of a word sounding wrong if you say it too many times
items.response: Chair. Chair. Chair. Chair. Chair. By the time you finish this description, one word has already lost all meaning. This week: semantic satiation, or as the hosts prefer to call it, the unraveling.
items.intent: Dramatize the psychological phenomenon of verbal repetition destabilizing meaning.

items.prompt: Give me a YouTube comment for a compilation of failed skateboard tricks where every person walks away fine
items.response: Every single one of them got up. Not one person stayed down. I'm writing this down in my journal tonight.
items.intent: Find unexpected inspiration and life advice in a sports blooper reel.

items.prompt: Write a closed caption for someone trying to remember if they turned off the stove while already on the highway
items.response: [70 MPH] [REVIEWING THIS MORNING'S EVENTS] [CERTAINTY AT 43% AND FALLING]
items.intent: Track the anxiety spiral that accelerates inversely to vehicle speed.

items.prompt: Write an artsy statement about a used candle stub
items.response: What remains is not failure but record. The stub is autobiography — evidence of every hour the flame consented to illuminate. I preserve it not for what it gives, but for what it has already given.
items.intent: Turn the remnant of a spent candle into a meditation on sacrifice and memory.

items.prompt: Write a cookbook headnote for peach cobbler with a Southern grandmother character who disapproved of the narrator
items.response: My grandmother disapproved of me in a general, ambient way that never quite resolved into specifics. The one exception was peach cobbler. When I made it right she would say nothing, which in her vocabulary was the highest praise available. I am still trying to earn that silence. This recipe is close. It is not yet there.
items.intent: Convey the weight of a difficult family relationship through perfecting a beloved dessert

items.prompt: Make a tasting menu for moving to a new city
items.response:
AMUSE-BOUCHE
Apartment, empty — smells of other lives

FIRST COURSE
Neighborhood, navigated by instinct

SECOND COURSE
Loneliness on a Saturday, full portion

INTERMEZZO
A stranger who becomes a regular

MAIN
The first night you don't consult a map

DESSERT
City, finally yours — slow-grown
items.intent: Describe the emotional journey of building a new life in an unfamiliar place.

items.prompt: make a unix man page for the experience of crying
items.response:
CRY(1)                   Human Expression Suite           CRY(1)

NAME
       cry - lacrimal pressure-relief valve

SYNOPSIS
       cry [--trigger event] [--type quiet|ugly|cathartic|inconvenient]

DESCRIPTION
       cry(1) releases accumulated emotional pressure via lacrimal ducts.
       May be triggered by sadness, joy, frustration, exhaustion, a
       particularly moving dog video, or no identifiable cause.

OPTIONS
       --trigger   Precipitating event. Optional; system may auto-trigger.
       --type      Output mode.
                   quiet       Low-visibility, high-frequency use case.
                   ugly        Maximum throughput. Not for public contexts.
                   cathartic   Full-cycle release. Recommended.
                   inconvenient
                               Fires during meetings, public transit, etc.

SIDE EFFECTS
       Nasal congestion. Facial redness. Post-cry calm (cathartic mode).
       Mascara state undefined after run.

NOTES
       Suppression is possible short-term via jaw-clench(1) but increases
       next-cycle intensity.

SEE ALSO
       grief(7), relief(7), laughter(1), exhaustion(7)

Human Expression Suite 1.0   2026                     CRY(1)
items.intent: Normalize crying as a functional and necessary release mechanism.

items.prompt: write a 404 page for a self-help site for when someone can't find their confidence
items.response:
404 — Confidence Not Found

This happens to more people than you'd think. Like, way more.

The page exists. It just doesn't believe anyone will visit it, so it hasn't loaded.

Try again. It's watching.
items.intent: Personify a lack of self-confidence through an error page that itself lacks confidence.

items.prompt: Give me alt text for a photo of an astronaut looking out a porthole at Earth. Focus on what looking back costs.
items.response: An astronaut in a white suit presses their helmet against a circular porthole, Earth reflected in the visor, the planet appearing impossibly small and impossibly fragile at once.
items.intent: Use the overview effect as a prompt for describing the psychological cost of perspective.

items.prompt: Write a deprecation notice for romantic idealism.
items.response:
DEPRECATION NOTICE

Worldview Module: Romantic Idealism (early build)
Status: DEPRECATED

The belief that love arrives fully formed, requires no maintenance, and survives on feeling alone has reached end-of-life. Users upgrading to mature attachment are advised that the new version requires ongoing labor, honesty, and the occasional dull Tuesday.
items.intent: Move past fairy-tale expectations about love toward a realistic and sustainable model of partnership.

items.prompt: Draft a layoff announcement email for a cybersecurity firm cutting its sales team after pivoting to a product-led growth model
items.response:
Subject: Evolving Our Go-to-Market Approach

Team,

As we shift to a product-led growth model, we are restructuring our commercial organization to match. This evolution, while exciting for our product trajectory, means that many current sales roles will no longer exist in their current form.

We are deeply grateful to the sales team for building the customer relationships that made our growth possible. Affected colleagues will receive information from HR by noon.

Onward,
Chief Revenue Officer
items.intent: Fire the sales team while crediting them for growth they enabled and blaming a business model pivot rather than their performance.

items.prompt: Write an official rule about employees who hold doors open for too long while chatting
items.response: Section 2.6 — Threshold Occupancy Restriction: Employees must not obstruct any doorway, hallway intersection, or stairwell landing for conversational purposes exceeding ninety (90) seconds. Continued conversation must be relocated to an unobstructed space. Repeat violations affecting egress pathways will be escalated to the Safety & Flow Committee.
items.intent: Enforce spatial efficiency on small-talk habits by treating doorway socializing as a safety issue.

items.prompt: Write a PIP for my habit of saying 'I'll start Monday' and then not starting
items.response:
PERFORMANCE IMPROVEMENT PLAN
Issuing Body: Self-Governance Committee
Pattern Under Review: Deferred Commencement Syndrome

Incident History: Over the past 14 months, subject has designated Monday as start date for: a fitness routine (7 times), a reading goal (4 times), a budget plan (3 times), a journaling habit (6 times), and a general "getting it together" initiative (ongoing).

Mondays elapsed in this period: 61. Initiatives sustained past first Monday: 0.

Root Cause: Monday functions as a psychological reset that provides relief from present discomfort without requiring present action.

Corrective Action:
