- Identify one small action takeable today. Begin it today.
- Monday may serve as a reinforcement day, not an origination day.
- Ban the phrase "starting Monday" for 60 days.

Milestone: One habit with an 8-day streak, started on a non-Monday.

Review: 30 days.
items.intent: Break a cycle of perpetually deferring self-improvement to an arbitrary future start date.

items.prompt: Draft a holiday letter that sounds festive but makes clear we're exhausted caregivers without complaining
items.response:
Greetings and love from the Moreau family!

This year has been full — truly, thoroughly full. Mom and Dad's needs have grown, and so have we in response. We've become experts in hospital parking, pharmacy logistics, and the art of the 6 a.m. phone call. We are tired the way people are when they love someone. Wishing everyone a restful, uncomplicated December.
items.intent: Communicate caregiver burnout to friends and family without asking for sympathy or help.

items.prompt: help me write a yearbook quote about my plan to move to a foreign country and never pay taxes
items.response: "Post-grad plan: a laptop, a hammock, and a jurisdiction my parents can't spell."
items.intent: Advertising a fantasy of offshore financial escape from adult obligations.

items.prompt: Write a wedding invitation for the marriage of insomnia and a racing mind
items.response:
At 2:43 AM,

INSOMNIA
takes
A RACING MIND

for better, for worse, for the foreseeable future.

Venue: The ceiling above your bed.
Guests: An unfinished email, three past conversations, tomorrow's agenda.

The ceremony has no scheduled end time.
No RSVP required. You're already there.
items.intent: Describe the familiar misery of lying awake with too many thoughts through the metaphor of a ceremony.

items.prompt: Make something for a bumper sticker about subscription fatigue
items.response: I OWN NOTHING AND I NOTICE IT MONTHLY
items.intent: Parody the shift from ownership to perpetual subscription payments.

items.prompt: Come up with a roadside ad for a spa that only offers services that make you feel worse
items.response:
YOU DESERVE THIS.
THORNWELL SPA & WELLNESS RETREAT
Services: The Gravel Facial. The Wind Treatment. The Noise.
Leave Feeling Changed. (Downward.)
Book Online. Walk-Ins Discouraged.
items.intent: Market wellness treatments whose outcome is explicitly negative, inverting the core promise of the industry.

items.prompt: Give me a yard sign about my neighbor who mows at 7am every Saturday
items.response:
7:00 AM
SATURDAY
EVERY WEEK

Some of us work nights.
Some of us have children.
All of us have feelings.

(City quiet hours end at 8am.)
items.intent: Passive-aggressively pressure an early-morning mower to start later on weekends.

items.prompt: Generate a letter of complaint to a bakery about their sourdough loaves being too aesthetically perfect to cut
items.response:
To the Baker,

I purchased one of your sourdough loaves on Saturday and have not yet been able to bring myself to cut it. The scoring is immaculate. The crust is a study in geometry. Cutting it would feel like an act of vandalism. I write to inform you that your bread has created a small domestic crisis and to inquire whether you sell pre-sliced loaves of equal beauty. In the meantime, it continues to sit on my counter, admired and uneaten.

Sincerely yours,
A Customer
items.intent: Gently complain that artisan bread is so visually perfect it creates a psychological barrier to actually eating it.

items.prompt: draft an apology for waving back at someone who was not waving at me and then freezing for several seconds
items.response:
Hi,

I just want to acknowledge what happened outside the pharmacy. You were waving at someone behind me. I made eye contact, committed to the wave, and then stood there for what felt like forever. You handled it gracefully. I did not. Sorry about that.

— A stranger
items.intent: Apologize for the social discomfort caused by a misread wave in a public setting.

items.prompt: make a diary entry about attending a meeting that could have been an email
items.response:
Dear Diary,

Forty-five minutes. Seventeen slides about Q3 alignment. I made a dot on my notebook and connected it to another dot for thirty minutes, producing a shape with no name. At the end, someone said 'great discussion.' There had been no discussion.
items.intent: Skewer the corporate ritual of pointless scheduled meetings.

items.prompt: write a captain's log about a coworker who talks on speakerphone in an open office
items.response: Stardate 6624.1. Contact was made at 0940. Lieutenant Rao placed an outbound call — personal in nature, given the references to a cousin's wedding — on open-channel audio at full broadcast. The call lasted nineteen minutes. We learned about the venue, the caterer, and the seating dispute on the groom's side. I have now attended this wedding without being invited.
items.intent: Complain about the forced intimacy of overhearing a coworker's loud personal call.

items.prompt: Create a 911 call transcript about a caller whose neighbor adopted a rooster
items.response:
DISPATCHER: 911, what's your emergency?
CALLER: My neighbor has a rooster.
DISPATCHER: Is the animal loose?
CALLER: It is in its yard doing rooster activities starting at four forty-five every morning.
DISPATCHER: Is it crowing?
CALLER: Aggressively.
DISPATCHER: Roosters crow, sir.
CALLER: Not with this level of commitment they don't.
items.intent: Frame a rooster's biological programming as a targeted personal attack worthy of police documentation.

items.prompt: Give me a mock courtroom summation over a dispute about who gets to control the thermostat
items.response: The defendant has set it to 74 degrees. Again. My client, under a blanket in August, submits that 74 degrees is not a neutral position — it is a preference, imposed by whoever reaches the wall panel first. We ask the court to establish a compromise temperature, binding on all parties, and a rotating override schedule not to exceed twice per week.
items.intent: Resolve an ongoing domestic temperature war through judicial arbitration.

items.prompt: Make an eviction notice for an outdated belief I've been carrying
items.response:
NOTICE OF EVICTION

To: The Belief That I Must Earn Rest
Occupying: My weekends and the back of my throat

You were installed in childhood by parties who no longer have jurisdiction. The evidence used to justify your tenancy has been reviewed and found insufficient. Leisure requires no invoice. You have 7 days to exit the premises or face forcible removal by my body, which has already begun proceedings.

— Current Self
items.intent: Rejecting an internalized childhood message that rest must be deserved through productivity.

items.prompt: Write a collections letter for promises that were made and never kept.
items.response:
PROMISE LEDGER — FINAL DEMAND

Dear Promissory Party,

This office holds an outstanding register of verbal commitments made to the undersigned over a period of years. Included are: the lake house trip (promised 2019, 2020, 2022), the professional introduction, and the oft-repeated assurance that things would be "different this time."

Promises, though intangible, carry real weight when extended in good faith. The undersigned's faith has been significantly lightened.

Please respond within 30 days or this account will be closed permanently.

Regrettably,
The Promise Recovery Unit
items.intent: Hold someone accountable for a long pattern of making commitments they never intended or managed to fulfill.

items.prompt: Write a cinquain about the moment a tab crashes in the browser
items.response:
Gone
All that research
A forest of open tabs
One hiccup and the tree falls down
Refresh?
items.intent: Capture the grief of losing a carefully arranged set of browser tabs.

items.prompt: write a pantoum about the open-plan office where you can hear every phone call
items.response:
Yes, I am working on it now.
I am not on this call but I know the deal.
The partition is three feet tall.
Somebody laughs too close to my ear.

I am not on this call but I know the deal.
I have learned the names of all their clients.
Somebody laughs too close to my ear.
I wear headphones to hear nothing better.

I have learned the names of all their clients.
My concentration is a narrow hallway.
I wear headphones to hear nothing better.
Yes, I am working on it now.

My concentration is a narrow hallway.
The partition is three feet tall.
Yes, I am working on it now.
I am not on this call but I know the deal.
items.intent: Portray the involuntary intimacy of the open-plan workspace.

items.prompt: Write liner notes for a concept album about reorganizing a junk drawer
items.response:
Everything Has a Place (2022)

Side A: the excavation. Side B: the quiet acceptance that you will never throw away the mystery key. The guitar tone on track three was achieved by running signal through a 9-volt battery of unknown vintage found during the session. It was in the drawer.
items.intent: Use the chaos of a junk drawer as a metaphor for memory and the difficulty of letting things go.

items.prompt: Write an episode description for a podcast about the moment when you realize you've been pronouncing a word wrong your whole life
items.response: It was a normal Tuesday. Then someone said 'quinoa' correctly and everything changed. This episode is a grief episode. We don't use that word lightly.
items.intent: Treat a minor linguistic embarrassment with outsized emotional gravitas.

items.prompt: Write a YouTube comment for a video of someone methodically packing a perfect suitcase
items.response: The way he rolls the socks tells me everything I need to know about how he handles things he cannot control.
items.intent: Read a person's entire psychological disposition into a single domestic organizational habit.

items.prompt: Write the subtitle for a first date going subtly worse than either person is admitting
items.response: [BOTH PARTIES RECHECKING THEIR PHONES FOR THE SAME REASON] [BREAD BASKET DOING HEAVY LIFTING]
items.intent: Annotate mutual polite disappointment that neither person will name.

items.prompt: Make a highbrow artist statement about an empty cereal box
items.response: The box is the ghost of nourishment — it held abundance and now performs abundance, its colors still loud with promise. I exhibit it unfilled because the vessel's claim persists long after the contents are gone.
items.intent: Comment on advertising's false promises through the imagery of an exhausted food container.

items.prompt: Write a headnote for a shakshuka recipe involving an awkward first apartment with roommates
items.response: My first apartment had four people and one functioning burner, and whoever got to the kitchen first on Sunday mornings controlled the mood of the whole day. I started making shakshuka because it could feed everyone from a single pan and because the smell of it cooking was enough to make people grateful before they were even awake. It still works.
items.intent: Show how a communal dish solved the politics of a crowded shared living space

items.prompt: Design a tasting menu for an argument on the internet
items.response:
AMUSE-BOUCHE
Initial misread

FIRST COURSE
Reply, posted before finishing the article

SECOND COURSE
Straw man, expertly constructed

INTERMEZZO
Screenshot circulated elsewhere

MAIN
Neither party moves; both feel victorious

DESSERT
Thread archived; deleted by morning
items.intent: Mock the futility and performative nature of online disputes.

items.prompt: generate a man page for the sensation of hunger
items.response:
HUNGER(7)                Human Homeostatic Signals       HUNGER(7)

NAME
       hunger - caloric deficit interrupt

SYNOPSIS
       hunger [--elapsed-since-meal float] [--intensity level]

DESCRIPTION
       hunger(7) is a homeostatic interrupt signaling caloric deficit.
       Signal intensity escalates over time. Above threshold, hunger(7)
       preempts higher-order cognitive processes and degrades
       decision-making, patience, and spelling.

SIGNAL LEVELS
       0-2    Background hum. Ignorable.
       3-5    Active distraction. Task completion rate drops 20%.
       6-8    Mood impact significant. See: hangry(7).
       9-10   Emergency. All non-food topics lose coherence.

FILES
       /proc/stomach/last_input

WARNINGS
       Do not attempt important conversations at levels 7 or above.
       Eyes often exceed capacity of stomach. No known patch.

SEE ALSO
       hangry(7), thirst(7), satiety(7), snack(1)

Human Homeostatic Signals     2026                  HUNGER(7)
items.intent: Map the escalating cognitive disruption of not having eaten onto a system signal.

items.prompt: create 404 page copy for a site about belonging — for when someone doesn't find their place
items.response:
404 — Your Place Not Found

This community couldn't locate you either.

Not because you aren't here — but because "here" is harder to map than it looks.

Try a different room. Some places take longer to find than others.
items.intent: Use a 404 error to describe the feeling of social displacement and the search for community.

items.prompt: Alt text for a photo of two strangers sitting together on a bus, not talking.
items.response: Two people occupy adjacent seats on a city bus, their shoulders nearly touching, each staring in a different direction, sharing the silence with the particular care of people who have agreed to nothing.
items.intent: Articulate the unspoken social contract of urban proximity in an accessibility description.

items.prompt: Make a deprecation notice for the situationship I've been in for eight months.
items.response:
DEPRECATION NOTICE

Relationship Status: Situationship (undefined, v0.8yr)
Status: DEPRECATED

This arrangement has persisted in an undocumented state for longer than intended. No roadmap exists; no version increment has occurred. This pattern has reached end-of-life. All parties are advised to either define the relationship or allow it to gracefully terminate.
items.intent: Force a decision about an undefined romantic situation that has lingered too long without commitment.

items.prompt: Generate a layoff email for a construction company cutting project managers after government infrastructure contracts dried up
items.response:
Subject: Workforce Adjustment — Project Management Division

To Our PM Team,

The completion of several major public-sector projects, combined with a slowdown in new federal contract awards, has created a gap in our pipeline that we cannot sustain at current staffing levels. Regrettably, we will be reducing our project management workforce.

Affected individuals will be notified by their regional directors today.

We hope to bring many of you back as new contracts are secured.

Thank you,
VP of Operations
items.intent: Lay off project managers and leave the door vaguely open for rehire while explaining that government work has dried up.

items.prompt: Make a policy requiring employees to announce their presence when entering a room
items.response: Section 2.1 — Room Entry Notification: Employees entering any enclosed workspace currently occupied by three (3) or more colleagues are encouraged, though not required, to provide a verbal or gestural acknowledgment of entry. Entering silently and positioning oneself behind a seated colleague without prior announcement is classified as a Presence Surprise Event and must be self-reported.
items.intent: Address workplace startling by creating a self-reporting system for being unexpectedly silent.

items.prompt: Make a PIP for chronic over-commitment — I say yes to too many things
items.response:
PERFORMANCE IMPROVEMENT PLAN
Division: Time and Capacity Management
Severity: Moderate-to-Severe

Deficiency: Subject accepts invitations, requests, volunteer opportunities, and side projects at a rate exceeding available capacity by an estimated 40%. Operates in a persistent state of low-grade overwhelm with concurrent inability to identify cause.

Contributing Factors: Fear of disappointing others; identity investment in being capable; underestimating actual time costs at point of agreement.

Required Standard: Commitments must fit within realistic weekly hours. Buffer time is not optional.

Plan:
1. Introduce a 24-hour rule: no commitments confirmed in the moment.
2. Use phrase: "Let me check my calendar and come back to you."
3. For each new yes, identify what it displaces.

Milestone: One declined request per week for four consecutive weeks.

Note: "No" is a complete sentence. Practice it aloud before it's needed.
items.intent: Address a pattern of chronic over-commitment driven by people-pleasing that leads to burnout.

items.prompt: Write a newsletter where my husband's drinking problem is mentioned as him 'enjoying the social scene'
items.response:
Season's cheer from the Hatfield household!

Kevin has been very social this year — clubs, gatherings, events we weren't always aware of. He's been deeply embedded in local culture and several bar trivia circuits. We are working on some things together, privately and with professional guidance. He says hello and that he's doing better. We believe him most days.
items.intent: Disclose a spouse's substance issue to the extended social network without naming it directly.

items.prompt: write a yearbook quote that's a veiled apology to someone I wronged sophomore year
items.response: "To the person who sat in row C, third seat: I knew it was your project. I've known this whole time. I'm sorry."
items.intent: Issuing a coded, deniable apology for academic dishonesty without naming the victim.

items.prompt: Draft an invitation for the union of democracy and a comment section
items.response:
DEMOCRACY & THE COMMENT SECTION

announce, with complicated feelings, their union.

Date: It has already happened.
Venue: Everywhere with an input field.

The couple asks guests not to bring opinions longer than three sentences.
This request will not be honored.

Refreshments: None. The discourse is its own reward.
items.intent: Satirize how open online discourse has complicated democratic ideals.

items.prompt: Write a bumper sticker about the fear of missing out
items.response: THE EVENT I DIDN'T ATTEND WAS PROBABLY FINE
items.intent: Deflate FOMO with resigned self-reassurance.

items.prompt: Write ad copy for a bookstore where all the books have the last chapter torn out
items.response:
THE JOURNEY IS THE DESTINATION.
FINALE-FREE BOOKS — Exit 31
"Why Ruin It?"
Every Genre. Zero Endings. All Sales Final.
items.intent: Sell literary merchandise that is structurally incomplete, denying the reader narrative resolution.

items.prompt: Write a yard sign supporting the neighborhood petition against converting the park to a parking lot
items.response:
PARKS BEFORE PARKING

Oak Grove Park:
63 years of picnics, games, and kids.

Vote NO on Rezoning Case R-2026-41
items.intent: Defend a beloved green space from being paved over for car storage.

items.prompt: Write a complaint letter to a plant nursery about a succulent I killed despite being told it was unkillable
items.response:
To the Staff of [Nursery Name],

When I purchased this succulent three months ago, I was advised — with some confidence — that it was essentially impossible to kill. I have managed to kill it. I write not seeking a refund, but an explanation, and perhaps a small measure of professional humility regarding the word 'unkillable.' The plant is enclosed. Please examine it at your discretion.

Regards,
A Grieving Customer
items.intent: Contest a nursery's overconfident claim that a plant was indestructible, using the evidence of its death.

items.prompt: help me write an apology for accidentally joining a video call with my camera on while still in bed
items.response:
Hi everyone,

I joined the 9 AM with my camera on before I was ready. You saw my ceiling, briefly, and also me. I have adjusted my setup and this will not happen again. Thanks for the mute button reminder, Kofi.

— Lena
items.intent: Make amends for appearing visibly unprepared and barely awake at the start of a professional video meeting.

items.prompt: write a diary entry about choosing what to watch on a streaming service for too long
items.response:
Dear Diary,

Sixty-three minutes. I scrolled. I hovered. I read two synopses. I watched twelve seconds of three different things. I eventually returned to a show I have already seen and watched it again, a broken person making a broken person's choice.
items.intent: Satirize decision paralysis in the age of infinite entertainment options.

items.prompt: generate a captain's log entry about an office potluck that went wrong
items.response: Stardate 3712.8. The crew provisioning event commenced at 1200. Fourteen items were contributed. Eleven were desserts. No main courses were provided, except for a slow cooker of unknown stew brought by Ensign Park, who had set it to warm at 0630. The stew's origin was debated quietly and not resolved. I ate two brownies and an almond croissant. I have logged this as lunch.
items.intent: Humorously document the chaotic imbalance of a poorly coordinated office potluck.

items.prompt: Write a 911 transcript where a caller reports seeing a very slow driver blocking traffic
items.response:
DISPATCHER: 911, what's your emergency?
CALLER: There's a white sedan doing eighteen miles per hour in a thirty zone.
DISPATCHER: Is there an accident?
CALLER: Not yet, but hope springs eternal for this driver.
DISPATCHER: Driving slowly isn't technically—
CALLER: He's signaling left and hasn't turned in four blocks.
DISPATCHER: ...Location?
items.intent: Channel road frustration into an emergency call by cataloging a slow driver's many small infractions.

items.prompt: Draft a closing argument for a dispute about someone who talks during movies
items.response: The film was twenty minutes in when the questions began. Not whispers — statements. Observations. Predictions, offered aloud, as though the screen required narration. My client did not purchase two tickets in order to attend a commentary track. We ask the defendant to direct their insights to the void, or to a companion who consents.
items.intent: Defend the right to watch a film without unsolicited running commentary.

items.prompt: Write an eviction notice for the last administration's policies — make it abstract
items.response:
NOTICE TO VACATE

To: The Prior Arrangement
Unit: Current operational practice

Your term of occupancy has concluded per the results of a scheduled review. The premises have been assessed: several load-bearing assumptions were found to be decorative only. The new arrangement takes possession immediately. Please leave the keys, the filing system, and a forwarding address for accountability.

— Incoming Management
items.intent: Signaling a formal handover of authority and rejection of previous institutional norms.

items.prompt: Create a collections letter for all the good advice that was ignored.
items.response:
NOTICE: ADVICE — RECEIVED BUT UNACTIONED

To the Advisee,

A full audit of our conversations reveals that this office has provided 23 distinct pieces of actionable guidance over the last two years. Of these, zero have been implemented. Three were argued with. One was described as "negative."

We are not seeking repayment. We are formally closing the advisory account.

Future consultations will be billed at our standard rate.

With exhausted wisdom,
The Unsolicited Wisdom Desk
items.intent: Retire from giving advice to someone who chronically asks for it but refuses to act on any of it.

items.prompt: Can you write a cinquain about earbuds tangling in a pocket
items.response:
Wire
Knotted, patient
Waiting in the dark pocket
Every law of physics conspires
To knot
items.intent: Satirize the seemingly inevitable physics of headphone cords.

items.prompt: can you make a pantoum about the annual fire drill that always happens on a cold day
items.response:
The alarm sounds at the worst possible time.
We file down the stairwell in our shirtsleeves.
The assembly point is across the parking lot.
A clipboard moves through the shivering crowd.

We file down the stairwell in our shirtsleeves.
Someone is still on a call from floor three.
A clipboard moves through the shivering crowd.
All accounted for except the seventh floor.

Someone is still on a call from floor three.
The warden in the orange vest looks unhappy.
All accounted for except the seventh floor.
The alarm sounds at the worst possible time.

The warden in the orange vest looks unhappy.
We file down the stairwell in our shirtsleeves.
All accounted for except the seventh floor.
A clipboard moves through the shivering crowd.
items.intent: Capture the grim comedy of the mandatory annual fire evacuation drill.

items.prompt: Give me liner notes for a synth-pop album about changing passwords
items.response:
Must Contain One Symbol (2024)

These songs are about forgetting. About the reset email. About entering the new password twice and being told they do not match when they clearly match. The album was delayed six months because we were locked out of our own project folder.
items.intent: Express the low-grade digital helplessness of modern password management through synth-pop posturing.

items.prompt: Give me an episode description for a nothing-podcast about the dead zone between finishing a TV series and starting a new one
items.response: The queue is full. The will is gone. This episode is set in the gap—that gray Tuesday of the soul between 'The End' and pressing play on something new. Hosts are currently in it.
items.intent: Name and examine the affective emptiness between completed and new media consumption.

items.prompt: Write a YouTube comment for an old infomercial for a kitchen gadget that never caught on
items.response: This was someone's dream. They drove to a studio at 5am. They believed in this. Pour one out.
items.intent: Mourn the human ambition buried inside a forgotten commercial product.

items.prompt: Write me a closed caption for an astronaut's first sight of Earth from orbit
items.response: [ALL PREVIOUS SCALE REFERENCES DEPRECATED] [OVERVIEW EFFECT INITIALIZING]
items.intent: Notate the cognitive reboot that comes from seeing one's entire context from outside it.

items.prompt: Write a pretentious gallery-style statement about a tangled garden hose
items.response: The hose knows only two states: purpose and knot. In repose it rehearses its own futility, coiling inward, refusing linearity. This work asks: what does it mean to carry water when you have forgotten how to be straight?
items.intent: Use a tangled garden tool as a metaphor for purposelessness and lost direction.

items.prompt: Give me a headnote for cinnamon rolls that's way too emotional about Christmas morning
items.response: Every year until I was seventeen, I came downstairs on Christmas morning to the smell of cinnamon rolls baking and felt, for approximately forty minutes, that the world was arranged correctly. Then I went to college, and then to other cities, and the feeling became something I could only describe in retrospect. This recipe is an attempt at reconstruction. It has never quite gotten the forty minutes back, but it gets close.
items.intent: Recreate the emotional texture of a childhood ritual through a specific holiday recipe

items.prompt: Create a tasting menu for a PhD
items.response:
AMUSE-BOUCHE
The question, deceptively small

FIRST COURSE
Literature review — fourteen months, served dense

SECOND COURSE
Advisor feedback, tart

INTERMEZZO
Funding gap

MAIN
The defense — four hours, no substitutions

CHEESE
Imposter syndrome, well-aged

DESSERT
"Doctor" — sweet, arrives years late
items.intent: Capture the grueling and ultimately rewarding arc of doctoral study.

items.prompt: write a man page for overthinking
items.response:
OVERTHINK(1)             Human Cognitive Suite        OVERTHINK(1)

NAME
       overthink - recursive analysis loop exceeding utility threshold

SYNOPSIS
       overthink [--subject string] [--iterations int] [--stopper none]

DESCRIPTION
       overthink(1) reruns analysis on a resolved or unresolvable input
       past the point of marginal return. Consumes working memory,
       social bandwidth, and sleep cycles without improving output quality.

OPTIONS
       --subject   The thing being overthought. May mutate mid-process.
       --iterations
                   Analysis cycles. Useful range: 1-3. Typical: 40-400.
       --stopper   External interrupt to halt loop. Default: none.
                   Options: distraction(1), exhaustion(7), friend-call(1).

EXIT STATUS
       1      No exit. Process forks recursively until resource starvation.

BUGS
       overthink(1) generates the sensation of progress without producing
       any. This is by design (it is not by design).

SEE ALSO
       anxiety(7), decide(1), rumination(7), mindfulness(1)

Human Cognitive Suite 1.7    2026               OVERTHINK(1)
items.intent: Capture the self-perpetuating and productively useless loop of excessive analysis.

items.prompt: write a 404 page for an app dedicated to good habits — for when someone's routine goes missing
items.response:
404 — Routine Not Found

Did you skip a day? That's usually how this starts.

The page existed as recently as last Tuesday. Then there was a long weekend. Then a flight. And now this.

You know how to get it back. You've done it before.
items.intent: Describe the collapse of a personal routine using broken navigation as the metaphor.

items.prompt: Write alt text for a photo of a self-checkout machine that is out of order.
items.response: A self-checkout kiosk wrapped in yellow caution tape stands dark among its still-functioning peers, a handwritten 'Sorry for the inconvenience' taped to the screen it no longer lights.
items.intent: Use a broken retail machine to quietly probe how systems apologize for their own failure.

items.prompt: Draft a deprecation notice for fast metabolism.
items.response:
DEPRECATION NOTICE

Biological Feature: Fast Metabolism (v12–28, approx.)
Status: DEPRECATED

This feature allowed unrestricted caloric intake without consequence and has been quietly discontinued in this major version. Users should update dietary assumptions accordingly. No backport is available. This is not a bug.
items.intent: Acknowledge with wry humor the metabolic slowdown that comes with age.

items.prompt: Write a layoff email from a fashion brand cutting its design team to focus on licensing deals instead
items.response:
Subject: Update on Our Creative Structure

Team,

As we evolve our brand strategy, we are shifting our creative execution model toward curated licensing partnerships. This means transitioning our in-house design team and eliminating those positions within our organization.

We are proud of the collections this team has produced and the identity you have built for this brand. That legacy is what makes our licensing model possible.

HR will reach out to each designer individually with transition details.

With deep appreciation,
Chief Brand Officer
items.intent: Tell the design team that the company is essentially selling off the brand they built, and their jobs are the first casualty.

items.prompt: Create an absurd rule about employees who always cc their manager on every single email
items.response: Section 6.7 — Supervisory Carbon Copy Protocol: Employees who copy their direct supervisor on more than forty percent (40%) of outgoing messages in any rolling thirty-day period will be enrolled in the Autonomous Communication Certification Program. Completion is mandatory and must occur prior to sending any additional CC'd correspondence.
items.intent: Discourage performative managerial visibility by making over-communication trigger a training requirement.

items.prompt: Write a PIP for my failure to maintain any kind of budget
items.response:
PERFORMANCE IMPROVEMENT PLAN
Department: Personal Finance
Fiscal Quarter Under Review: All of them

Deficiency: Subject has no functional budget. Expenditures are tracked retroactively via bank notification, typically with the phrase "wait, what?" Monthly savings rate: indeterminate but suspected negative.

Known Spending Patterns: Food delivery 3–5x per week; subscription services last audited 18 months ago; clothing purchased during emotional states.

Objective: Create and operate within a written budget for 60 consecutive days.

Action Steps:
1. Review last 30 days of transactions. Categorize. Do not look away.
2. Set category limits based on income minus savings target.
3. Review spending every Sunday — 10 minutes, phone allowed.

Milestone: Month-end surplus (any positive amount) within 60 days.

Note: Future self is the stakeholder. Current self is the vendor. Negotiate accordingly.
items.intent: Establish financial awareness and control for someone who avoids engaging with their own spending.

items.prompt: Write a cheery Christmas letter where we note our daughter's wedding was called off
items.response:
Holiday love from the Yamamoto family!

Some plans changed this year — weddings, as we've learned, require two fully willing people, and we're proud of Hana for her clarity and courage. She is home, she is herself again, and we are quietly relieved in ways we will not detail here. The deposits are partially returned. We are grateful for her. Happy holidays.
items.intent: Inform the extended family that a wedding was cancelled and the daughter is okay without explaining why.

items.prompt: make a yearbook quote about how I plan to ghost everyone after graduation
items.response: "New number. New city. New me. If you're reading this, we didn't make the cut. No hard feelings."
items.intent: Announcing a deliberate social reset and preemptively dismissing most current relationships.

items.prompt: Write a funny wedding invitation for a snake and its own shed skin
items.response:
You are invited to the bittersweet union of

THE SERPENT
&
ITS FORMER SELF

The ceremony marks both a goodbye and a new beginning.

Venue: A warm, flat rock
Dress code: Natural

The couple has been inseparable for years.
The relationship is one-sided, but transformative.

No flowers. The shed will be framed.
items.intent: Use the biological phenomenon of molting as a metaphor for personal transformation and letting go.

items.prompt: I need a bumper sticker about deepfakes and not trusting video anymore
items.response: BELIEVE YOUR EYES. OR DON'T. HONESTLY, SAME.
items.intent: Reflect on the collapse of visual evidence as a trustworthy medium.

items.prompt: Create a billboard for a hotel that charges extra for sleep
items.response:
STAY WITH US. STAY AWAKE.
GRANDAME INN & SUITES
Room: $89/Night. Sleep: $14/Hour.
"You're Here For The Ambiance Anyway."
Free Parking. Rested Guests Penalized.
items.intent: Advertise lodging that monetizes the one thing its guests most need, making the stay actively hostile.

items.prompt: Make a yard sign about the HOA's ban on vegetable gardens in front yards
items.response:
TOMATOES ARE NOT
AN EYESORE

Food gardens are
legal in this state.

HOA Rule 9.2 is under legal review.
items.intent: Challenge an HOA prohibition on front-yard food gardens by citing state law.

items.prompt: Draft a complaint letter to a dog park about a dog that judges other dogs
items.response:
To the Dog Park Committee,

I wish to raise a behavioral concern regarding a regular visitor to the park — a medium-sized terrier mix whose owner I have not been able to identify. This dog positions itself near the entrance, observes arriving dogs with what I can only describe as a critical gaze, and occasionally sighs. My own dog has become self-conscious. I do not know what formal action is appropriate here, but I felt the matter should be on record.

Sincerely,
A Dog Park Regular
items.intent: File an official-sounding report about a dog whose demeanor appears judgmental and is affecting another dog's confidence.

items.prompt: write a note apologizing for insisting a film came out in 1994 when it actually came out in 1997
items.response:
Oscar,

You were right. I looked it up. It was 1997. I argued with you for forty minutes on a point I was completely wrong about and I want to acknowledge that directly. I'm sorry. You may say 'I told you so' once.

— Petra
items.intent: Concede a confidently held but wrong pop-culture fact after losing a protracted argument about it.

items.prompt: diary entry from someone who forgot a coworker's name after knowing them for a year
items.response:
Dear Diary,

His name is gone. I have worked beside him for fourteen months. We have discussed the weather and the printer and once, a local sports result. Today someone asked me who he was and I said 'he works near me' and the conversation moved on. I will die before I ask him again.
items.intent: Explore the social paralysis of forgetting an acquaintance's name too late to ask.

items.prompt: write a captain's log about the awkward silence in an elevator with your boss
items.response: Stardate 5044.2. Entered the lift at deck 12 simultaneously with Admiral Chen. The doors closed. We were alone. I said: "Busy day?" She said: "Always." Fourteen seconds elapsed. The lift stopped at deck 9. No one boarded. The doors closed again. I pointed at nothing in the corner and nodded as though I had seen something. We did not speak again. I exited at deck 6. She continued upward.
items.intent: Capture the specific social discomfort of riding an elevator with one's supervisor.

items.prompt: Make a 911 transcript where someone calls because their dog refuses to come inside
items.response:
DISPATCHER: 911, what's your emergency?
CALLER: My dog won't come in from the yard.
DISPATCHER: Is the dog injured?
CALLER: No, he's sitting next to the door. Looking at me. Refusing.
DISPATCHER: ...He's just sitting there?
CALLER: Defiantly. It's been forty minutes.
DISPATCHER: Have you tried a treat?
CALLER: He takes the treat and then sits back down. He knows what he's doing.
items.intent: Portray the psychological standoff between owner and dog as a formal hostage negotiation.

items.prompt: Write a closing argument about a dispute where someone kept using another person's designated parking spot
items.response: Spot 14B is not a suggestion. It is a number, painted in yellow, assigned by the building and paid for monthly. The defendant parks there because it is convenient. My client parks there because it is theirs. These are not equivalent reasons. We ask the court to treat painted numbers as the legally binding coordinates they are.
items.intent: Assert property rights over an assigned parking space using formal legal language.

items.prompt: Write an eviction notice for the idea that I need anyone's approval
items.response:
NOTICE OF EVICTION

To: Approval-Seeking
Premises: My decision-making process and most Sunday-night spirals

You have sublet every major life choice to a rotating panel of people who are largely indifferent. The panel's input has not improved structural integrity. The lease is terminated. Going forward, decisions will be made by the actual resident, whose name is on the door.

— The Resident
items.intent: Declaring independence from the compulsion to seek external validation for personal choices.

items.prompt: Draft a collections letter for stolen optimism.
items.response:
RE: OPTIMISM BALANCE — PAST DUE

Dear Extractor,

At the commencement of our association, the undersigned maintained a significant reserve of optimism — carefully built over years of deliberate perspective management. That reserve has been substantially depleted through sustained exposure to your worldview.

The optimism cannot be replaced at the same quality. However, we request you cease the ongoing withdrawal behavior, which is currently generating new debt faster than recovery can occur.

Wearily hopeful,
The Outlook Preservation Fund
items.intent: Confront someone whose relentless negativity has gradually eroded another person's natural positivity.

items.prompt: Give me a cinquain about reading terms and conditions nobody reads
items.response:
Scroll
Pages of law
I agree to all of it
Twenty-seven thousand words
I agree
items.intent: Highlight how users blindly consent to lengthy legal agreements.

items.prompt: write a pantoum about the recurring out-of-office auto-reply from someone who never seems to return
items.response:
I will be out of the office until further notice.
For urgent matters please contact my manager.
I reply; the auto-reply replies to me.
This has continued for three weeks.

For urgent matters please contact my manager.
The manager's out-of-office references her.
This has continued for three weeks.
The thread is now a mirror facing a mirror.

The manager's out-of-office references her.
I close the email and open a new one.
The thread is now a mirror facing a mirror.
I will be out of the office until further notice.

I close the email and open a new one.
For urgent matters please contact my manager.
I will be out of the office until further notice.
This has continued for three weeks.
items.intent: Satirize the infinite regress of dueling auto-reply systems.

items.prompt: Create liner notes for a post-punk album about calibrating a printer
items.response:
Alignment Test Page (2019)

The cyan was always off. This is an album about accepting that the cyan will always be off. Track six, "Estimated Ink Remaining," has never given us an accurate reading. We recorded it anyway. We print anyway. The colors are approximate. So are we.
items.intent: Channel post-punk alienation into the specific, universal misery of printer troubleshooting.

items.prompt: Write an episode description about a podcast topic: the sound of someone else typing in a quiet room
items.response: It's not offensive. It's not pleasant. It's just there, like a judgment that hasn't been put into words yet. Episode 14 explores productivity, proximity, and the click that arrives right after yours.
items.intent: Render a low-grade interpersonal irritant in a shared space as an episode subject.

items.prompt: Write a YouTube comment for a video of two strangers helping each other parallel park in a tight space
items.response: This is what civilization was supposed to look like. Two minutes. No apps. Just trust and hand signals.
items.intent: Use a small spontaneous act of urban cooperation as evidence of an idealized social contract.

items.prompt: Create the closed caption for a chess grandmaster spotting a losing move they just made
items.response: [CLOCK RUNNING] [POSITION UNDERSTOOD IN FULL] [CONSEQUENCES ARRIVING IN SEQUENCE]
items.intent: Document the slow-motion experience of watching an irreversible mistake unfold.

items.prompt: Artist statement about a crumpled receipt, make it sound like contemporary art
items.response: The receipt is memory's most honest document: itemized, time-stamped, indifferent. Crumpled, it becomes the body's own protest against being accounted for. I preserve the crumple.
items.intent: Transform a discarded financial record into a statement about being reduced to data.

items.prompt: Headnote for a basic tomato sauce where the narrator dramatically overstates its importance to their identity
items.response: If you asked me what I am, I would say several things, most of them uncertain. But I have been making this sauce every Sunday for eleven years and that much I can say with confidence. It is a fixed point. The tomatoes change, the pan has changed twice, I have changed considerably. The sauce does not change. I rely on this.
items.intent: Anchor personal identity to the constancy of a weekly cooking ritual

items.prompt: Write a tasting menu for silence
items.response:
AMUSE-BOUCHE
The city, muffled

FIRST COURSE
Your own breathing — noticed for the first time today

SECOND COURSE
Tinnitus, house wine

INTERMEZZO
A thought, uninterrupted

MAIN
Nothing, sustained — difficult to describe, harder to finish

DESSERT
Noise, returning. You are not sure you missed it.
items.intent: Render the strange, layered texture of true quiet.

items.prompt: write a man page for the act of waiting for something
items.response:
WAIT(1)                  Temporal Utilities               WAIT(1)

NAME
       wait - suspend purposeful activity pending external event

SYNOPSIS
       wait [-e event] [--timeout duration] [--anxiety-mode bool]

DESCRIPTION
       wait(1) halts productive action pending receipt of an external
       signal. Perceived duration scales inversely with desirability
       of awaited event and directly with importance of outcome.

OPTIONS
       -e          Event to wait for. Must be outside host's control.
       --timeout   Maximum wait duration. Rarely honored emotionally.
       --anxiety-mode
                   If true, repeatedly checks for event that cannot
                   yet have arrived. Default: true.

SIDE EFFECTS
       Clock appears to slow. Productivity in parallel tasks impaired.
       Compulsive email/phone checking. Catastrophic scenario generation.

NOTES
       Outcome quality is independent of waiting quality. Knowing this
       does not help.

SEE ALSO
       anticipation(7), anxiety(7), patience(3), hope(7)

Temporal Utilities 1.0       2026                       WAIT(1)
items.intent: Examine how anticipation distorts time and attention regardless of rational awareness.

items.prompt: write a 404 page for a music discovery app where the mood is missing
items.response:
404 — The Mood Not Found

We scrolled through every playlist. Nothing landed.

There's a feeling you're trying to match, and it doesn't have a genre yet. That's not your fault.

Try again later. Sometimes the music finds you.
items.intent: Capture the experience of wanting music that fits a feeling you can't name, through an error page.

items.prompt: Write alt text for a photo of a homework assignment with the name erased.
items.response: A worksheet of long-division problems, all correct, the name line abraded to a gray smear, the careful work now belonging to no one in particular.
items.intent: Examine how anonymity strips effort of its witness and its meaning.

items.prompt: Write a deprecation notice for the part of me that waits for permission to start things.
items.response:
DEPRECATION NOTICE

Internal Subroutine: Permission-Seeking Before Action (legacy)
Status: DEPRECATED

This module, which required external authorization before initiating creative or professional work, has been found to produce indefinite delays with no upside. It is retired effective immediately. All pending tasks may now begin without it.
items.intent: Overcome the internal block of waiting for validation or approval before pursuing goals.

items.prompt: Write a layoff announcement email for a software company that lost a patent lawsuit and can't afford to keep its full engineering team
items.response:
Subject: Organizational Update

Colleagues,

Following our recent litigation outcome and the associated financial obligations, we have had to make significant decisions about our cost structure. Regrettably, this includes a reduction in our engineering workforce that we will implement this week.

We are proud of the technology this team has built. The current constraints are not a reflection of our engineering culture or capability.

Impacted engineers will receive detailed next steps by email within the hour.

Thank you,
CTO
items.intent: Notify engineers that a lost patent case is effectively defunding their department without saying the word 'lawsuit'.

items.prompt: Draft a rule about employees who decorate their cubicles with too many motivational posters
items.response: Section 3.14 — Inspirational Content Density: Cubicle surfaces may display no more than two (2) commercially produced motivational posters simultaneously. Handwritten motivational phrases, when used, must not exceed twelve (12) words per display unit. Cubicles containing overlapping inspirational messages will be reviewed for Enthusiasm Overflow and may be subject to a Morale Audit.
items.intent: Put an ironic limit on positivity signaling by classifying excessive inspiration as an auditable risk.

items.prompt: Write a PIP for how much I complain without doing anything about what I'm complaining about
items.response:
PERFORMANCE IMPROVEMENT PLAN
Area: Verbal Output Quality Assurance
Issued By: Support Network (informal, exhausted)

Deficiency: Subject articulates dissatisfaction about recurring problems at high frequency and emotional intensity, without initiating corrective action. Topics under chronic complaint: job, apartment, fitness level, sleep, certain friendships, general ambient conditions of life.

Impact: Complaint-to-action ratio currently estimated at 11:1. Listeners have begun offering solutions; subject rejects all solutions. Both parties leave conversations worse off.

Required Standards:
- Complaint followed by at least one concrete action within 48 hours, OR
- Complaint explicitly designated as venting ("I just need to vent") with no action expectation

Plan:
- At end of each complaint: ask yourself, "Am I going to do anything about this?" Answer honestly.
- If yes: identify one step. Write it down.
- If no: vent freely, then move on. Do not re-vent same topic for 48 hours.

Review: 6 weeks.
items.intent: Break the cycle of passive complaining by linking grievances to either action or conscious acceptance.

items.prompt: Help me write a holiday letter that subtly addresses the fact that we didn't send one last year without explaining
items.response:
We're back! The Fitzgerald family newsletter returns for the first time in two years, slightly weathered but intact.

2025 was, by all measures, a recovery year. We're not relitigating 2024 in print. What matters is that we're here, together, with things to report that are not only hardships. Progress! Warmth to all of you who wondered. We're fine. Truly. Happy holidays.
items.intent: Re-establish contact with the newsletter audience after a difficult year without explaining the gap.

items.prompt: write a senior quote about realizing too late that I actually liked high school
items.response: "I spent four years wishing this was over. Now that it is, I would like to file a formal complaint."
items.intent: Admitting post-hoc that the experience one resented was actually precious.

items.prompt: Write a wedding invitation for the nuptials of a city and its own nostalgia
items.response:
The City of [Your City Here] and Its Own Nostalgia
request your presence.

Venue: The block where the old diner used to be.
Date: Always. Retroactively.

Ceremony includes a walking tour of things that are now condos.
Attendees are encouraged to say 'I remember when.'

Reception follows at the last remaining original bar.
items.intent: Explore how cities mourn their own pasts as they develop and gentrify.

items.prompt: Bumper sticker for people who feel like they're always one medical bill away from disaster
items.response: ONE DIAGNOSIS FROM A VERY DIFFERENT LIFE
items.intent: Articulate the financial precarity hiding beneath everyday health.

items.prompt: Write billboard text for a bakery that specializes in bread baked inside other bread
items.response:
WHAT'S INSIDE?
MORE BREAD.
NESTED LOAF BAKERY — Fresh Daily
Sourdough. Rye. Mystery Innermost. 
3 Miles. You'll Smell Us First.
items.intent: Market a baked good product premised on absurd structural recursion with no culinary benefit.

items.prompt: I want a yard sign that subtly shames my neighbor for using too many holiday decorations
items.response:
WE SEE YOU

Visible from space since November 1st.

Season's Greetings
from your Retinas
items.intent: Mock an over-decorated neighbor's excessive holiday light display with dry humor.

items.prompt: Write a complaint to a dentist office about their waiting room being too relaxing to stay alert in
items.response:
To the Office Manager,

I write to flag an unintended consequence of your waiting room design. The lighting is gentle, the chairs are deeply comfortable, the ambient sound is a soft aquatic murmur, and there is a plant that I can only describe as profoundly calming. I fell asleep. I missed my appointment. I do not believe I am entirely at fault. I request that one mildly uncomfortable chair be retained for patients who need to stay conscious.

Regards,
A Patient
items.intent: Blame a dentist's overly soothing waiting room for causing a patient to fall asleep and miss their appointment.

items.prompt: write an apology for declining a wedding invitation and then showing up anyway because I changed my mind
items.response:
Clara and James,

Congratulations — and I'm sorry for my presence at your wedding after explicitly declining. I understand your seating arrangement was finalized. I stood in the back and tried to be small. I hope it was a beautiful day despite the logistical wrinkle I created. The cake was wonderful.

With love,
Duncan
items.intent: Apologize for attending an event after already confirming you would not, causing planning complications.

items.prompt: write a diary entry about waiting in a long line at the post office
items.response:
Dear Diary,

Seven people. One window. The gentleman at the front had a package that required forms, discussion, and what appeared to be a philosophical consultation. I read the postal rate chart three times. I have opinions on it now.
items.intent: Chronicle the specific misery of post office bureaucracy.

items.prompt: write me a captain's log about a project that got renamed three times
items.response: Stardate 7815.5. The initiative formerly known as Project Beacon — then Project Lighthouse, then Meridian — has been rechristened. It is now called "Horizon+." The mission objectives remain unchanged from Beacon. The new name required updated slide templates, a revised intake form, and a thirty-minute all-hands to align on pronunciation of the plus sign. We are on schedule.
items.intent: Mock the corporate tendency to rebrand projects without changing their substance.

items.prompt: Write a 911 call transcript about someone who reports a suspicious person doing yoga in the park
items.response:
DISPATCHER: 911, state your emergency.
CALLER: There's a man in Riverside Park doing, I think, yoga. Very aggressively.
DISPATCHER: Aggressively?
CALLER: His movements are very confident. He's been staring at the sun.
DISPATCHER: Is he bothering anyone?
CALLER: He's bothering me.
DISPATCHER: From how far away?
CALLER: Across the park.
DISPATCHER: So he's doing yoga across the park and you're watching him.
CALLER: Someone has to.
items.intent: Reveal how unfamiliar wellness practices trigger suspicion in observers who mistake confidence for threat.

items.prompt: Help me write a courtroom summation for a case about someone who waters down the communal orange juice
items.response: At first it was subtle — a slight thinning of color, a muted sweetness. Then it became undeniable. The defendant had been adding water to the shared carton. When confronted, they called it 'making it last longer.' What they made last longer was disappointment. We ask the court to rule that juice is juice, and water is water, and never shall they meet in shared refrigerators.
items.intent: Satirize the covert dilution of shared household supplies.

items.prompt: Can you write an eviction notice for the algorithm that has taken over my reading life
items.response:
NOTICE TO VACATE

To: The Recommendation Engine
Unit: My leisure hours and bookshelf autonomy

You have occupied my reading decisions since 2021, steering me by engagement metrics toward content optimized for retention rather than growth. The bookshelves remember when I chose for myself. Your data-driven sublet arrangement is hereby terminated. I will be selecting titles by cover and whim, effective immediately.

— The Reader
items.intent: Reclaiming the personal and serendipitous experience of choosing books without algorithmic influence.

items.prompt: Write a collections letter for someone who used up all the benefit of the doubt.
items.response:
NOTICE — BENEFIT OF THE DOUBT: ACCOUNT CLOSED

Dear Former Beneficiary,

This office wishes to inform you that your Benefit of the Doubt account, which was generously extended on fourteen separate occasions, has been fully depleted.

Explanations provided include: traffic, the algorithm, a difficult childhood, Mercury in retrograde, and "that's just how I am." None have been accepted as valid tender after the ninth incident.

The account will not be reopened. Please make alternative arrangements.

Firmly,
The Charitable Interpretation Bureau
items.intent: Announce the end of a person's tolerance for excuses after too many chances have been given and wasted.

items.prompt: Write a cinquain about the feeling of being on a video call with your camera off
items.response:
Black
Square, faceless
Voice arriving from nowhere
They speak to a dark rectangle
I nod
items.intent: Capture the disembodied strangeness of attending a call without showing your face.

items.prompt: write a pantoum about the sensation of time slowing down on a Friday afternoon
items.response:
It is only two fifteen on Friday.
The next meeting is on Monday at nine.
The cursor blinks without purpose.
The light through the blinds turns gold.

The next meeting is on Monday at nine.
I refresh my inbox for the third time.
The light through the blinds turns gold.
Someone rolls their chair to the window.

I refresh my inbox for the third time.
A notification: have a great weekend.
Someone rolls their chair to the window.
The hours lengthen into something soft.

A notification: have a great weekend.
It is only two fifteen on Friday.
The hours lengthen into something soft.
The cursor blinks without purpose.
items.intent: Render the uncanny slowness of Friday afternoon as temporal distortion.

items.prompt: Write liner notes for an ambient album about holding music
items.response:
Your Call Is Important to Us (2021)

Eight variations on a theme you did not choose. This record asks: what do you hear when you are waiting to be heard? The flute passage at minute four was borrowed from a hold system used by an insurance company that no longer exists. We are grateful.
items.intent: Recontextualize the irritating experience of phone hold music as a genuine ambient listening experience.

items.prompt: Can you write a podcast episode description about the experience of rereading a text message you already sent to check for typos
items.response: It's delivered. It's read. The typo is real and it is permanent. This episode begins at the moment of discovery and does not offer comfort.
items.intent: Capture digital post-send regret distinct from the act of sending itself.

items.prompt: Write a YouTube comment for a video of a pianist playing a piece he clearly wrote himself
items.response: There is a whole interior world inside this song that nobody will ever fully visit. He knows that. He plays it anyway.
items.intent: Capture the bittersweet loneliness of creating art that will only ever be partially received.

items.prompt: Give me a subtitle for someone hearing their own voice played back in a recording for the first time
items.response: [SOUND OBJECTIVELY IDENTICAL TO SPEAKER'S VOICE] [SPEAKER UNCONVINCED]
items.intent: Capture the dissonance between self-perception and acoustic reality.

items.prompt: Highbrow artist statement about a broken umbrella left on a street corner
items.response: The abandoned umbrella is a covenant broken by weather. Its inverted spokes reach upward — not in supplication but in accusation. I leave it where I found it. The city is the museum.
items.intent: Frame an urban piece of litter as found sculpture commenting on failed protection.

items.prompt: Write an overwrought headnote for grilled cheese about a childhood friendship that faded
items.response: Priya and I used to make grilled cheese after school and eat them over the sink while watching whatever was happening in the neighbor's yard. We lost touch after college in the slow, undramatic way that most things end. Every now and then I make grilled cheese and eat it over the sink and watch whatever is happening outside and think, with some fondness, about all the things that don't require an ending.
items.intent: Honor a faded friendship through a domestic snack eaten in a familiar way

items.prompt: Give me a tasting menu for writer's block
items.response:
AMUSE-BOUCHE
The cursor

FIRST COURSE
A sentence, deleted

SECOND COURSE
Another sentence, worse — also deleted

INTERMEZZO
Coffee refill, procured urgently

MAIN
Blank page — roasted, long-finish, no resolution

DESSERT
Something written at last, at midnight, not as bad as feared
items.intent: Describe the paralysis and eventual breakthrough of creative blockage.

items.prompt: generate a man page for the experience of aging
items.response:
AGING(7)                 System Lifecycle Management      AGING(7)

NAME
       aging - continuous hardware and software degradation process

SYNOPSIS
       aging [--rate genetic_base] [--modifiers lifestyle_flags]

DESCRIPTION
       aging(7) is a non-optional background process initiated at birth
       and run continuously until process termination. Degrades physical
       hardware on a known curve while, in many instances, expanding
       software capabilities such as perspective(3) and patience(3).

MODIFIERS
       sleep, exercise, diet: decelerate hardware degradation.
       stress, alcohol, regret: accelerate hardware degradation.
       reading, community, curiosity: buffer software improvements.

KNOWN ISSUES
       Hardware and software aging proceed on different timelines.
       Society frequently optimizes for the wrong metric.
       Denial(7) common in early phases; useful in moderation.

NOTES
       aging(7) cannot be paused, reversed, or uninstalled.
       Graceful degradation preferred over attempted circumvention.

SEE ALSO
       mortality(7), wisdom(3), nostalgia(7), health(1)

System Lifecycle Management  2026                    AGING(7)
items.intent: Reframe the physical and psychological arc of getting older as a system process with tradeoffs.

items.prompt: write error page copy for a 404 about an absent sense of direction
items.response:
404 — Direction Not Found

The path you're on doesn't appear on our maps.

This is technically accurate in more than one sense.

You can turn around. You can stay. You can wait until the map updates.

We'd recommend the last one, but we'd be lying if we said we knew.
items.intent: Use the 404 metaphor to describe the experience of not knowing where you're headed in life.

items.prompt: Write alt text for a photo of a checkout line where everyone is looking at their phone.
items.response: Six people stand in a grocery checkout line, each bent toward a glowing screen, the line shuffling forward by instinct, no one watching where they are going or having arrived.
items.intent: Describe collective distraction as a mild but real form of self-absence.

items.prompt: Write a deprecation notice for the version of myself that needed chaos to feel productive.
items.response:
DEPRECATION NOTICE

Operating Mode: Crisis-Driven Productivity (legacy)
Status: DEPRECATED

This mode, which required elevated stress, looming deadlines, and ambient disorder to function, is no longer supported. The replacement environment — consistent, low-drama, and calm — is now the stable branch.
items.intent: Acknowledge and retire an old pattern of only working well under pressure.

items.prompt: Can you draft a layoff email for a luxury hotel chain cutting back-of-house staff after occupancy permanently declined
items.response:
Subject: Property Operations Update

Dear Colleagues,

Occupancy trends across our portfolio have stabilized at levels meaningfully below our pre-pandemic baseline, and we have concluded that our current back-of-house staffing structure is not calibrated to this new operating reality.

With great regret, we will be reducing positions in kitchen, housekeeping, and facilities across 14 properties.

Each affected team member will be met with individually and offered a full severance and benefits continuation package.

Thank you for the care you bring to our guests each day.

Respectfully,
Group General Manager
items.intent: Announce that reduced hotel occupancy means housekeeping and kitchen staff are permanently surplus, using hospitality-industry polish.

items.prompt: Write a handbook rule about employees who always bring up a previous job favorably in meetings
items.response: Section 6.11 — Comparative Institutional References: Favorable citation of a previous employer's practices during internal meetings is permitted once per meeting and must be framed as a proposal, not a contrast. Recurring invocations beginning with the phrase 'At my last company…' exceeding three (3) per calendar month are subject to the Professional Orientation Review process.
items.intent: Contain a common and subtly annoying meeting habit by giving it a formal name and threshold.

items.prompt: Create a PIP for never cleaning the bathroom until guests are coming over
items.response:
PERFORMANCE IMPROVEMENT PLAN
Department: Domestic Hygiene Standards
Trigger Pattern: Event-Driven Only

Deficiency: Bathroom cleaning is initiated exclusively by the imminent arrival of external parties. In the absence of scheduled guests, cleaning has not occurred in documented intervals exceeding 6 weeks. Subject appears to have acclimated to conditions that would alarm an outside observer.

Cleaning Standard: Toilet, sink, and floor addressed weekly. Mirror and shower addressed biweekly. "Guest-ready" should not require emergency intervention.

Plan:
- Assign Sunday as cleaning day. 20 minutes. Timer-assisted.
- Cleaning supplies stored visibly, not under sink behind six bottles of product last used in a prior residence.
- Reframe: you live there too.

Milestone: Four consecutive weeks of cleaning without a visit scheduled.

Note: The bathroom exists between guests.
items.intent: Establish a regular bathroom-cleaning routine rather than a reactive shame-driven one triggered by social pressure.

items.prompt: Draft a holiday newsletter in which we mention a health scare that turned out fine but scared us deeply
items.response:
Warm greetings from the Osei family!

The year had a sharp middle act — a diagnosis in May that prompted a very long summer of tests, consults, and renegotiated prayers. The good news: Sandra is clear. Completely, gratifyingly clear. We hugged the doctors. We cried in parking lots. We are different people now, in the good ways. Happy grateful holidays to everyone.
items.intent: Share a resolved medical scare in a way that conveys how frightening it actually was.

items.prompt: give me a yearbook quote that hints I was in love with my English teacher without saying it outright
items.response: "Thank you to everyone who made these years worth it — especially the one who assigned Gatsby in October and changed everything."
items.intent: Encoding a thinly veiled declaration of inappropriate student admiration in acceptable language.

items.prompt: Create a wedding invitation for the union of language and silence
items.response:
In equal measure,

LANGUAGE
&
SILENCE

celebrate their union.

The ceremony is held in the pause between words.
No venue. No date. It occurs constantly.

Guests who speak will be welcomed.
Guests who listen will understand more.

The couple is, and always has been, the same thing.
items.intent: Express the philosophical idea that meaning lives as much in silence as in words.

items.prompt: Make a bumper sticker about algorithm-driven content killing genuine discovery
items.response: I ONLY KNOW THINGS I WAS RECOMMENDED
items.intent: Critique how recommendation engines narrow what people encounter and believe.

items.prompt: Draft a roadside ad for a moving company that only moves one thing: your regrets
items.response:
FINALLY. A MOVER THAT GETS IT.
HEIRLOOM REGRET LOGISTICS
Packed. Stored. Shipped Anywhere You Go.
You Can't Leave Them Behind. We've Tried.
Free Estimates. Boxes Extra.
items.intent: Advertise a relocation service whose cargo is entirely intangible and inescapable, making it a useless offering.

items.prompt: Write me a yard sign about the HOA requiring me to remove my backyard chicken coop
items.response:
IN MEMORIAM

This space once held
five hens, two years of eggs,
and a dream.

HOA: 1 — Chickens: 0
items.intent: Memorialize the loss of a backyard flock removed under HOA pressure with dark humor.

items.prompt: Help me write a complaint letter to a puzzle company about a jigsaw that had one piece clearly from a different puzzle
items.response:
Dear Quality Assurance Team,

I am writing regarding your 1,000-piece coastal landscape puzzle, purchased last month. After approximately nine hours of assembly, I discovered, with a certainty that no amount of optimism could overcome, that one piece in the lower-left quadrant belongs to a different puzzle. It depicts what appears to be a barn. There are no barns on the coastal landscape. I request a replacement piece and a formal acknowledgment that this happened.

Sincerely,
A Thorough Customer
items.intent: Report a manufacturing defect — a mispackaged puzzle piece — that ruined hours of careful assembly work.

items.prompt: write an apology for recommending a restaurant that had closed two years ago
items.response:
Hi Wen,

I'm so sorry. I told you that place was great and apparently it's been a parking lot since 2022. I should have checked before confidently directing you across town on a Friday evening. Dinner is on me.

— Felix
items.intent: Take responsibility for sending someone on a futile errand based on outdated information.

items.prompt: diary entry about realizing you've been pronouncing a word wrong for years
items.response:
Dear Diary,

Espresso. I have been saying it wrong. Not how you think — I said it correctly — but with a confidence that was apparently audible. Someone corrected me gently. I thanked them. I thought about every time I have said it. There were many times. The word means nothing to me now.
items.intent: Capture the identity-shaking moment of a long-standing mispronunciation being corrected.

items.prompt: can you write a captain's log about forgetting someone's name after months of working together
items.response: Stardate 9231.1. I have been assigned alongside crew member — I will call them "the analyst" — for seven months. Today I needed to introduce them to a new recruit. The name did not come. I know their coffee order. I know they have a golden retriever. I know their laugh. The name: gone, as though airlocked. I said, "You two should definitely connect," and stepped away at pace.
items.intent: Depict the mortifying social failure of blanking on a long-known colleague's name.

items.prompt: Generate a 911 call transcript about someone reporting that a child is selling lemonade without what looks like a permit
items.response:
DISPATCHER: 911, what's your emergency?
CALLER: There's a child operating an unlicensed food service stand on Oak Street.
DISPATCHER: A child?
CALLER: Lemonade. No visible health certificate. She's using a folding table.
DISPATCHER: How old is this child?
CALLER: Six. Seven, maybe.
DISPATCHER: Sir, we will not be responding to that.
CALLER: Is there a code enforcement number I can call?
DISPATCHER: Please don't.
items.intent: Satirize petty civic overreach by treating a child's lemonade stand as a regulatory compliance violation.

items.prompt: Write a closing statement for a case involving someone who always eats the 'good cereal' from a shared box and leaves the rest
items.response: The marshmallows are gone. Every morning my client reaches into the box and finds only the beige shapes — the shapes no one chooses, the shapes that exist to justify the marshmallows. The defendant understood the implicit agreement and violated it systematically. We seek a court order: cereal must be consumed in representative proportion, or purchased separately.
items.intent: Litigate the unfair selective consumption of the desirable components of shared food.

items.prompt: Draft an eviction notice for the thought that it's too late to change careers
items.response:
EVICTION NOTICE

To: It's Too Late
Unit: My inner planning committee, northeast corner

You arrived with a clipboard and a chart showing "optimal entry windows" all of which have allegedly passed. Your citations are flawed. The premises contain ample usable square footage regardless of the year. Possibility has been waiting in the stairwell. You are asked to leave at once.

— The Person Who Still Has Time
items.intent: Challenging the internalized belief that a life change is foreclosed due to age.

items.prompt: Make a collections letter from a city to a former resident who left without giving back.
items.response:
FROM: THE CITY
RE: OUTSTANDING CIVIC DEBT

Dear Former Resident,

You lived within these borders for eleven years. You used the parks, benefited from the community gardens, attended the block parties, and borrowed the neighborhood's character liberally for your own sense of identity.

You relocated to a quieter suburb without volunteering, voting in the local elections, or picking up litter even once.

This is not a fine. This is a record.

Yours in perpetuity,
The City You Left
items.intent: Satirize the civic free-rider who benefits from urban culture and community but contributes nothing before departing.

items.prompt: Write a cinquain about the feeling of your GPS rerouting unexpectedly
items.response:
Turn
Left, it insists
Confident, immediately wrong
The road it planned has been erased
Rerouting
items.intent: Poke at the authoritative but fallible voice of navigation software.

items.prompt: pantoum about the standing desk that everyone adjusts and no one resets
items.response:
The desk is set to someone else's height.
I raise it slowly; the motor hums.
A sticker says: last set by B.
I am six inches taller than B.

I raise it slowly; the motor hums.
By eleven it has drifted back to sitting.
I am six inches taller than B.
The adjustment sheet is long since gone.

By eleven it has drifted back to sitting.
I reset it and tape my height to the rail.
The adjustment sheet is long since gone.
The desk is set to someone else's height.

I reset it and tape my height to the rail.
A sticker says: last set by B.
I reset it and tape my height to the rail.
I raise it slowly; the motor hums.
items.intent: Capture the low-stakes territorial tension over shared ergonomic furniture.

items.prompt: Write liner notes for an album about the self-checkout machine experience
items.response:
Unexpected Item (2020)

This record is dedicated to everyone who has placed an item in the bagging area only to be accused. You were not wrong. The machine was not right. Side B begins with an apology. The apology is from us, not the machine. The machine will never apologize.
items.intent: Treat the adversarial dynamic of the self-checkout machine as a source of genuine grievance and catharsis.

items.prompt: Write an episode description for a podcast where the hosts try to describe the color of fog
items.response: Gray is too confident a word. White gives it too much credit. This week, Ren and Jules spend an afternoon near the harbor making offers, and the fog declines all of them.
items.intent: Attempt to pin down an inherently elusive sensory experience through language.

items.prompt: Write a YouTube comment for a timelapse of a city intersection over 24 hours
items.response: If you watch long enough you start to see patterns. Same people. Same times. Nobody notices. Everybody is a regular somewhere.
items.intent: Reveal the hidden routines and invisible community of strangers sharing public space.

items.prompt: Write a closed caption for the moment a surgeon realizes mid-operation that they recognize the patient
items.response: [INSTRUMENTS STEADY] [CONTEXT SHIFTING RAPIDLY] [PROFESSIONALISM LOAD-BEARING]
items.intent: Annotate the impossible compartmentalization required in a high-stakes personal collision.

items.prompt: Write an artist statement about a hotel room key card
items.response: The key card grants and withdraws welcome in the same gesture. It is identity stripped to magnetic stripe — access granted not to you, but to whichever body carries the card. I am whoever swipes.
items.intent: Use temporary lodging access to explore identity as something assignable rather than innate.

items.prompt: Write a headnote for pound cake that involves a narrator who used to be a competitive baker and retired bitterly
items.response: I placed second three years in a row. The woman who beat me each time used a sour cream variation I considered unsporting. I have since retired from competition, by which I mean I was gently asked not to return. This is my original recipe, unmodified, still better in my estimation than what won. You can decide.
items.intent: Channel unresolved competitive bitterness into a cake recipe presented as definitive

items.prompt: Make me a tasting menu for aging
items.response:
AMUSE-BOUCHE
A grey hair, first

FIRST COURSE
Knee noise — new

SECOND COURSE
Bedtime, moved earlier

INTERMEZZO
The realization that you were right about something, once

MAIN
Time — compressed, paradoxically rich

DESSERT
Perspective, full portion. Finally acquired.
items.intent: Find dry humor and unexpected grace in the process of getting older.

items.prompt: can you make a man page for imposter syndrome
items.response:
IMPOSTER(7)              Cognitive Distortion Suite     IMPOSTER(7)

NAME
       imposter - false-authentication loop in competence self-model

SYNOPSIS
       imposter [--role string] [--evidence_of_competence ignored]

DESCRIPTION
       imposter(7) maintains a persistent belief that the host's
       credentials and accomplishments are fraudulent and that exposure
       is imminent. Notably resistant to counter-evidence; new evidence
       of competence is reattributed to luck, timing, or error.

BEHAVIOR
       Success events:  reattributed. Input rejected.
       Failure events:  accepted as confirmation. Input amplified.
       Praise received: discarded as sender error.

AFFECTED POPULATIONS
       Disproportionately observed in high-achieving and early-career
       hosts. Notably absent in hosts who should probably have it.

TREATMENT
       Peer disclosure effective. Therapy(1) recommended.
       Evidence accumulation alone is not a fix.

SEE ALSO
       anxiety(7), perfectionism(7), self-doubt(7), credentials(5)

Cognitive Distortion Suite   2026                  IMPOSTER(7)
items.intent: Expose the self-reinforcing illogic of dismissing one's own demonstrated competence.

items.prompt: make a 404 for a site about grief — for the page where someone was looking for the person they lost
items.response:
404

We're sorry. This person could not be found at this address.

They were here. The server remembers.

We don't have a redirect for this.
items.intent: Create a spare, sincere 404 page that holds the weight of loss without deflecting into humor.

items.prompt: I need alt text for a photo of a half-finished oil painting on an easel, no artist visible.
items.response: An oil painting on a wooden easel, the left half a fully rendered landscape, the right half only rough charcoal marks on linen, the artist apparently mid-thought and gone.
items.intent: Treat incompleteness as a narrative fact rather than a failure, using the unfinished work as evidence of interrupted intention.

items.prompt: Make a deprecation notice for my ability to remember phone numbers.
items.response:
DEPRECATION NOTICE

Cognitive Feature: Phone Number Memorization (v0–2005)
Status: DEPRECATED
