This capability was rendered non-essential by contact management software and has since atrophied completely. No recovery path is known. In a power outage, you cannot reach anyone. This is not considered a priority issue.
items.intent: Reflect on the cognitive skills lost to smartphones with mild alarm.

items.prompt: Write a layoff email for a legal tech startup that ran out of runway before reaching profitability
items.response:
Subject: A Message from Our CEO

Team,

I owe you directness today. Despite our best efforts to close additional funding, we have been unable to do so before reaching the end of our current runway. We cannot continue at our current scale.

Effective today, we are reducing our team by 60%. Affected employees will receive severance equal to six weeks' base salary and immediate vesting of accrued equity.

I am grateful for every day we built this together.

Alex
items.intent: Admit an outright cash failure at a legal tech startup and announce a gutting layoff with unusual specificity on severance terms.

items.prompt: Make an official policy about employees who send emails at 11 PM and expect immediate responses
items.response: Section 6.13 — Off-Hours Message Expectation Standards: Emails transmitted between 9:00 PM and 6:00 AM local recipient time carry no implicit response obligation until the following business day. Senders who mark routine correspondence Urgent outside standard hours will have their Urgency Credibility Score reviewed quarterly.
items.intent: Push back on after-hours communication pressure by formally deprecating urgency claims sent at night.

items.prompt: Write a PIP for my tendency to doomwatch TV for hours when I'm anxious instead of addressing what's bothering me
items.response:
PERFORMANCE IMPROVEMENT PLAN
Case: Anxiety-Triggered Passive Consumption
Division: Emotional Regulation

Pattern: When subject experiences elevated stress or unresolved emotional tension, they initiate TV or streaming sessions of 2–5 hours. Content selected skews toward familiar comfort material, crime procedurals, or reality competition shows requiring no cognitive investment.

Impact: Source of anxiety remains unaddressed. By session end, original discomfort is joined by lost time and mild self-recrimination.

Objective: Distinguish between intentional rest (legitimate) and avoidance consumption (addressed here).

Plan:
1. Before starting a session: name what you're feeling. Thirty seconds.
2. If anxious: one episode allowed. After one episode, assess whether avoidance is occurring.
3. If avoidance: write three sentences about the actual problem. Then decide.

Milestone: Address source of two stress episodes directly within 45-day window.

Note: The show will be there. The problem will also be there, but worse.
items.intent: Replace anxiety-avoidance TV habits with even minimal direct engagement with the source of stress.

items.prompt: Write a holiday letter where we gently acknowledge that the kids don't come home for the holidays anymore
items.response:
Greetings from a quiet December at the Larssen home.

The children — and they are very much adults now — had their own plans this year, as adults do. We FaceTimed at length and with feeling. The table was smaller, the house more echo-y, the wine opened earlier. We are adjusting to this new configuration of family with only moderate wistfulness. Happy holidays from just the two of us.
items.intent: Process the empty-nest holiday season for an audience of relatives who know the family well.

items.prompt: write a yearbook quote about planning to vote for myself in every election I can't win
items.response: "One day I'll be on the ballot. Until then, a write-in is still a vote."
items.intent: Signaling political ambition while admitting a current lack of any realistic path to office.

items.prompt: Write a wedding invitation for fire and a forest that's been through drought
items.response:
It is with great urgency that

FIRE
announces its union with
THE DROUGHT-STRICKEN FOREST

Date: This summer. Possibly the next.
Venue: Everywhere conditions are met.

There will be no guests. Only witnesses.
The ceremony is not a celebration.

Water donations accepted.
items.intent: Use the wedding form to grimly invoke the climate crisis and the increasing frequency of wildfires.

items.prompt: Write a bumper sticker about the anxiety of constant productivity culture
items.response: OPTIMIZING MYSELF INTO SOMEONE I DON'T RECOGNIZE
items.intent: Critique self-improvement culture that erodes authentic identity.

items.prompt: Write billboard copy for a bank that only lends money to people who don't need it
items.response:
CONGRATULATIONS ON NOT NEEDING US.
CORNERSTONE PREFERRED LENDING
Loans For The Already Comfortable
Apply Today. Approval Requires Proof You're Fine.
Low Rates. High Standards. No Point.
items.intent: Market financial services structured around a lending criterion that excludes everyone who would benefit.

items.prompt: Create a yard sign opposing a neighbor's plan to put up a massive privacy fence
items.response:
12-FOOT FENCE
IN A 6-FOOT ZONE

Block the view or block the code?

Call Building & Zoning: (555) 348-9012
items.intent: Alert neighbors and authorities that a proposed fence violates local height limits.

items.prompt: Draft a formal complaint to a hotel spa about the relaxation room being too quiet — an oppressive silence
items.response:
To the Spa Director,

I visited your relaxation suite last Thursday and found the silence, while clearly intentional, to have crossed a threshold from 'peaceful' into 'unnerving.' The room was so quiet that I became acutely aware of my own breathing, then my heartbeat, then several sounds I cannot identify and prefer not to think about. A very small amount of ambient noise — water, perhaps, or distant wind — would help guests relax rather than conduct an unwilling audit of their own bodies.

Thank you,
A Guest
items.intent: Request that a spa add minimal ambient sound to prevent an oppressive silence from becoming the opposite of relaxing.

items.prompt: write an apology note for loudly humming at a library for an indeterminate amount of time before being shushed
items.response:
To whoever shushed me at the Riverside branch on Tuesday,

You were right to do it. I had no idea I was doing it, which is perhaps the most embarrassing part. I appreciate the intervention. I'll check in with myself more often.

Apologetically,
A patron
items.intent: Acknowledge making disruptive ambient noise in a quiet space without realizing it.

items.prompt: write a diary entry about accidentally liking an old post while social media stalking
items.response:
Dear Diary,

I went back three years. I was cautious. I was silent. And then — the tap — a like, given, visible, irrevocable. I unliked it in four seconds. But those four seconds are forever. They know. I have deleted the app.
items.intent: Dramatize the social media horror of leaving a trace where none should exist.

items.prompt: write a captain's log about a meeting that could have been an email
items.response: Stardate 4466.3. We convened six officers to discuss the updated file-naming convention. The convention was presented in a single slide. It read: use underscores, not spaces. The meeting ran thirty-five minutes. There were follow-up questions. One officer requested a working group. The working group has been scheduled for next Wednesday. I have sent myself a calendar block titled: grieving.
items.intent: Bemoan the chronic waste of scheduling meetings for trivial information that could be written down.

items.prompt: Draft a 911 transcript about a caller who is upset that their neighbor got a leaf blower
items.response:
DISPATCHER: 911, what's your emergency?
CALLER: My neighbor purchased a gas-powered leaf blower.
DISPATCHER: Has he been using it?
CALLER: Every Sunday. For twenty-two minutes.
DISPATCHER: Is that against local ordinance?
CALLER: The ordinance is in my heart.
DISPATCHER: That's not enforceable.
CALLER: Then what are you for?
items.intent: Express noise sensitivity and neighbor resentment through a demand that feelings constitute legal authority.

items.prompt: Can you draft a summation for a plaintiff who objects to a coworker chewing loudly in an open office
items.response: The defendant eats at their desk. This alone is not a crime. But the sounds that accompany their eating — the rhythmic, percussive, open-mouthed symphony — constitute a form of involuntary shared experience. My client wears headphones. The sounds still arrive. We ask not for silence, but for closed lips, as nature and basic acoustics intended.
items.intent: Make a formal case for enforcing quiet eating norms in shared workspaces.

items.prompt: Write an eviction notice for a friendship that has become one-sided
items.response:
NOTICE TO VACATE

To: The Arrangement We've Been Calling a Friendship
Premises: My emotional bandwidth

Review of the ledger shows consistent occupancy by one party with maintenance, repairs, and emotional labor performed exclusively by the other. This is not a shared tenancy. It is a storage arrangement. The unit will be reclaimed and rented to someone who intends to live here.

— The One Who Called Last
items.intent: Acknowledging and ending a relationship in which the emotional investment is no longer reciprocal.

items.prompt: Draft a collections letter for a person who owes their younger self an explanation.
items.response:
NOTICE FROM YOUR YOUNGER SELF — EXPLANATION OUTSTANDING

Dear Older Version,

Several of the choices you made in the intervening years require clarification. Specifically: why the dream was abandoned in 2014, why you became so serious about parking, and why you now eat lunch at your desk voluntarily.

The undersigned (age 14) was promised something different. A brief written explanation would be appreciated. Please do not say "you'll understand when you're older."

Waiting,
You, Before
items.intent: Create a poignant and funny demand for self-explanation addressed from a person's idealistic younger self.

items.prompt: Make a cinquain about the glow of a charging cable in the dark
items.response:
Thread
Of orange light
Snaking across the floor
The only warmth in the room is
Plugged in
items.intent: Find quiet beauty in the mundane overnight charging ritual.

items.prompt: write a short pantoum about the weekly one-on-one that never gets to the real point
items.response:
How is everything going? Pretty good.
We talk about the project for ten minutes.
I had something I wanted to say.
The clock nudges toward the next hour.

We talk about the project for ten minutes.
The air holds a question I keep swallowing.
The clock nudges toward the next hour.
See you next week, we both say.

The air holds a question I keep swallowing.
I walk back to my desk with the thing unsaid.
See you next week, we both say.
How is everything going? Pretty good.

I walk back to my desk with the thing unsaid.
I had something I wanted to say.
See you next week, we both say.
The clock nudges toward the next hour.
items.intent: Portray the emotional avoidance embedded in routine check-in meetings.

items.prompt: Make liner notes for a jazz fusion album about reheating leftovers
items.response:
Still Good (2023)

The cold center. The hot edge. The second attempt. These are improvisations on transformation — on the question of whether what you have is what you wanted or only what remains. The drummer insisted on recording "Day Three" even though we all said it was time to let it go.
items.intent: Use the culinary ambivalence around leftovers as a jazz metaphor for compromise and diminishing returns.

items.prompt: Write me a podcast episode description for an episode about trying to remember a dream right after waking up
items.response: There was a staircase. Or a field. Something was either chasing you or welcoming you—it was hard to tell. By the time you showered, it was gone. This episode reconstructs the ruins.
items.intent: Explore the rapid dissolution of dream memory as a strange, slightly mournful subject.

items.prompt: Write a YouTube comment for a video about an arcade game from 1987 that nobody remembers
items.response: Somewhere there is a 47-year-old who was the best in the world at this for six weeks in the summer of 1991 and has never mentioned it to anyone.
items.intent: Honor the forgotten peak achievements of ordinary people that history never recorded.

items.prompt: Give me a subtitle for the sound design in a scene where nothing is happening but something is wrong
items.response: [REFRIGERATOR HUM] [CLOCK TICKING] [ONE SOUND THAT SHOULD NOT BE THERE]
items.intent: Establish dread through the grammar of domestic normalcy with a single deviation.

items.prompt: Make a serious artist statement about a burnt piece of toast
items.response: The toast exceeded its mandate. Blackened, it carries the record of distraction — a minute of inattention made indelible, the bread's carbon diary of my absence from the kitchen.
items.intent: Treat a cooking mistake as a physical artifact of inattention and domestic failure.

items.prompt: Headnote for black bean tacos about the first meal someone cooked for themselves after moving out at 18
items.response: I moved out at eighteen with forty dollars and a box of things that didn't belong together. The first meal I made in my own kitchen was black bean tacos because they were cheap, because I'd watched my mother make them enough times to approximate, and because I needed to eat something I had made myself in a space that was only mine. They were fine. I was ecstatic.
items.intent: Mark the emotional significance of independence through the simplest possible meal

items.prompt: Write a tasting menu for bureaucracy
items.response:
AMUSE-BOUCHE
The form — wrong form

FIRST COURSE
The correct form, found after twenty minutes

SECOND COURSE
Hold music, warm and structureless

INTERMEZZO
Your call is very important to us

MAIN
Human, reached — brief, unresolved

DESSERT
The problem persists. A new form is available.
items.intent: Satirize the endless loop of institutional red tape.

items.prompt: write a man page for the act of forgetting something important
items.response:
FORGET(1)                Human Memory Suite              FORGET(1)

NAME
       forget - unscheduled eviction of flagged memory entry

SYNOPSIS
       forget [--item string] [--context critical|trivial]
       [--timing worst_possible]

DESCRIPTION
       forget(1) silently removes a memory entry without notification.
       Operates without consent. Preferentially targets items marked
       important, committed via verbal promise, or involving birthdays.

BEHAVIOR
       Items stored with high emotional weight: retained.
       Items stored with deliberate effort: 40% retention at 24h.
       Items you were certain you'd remember: evicted immediately.

OPTIONS
       --context critical
                   Guarantees --timing worst_possible.
       --timing    Default: worst_possible. Not configurable.

RECOVERY
       Partial: environmental cue, smell, or the moment you stop trying.
       Full: it's gone.

BUGS
       Cringeworthy memories from 1997 are fully intact.
       The name of the person you just met: unavailable.

SEE ALSO
       memory(3), remember(1), nostalgia(7), embarrassment(7)

Human Memory Suite 1.0       2026                    FORGET(1)
items.intent: Satirize memory's selective and perversely timed failures.

items.prompt: write a 404 for a creative writing platform — for when someone's voice has gone quiet
items.response:
404 — Your Voice Not Found

It was here. You've used this page before.

Sometimes a voice goes quiet between drafts. That's not deletion — that's drafting.

Leave a blank document open. It'll come back when it's ready.
items.intent: Describe the silence of a creative person who has lost their artistic voice using an error page.

items.prompt: Alt text for a photo of a filing cabinet with one drawer open and papers overflowing.
items.response: A gray metal filing cabinet stands against a beige wall, the third drawer hanging open under the weight of folders that have grown beyond any system meant to contain them.
items.intent: Use administrative overflow as a metaphor for the moment a system exceeds its own logic.

items.prompt: Draft a deprecation notice for the habit of eating lunch at my desk.
items.response:
DEPRECATION NOTICE

Behavior: Desk Lunch (enterprise edition)
Status: DEPRECATED

This practice — consuming food within arm's reach of email — provides zero cognitive restoration and a keyboard with crumbs in it. All-day context-switching without a break has been flagged as a performance risk. Migrate to eating elsewhere, even if elsewhere is just a different chair.
items.intent: Reclaim a genuine midday break instead of eating while continuing to work.

items.prompt: Draft a short layoff email for a telecom company downsizing its customer service team after implementing AI chat support
items.response:
Subject: Customer Experience Organization Update

Dear Team,

As our AI-powered support platform has reached full deployment, we have been reviewing the structure of our Customer Experience organization. We are now in a position to serve our customers effectively with a smaller, more specialized team.

Unfortunately, this means eliminating a significant number of customer service agent roles. Impacted employees have been emailed individually.

Thank you for setting the standard this technology is now built to replicate.

Sincerely,
SVP, Customer Experience
items.intent: Tell customer service workers that the AI trained on their work is now replacing them, dressed in the language of advancement.

items.prompt: Write a rule about employees who take conference room bookings they end up not using
items.response: Section 5.9 — Conference Room Utilization Assurance: Rooms booked for thirty (30) minutes or more that show no occupant within eight (8) minutes of the reserved start time are considered abandoned and may be claimed by any employee. Employees whose reserved rooms are abandoned on two (2) or more occasions in a quarter will lose priority booking status for sixty (60) days.
items.intent: Create a tiered penalty system for conference room squatting as a form of resource misuse.

items.prompt: Draft a PIP for someone who constantly starts sentences with 'I should really...' but never follows through
items.response:
PERFORMANCE IMPROVEMENT PLAN
Subject: Declarative Intent Without Executive Follow-Through

Pattern Identified: Subject produces verbal commitments prefixed with 'I should really' at a rate of 4–8 per day. Topics include exercise, email responses, calling specific people, organizing a space, learning something, or addressing an ongoing low-grade problem. Completion rate of 'I should really' items: approximately 12%.

Function of Phrase: Provides partial psychological credit for intention while requiring no action. Maintains self-image as a person who knows what they ought to do.

Required Change: 'I should really' must be followed, within 90 seconds, by either:
a) A scheduled time to do the thing, or
b) Explicit acknowledgment that you will not do it (acceptable; clarifying).

Plan:
- For one week: log every 'I should really' statement. Review at week's end.
- Convert top three items to calendar events.

Review: 30 days. Phrase should appear less; completion should appear more.
items.intent: Convert perpetual low-stakes verbal intentions into actual decisions — either action or conscious abandonment.

items.prompt: Write a newsletter where the family project we hyped last year went nowhere
items.response:
Seasonal greetings from the Bautista family!

You may recall our enthusiastic 2024 announcement about the family food truck. We are delighted to report: it taught us a great deal about licensing, margins, and ourselves. The truck has been sold to a lovely couple in Tucson. We remain foodies at heart. The kitchen at home is very well-equipped now. Onward to the next vision!
items.intent: Quietly kill off a failed venture that was announced with fanfare in a previous newsletter.

items.prompt: write a yearbook quote about how I've been keeping a journal of everything embarrassing that happened to other people
items.response: "Four hundred and twelve pages. Hardcover. Indexed alphabetically. You know who you are."
items.intent: Warning classmates that a meticulous private record of their humiliations exists.

items.prompt: Make a wedding invitation for a shadow and the person it belongs to
items.response:
THE SHADOW
&
THE ONE WHO CASTS IT

have been together since birth and see no reason to stop.

Venue: Any lit surface
Time: When the angle is right

The union is entirely dependent on one party.
There have been disagreements at noon.
Evening brings reconciliation.

No ceremony. Just acknowledgment.
items.intent: Meditate on the inseparable and sometimes overlooked relationship between a person and their own darkness.

items.prompt: Give me a bumper sticker about the collapse of shared reality and truth online
items.response: LIVING IN YOUR TIMELINE. NOT MINE.
items.intent: Name the fracturing of a common informational reality across communities.

items.prompt: Write an ad for a pharmacy that only stocks placebos, and is proud of it
items.response:
BELIEVE HARDER.
SUGAR PINES PHARMACY
Full-Spectrum Placebo Solutions Since 1994
Ask Our Pharmacist. She's Also A Placebo.
Exit 18. Results May Vary. (They Won't.)
items.intent: Promote a medication retailer whose entire stock is medically inert, transparently advertising its own uselessness.

items.prompt: Make a yard sign asking my neighbors to stop blowing their leaves into my yard
items.response:
LEAVES FLOW DOWNHILL

Unless your property is uphill,
those aren't your leaves anymore.

Ours stop at the property line.
items.intent: Confront a neighbor who blows yard debris onto their lawn using indirect, dry reasoning.

items.prompt: Write a complaint letter to a bookshop about their staff recommendations being too good — I keep buying books I don't have time for
items.response:
To the Booksellers,

I write in a spirit of begrudging admiration mixed with genuine distress. Your staff recommendation cards are, without exception, compelling, accurate, and persuasive. I have now purchased seventeen books in four months based solely on these recommendations. I have read two of them. The remaining fifteen form a tower on my nightstand that constitutes both a structural and an existential concern. I ask that you consider occasionally recommending something less interesting.

Warm regards,
A Very Good Customer
items.intent: Humorously blame a bookshop's excellent curation for causing an unmanageable accumulation of unread books.

items.prompt: help me write an apology for confidently giving someone wrong directions and then seeing them drive off
items.response:
Hi,

If you were trying to get to Bellmore Street and I pointed you toward the bypass — that was me, and I was wrong. I realized about thirty seconds after your car disappeared. I'm sorry for the detour. I hope you found it eventually.

— A bystander
items.intent: Apologize for giving confidently wrong directions that likely sent someone the wrong way.

items.prompt: journal entry about coming home and realizing you left the porch light on all day
items.response:
Dear Diary,

The light was on when I arrived. It had been on when I left. All day. Burning for no one, illuminating the daylight. I turned it off with the energy of a person who has already lost. The electric bill will reflect my character.
items.intent: Use a trivial wasteful mistake as a mirror for self-reproach.

items.prompt: captain's log entry about submitting an expense report
items.response: Stardate 8120.7. Initiated the quarterly disbursement filing. The system required a category for each item. The conference meal had no matching category. I selected "Client Entertainment." I entertained no clients. I attended alone. The system flagged the taxi for missing a receipt. I have the receipt. The portal will not accept images above 2MB. My receipt is 2.1MB. I have trimmed it. It is 2.09MB. The portal says no.
items.intent: Dramatize the needless friction of submitting corporate expense reports.

items.prompt: Write a 911 call transcript where someone reports that the self-checkout machine keeps beeping
items.response:
DISPATCHER: 911, state your emergency.
CALLER: The self-checkout at the market is telling me there's an unexpected item in the bagging area. There's not.
DISPATCHER: Did you ask a store employee?
CALLER: Three of them. They tapped it and left.
DISPATCHER: I'm not sure what you'd like us to do.
CALLER: Bear witness.
DISPATCHER: I can't dispatch officers to bear witness.
CALLER: Then what is society?
items.intent: Frame the helpless frustration of malfunctioning retail automation as a philosophical crisis requiring state intervention.

items.prompt: Write a closing argument for a dispute about someone who never pays their fair share when splitting a bill
items.response: The check arrived. Phones appeared. Calculators were consulted. And yet, when the totals resolved, the defendant's contribution was, once again, optimistic — a number closer to what they wished they had ordered than what they had. My client does not seek charity. They seek arithmetic. We ask the court to mandate tip inclusion and rounding up.
items.intent: Call out consistent underpayment during group restaurant bill-splitting.

items.prompt: Make an eviction notice for the version of the future I imagined at 22
items.response:
NOTICE OF EVICTION

To: The Plan
Unit: My expectations, long-term wing

You have resided in the premises since early adulthood, insisting the actual future comply with your floor plan. The actual future has not. Your renovations — marriage by 28, career certainty, and a dog named something literary — remain uninhabited. The real tenant, which is simply what happened, holds the only valid occupancy. Please surrender the blueprint.

— Present Tense
items.intent: Making peace with the difference between one's youthful expectations and how life actually unfolded.

items.prompt: Write a collections letter for borrowed confidence that was never returned.
items.response:
CONFIDENCE LENDING STATEMENT — FINAL NOTICE

Dear Recipient,

Before the interview, the presentation, and the difficult conversation with your landlord, this office supplied you with the full measure of its confidence reserves. You were told you could do it. You were reminded of your own strengths. You were seen.

The undersigned is now facing their own difficult season and finds the confidence tank empty.

A reciprocal deposit is requested. Even a partial one.

Bravely,
The Confidence Recovery Desk
items.intent: Ask someone to return the emotional bolstering they were given by now offering the same support in return.

items.prompt: Write a cinquain about the moment an AI gives a confidently wrong answer
items.response:
Sure
And absolutely
Wrong — the dates, the names, the facts —
Delivered with such warm assurance
I believed
items.intent: Satirize the fluent self-confidence of large language model hallucinations.

I take the stairs to avoid the elevator wait.
Something in the wiring remembers a complaint.
The flicker happens once, then holds.

I take the stairs to avoid the elevator wait.
Each day I calculate the same small risk.
The flicker happens once, then holds.
I count my footsteps without meaning to.

Each day I calculate the same small risk.
The light above knows my schedule by now.
I count my footsteps without meaning to.
The fluorescent tube stutters on the third floor landing.

The light above knows my schedule by now.
Something in the wiring remembers a complaint.
I count my footsteps without meaning to.
I take the stairs to avoid the elevator wait.

items.prompt: Write liner notes for an album about the experience of reading assembly manuals for office chairs
items.response:
Lumbar Support (2018)

Five bolts remain. This is the central fact of the album. The gasket in track three is decorative. The height adjustment lever has two positions: acceptable and the other one. We dedicate this record to the chair we assembled before reading the warnings. We sit in it still.
items.intent: Extract dark comedy and quiet resignation from the laborious process of assembling ergonomic office furniture.

items.prompt: Describe a podcast episode about the specific awkwardness of being the first person at a party
items.response: You are on time. This is your punishment. Episode 19 documents the seventeen minutes before anyone else arrives, when you and the host pretend to both be very interested in the snack table.
items.intent: Capture the social discomfort of early arrival at a social event.

items.prompt: Write a YouTube comment for a compilation video of vending machines working perfectly
items.response: This is propaganda. I am still not over what happened to me in 2014. The machine knows what it did.
items.intent: Dramatize a personal grievance against vending machines by treating a satisfying compilation as emotional betrayal.

items.prompt: Write the closed caption for a wolf howling alone at 3am in a forest no human has entered in decades
items.response: [INTENDED RECIPIENT: UNKNOWN] [SIGNAL BROADCAST REGARDLESS]
items.intent: Reframe animal communication as a transmission without a guaranteed receiver.

items.prompt: Write a pretentious gallery description for a stack of junk mail
items.response: Each envelope was addressed to me and not to me — it named my name while knowing nothing of it. This archive of unwanted attention constitutes a portrait assembled by strangers who need something from the portrait's subject.
items.intent: Reframe unsolicited advertising mail as an unwitting collective portrait of the recipient.

items.prompt: Write a headnote for pho where the narrator describes a complicated relationship with their cultural heritage
items.response: My mother made pho for every illness, every cold, every heartbreak she considered serious enough to boil bones for. I spent years being embarrassed by things I no longer understand how I could have been embarrassed by. Now I make pho badly, and I call her to ask what I've done wrong, and she tells me, and this is, I think, the point.
items.intent: Reconcile cultural inheritance and parental connection through a recipe that requires help

items.prompt: Design a tasting menu for a midlife crisis
items.response:
AMUSE-BOUCHE
A sports car brochure, bookmarked

FIRST COURSE
Youth, remembered inaccurately

SECOND COURSE
A question with no clean answer — served room temperature

INTERMEZZO
New haircut

MAIN
The pivot — career, hobby, or both

DESSERT
Acceptance. Comes around; not for everyone tonight.
items.intent: Gently mock the clichés of reckoning with middle age.

items.prompt: make a man page for the experience of being stuck in a conversation you want to leave
items.response:
TRAPPED-CONVERSATION(1)  Social Interaction Suite   TRAPPED-CONVERSATION(1)

NAME
       trapped-conversation - conversation with no viable exit path

SYNOPSIS
       trapped-conversation [--location corner|elevator|party] SPEAKER

DESCRIPTION
       trapped-conversation(1) describes a conversational state in which
       the host is a non-consenting listener and all exit strategies have
       been evaluated as socially costly. Duration is determined entirely
       by SPEAKER.

EXIT STRATEGIES (success rates)
       fake-phone-call(1)     42% — requires prior setup
       point-behind-speaker   31% — "oh, is that [name]?"
       sudden-drink-need      55% — most natural
       honest-exit            12% — theoretical

OPTIONS
       --location  Constrains viable exit strategies. Elevator removes all.

SIGNALS
       SIGTERM  Not acknowledged by SPEAKER.
       SIGKILL  Not available.

SEE ALSO
       small-talk(1), escape(1), politeness(3), social-anxiety(7)

Social Interaction Suite 2.0 2026    TRAPPED-CONVERSATION(1)
items.intent: Expose the social trap of polite captivity in an unwanted conversation.

items.prompt: write a 404 page for a professional development site where someone's career path is missing
items.response:
404 — Career Path Not Found

The trajectory you were following has not been located.

This URL was last valid around Q3, when there was a reorg. We've been rebuilding the sitemap since then.

Check LinkedIn. Or don't. That might make it worse.
items.intent: Capture professional disorientation after a life disruption through web navigation metaphors.

items.prompt: Write alt text for a photo of a vending machine at the end of a long empty hallway.
items.response: A vending machine glows at the end of a fluorescent hallway, its selections arranged in identical rows, humming its readiness to no one, which it has been doing since before the hallway was emptied.
items.intent: Give a vending machine a kind of patient, unconditional service-consciousness that becomes eerie in isolation.

items.prompt: Write a deprecation notice for the belief that busyness is the same as importance.
items.response:
DEPRECATION NOTICE

Cognitive Shortcut: Busyness = Worth (v∞)
Status: DEPRECATED

This equation has been tested extensively and found to be false. Many busy people produce nothing of note; many still people produce a great deal. The metric is being retired. A replacement is under development and will ship when it is ready.
items.intent: Reject the cultural equation of overwork with value and productivity.

items.prompt: Write a layoff email for a biotech firm that's spinning off a division and leaving some employees without a seat at either company
items.response:
Subject: Update on the Helix Spinoff and Transition

Colleagues,

As the formal separation of Helix Therapeutics is now complete, we want to provide clarity on team placements. The majority of employees have been placed within either the parent company or Helix in aligned roles.

Unfortunately, a subset of colleagues — primarily within shared services — did not receive an offer from either entity. These individuals are being notified directly today.

We are committed to robust transition support for everyone affected.

With appreciation,
Chief People Officer
items.intent: Inform employees caught between two companies that they have been dropped through the gap of a spinoff with no home on either side.

items.prompt: Draft a policy about employees who never update their out-of-office messages
items.response: Section 6.9 — Automated Absence Notification Hygiene: Out-of-office messages must be deactivated within one (1) business hour of the employee's return. Messages that have been active for more than five (5) business days past the stated return date will be flagged by IT, and the employee's digital availability status will be set to Ambiguous pending manual correction.
items.intent: Invent a new status category — Ambiguous — to capture employees who drift through the digital cracks of return-to-office.

items.prompt: Make a PIP for my inability to sit with boredom without immediately reaching for my phone
items.response:
PERFORMANCE IMPROVEMENT PLAN
Area: Attentional Autonomy
Review Period: 6 weeks

Deficiency: Subject cannot sustain unstimulated wakefulness for more than 40–70 seconds before initiating phone retrieval. Triggers include: waiting in line, sitting at a red light (passenger), commercial break, any pause in conversation, and lying in bed with a thought they do not wish to pursue.

Impact: Reduced tolerance for discomfort; diminished capacity for reflection; inability to be bored in ways historically associated with creative thought.

Objective: Sustain phone-free idle periods of 5 minutes, daily.

Plan:
- Designate one daily context as phone-free: meals, morning coffee, short walks.
- When impulse arises, observe it for 10 seconds before acting.
- Note what thoughts appear in the absence of input.

Milestone: 10 consecutive phone-free meals.

Note: Boredom is not a problem. It is a state. You are allowed to be in it.
items.intent: Rebuild a tolerance for unstimulated moments and reduce reflexive phone dependency.

items.prompt: Help me write a holiday letter that doesn't mention my miscarriage but explains why we seem subdued
items.response:
Quieter wishes from the Eriksson family this season.

We had a tender year — one that asked more of us than we expected, that made small joys feel enormous and big plans feel unnecessary. We are grateful for the warmth of friends who didn't ask too many questions and loved us anyway. We are healing in private, which is what we need. Softer, kinder holidays to all.
items.intent: Signal grief over a pregnancy loss to close friends without stating what happened.

items.prompt: write a yearbook quote for someone who was way too online and is only now realizing it
items.response: "I gave my best years to a website that is now a different website. The terms of service changed. So did I."
items.intent: Mourning the time and identity lost to a platform that evolved beyond recognition.

items.prompt: Write a wedding invitation for the merger of overthinking and a simple decision
items.response:
A SIMPLE DECISION
(what to have for lunch)
&
OVERTHINKING

are pleased to announce their union.

Date: Right now. It's getting cold.
Venue: Your frontal lobe.

The ceremony is expected to last 45 minutes.
All options are technically fine.
Guests are asked not to rush things.

There will be no consensus.
items.intent: Lovingly mock the tendency to turn minor choices into exhausting mental marathons.

items.prompt: Write a bumper sticker about remote work making every room feel like an office
items.response: I USED TO HAVE A HOME. NOW I HAVE A WORKFLOW.
items.intent: Express how remote work colonizes domestic space and life.

items.prompt: Give me roadside copy for a locksmith who only makes keys that open nothing
items.response:
YOUR KEY IS READY.
BRINKLEY'S CUSTOM KEY STUDIO
Finely Cut. Precisely Useless.
Decorative Keys For Undiscovered Doors
Call Ahead. We're Very Busy For Some Reason.
items.intent: Market a locksmith whose product is artisanal but non-functional, serving no security or access purpose.

items.prompt: Write a yard sign for our neighborhood's opposition to a proposed Airbnb rental next door
items.response:
THIS IS A NEIGHBORHOOD
NOT A HOTEL DISTRICT

No Short-Term Rentals
on Elmwood Court

Meeting: Thurs. 7pm • 14 Elmwood Ct.
items.intent: Organize resistance against a neighbor converting their home into a short-term rental property.

items.prompt: Write a formal complaint letter to a city council about a park bench that faces the wrong direction
items.response:
To the Parks and Recreation Committee,

I write regarding the bench installed last spring in Meridian Park, near the east fountain. The bench faces the maintenance shed. The maintenance shed is unremarkable. Meanwhile, directly behind the bench, at an angle of approximately 180 degrees, lies one of the most pleasant views in the park: the pond, the willows, and the small bridge. I ask only that the bench be rotated. The view is there. The bench merely refuses to acknowledge it.

Respectfully,
A Citizen
items.intent: Petition for a park bench to be turned around so it faces the scenic view it is currently ignoring.

items.prompt: write an apology note for accidentally liking a photo from 2016 while stalking someone's profile
items.response:
Hey Rowan,

I want to address the like on your 2016 Tenerife post. I unliked it within two seconds but I know that doesn't undo the notification. There's no good explanation. I'm sorry.

— Imogen
items.intent: Confess to deep-scrolling someone's social media history and leaving accidental evidence.

items.prompt: write a diary entry about accidentally sending a text to the wrong person
items.response:
Dear Diary,

I sent it to Greg. It was not for Greg. It was a message about Greg, written with the candor one uses when Greg is not the recipient. He replied: 'lol.' I have not responded. I may never respond. Greg and I now share a truth neither of us asked for.
items.intent: Depict the horror and strange intimacy of a misdirected text message.

items.prompt: write a captain's log about a surprise desk visit from an executive
items.response: Stardate 1855.0. At 1030 the Vice Admiral appeared at my station unannounced. She asked how things were going. I said: good, staying busy. She nodded. She asked what I was working on. I explained. Her eyes tracked toward the door. She said "keep it up" and departed. Total contact: ninety seconds. I have spent the subsequent hour wondering what she meant by "keep it up."
items.intent: Capture the anxiety-inducing ambiguity of a brief, unscheduled visit from senior leadership.

items.prompt: Create a 911 transcript where the caller reports a missing sock
items.response:
DISPATCHER: 911, what is your emergency?
CALLER: I lost a sock.
DISPATCHER: I'm sorry?
CALLER: Argyle. Navy and burgundy. Went into the dryer, did not come out.
DISPATCHER: Ma'am, that is not—
CALLER: It was from a set. The set is now ruined.
DISPATCHER: Have you checked inside other clothes?
CALLER: I've been checking for three weeks. It's gone. I need a case number.
items.intent: Channel the genuine despair of laundry loss into a formal missing-item report through emergency services.

items.prompt: Write me a courtroom summation about a case where someone kept putting empty containers back in the fridge
items.response: Exhibit A: one container of yogurt, photographed in situ. Contents: zero grams. The lid was replaced. The container was returned to the shelf. Some trace of yogurt remained along the inner wall — enough to suggest it was once full, not enough to constitute a serving. This is the defendant's system. It is not a system. We ask for its discontinuation.
items.intent: Establish that returning empty containers to the fridge is a form of domestic fraud.

items.prompt: Write an eviction notice for the noise in my head after a difficult meeting
items.response:
NOTICE TO VACATE

To: The Replay
Unit: My post-meeting mental theater

You have screened the same 90-second clip seventeen times since 4 p.m. The lighting is unflattering. The interpretation is contested. The rest of the brain needs this room for sleep. You have until midnight to cease operations. The theater will be dark thereafter.

— The Night Shift
items.intent: Trying to quiet the mental loop that replays an uncomfortable professional moment.

items.prompt: Create a collections letter for years of emotional availability that went unrecompensed.
items.response:
EMOTIONAL AVAILABILITY — ACCOUNT REVIEW

Dear Long-Term Client,

An audit of our relationship spanning nine years reveals that this office maintained consistent, responsive emotional availability: answered calls during work hours, held space during crises, and provided comfort in three distinct time zones while traveling.

A reciprocal availability was assumed but never materialized. When the undersigned experienced difficulty, the account was found to be empty.

We do not require interest. We require presence.

Hopelessly clearly,
The Emotional Ledger Office
items.intent: Name the exhaustion of being the emotionally available one in a relationship that never offered the same in return.

items.prompt: Write a cinquain about the sound of a mechanical keyboard in an open office
items.response:
Clack
Rhythmic, assertive
Announcing every thought
Twelve desks away, someone hates
You deeply
items.intent: Skewer the social friction caused by loud keyboard typing in shared workspaces.

items.prompt: write a pantoum about the onboarding checklist that gets recycled for every new hire
items.response:
Welcome to the team, here is your checklist.
Item one: complete your I-9 by Friday.
Every name that filled this form before you is gone.
The pdf is dated three years ago.

Item one: complete your I-9 by Friday.
IT will set up your accounts in two to three days.
The pdf is dated three years ago.
Some items on the list refer to a system we no longer use.

IT will set up your accounts in two to three days.
You will be assigned a buddy during your first week.
Some items on the list refer to a system we no longer use.
The buddy program ended in the reorganization.

You will be assigned a buddy during your first week.
Welcome to the team, here is your checklist.
The buddy program ended in the reorganization.
Every name that filled this form before you is gone.
items.intent: Critique the gap between onboarding documentation and organizational reality.

items.prompt: Can you write liner notes for an album about train schedules and delays
items.response:
Revised Estimated Arrival (2022)

We wrote this album in the intervals between what was promised and what occurred. The time signature changes on track five reflect a service disruption for which we apologize. Signal failure has never sounded like this. We did not plan it that way either.
items.intent: Transform the chronic disappointment of public transit delays into a structured musical narrative.

items.prompt: I want a description for a podcast ep about the feeling of stepping outside and realizing the weather is completely different from what you expected
items.response: The coat was a mistake. The coat is always a mistake, but today in a new direction. Episode 31 is about the betrayal of forecasts, the failure of intuition, and a jacket left on a chair.
items.intent: Frame meteorological disappointment as a recurring existential pattern.

items.prompt: Make a YouTube comment for a video of a very small boat navigating a very large harbor
items.response: There is a courage specific to small things moving through enormous systems and I think about it constantly.
items.intent: Use a visual of scale to articulate something about navigating overwhelming social or institutional structures.

items.prompt: Create a subtitle for a therapist's face during a session with an unusually difficult client
items.response: [EXPRESSION: REGULATED] [NEUTRAL CURIOSITY: MAINTAINED] [SUPERVISION APPOINTMENT: MENTAL NOTE]
items.intent: Annotate the professional labor of emotional management in a helping profession.

items.prompt: Write an art statement about a half-empty tube of toothpaste
items.response: The tube negotiates between fullness and end. I roll it from the bottom in small daily economies, extracting the last from the reluctant container. This is not hygiene — it is the rehearsal of scarcity.
items.intent: Use a nearly depleted toiletry to meditate on resource anxiety and daily ritual.

items.prompt: Headnote for a simple apple cake that obsesses over autumn in an embarrassing way
items.response: I am aware that autumn enthusiasm is now considered a personality defect. I am aware of the jokes. I make this cake from the first week of October through Thanksgiving and I feel no remorse. The apples are good, the spices are correct, and if the light through the kitchen window in October is doing something unreasonable, I am not responsible for that.
items.intent: Defend unabashed seasonal sentimentality while presenting a cozy autumn dessert

items.prompt: Can you make a tasting menu for déjà vu?
items.response:
AMUSE-BOUCHE
This, before

FIRST COURSE
The feeling that the menu has already been read

SECOND COURSE
A face — familiar, sourceless

INTERMEZZO
Time, briefly doubled

MAIN
The moment it slips — full evaporation

DESSERT
Certainty you cannot support
items.intent: Render the unsettling flicker of déjà vu through the very form experiencing it.

items.prompt: write a man page for the experience of feeling proud of yourself
items.response:
PRIDE(7)                 Human Affective States            PRIDE(7)

NAME
       pride - self-directed achievement validation signal

SYNOPSIS
       pride [--source achievement] [--audience self|external]

DESCRIPTION
       pride(7) is a positive self-referential signal emitted when the
       host's output is evaluated against internal standards and found
       to exceed them. Unlike external praise, pride(7) originates from
       the host's own evaluation process. This makes it more durable.

OPTIONS
       --source    Triggering achievement. May be large or small.
                   Finishing a hard thing counts. Getting out of bed counts.
       --audience  self: quiet, sustainable signal.
                   external: requires refresh. Not a stable source.

DISTINCTION FROM ARROGANCE
       pride(7) is self-referential and proportional.
       arrogance(7) compares self to others and overclaims.
       These are not the same binary. One is healthy.

SEE ALSO
       self-worth(7), arrogance(7), shame(7), accomplishment(1)

Human Affective States 1.5   2026                    PRIDE(7)
items.intent: Distinguish healthy self-recognition from ego and affirm its value.

items.prompt: draft a 404 for a site about wonder — for when someone can't find their curiosity anymore
items.response:
404 — Curiosity Not Found

Odd. It was just here.

You used to click on everything. Strange links, dead ends, pages three levels deep in a Wikipedia spiral.

The internet hasn't changed. Maybe look up from the feed for a second.
items.intent: Mourn the loss of intellectual curiosity in the age of passive content consumption via a 404 page.

items.prompt: Write alt text for a photo of a Wi-Fi router with all its lights blinking.
items.response: A small black router sits on a bookshelf, its lights cycling amber and green in overlapping rhythms, transmitting its invisible insistence into every corner of a room where no one is currently online.
items.intent: Render the invisible infrastructure of connection as quietly absurd when no one benefits from it.

items.prompt: Draft a deprecation notice for the friendship I had with someone who always made everything about themselves.
items.response:
DEPRECATION NOTICE

Relationship: [Contact] — One-Directional Listening Contract
Status: DEPRECATED

This connection featured asymmetric emotional labor with persistent I/O imbalance: high outbound, near-zero inbound. Despite multiple informal patches, core behavior remained unchanged. End of support issued without regret.
items.intent: Let go of a draining friendship where one person consistently dominated every conversation.

items.prompt: Write a brief layoff email for a car manufacturer closing an assembly plant after shifting to EV-only production elsewhere
items.response:
Subject: Cascade City Plant — Future Operations

To Our Cascade City Team,

Following our accelerated transition to all-electric vehicle production, we have reached the difficult decision to cease operations at the Cascade City Assembly Plant by end of Q2. This decision affects approximately 1,800 positions.

We are working with our union partners to honor all obligations under our collective agreement, and we will be opening a dedicated transition center on-site beginning next month.

Your craftsmanship has powered this company for 31 years. We do not take this lightly.

With sincere respect,
Chief Manufacturing Officer
items.intent: Close a legacy plant of nearly two thousand workers and frame EV progress as the reason, softened by nostalgia for the plant's history.

items.prompt: Write a company policy about the correct way to load the office dishwasher
items.response: Section 4.12 — Dishwasher Loading Compliance: All items placed in the communal dishwasher must face inward toward the spray arm in accordance with the Vessel Orientation Diagram posted inside the appliance door. Items found facing outward following a completed cycle will be returned to the responsible party's desk, unwashed, with an attached Remediation Notice.
items.intent: Take a household chore argument and formalize it into a multi-step corrective action process complete with documentary evidence.

items.prompt: Write a PIP for my tendency to over-explain myself in every conversation like I'm always on trial
items.response:
PERFORMANCE IMPROVEMENT PLAN
Area: Verbal Efficiency & Self-Defense Reduction

Deficiency: Subject appends extensive justifications to ordinary statements, decisions, and preferences. Asking subject for a restaurant recommendation produces a 4-minute pre-apology. Declining a social event requires a minimum of three reasons, none solicited.

Root Cause: Anticipatory defense against assumed judgment. Subject treats all social interaction as a proceeding in which their choices require explanation to a jury that has not convened.

Objective: State preferences and decisions directly. Elaborate only when asked.

Plan:
1. Practice the short answer. Say it. Then stop.
2. Note when you are about to add clause number three. Ask: was clause two enough?
3. Reminder: no one is taking notes.

Milestone: One week of answering direct questions with one sentence, without follow-up clarification.

Note: Most people forget what you said before you finished saying it.
items.intent: Reduce compulsive over-justification rooted in anxiety about others' judgment.

items.prompt: Draft a holiday newsletter that explains our kid was held back a grade without making it the main story
items.response:
Happy holidays from the Weston family!

Liam is thriving in 4th grade — a grade he is committed to mastering fully this time around, with enthusiasm and better organizational systems. His teacher is wonderful. He's made new friends. We've all learned that success has its own timeline. In other news, Mia made the honor roll and Dad ran a 5K. Love to all!
items.intent: Inform extended family that a child was held back a grade without making it sound devastating.

items.prompt: write a yearbook quote that sounds motivational but is actually about quitting something
items.response: "Sometimes knowing when to stop is the whole skill. Goodbye, varsity swim."
items.intent: Framing a dropout decision as wisdom rather than giving up.

items.prompt: Draft a wedding invitation for a bookshelf and all the books that haven't been read
items.response:
THE BOOKSHELF
&
THE UNREAD

are pleased to announce their committed, long-term relationship.

Venue: Your living room wall
Date: Ongoing, since the last sale

The shelf asks nothing of the books.
The books ask nothing of the shelf.
Both are content with the arrangement.

New additions always welcome.
items.intent: Celebrate the guilt-free accumulation of books one intends to read but never quite does.

items.prompt: Bumper sticker about the anxiety of choosing from too many options
items.response: PARALYZED BY INFINITE POSSIBILITY SINCE 2009
items.intent: Satirize how abundance of choice produces a peculiarly modern inaction.

items.prompt: Draft a billboard for a tailor who exclusively makes suits two sizes too small
items.response:
YOU'LL GROW INTO IT.
BELGRAVE BESPOKE TAILORING
Hand-Stitched. Hand-Sized. Confidently Wrong.
"The Fit Is Part Of The Journey."
By Appointment. No Refunds. We Warned You.
items.intent: Advertise custom clothing made to a sizing standard that ensures the garment will never fit the wearer.

items.prompt: Give me a yard sign about how the HOA flagged my lawn for being 'too brown' during a drought
items.response:
IT'S CALLED A DROUGHT

Water restrictions are in effect.
Brown lawns are the law.

Fine us. We'll see you in court.
items.intent: Protest an HOA fine for a brown lawn during legally mandated water restrictions.

items.prompt: Write a complaint to a candle company about a scent named something completely unrelated to how it smells
items.response:
Dear Customer Experience Team,

I purchased your candle labeled 'Mountain Morning' in good faith. It smells like a dentist's office. I do not mean this as a compliment. I have spent considerable time in the mountains; they do not smell like this. I understand that candle naming is an art with significant creative latitude, but 'Mountain Morning' sets an expectation that 'clinical mint with an undertone of antiseptic' does not meet. I would appreciate either a reformulation or a renaming.

Sincerely,
A Customer
items.intent: Challenge a candle company's misleading product name that bears no sensory relationship to the actual scent.

items.prompt: draft an apology for insisting on splitting a restaurant bill to the exact cent when everyone just wanted to split it evenly
items.response:
Gang,

I'm sorry about the calculator at dinner. You all just wanted to divide by five and move on, and I itemized everything including the bread. It took eleven minutes. I will let it go next time.

— Harriet
items.intent: Apologize for over-engineering a group bill split and wasting everyone's time with excessive precision.

items.prompt: make a diary entry about reading a very long terms and conditions document
items.response:
Dear Diary,

I read it. I actually read it. Eleven pages of rights I am waiving and data I am providing and arbitration I am agreeing to. At the end I clicked Accept, because what was I going to do? Not click Accept? I clicked Accept.
items.intent: Express the helpless capitulation of digital consent rituals.

items.prompt: write a captain's log about a colleague who cc's everyone on every single email
items.response: Stardate 6309.4. Another transmission from Lieutenant Fenn arrived, addressed to — by my count — twenty-three crew members, including the division head and two people in a separate timezone who have never been in the same meeting. The message concerned a formatting update to the internal wiki. It required no response. It received nine responses. I was cc'd on all of them. I have set up a filter. The filter does not help. She cc's the filter.
items.intent: Satirize the email overload created by colleagues who compulsively over-copy recipients.

items.prompt: Write a 911 call transcript about someone who calls to complain that a dog is wearing a Halloween costume and it's not Halloween
items.response:
DISPATCHER: 911, what's your emergency?
CALLER: There's a golden retriever on Cedar Avenue in a shark costume.
DISPATCHER: Is the dog in distress?
CALLER: Possibly. I can't tell under the costume.
DISPATCHER: Is the owner present?
CALLER: She's laughing at it. Taking photos.
DISPATCHER: The dog being dressed up isn't—
CALLER: It's May.
items.intent: Express seasonal propriety outrage by treating an off-holiday costume as an urgent disorder requiring response.

items.prompt: Draft a closing argument for a case about a neighbor who lets their dog bark continuously without intervening
items.response: The dog began at 7 a.m. My client knows this because they were awake. They were awake because the dog had already been barking. The defendant's property line ends at the fence. The sound does not. We ask the court to recognize that pet ownership includes, as a core obligation, management of the pet's impact on persons who did not choose to adopt it.
items.intent: Argue that a neighbor's passive acceptance of their barking dog constitutes a nuisance.

items.prompt: Write an eviction notice for entropy — the tendency of things to fall apart
items.response:
NOTICE TO QUIT

To: Entropy
Property: Everything, slowly

You have operated on this property under the assumption that degradation is your prerogative. Corners have been allowed to soften. Paint has been permitted to peel. Drawer contents have achieved autonomy. While your claim is thermodynamically valid, it is not welcome here. You will be contested daily, by maintenance, intention, and small acts of order.

— The Tenant Who Pushes Back
items.intent: Committing to regular upkeep and the conscious resistance of gradual decay.

items.prompt: Write a collections letter for a word that was taken out of context and weaponized.
items.response:
RE: UNAUTHORIZED USE OF QUOTED MATERIAL

Dear Misquoter,

On the evening in question, the undersigned said "sometimes I think about quitting" in the context of a difficult Wednesday. That statement has since been repeated in three conversations as evidence of instability, forwarded to two mutual parties, and referenced in an argument about something entirely unrelated.

This constitutes misappropriation of spoken language. We request a correction of the record and cessation of further circulation.

Precisely,
The Office of Contextual Integrity
items.intent: Address the harm caused by someone who took a private, vulnerable statement and used it against the speaker.

items.prompt: Give me a cinquain about a photo taken and immediately deleted
items.response:
Frame
Lifted, composed
A moment held in glass
Not quite right — bin — gone before
It lived
items.intent: Reflect on perfectionism and the disposability of digital images.

items.prompt: write a pantoum about maintaining a digital to-do list that never gets shorter

I add the task; I do not do the task.
The green circle waits to be checked.
The list is longer than when the week began.
I reprioritize and feel productive.

The green circle waits to be checked.
Some items have waited since November.
I reprioritize and feel productive.
This feeling lasts the length of a coffee.

Some items have waited since November.
I archive them into a someday bucket.
This feeling lasts the length of a coffee.
I add the task; I do not do the task.

I archive them into a someday bucket.
The list is longer than when the week began.
I add the task; I do not do the task.
The green circle waits to be checked.
