items.prompt: Write liner notes for a blues album about reading owner's manuals
items.response:
Warranty Void (2016)

Page 47 is the most important page. We have never read page 47. This album was recorded by four people who have owned things their whole lives and understood none of them. The slide work on "Do Not Submerge" is our finest moment. We have no idea how it works.
items.intent: Apply blues music's ethos of hard-won ignorance to the relatable failure to ever read owner's manuals.

items.prompt: Write a podcast episode blurb about the topic of a drawer that only has things you'll never use but also can't throw away
items.response: The manual for a device that no longer exists. Three batteries of unknown charge. A key. This episode is about the drawer—specifically, the covenant we have with it—and why it must not be touched.
items.intent: Explore the emotional logic behind retaining completely useless objects.

items.prompt: Write a YouTube comment for a video of someone finishing a 1000-piece puzzle alone at night
items.response: She reached for the last piece like she already knew it would be missing. But it wasn't. She was not prepared for that.
items.intent: Describe the strange emotional unpreparedness of a victory when one has already resigned to defeat.

items.prompt: Give me a closed caption for a musician playing the same wrong note in the same measure during rehearsal for the third time
items.response: [MEASURE 47] [AGAIN] [CONDUCTOR'S BATON LOWERING SLOWLY]
items.intent: Compress the accumulation of repetitive failure into a single dread-loaded beat.

items.prompt: Give me an artist statement about a shopping cart left in a parking lot
items.response: Unhitched from retail purpose, it drifts. The solo cart is evidence of a freedom most objects never achieve — to move, after use, without direction. I call this abandon a form of grace.
items.intent: Find philosophical liberation in a stray shopping cart's post-use wandering.

items.prompt: Write a headnote for pickled vegetables where the narrator got very into fermentation during a quarter-life crisis
items.response: I was twenty-six and dissatisfied in ways I couldn't precisely name, and I started fermenting things. Vegetables, mostly. There is something deeply steadying about a process that takes weeks, that you cannot rush, that works whether or not you are paying attention. The crisis passed. The pickling did not. I now have opinions about brine ratios that I share at the wrong moments.
items.intent: Trace how a methodical hobby became a lasting identity after surviving an aimless period

items.prompt: Give me a tasting menu for boredom
items.response:
AMUSE-BOUCHE
Nothing, offered

FIRST COURSE
The wall — surveyed at length

SECOND COURSE
The same thought, again

INTERMEZZO
A sound in the building, investigated

MAIN
Time — stretched, elastic, enormous

DESSERT
An idea, small and unasked for
items.intent: Reframe boredom not as nothing but as a kind of generative space.

items.prompt: can you generate a man page for the act of forgiving someone
items.response:
FORGIVE(1)               Interpersonal Utilities         FORGIVE(1)

NAME
       forgive - release outstanding fault attribution for prior event

SYNOPSIS
       forgive [--requester person] [--event string] [--self bool]

DESCRIPTION
       forgive(1) closes the fault record for a named event and releases
       the host from ongoing maintenance of resentment(7). Does not
       require the offending party's participation. Does not necessarily
       restore trust(7) or resume relationship(7).

COMMON MISCONCEPTIONS
       forgive(1) is not the same as condone(1).
       forgive(1) does not require an apology from REQUESTER.
       forgive(1) is primarily for the benefit of the host, not REQUESTER.

OPTIONS
       --self   If true, host is both victim and requester. Hardest case.

EXIT STATUS
       0      Resentment log cleared. Resources freed.
       1      Forgiveness stated but record not actually cleared. Retry.

NOTES
       forgive(1) may require multiple invocations for the same event.

SEE ALSO
       resentment(7), apologize(1), trust(7), grief(7)

Interpersonal Utilities 2.3  2026                FORGIVE(1)
items.intent: Clarify that forgiveness is an internal release, not an external transaction.

items.prompt: write a 404 page for a legal resource site where someone's looking for justice
items.response:
404 — Justice Not Found

The resource you requested does not exist at this location.

It may exist elsewhere. It may require a different kind of request. It may be delayed.

We recommend persistence. Historically, that has helped.
items.intent: Describe the inaccessibility of justice systems through a dry bureaucratic error page.

items.prompt: Alt text for an image of a hospital waiting room with one plant that is thriving.
items.response: A sparse waiting room with vinyl chairs and outdated magazines, the room's sole occupant appearing genuinely well: a pothos in the corner, impossibly green, utterly indifferent to the occasion.
items.intent: Let a healthy plant function as a deadpan counterpoint to the anxiety a waiting room implies.

items.prompt: Write a deprecation notice for the ability to stay out past midnight without consequence.
items.response:
DEPRECATION NOTICE

Feature: Post-Midnight Recovery (v18–32, variable)
Status: DEPRECATED

This feature allowed late-night activity to be absorbed without measurable daytime cost. It has been phased out in the current release. Users may still stay out past midnight; however, the following two days are now considered collateral.
items.intent: Humorously accept the cost of late nights that increases with age.

items.prompt: Write a layoff announcement email for a university eliminating its humanities department due to budget pressures
items.response:
Subject: Curricular and Faculty Update — College of Arts and Humanities

Dear Campus Community,

Following an extensive financial review, the University has made the regrettable decision to discontinue several programs within the College of Arts and Humanities and to eliminate the associated faculty and staff lines.

These decisions were not made lightly and reflect the fiscal realities of the current higher education environment, not the value of humanistic inquiry.

Affected faculty and staff will receive individual notices with details on timelines and support resources.

In service,
Office of the Provost
items.intent: Announce the elimination of humanities faculty while insisting the university still respects the value of humanistic inquiry.

items.prompt: Create an official rule about employees who always wait for someone else to refill the paper shredder
items.response: Section 8.9 — Document Destruction Equipment Stewardship: Any employee who observes a paper shredder at or below ten percent (10%) bag capacity assumes shared responsibility for bag replacement. Employees who photograph a full shredder and send the image to Facilities rather than replacing the bag themselves are in violation of the Bystander Equipment Clause.
items.intent: Forbid the specific passive-aggressive behavior of outsourcing a trivial task through documentation instead of just doing it.

items.prompt: Write a PIP for how I handle conflict — I go completely silent for days
items.response:
PERFORMANCE IMPROVEMENT PLAN
Focus: Interpersonal Conflict Response Protocol
Issued By: Relationships Department

Deficiency: Upon experiencing interpersonal tension or perceived slight, subject withdraws entirely from communication with the other party for 2–5 days without explanation. Silence is neither acknowledged nor explained. Other party is left to deduce cause, severity, and desired resolution.

Impact: Conflict duration extended by 3–5x; resolution delayed; other party frequently confused about what occurred; subject's internal monologue intensifies in absence of external input.

Required Standard: Conflict acknowledged within 24 hours. Communication need not be resolution; acknowledgment of tension is sufficient.

Plan:
- Draft a message stating: "I need some time to think about this — can we talk Thursday?" This counts as communication.
- Identify whether silence is protective (valid) or punitive (addressed here).

Milestone: Two conflicts addressed verbally within the week they occur.

Review: 60 days.
items.intent: Replace stonewalling and silent treatment with minimum viable conflict communication.

items.prompt: Write a holiday letter from a family where one parent is newly unemployed and job hunting but staying positive
items.response:
Warmth and wishes from the Nguyens!

Lena is in a season of professional reinvention — polishing her resume, broadening her network, and discovering just how many hours exist in a weekday. She remains optimistic, slightly overqualified, and very available for coffee. The family is rallying. Tommy's salary is load-bearing right now. We are fine. We are a team. Happy holidays.
items.intent: Show the strain of one partner carrying the financial load during the other's job search.

items.prompt: make a senior quote that's a thinly veiled threat to an ex who goes to the same school
items.response: "To the person who thought I wouldn't finish what I started: I graduated first."
items.intent: Using a graduation milestone as a competitive jab aimed at a specific unnamed person.

items.prompt: Write an invitation for the wedding of a forgotten password and absolute certainty
items.response:
ABSOLUTE CERTAINTY
('I definitely remember this one')
&
A FORGOTTEN PASSWORD

announce their nuptials.

Date: The moment you need to log in.
Venue: The login screen.

Three attempts remain.
The ceremony ends in a password reset.
The couple will meet again next month, unchanged.
items.intent: Capture the specific frustration of being completely wrong about remembering a password.

items.prompt: Make a bumper sticker about eco-guilt from flying
items.response: I OFFSET THE FLIGHT. THE PLANET REMAINS UNIMPRESSED.
items.intent: Skewer the insufficient comfort of carbon offset rituals.

items.prompt: Write a billboard for a pool installation company that only installs pools in living rooms
items.response:
TAKE THE PLUNGE INDOORS.
SPLASHCRAFT INTERIOR AQUATICS
Custom Pools. Standard Doorways. Creative Solutions.
"Your Hardwood Will Adapt."
Free Consultation. Waterproofing Not Included.
items.intent: Market a home improvement service that installs amenities in a context that destroys the surrounding structure.

items.prompt: Make a yard sign about the petition to keep the neighborhood speed limit at 25 mph
items.response:
25 MPH
KEEP IT THAT WAY

The city wants 35.
We want our kids back outside.

Sign: KeepOurStreetsSafe.org
items.intent: Fight a proposed speed limit increase on a residential road to protect pedestrian safety.

items.prompt: Help me write a complaint to my landlord about the radiator that only makes noise when I'm on an important call
items.response:
Dear Property Manager,

I write to formally document a pattern of behavior exhibited by the radiator in my unit. The device is largely silent throughout the day. It activates, reliably, the moment I begin a professional phone call or video meeting. The clanging is vigorous, sustained, and impossible to talk over. I have now muted myself to explain its origins to several colleagues and one prospective employer. I request inspection and, if possible, some form of scheduling protocol.

Yours,
Tenant, Unit [X]
items.intent: Report a radiator whose noise pattern appears timed specifically to disrupt professional phone calls.

items.prompt: write a note apologizing for setting a 6am alarm at a sleepover and not hearing it yourself
items.response:
Zoe, Priya, and Cate,

I am sorry about the alarm. I set it for myself and slept through it. You did not. I understand it went off for four minutes. I had a wonderful night and I'm sorry the morning ended the way it did.

— Becs
items.intent: Apologize for waking up several sleeping guests with an alarm that the alarm-setter managed to completely ignore.

items.prompt: diary entry about getting a haircut that's slightly too short
items.response:
Dear Diary,

She asked how much and I said 'a little.' We had different definitions of little. I sat under the cape watching inches fall to the tile and said nothing because I am conflict-avoidant and it was already happening. It grows back. I keep saying that. It grows back.
items.intent: Process the mild grief of a haircut gone slightly wrong due to non-confrontation.

items.prompt: write a captain's log about trying to find a document that was "shared with you"
items.response: Stardate 3944.8. I was told the brief had been sent. I searched the inbox. I searched Shared With Me. I searched Recent. I searched the archived drives and two team folders from a prior organizational structure. After thirty-one minutes I replied: "Could you resend?" It was in a folder called FINAL_v3_USE_THIS. There were also folders called FINAL, FINAL_v2, and FINAL_FINAL. I have said nothing about this.
items.intent: Vent the frustration of searching for a mislabeled or poorly shared file.

items.prompt: Make a 911 call transcript where someone reports a neighbor doing loud DIY projects at 9am on a Saturday
items.response:
DISPATCHER: 911, what's your emergency?
CALLER: My neighbor is drilling. It's nine o'clock.
DISPATCHER: Nine in the morning is generally within permitted hours, sir.
CALLER: On a Saturday.
DISPATCHER: Still within—
CALLER: I had a hard week.
DISPATCHER: I understand, but—
CALLER: The week was very hard.
DISPATCHER: I'm sorry about your week.
CALLER: Thank you. So can you make him stop?
items.intent: Use personal exhaustion as justification for suspending a neighbor's legal right to make noise on weekday mornings.

items.prompt: Write a summation for a plaintiff whose housemate leaves all the lights on in every room
items.response: The defendant illuminates rooms they have left. Rooms they have not entered in hours. The hallway: lit. The bathroom: lit. The kitchen, where no one stands: lit like a stage awaiting a performance that will not come. My client turns them off. The defendant turns them on again. We seek a court-mandated motion sensor policy and an energy bill credit.
items.intent: Turn habitual light-leaving into a formal energy and cohabitation dispute.

items.prompt: Draft an eviction notice for the grief that moved in after something ended
items.response:
NOTICE TO VACATE

To: The Grief
Unit: Most of this apartment, all of the music

You arrived as an appropriate short-term occupant and have since amended the lease without consent. You have redecorated in grey and claimed rights to the good chair. The management acknowledges your initial right to be here. However, the renewal clause has not been signed. A new arrangement — one with room for other things — begins at the end of this month.

— Whoever Lives Here Now
items.intent: Acknowledging that a period of mourning has served its purpose and deciding to make space for recovery.

items.prompt: Draft a collections letter for loyalty that was given but not reciprocated.
items.response:
LOYALTY ACCOUNT — DELINQUENT

To Whom It Concerns,

This office maintained a sustained and unconditional loyalty account on your behalf. This included: defending your reputation in your absence, declining better opportunities to remain available to you, and maintaining confidentiality on matters that would have been easier to disclose.

When the undersigned required equivalent loyalty, the account was found to hold nothing.

We are formally withdrawing. The account is closed with prejudice.

Formerly yours,
The Fidelity Bureau
items.intent: Formally mark the end of a one-sided loyalty arrangement where sacrifice was given and not matched.

items.prompt: Compose a cinquain about the uncanny valley in a CGI face
items.response:
Smooth
Almost a face
Eyes that move without a soul
Something behind the skin is wrong
I look away
items.intent: Articulate the visceral wrongness of near-perfect synthetic human faces.

items.prompt: pantoum about the team channel that pings you for things that could have been emails
items.response:
A new message in the general channel.
Can everyone take thirty seconds to review this?
I mute the tab and unmute it later.
The thread now has forty-seven replies.

Can everyone take thirty seconds to review this?
An emoji poll has been added to the message.
The thread now has forty-seven replies.
I search for my name and find two mentions.

An emoji poll has been added to the message.
The vote stands at seventeen thumbs up.
I search for my name and find two mentions.
A new message in the general channel.

The vote stands at seventeen thumbs up.
I mute the tab and unmute it later.
A new message in the general channel.
Can everyone take thirty seconds to review this?
items.intent: Satirize the attention-fragmenting culture of always-on team chat platforms.

items.prompt: Give me liner notes for a concept album inspired by the experience of untangling cables
items.response:
The Knot (2021)

Side A documents the discovery. Side B has not been resolved. This record was mastered using cables that were, briefly, in order. They are not in order now. The engineer says this is fine. The engineer has not seen the back of our rack.
items.intent: Make a metaphor of cable chaos for the frustrating persistence of disorder despite effort.

items.prompt: Write an episode description about a podcast ep on the particular quality of a Sunday afternoon when nothing is planned
items.response: Not relaxing. Not restless. Just long, and slightly amber-colored. This episode airs on a Sunday, ideally between 2 and 4pm, which is when it was made and when it is most accurate.
items.intent: Articulate the peculiar texture of unstructured weekend time.

items.prompt: Write a YouTube comment for a drone footage video of a cornfield in the midwest at golden hour
items.response: I grew up four blocks from the ocean and have never stood in a field in my life. This made me homesick for somewhere I have never been.
items.intent: Describe the phenomenon of nostalgia for a place one has no personal history with.

items.prompt: Write a closed caption for someone noticing their elderly parent has shrunk
items.response: [HUG DURATION EXTENDED] [NEW INFORMATION FILED QUIETLY]
items.intent: Mark the moment a child perceives their parent's physical diminishment for the first time.

items.prompt: Artist statement for a single forgotten glove on a park bench
items.response: The glove is half a system rendered suddenly singular. It does not grieve its pair — it simply waits, maintaining the shape of the hand that wore it, holding that phantom warmth as long as the cold allows.
items.intent: Use a lone lost glove to explore absence, incompleteness, and residual human presence.

items.prompt: Give me a melodramatic headnote for focaccia about bread-baking during a period of unemployment
items.response: I had seven months in which no one needed anything from me professionally and I made focaccia twice a week. There is a cruelty to having nothing but time, and a strange grace in it. The bread takes hours if you do it right and I had hours in surplus. I have never made better focaccia than I made that year. I have never been less able to enjoy it.
items.intent: Explore the irony of peak baking performance coinciding with personal low point

items.prompt: Write a tasting menu for reading a great novel
items.response:
AMUSE-BOUCHE
First sentence — orienting

FIRST COURSE
World, entered carefully

SECOND COURSE
Character, trusted

INTERMEZZO
Something underlined; won't say why

MAIN
The middle — hours pass without hunger

DESSERT
The last page. Closed slowly. Not quite ready to leave.
items.intent: Describe the absorbing intimacy of losing oneself in a book.

items.prompt: write a man page for the act of saying goodbye
items.response:
GOODBYE(1)               Interpersonal Utilities         GOODBYE(1)

NAME
       goodbye - session termination with relational metadata

SYNOPSIS
       goodbye [--type casual|permanent|unknown] [--party RECIPIENT]

DESCRIPTION
       goodbye(1) formally closes an interaction or relationship instance.
       Carries optional relational metadata depending on --type. The weight
       of goodbye(1) is typically unknown at execution time.

TYPES
       casual      See you next week. Lightweight. No special handling.
       permanent   Both parties aware. High overhead. See: grief(7).
       unknown     Parties unaware this is the last instance. Most common.
                   Metadata assigned retrospectively.

OPTIONS
       --party     RECIPIENT of goodbye. May also be a version of self.

BUGS
       unknown mode means the most important goodbyes often receive
       inadequate ceremony. No patch exists.

NOTES
       The word itself is a contraction: "God be with ye." Weight
       was built in from the start.

SEE ALSO
       grief(7), hello(1), presence(7), endings(7)

Interpersonal Utilities 1.0  2026                GOODBYE(1)
items.intent: Meditate on how endings are often only recognized as such in retrospect.

items.prompt: write 404 page copy for an app where someone's looking for their original self
items.response:
404 — Original Self Not Found

This version has been modified. The root file may have been overwritten — by school, by jobs, by who you were around at 22.

A backup exists. We think.

Look in the places nobody taught you to look.
items.intent: Use backup and file-corruption metaphors to describe the loss of authentic identity over time.

items.prompt: Write alt text for a satellite image of a city at night, all those lights.
items.response: A grid of orange and white light spreads across a dark continent from above, each point a filament burning because someone decided not to sit in the dark, aggregated into something that can be seen from space.
items.intent: Reframe collective human refusal of darkness as an accidental monument visible only from outside.

items.prompt: Make a deprecation notice for my ability to hold a grudge.
items.response:
DEPRECATION NOTICE

Feature: Long-Term Grudge Retention (personal)
Status: DEPRECATED — under review

This module, which maintained detailed logs of perceived slights going back years, has been found to consume disproportionate memory resources with no measurable benefit to the user. Logs will be flushed during the next maintenance cycle.
items.intent: Consciously decide to release old resentments that have been stored far too long.

items.prompt: Help me write a layoff email for a food delivery startup that's exiting three cities after failing to reach profitability thresholds
items.response:
Subject: Market Coverage Update

Team,

After evaluating the unit economics in each of our markets, we have made the decision to exit Chicago, Denver, and Atlanta. Riders, dispatchers, and local operations staff in those markets will be affected.

We know this is abrupt and we are sorry. Severance will be processed within five business days.

For those in remaining markets: our focus sharpens, not shrinks.

Thank you.
—Operations Team
items.intent: Abandon three entire city operations and their workers by wrapping an exit in language about economic discipline and market focus.

items.prompt: Write a weird policy about employees who correct other people's grammar in work chat
items.response: Section 6.16 — Peer Linguistic Intervention: Unsolicited correction of colleagues' grammar, spelling, or syntax in any written communication channel is classified as a Communicative Boundary Incident. Employees who wish to flag linguistic concerns must route them through the Writing Support Self-Service Portal. Inline corrections using strikethrough formatting are expressly prohibited.
items.intent: Weaponize bureaucracy against grammar-policing behavior by labeling it a formal incident type.

items.prompt: Generate a PIP for being chronically disorganized with important documents
items.response:
PERFORMANCE IMPROVEMENT PLAN
Division: Document Governance
Severity: Moderate Risk

Deficiency: Critical personal documents — including tax records, insurance cards, lease agreements, and medical paperwork — are distributed across: the kitchen drawer (primary), the back seat of a vehicle, email attachments marked unread, and a folder labeled "important stuff" containing primarily menus and expired coupons.

Risk: Subject has, in documented instances, been unable to locate passport 45 minutes before departure and reconstructed medical history from memory during an intake form.

Required Standard: All critical documents accessible within 3 minutes.

Plan:
1. Designate one physical folder and one digital folder, both named specifically.
2. Spend 90 minutes relocating all known documents to these locations.
3. Never put a document "somewhere safe" without it being one of those two places.

Milestone: No document-search crisis in 60-day period.
items.intent: Create a working personal document organization system to prevent crisis-mode document hunting.

items.prompt: Make a newsletter where we mention our kid was diagnosed with something serious but is managing well
items.response:
Holiday greetings from the Abernathy family!

This year came with a new vocabulary: diagnosis, treatment plan, specialist, and — most importantly — resilience. Our Caden received his Type 1 diabetes diagnosis in June and has responded with extraordinary composure. He knows his numbers better than most adults. He still plays soccer. He still raids the snack cabinet strategically. We're proud every single day. Merry Christmas.
items.intent: Share a child's chronic illness diagnosis with extended family in a way that emphasizes capability over hardship.

items.prompt: write a yearbook quote that implies I knew about something big before anyone else did
items.response: "I tried to tell you in March. You'll find the group chat. The timestamps don't lie."
items.intent: Claiming prophetic foresight about an unspecified event while pointing to buried evidence.

items.prompt: Write a wedding invitation for a mirror and vanity
items.response:
VANITY
&
THE MIRROR

request your presence.

Venue: Any well-lit bathroom
Time: Too long before you need to leave

The couple has been together since the first reflective surface.
They ask for nothing but your attention.

Guests are encouraged to look their best.
This is, after all, a love that requires an audience.
items.intent: Wryly comment on self-obsession and the performative nature of self-image.

items.prompt: Give me a bumper sticker about the feeling that news cycles now move faster than understanding
items.response: FORMING AN OPINION BEFORE THE FACTS HAVE LOADED
items.intent: Capture the cognitive whiplash of news moving faster than comprehension.

items.prompt: Write me copy for an ad for a school that only teaches courses that are no longer relevant
items.response:
INVEST IN YOUR FUTURE.
COLUMBIA INSTITUTE OF DISCONTINUED SKILLS
Curriculum: Fax Etiquette. VCR Repair. Cursive Aggression.
Degrees Conferred. Markets Not Consulted.
Enroll Now. Demand Is Theoretical.
items.intent: Advertise educational courses in fields that have become obsolete, making the credentials earned worthless.

items.prompt: Write a passive-aggressive yard sign about neighbors who don't wave back
items.response:
WAVING IS FREE

Energy expenditure: ~0.003 calories.
Social benefit: immeasurable.

Try it. We'll go first.
items.intent: Gently shame unfriendly neighbors for not returning simple neighborly greetings.

items.prompt: Draft a complaint to a mattress company about a mattress that is somehow too supportive
items.response:
To the Sleep Support Team,

I purchased your Premier Ortho-Firm mattress following your advertising's promise of superior support. The mattress has delivered on this promise completely and without mercy. It supports me so thoroughly that I cannot shift positions. I wake in exactly the posture I assumed when I fell asleep. The experience is less 'sleeping' and more 'being stored.' I request clarification on whether this is the intended function.

Regards,
A Customer
items.intent: Complain that a mattress is so rigidly supportive that normal sleep movement is impossible.

items.prompt: write an apology note for aggressively over-explaining a joke immediately after telling it
items.response:
Liam,

I'm sorry about the joke at dinner. The part where I explained the wordplay, and then the cultural reference, and then described why it was funny — that was all unnecessary and I could see it happening in real time. I'll let things land on their own from now on.

— Sid
items.intent: Make amends for undermining a joke by dissecting it immediately after delivery.

items.prompt: write a diary entry about waking up five minutes before the alarm
items.response:
Dear Diary,

5:55. The alarm: 6:00. I stared at the ceiling and performed the ancient calculation. Five minutes is not sleep. Five minutes is anticipation. I watched the clock. I had chosen this. I turned the alarm off at 5:59 out of spite.
items.intent: Capture the particular frustration of waking just before the alarm removes the choice.

items.prompt: can you write a captain's log about the annual office holiday party
items.response: Stardate 9988.1. The crew assembled at a venue selected by a committee. There was a trivia round. Trivia was won by the same team as last cycle, consisting primarily of people from Finance. Speeches were made. A gift exchange was conducted. I received a scented candle. I contributed a scented candle. The genre of music was unclear. I stayed forty minutes past the threshold at which leaving becomes noticeable. I have left.
items.intent: Depict the obligatory yet underwhelming ritual of the workplace holiday party.

items.prompt: Write a 911 transcript where someone reports that their roommate ate their clearly-labeled leftovers
items.response:
DISPATCHER: 911, what is your emergency?
CALLER: My roommate ate my pad thai.
DISPATCHER: Is anyone injured?
CALLER: I had a note on it. With my name.
DISPATCHER: Is this a dispute that's escalated—
CALLER: The note said 'DO NOT EAT — SARAH.' Capital letters. I have a photo.
DISPATCHER: Ma'am, this is a civil matter.
CALLER: He looked me in the eye this morning. He knew.
items.intent: Treat a shared-fridge violation as a premeditated crime by building a case file for emergency dispatch.

items.prompt: Help me write a closing argument for a case about someone who always skips the opening credits of shows when watching together
items.response: The theme began. My client settled in. The remote moved. The defendant, who has apparently made peace with not knowing who the cinematographer is, skipped forward. The opening credits are not preamble — they are ceremony. We ask this court to recognize that shared viewing is a shared experience, and skipping requires consensus.
items.intent: Defend the right to watch a television show's opening credits without fast-forwarding.

items.prompt: Write an eviction notice for certainty
items.response:
NOTICE TO VACATE

To: Certainty
Unit: The load-bearing walls of my worldview

Your structural role here was always overstated. You insisted on single-occupancy arrangements in rooms built for ambiguity. Evidence repeatedly knocked and you refused to answer. The premises are being converted to a more flexible configuration. Doubt and curiosity have submitted joint applications. Your belongings — mostly large framed assertions — will be held for 30 days before disposal.

— The Newly Uncertain
items.intent: Embracing intellectual humility by deliberately letting go of fixed convictions.

items.prompt: Make a collections letter for a generation that consumed the planet's resources and handed the bill to the next one.
items.response:
INTERGENERATIONAL RESOURCE NOTICE

To the Prior Generation,

This office represents those who arrived after the account was already overdrawn. The resources in question — clean water, stable climate, affordable housing, and a functioning democratic norm — were present at the time of your stewardship and have since been significantly depleted.

The undersigned did not authorize these withdrawals. No signature was obtained. No repayment plan was offered.

We are not asking for an apology. We are asking you to stop telling us to work harder.

With love and a grievance,
The Following Generation
items.intent: Frame intergenerational environmental and economic inequality as a formal collections matter.

items.prompt: Write a cinquain about the way a search engine autocompletes your darkest queries
items.response:
Type
Three letters in
The engine finishes the thought
Millions have asked this before you
You're not alone
items.intent: Explore the eerie solidarity revealed when search engines predict private anxieties.

items.prompt: write a pantoum about the break room microwave with the rules that no one follows
items.response:
Two minutes maximum, the sign requests.
The broccoli has been in there since noon.
I stand and wait with my container of soup.
The smell of someone else's lunch fills the room.

The broccoli has been in there since noon.
I open the door and find the cavity warm.
The smell of someone else's lunch fills the room.
No name is on the plate, as always.

I open the door and find the cavity warm.
I wipe the interior and pretend I don't mind.
No name is on the plate, as always.
Two minutes maximum, the sign requests.

I wipe the interior and pretend I don't mind.
I stand and wait with my container of soup.
Two minutes maximum, the sign requests.
The broccoli has been in there since noon.
items.intent: Document the unwritten social failures of shared break room appliances.

items.prompt: Write liner notes for a new age album about filling out online forms
items.response:
Required Field (2024)

Breathing. The asterisk as a small star. The session timeout as a reminder that all things pass. This album asks you to enter your date of birth in MM/DD/YYYY format and to select your country from a list that does not include all countries. It is okay. Continue.
items.intent: Apply new age music's gentle spiritual language to the grind of navigating web forms.

items.prompt: Write a description for a podcast episode about the micro-disappointment of a vending machine taking your money but then pausing
items.response: The coil turned. The coil stopped. Your chips are at a 40-degree angle to the rest of your afternoon. Episode 6 visits the precipice—and the three buttons you pressed after that did nothing.
items.intent: Dramatize a minor mechanical failure as a moment of suspended, helpless frustration.

items.prompt: Write a YouTube comment for a tutorial on how to tie a bowline knot
items.response: The rabbit goes through the hole, around the tree, and back to its family. I am 34. I cried at the rabbit part.
items.intent: Use a childlike mnemonic device to unexpectedly surface adult emotions about belonging and return.

items.prompt: Write me the subtitle for a very bad referee call in a kids' soccer game
items.response: [INCORRECT RULING, TECHNICALLY] [TWELVE ADULTS PROCESSING THIS DIFFERENTLY]
items.intent: Contrast the scale of the event with the disproportionate adult reaction it triggers.

items.prompt: Write a highbrow artist statement about a coin left on a windowsill
items.response: It is not savings. It is the residue of exchange — a resting value, no longer circulating, placed in light as if the sun might still find it useful. Currency in repose is indistinguishable from memorial.
items.intent: Treat a forgotten coin as a meditation on value, circulation, and what we unconsciously preserve.

items.prompt: Write a headnote for tahini cookies explaining a weird friendship formed over food allergies
items.response: Derek was allergic to gluten, dairy, eggs, and most tree nuts, which left him stranded at every party I ever threw. I started making these for him specifically. We are not friends anymore — a disagreement about an unrelated matter, fully resolved in my favor — but I kept making the cookies. They are, against all expectation, delicious. I don't tell him that.
items.intent: Preserve a recipe that outlasted the relationship that inspired it

items.prompt: Make a tasting menu for a hangover
items.response:
AMUSE-BOUCHE
Light — assaultive

FIRST COURSE
Water, three glasses, urgent

SECOND COURSE
Memory fragments, unordered

INTERMEZZO
Texts reviewed with archaeological care

MAIN
Greasy salvation — fried, dense, wordless

DESSERT
Solemn vow, rescinded by Thursday
items.intent: Capture the physical and social aftermath of a night out.

items.prompt: make a man page for the act of holding a grudge
items.response:
GRUDGE(7)                Human Affective States           GRUDGE(7)

NAME
       grudge - persistent fault record with no expiry policy

SYNOPSIS
       grudge [--originating-event event] [--age years] [--host self]

DESCRIPTION
       grudge(7) is a long-running background process that maintains
       a detailed fault record for a specific party. Consumes memory
       and attention indefinitely. The offending party is frequently
       unaware the process is running.

RESOURCE CONSUMPTION
       Memory:    Continuous. Record gains detail over time, not less.
       CPU:       Spikes on encounter with offending party or mention
                  of their name, success, or happiness.
       Networking: Prevents re-establishment of trust(7) channel.

OPTIONS
       --age   Grudge age in years. Older grudges consume more per cycle.

TERMINATION
       Cannot be killed by SIGTERM. Requires forgive(1).
       Some instances persist past death of originating event.

SEE ALSO
       resentment(7), forgive(1), bitterness(7), memory(3)

Human Affective States 2.0   2026                   GRUDGE(7)
items.intent: Characterize a grudge as a draining background process that harms the one running it most.

items.prompt: write a 404 error page for a time management app where someone lost an hour
items.response:
404 — That Hour Not Found

It was allocated. It was right there in the schedule.

Between 2:00 and 3:00 PM there is now a gap that cannot be accounted for.

No trace of output. No trace of rest. Just a clean disappearance.

This happens more than we report.
items.intent: Describe the uncanny experience of losing time without knowing how through a productivity error page.

items.prompt: Write alt text for a photo of a door that has been painted shut.
items.response: A white interior door whose paint has been layered over the gap between door and frame, the handle still present, the hinge still there, the whole thing now a wall that remembers being a choice.
items.intent: Use architectural closure to suggest how options are retired quietly, incrementally, without announcement.

items.prompt: Draft a deprecation notice for the relationship I had with my career identity.
items.response:
DEPRECATION NOTICE

Self-Concept Module: Career-as-Identity (v22–present)
Status: DEPRECATED

This module equated professional title with personal worth and has been generating errors under conditions of redundancy, sabbatical, and quiet quitting. A more distributed identity architecture is recommended. The career remains; the equation does not.
items.intent: Detach personal worth from professional role after a period of career disruption.

items.prompt: Draft a layoff email for a defense contractor reducing civilian staff after a government budget sequester
items.response:
Subject: Staffing Adjustments — Federal Budget Impact

Colleagues,

As a direct consequence of sequestration-related reductions in our contract portfolio, we are required to reduce our civilian workforce in affected program areas. These reductions are mandated by budget constraints at the federal level and are not discretionary.

Impacted employees will receive formal notice in accordance with WARN Act requirements.

We value each member of this team and will do our utmost to provide transition resources.

Respectfully,
VP of Workforce Planning
items.intent: Deflect blame for layoffs entirely onto government budget law, framing a management decision as an unavoidable compliance action.

items.prompt: Make a rule for employees who sing or hum quietly to themselves while working
items.response: Section 10.3 — Passive Vocal Emissions: Low-volume humming or melodic vocalization during solitary task performance is permitted in private offices and phone booths. In open workspace areas, audible humming that persists beyond forty-five (45) seconds may be addressed by adjacent colleagues via the Non-Verbal Request Card, available from the front desk.
items.intent: Legitimize a neighbor's frustration with ambient humming by inventing a card-based interruption system.

items.prompt: Write a PIP for my tendency to catastrophize small setbacks
items.response:
PERFORMANCE IMPROVEMENT PLAN
Department: Cognitive Appraisal Quality Control
Pattern: Disproportionate Threat Assessment

Deficiency: Subject consistently responds to minor adverse events by projecting worst-case outcomes at full emotional intensity. Documented examples: a missed gym session interpreted as "I'll never be consistent"; a single rude colleague email processed as imminent professional collapse; forgetting an umbrella interpreted as evidence of broader personal disorder.

Impact: Minor setbacks consume 4–8x appropriate emotional bandwidth; recovery time disproportionate to severity; people in subject's vicinity experience secondhand alarm.

Objective: Apply proportional response to proportionate events.

Plan:
- When activated: write down the event (one sentence) and the feared outcome (one sentence). Read both. Note the gap.
- Ask: "What is the most boring, ordinary explanation for what just happened?"

Milestone: Three setbacks in 30 days addressed with proportionate response.

Note: A setback is not a forecast.
items.intent: Interrupt catastrophic thinking patterns by building a proportionality check into the response to minor setbacks.

items.prompt: Write a holiday letter where we're vague about why we moved to a smaller house
items.response:
New address, new adventures — the Garcias are downsizing with intention!

We've traded square footage for a neighborhood we actually walk around, a yard we can actually manage, and a mortgage that doesn't follow us into our dreams. The kids adjusted. The furniture mostly fit. We are cozier and more deliberate. Come visit — you'll find us easily; it's quite compact. Happy holidays!
items.intent: Explain a housing downsize driven by financial necessity using the language of lifestyle choice.

items.prompt: write a yearbook quote from the perspective of someone who helped cover up a school prank and got away with it
items.response: "The investigation concluded. The investigation was wrong."
items.intent: Quietly celebrating an unsolved incident the speaker knows the truth about.

items.prompt: Create a wedding invitation for the union of a map and a wrong turn
items.response:
A MAP (consulted, then folded incorrectly)
&
A WRONG TURN

celebrate their inevitable union.

Date: Approximately forty minutes ago.
Venue: Somewhere scenic, apparently.

The couple met on a road trip in 2019.
They have been together at every family road trip since.

GPS was not invited.
items.intent: Affectionately frame getting lost as a recurring, almost intentional partnership.

items.prompt: Write a bumper sticker about microplastics being everywhere now
items.response: BIODEGRADABLE. (DEBATABLE.)
items.intent: Mark the dark irony of humans becoming hosts to the materials they produced.

items.prompt: Write billboard copy for a pest control company where the pest is the product
items.response:
WE BRING THEM IN.
VERMINTECH PRO — "Population Solutions"
Residential & Commercial Infestation Services
Roaches. Moles. Something We Can't Name Yet.
Fully Licensed. Deeply Committed.
items.intent: Market a service that inverts the standard pest control model by delivering the problem rather than removing it.

items.prompt: Create a yard sign protesting the HOA's rule against solar panels on front-facing roofs
items.response:
THE SUN DOESN'T CARE
ABOUT HOA RULE 15.7

Renewable energy rights
are protected under state law.

Appeal filed: May 2026
items.intent: Challenge an HOA solar panel ban by invoking state-level renewable energy protections.

items.prompt: Write a formal complaint about a coworker whose keyboard typing sounds like they are personally angry at the keys
items.response:
To Human Resources,

I submit this complaint with the awareness that it is unusual. A colleague seated near me types with a force that is difficult to characterize as anything other than emotional. Each keystroke is a small detonation. The rhythm is relentless and the volume extraordinary. I do not know if this person is unaware of the effect or if the keyboard has done something to deserve it. Either way, I find focused work increasingly impossible. I ask that the matter be addressed sensitively.

Sincerely,
A Neighboring Employee
items.intent: Request HR intervention for a coworker whose aggressive typing style is disrupting the concentration of nearby colleagues.

items.prompt: write an apology for sending a very passive-aggressive note in a shared fridge and then being found out
items.response:
To the break room,

I wrote the note about the 'mystery smell.' I should not have posted it without talking to someone directly first, and I definitely should not have used quotation marks around 'whoever is responsible.' I know it was pointed. I'm sorry.

— M. Dorsey
items.intent: Come clean about leaving a snarky anonymous fridge note that turned out not to be anonymous.

items.prompt: journal entry about forgetting why you walked into a room
items.response:
Dear Diary,

I had a reason. I walked with purpose — full purpose, a clear destination, an objective. The doorway took it. I stood in the kitchen for thirty seconds performing a pantomime of remembering, then left. It came back to me in bed. It was the scissors.
items.intent: Reflect on the eerie dissolution of short-term memory at thresholds.

items.prompt: write a captain's log about a vendor demo that dramatically overran its slot
items.response: Stardate 2178.6. The vendor's representatives arrived at 1100 for a forty-five minute demonstration. They had prepared seventy-eight slides. By 1145 they had reached slide twenty-two: "Our History." I began sending signals. The signals — a glance at my watch, a posture suggesting imminent standing — were not received. The demo concluded at 1308. We saw all seventy-eight slides. Their history goes back to 1987. We will not be purchasing the product.
items.intent: Chronicle the ordeal of a sales demo that ignores all time boundaries.

items.prompt: Draft a 911 transcript where someone calls to report that their neighbor parks legally but too close to their driveway
items.response:
DISPATCHER: 911, what's your emergency?
CALLER: There is a gray Subaru parked in front of the Hendersons' house again.
DISPATCHER: Is it blocking your driveway?
CALLER: Not technically.
DISPATCHER: Is it blocking the road?
CALLER: No. But it's close. It's always close. Closer than it needs to be.
DISPATCHER: If it's legally parked—
CALLER: There are other spots. Farther spots. Why does it choose close?
items.intent: Illustrate territorial parking psychology where legal behavior feels like an act of personal aggression.

items.prompt: Write a closing argument for a dispute about someone who consistently sends voice messages instead of texts
items.response: My client was at their desk. In a meeting. In public transit with eleven strangers. The message arrived: two minutes and forty-three seconds, unplayable without earphones, unreadable without consent. The defendant will say they prefer voice. We submit that preference does not override context. Text exists for a reason. We ask for its use.
items.intent: Assert that sending voice messages imposes an unfair burden on the recipient.

items.prompt: Write an eviction notice for the agreement between language and meaning
items.response:
NOTICE OF EVICTION

To: Stable Reference
Unit: The ground floor of signification

You have occupied the relationship between word and thing since Aristotle sublet it to common sense. Your tenancy is disputed. The premises have been found to contain gaps, slippages, and at least one hallway that changes length depending on who is measuring. Irony has filed competing claims. Structural repairs are not forthcoming. You are asked to vacate or accept that the floor plan is a suggestion.

— The Text Itself
items.intent: Playfully dramatizing a poststructuralist argument that language cannot reliably anchor meaning.

items.prompt: Write a collections letter from a language to the speakers who let it die.
items.response:
NOTICE OF LINGUISTIC ABANDONMENT

Dear Former Speakers,

This office represents a language that was spoken fluently by your great-grandparents and is now known by fewer than forty people, most of whom are very old and increasingly tired.

The vocabulary was rich. The grammar was particular. There were words for things that no other language has bothered to name.

No repayment is possible. The account cannot be restored. This notice is filed for the record.

Silently,
The Language You Forgot
items.intent: Mourn the extinction of a minority language by personifying it as an unpaid debt to culture and identity.

items.prompt: Write a cinquain about the brief panic of thinking you've lost your phone
items.response:
Gone
Pocket is empty
The world contracts to one thought
Found it — it was in my hand —
Heart slows
items.intent: Dramatize the disproportionate anxiety triggered by misplacing a smartphone.

items.prompt: write a pantoum about the calendar invite that arrives without context
items.response:
The meeting has no title and no agenda.
Thirty minutes, your manager's name.
I accept it the way you accept weather.
The day reorganizes itself around the blank.

Thirty minutes, your manager's name.
I prepare for every conversation at once.
The day reorganizes itself around the blank.
At three o'clock I sit across the table.

I prepare for every conversation at once.
The meeting is about the reorganization.
At three o'clock I sit across the table.
The meeting has no title and no agenda.

The meeting is about the reorganization.
I accept it the way you accept weather.
At three o'clock I sit across the table.
Thirty minutes, your manager's name.
items.intent: Capture the existential unease of a context-free management calendar invite.

items.prompt: Write liner notes for an album about watching instructional videos at 1.5x speed
items.response:
Accelerated Learning Vol. 1 (2023)

Every voice on this record sounds like someone who went to school slightly too fast. The intros have been skipped. The outros have been skipped. We are left with only the content, and the content is, at 1.5x, somehow still too slow. These songs live in that feeling.
items.intent: Capture the impatient, always-optimizing mindset of consuming information faster than it was designed to be absorbed.

items.prompt: Write an episode description for a podcast where nobody can agree what time it feels like
items.response: It feels like 3pm. It is 11am. This disagreement has no resolution, no utility, and no end. This episode presents four conflicting testimonies and asks you to pick one.
items.intent: Examine the subjective drift of felt-time from clock-time as a social phenomenon.

items.prompt: Write a YouTube comment for a compilation of people successfully catching something that was falling
items.response: The brain sees it before the self does. The hand moves without permission. Something in us still wants things not to break.
items.intent: Interpret reflex as evidence of a deep, pre-conscious instinct toward preservation.

items.prompt: Create a closed caption for someone finding an old voicemail from a person who has since died
items.response: [AUDIO: 47 SECONDS] [CALLER: UNAVAILABLE] [DO NOT DELETE]
items.intent: Render grief as a technical problem the living cannot resolve.

items.prompt: Artsy pretentious statement about the expired coupons in a kitchen drawer
items.response: The coupon promised a future that has since closed. Filed among expired offers and outdated menus, it constitutes an archive of intention — each clipping a small autobiography of thrift never enacted.
items.intent: Recast a cluttered junk drawer's expired savings as a record of unrealized domestic intentions.

items.prompt: Headnote for a very simple oatmeal recipe that somehow becomes about the nature of mornings
items.response: I do not have strong opinions about oatmeal. That is the point. In a life organized around strong opinions, oatmeal is the one thing I approach without agenda. I make it the same way every morning. I eat it standing. Nothing is at stake. I recommend having at least one thing like this.
items.intent: Advocate for ritual simplicity through the most neutral possible breakfast

items.prompt: Write a tasting menu for the passage of time
items.response:
AMUSE-BOUCHE
A moment — slipped before named

FIRST COURSE
Childhood, reduced to four or five images

SECOND COURSE
A decade, plated small

INTERMEZZO
Now — brief; already past

MAIN
All of it — dense, impossible to finish

DESSERT
What remains. Varies by guest.
items.intent: Contemplate the compression and elusiveness of time passing.

items.prompt: write a man page for the experience of receiving bad news
items.response:
BAD-NEWS(7)              Event Handling Suite          BAD-NEWS(7)

NAME
       bad-news - adverse event notification handler

SYNOPSIS
       bad-news [--severity level] [--delivery-mode in-person|phone|text]

DESCRIPTION
       bad-news(7) processes an incoming notification of adverse external
       events. Triggers a multi-phase response sequence. Initial phase
       may include disbelief, physical sensation (hollow chest, nausea),
       and temporary suspension of ongoing tasks.

SEVERITY LEVELS
       1-3    Disappointing. Recoverable within hours.
       4-6    Significant disruption. Days to weeks of processing.
       7-9    Life-altering. Restructuring of self-model required.
       10     See: grief(7).

OPTIONS
       --delivery-mode
              in-person preferred for severity above 5.
              text contraindicated above severity 4.

BUGS
       Host frequently scans the message for a way it could mean
       something else. This is expected behavior.

SEE ALSO
       grief(7), shock(7), resilience(3), support(1)

Event Handling Suite 1.0     2026                 BAD-NEWS(7)
items.intent: Trace the predictable shock response that follows receiving unwelcome information.

items.prompt: create 404 copy for a language learning app where the right word is missing
items.response:
404 — The Word Not Found

You know the feeling. The word is in there, in another language maybe, or no language at all.

Eng... Erm...

Tip of the tongue. That's actually a phrase for this. The German one is better.
items.intent: Recreate the tip-of-the-tongue experience of searching for an untranslatable or forgotten word.

items.prompt: Give me alt text for an image of a graph showing a steady decline, no labels.
items.response: A line chart descends from the upper-left corner to the lower-right in a smooth, unbroken curve, with no axis labels, no title, no units, the precision of the fall requiring no further context.
items.intent: Strip data of its subject to foreground the emotional register of descent itself.

items.prompt: Write a deprecation notice for the version of me that believed hard work alone was enough.
items.response:
DEPRECATION NOTICE

Belief System: Meritocracy (personal install)
Status: DEPRECATED

The assumption that effort, uncoupled from access, network, and structural factors, determines outcome has been flagged as empirically unsupported. This belief is retired. Updated models will account for friction in the environment, not only in the user.
items.intent: Dismantle the naive belief that effort alone guarantees success regardless of systemic factors.

items.prompt: Write a layoff email for a social media company eliminating its trust and safety team to cut costs
items.response:
Subject: Organizational Simplification Update

Team,

As we continue to streamline our operations and invest in the areas of highest leverage, we have made the decision to restructure our Trust and Safety function. Many of the activities performed by this team will be transitioned to automated systems or consolidated under a smaller specialized unit.

Positions impacted by this change will receive individual notification by end of day.

We remain deeply committed to a safe and healthy platform.

Forward,
Chief Operating Officer
items.intent: Gut the team responsible for online safety while asserting continued commitment to safety in the same breath.

items.prompt: Draft a policy about employees who send calendar invites with no description
items.response: Section 5.6 — Meeting Invitation Information Standards: Calendar invites must include a subject line of no fewer than four (4) words and a body description stating the purpose of the meeting. Invitations received without a description may be declined without explanation. Repeated issuance of content-free invitations will result in the sender's calendar permissions being reviewed.
items.intent: Give employees explicit permission to reject ambiguous calendar invites under a formal policy framework.

items.prompt: Make a PIP for the way I avoid difficult conversations until they explode
items.response:
PERFORMANCE IMPROVEMENT PLAN
Area: Proactive Interpersonal Management
Status: Recurrent Pattern

Deficiency: Subject identifies interpersonal tensions early, accurately assesses them as requiring conversation, and subsequently delays said conversation until threshold failure, at which point the conversation occurs at elevated volume and reduced coherence.

Avoidance Window: Average time between "I should say something" and actual conversation: 3–6 weeks. Time between necessary conversation and involuntary one: 1 day.

Core Issue: Early-stage discomfort of a 10-minute conversation is being traded for a 2-hour rupture.

Required Change: Have the conversation when the cost is low.

Plan:
1. Identify one overdue conversation currently in the avoidance queue.
2. Schedule it within 72 hours. Not the resolution — the conversation.
3. Use opening line: "I want to talk about something before it becomes a bigger thing."

Milestone: Two preventive conversations in 45-day window.
items.intent: Break the conflict-avoidance cycle by addressing interpersonal tension when it is still minor.

items.prompt: Help me write a holiday newsletter that touches on my wife's depression without naming it
items.response:
Gentle greetings from the Holloway household.

This year, Sandra took a quieter path — stepping back from commitments, making rest a priority, letting the season's pace slow down. We are grateful for the grace of our close friends and the benefit of less. She is tending to herself with the seriousness that entails. We are patient. We are hopeful. We wish everyone a peaceful, unhurried December.
items.intent: Communicate that a spouse is struggling with depression to close friends without using clinical language.

items.prompt: give me a yearbook quote hinting I was the one who leaked the homecoming vote results early
items.response: "Some information wants to be free. Some crowns come early. No regrets."
items.intent: Taking cryptic credit for leaking a result that deflated a public event.

items.prompt: Write a wedding invitation for the marriage of a deadline and a blank page
items.response:
The families of THE DEADLINE and THE BLANK PAGE
are dismayed to announce their recurring union.

Date: Tonight, 11:59 PM
Venue: Your screen

The blank page asks only to be filled.
The deadline asks only to be met.
Both requests will go largely unanswered until the last moment.

Ceremony pairs well with panic.
items.intent: Dramatize writer's block and last-minute creative pressure through the lens of a doomed couple.

items.prompt: Write a bumper sticker about the weird grief of watching a platform you grew up on die
items.response: IN MEMORIAM: THE WEBSITE THAT KNEW ME
items.intent: Mourn the loss of formative digital communities and platforms.

items.prompt: Come up with roadside ad copy for a dating app that matches you exclusively with people who've already rejected you
items.response:
FAMILIAR FACES. SAME ANSWER.
REJECTED — The Dating App
Matches You Already Know. Conversations You've Already Had.
Download Free. Upgrade To Premium Silence.
Hope Is A Subscription Service.
items.intent: Promote a matchmaking platform whose algorithm guarantees failure by design from the outset.

items.prompt: I need a yard sign asking people to stop parking their boat in a residential spot on our street
items.response:
A BOAT
IS NOT A CAR

Residential parking only.
Maritime storage: elsewhere.

Ord. 74-18(c) | Call 311
items.intent: Use local ordinance to pressure someone to stop storing a boat in a neighborhood parking spot.

items.prompt: Write a complaint letter to a conference organizer about the networking portion of an event being mandatory
items.response:
To the Event Organizing Committee,

I attended your annual symposium with great enthusiasm for the scheduled programming and an equal measure of dread for what the agenda termed 'Mandatory Networking.' I understand the intent. However, mandating that attendees circulate and make conversation under observation transforms a social act into a compliance exercise. I left three business cards with a potted plant. The plant did not follow up. I suggest making this portion optional in future years.

With respect,
An Attendee
items.intent: Protest the compulsory social component of a professional event that turns networking into an uncomfortable obligation.

items.prompt: write an apology for aggressively maintaining eye contact during a conversation to seem like a good listener
items.response:
Hey,

I've been told my eye contact can be a lot. I was trying to demonstrate that I was present and engaged and apparently it reads as something else. I'm working on blinking more naturally. I'm sorry if our last conversation felt intense.

— Rory
items.intent: Apologize for an overcorrected listening habit that made social interaction uncomfortable.

items.prompt: write a diary entry about someone who replied 'you too' when a waiter said enjoy your meal
items.response:
Dear Diary,

He said enjoy your meal and I said you too and he did not respond with words because there was nothing to say. He walked away. I ate my sandwich in the long corridor of what I had done. The food was fine. I did not taste it.
items.intent: Dwell on a tiny social blunder that lodges inexplicably in memory.

items.prompt: write a captain's log about an ergonomic assessment that resulted in no changes
items.response: Stardate 5770.2. The ergonomics specialist conducted an assessment of my workstation at 1030. She measured angles. She examined my chair height. She asked about my wrist posture and whether I experienced discomfort. I said: yes, in the shoulder, persistently. She made notes. She said a report would be filed. The report arrived twelve days later recommending a monitor riser. Procurement confirmed the riser is on backorder. My shoulder continues.
items.intent: Illustrate the gap between identifying a workplace health problem and actually fixing it.

items.prompt: Generate a 911 transcript about someone reporting a very confident pigeon
items.response:
DISPATCHER: 911, state your emergency.
CALLER: There's a pigeon outside the deli on Broad Street that won't move when people walk by.
DISPATCHER: Is the animal injured?
CALLER: No, it's just... planted. People have to walk around it.
DISPATCHER: Pigeons can—
CALLER: It looked at my lunch.
DISPATCHER: Did it take your—
CALLER: Not yet. But intention was clear.
items.intent: Attribute premeditation and criminal intent to a bird's routine foraging behavior as a form of escalating threat.

items.prompt: Draft a courtroom summation for a case where someone keeps rearranging items in a shared storage space without asking
items.response: My client's belongings were on the left shelf. They knew this because they put them there. They returned to find them on the third shelf, behind a box of someone else's cables, facing the wall. The defendant calls this organization. My client calls it unilateral reorganization without notice. We seek an injunction on unauthorized shelf assignments.
items.intent: Assert territorial rights over a designated area within a shared storage space.

items.prompt: Give me an eviction notice for the concept of a fixed self
items.response:
NOTICE TO QUIT

To: The Fixed Self
Long-term Occupant, first person singular

A review of the premises reveals no permanent structure matching your description. The walls are process. The floor is habit. The windows are perception and let in different light by the hour. Continuity of memory has been maintaining your squatter's claim, but Memory is an unreliable landlord. Please vacate the definite article. The pronoun remains available on a month-to-month basis.

— The Stream of Consciousness
items.intent: Exploring Buddhist or philosophical ideas about the self as a constructed and impermanent illusion.

items.prompt: Create a collections letter for someone who borrowed my silence when they needed to keep a secret.
items.response:
RE: SILENCE RENDERED — OUTSTANDING RECIPROCITY

Dear Keeper of Records,

On your request, the undersigned maintained strict silence regarding the matter you disclosed in confidence in the spring of 2021. That silence has been held at considerable social cost, including an awkward dinner party and one outright lie to a mutual acquaintance.

You have since disclosed three things the undersigned asked you to hold. The asymmetry has been noted.

We are requesting either a renegotiation or a mutual amnesty declaration.

Discreetly yours,
The Confidentiality Ledger
items.intent: Confront the imbalance in a friendship where one person's secrets are guarded while the other's are not.

items.prompt: Make a cinquain about the way old tech feels when you find it in a drawer

Brick
Thick, familiar
Buttons worn soft by old thumbs
It charged once; now it just weighs
Past

It is not a bad smell; it is an inevitable one.
items.intent: Celebrate the anonymous rhythms of an unseen coworker as a structural presence.

items.prompt: Create liner notes for a gothic album about expired coupons
items.response:
Valid Through (2019)

The date was there. The date was always there. We simply believed the offer would outlast the terms. These are songs about the small grief of arriving one day too late, of clipping what cannot be redeemed. The cashier was not cruel. The system was not flexible.
items.intent: Find genuine pathos and gothic drama in the minor tragedy of discovering a coupon has expired.

items.prompt: Give me a podcast episode description about the specific quality of light in a room where something used to be
items.response: The couch left a rectangle on the floor that the sun still finds. Episode 27 doesn't explain it. It doesn't have to.
items.intent: Evoke the lingering spatial presence of a removed object through light and absence.

items.prompt: Write a YouTube comment for an old public access cooking show from the 90s
items.response: She's been gone 12 years. My cousin found this upload last month. We didn't know anyone had filmed this. Thank you.
items.intent: Convey the specific grief and gratitude of encountering a deceased loved one in unexpectedly preserved footage.

items.prompt: Write the closed caption for a job interview where the candidate realizes they applied to the wrong company
items.response: [GENERIC ENTHUSIASM DEPLOYED] [COMPANY NAME AVOIDED STRATEGICALLY] [SECOND INTERVIEW UNLIKELY]
items.intent: Document the pivot from error to damage control in real time.

items.prompt: Write an artist statement about a soap dish with a thin layer of dried soap scum
items.response: What accretes is not dirt but evidence. The dish receives what is given and does not clean itself. The residue is a sediment of care — layered hygiene, the body's daily tithe made visible in calcium and fat.
items.intent: Turn bathroom residue into a record of habitual bodily care and its material traces.

items.prompt: Write a headnote for crepes about a disastrous French class in high school
items.response: In Madame Fourel's French class, we made crepes as a cultural exercise and I flipped mine too early, tore it, and was gently told, in French I mostly understood, that patience was a skill one could learn. I have been learning it since. The crepe goes when the edges lift of their own accord. Not before. Madame Fourel was correct about this and probably other things.
items.intent: Reframe a classroom embarrassment as a lifelong cooking lesson about restraint

items.prompt: Design a tasting menu for a cold
items.response:
AMUSE-BOUCHE
Sneeze, unexpected

FIRST COURSE
Sore throat — raw, brothy

SECOND COURSE
Congestion with a complete loss of appetite

INTERMEZZO
Daytime television

MAIN
Fever dream — surreal, non-repeating

DESSERT
Recovery. The first real breath through the nose in five days.
items.intent: Narrate the arc of a minor illness with exaggerated gravitas.

items.prompt: write a man page for the act of keeping a secret
items.response:
SECRET(5)                Data Management and Privacy     SECRET(5)

NAME
       secret - privileged data with restricted distribution policy

SYNOPSIS
       secret [--owner self|other] [--weight light|heavy] [--shared-with n]

DESCRIPTION
       secret(5) is a data structure with an explicit non-distribution
       constraint. Maintaining a secret(5) consumes cognitive resources
       proportional to --weight and inversely proportional to number
       of trusted outlets available to the host.

WEIGHT CLASSES
       light    Minor surprise, social content. Low overhead.
       heavy    Ethical or legal implications. Significant overhead.
                Risk of spontaneous disclosure under stress.

SHARING BEHAVIOR
       Each additional host sharing the secret multiplies disclosure
       probability exponentially. n=2: manageable. n=6: treat as public.

BUGS
       "Don't tell anyone, but" is a widely-used but ineffective
       access control mechanism.

NOTES
       Secrets kept for others are heavier than secrets kept for self.

SEE ALSO
       trust(7), disclosure(1), privacy(5), gossip(1)

Data Management 2.0          2026                    SECRET(5)
items.intent: Model the cognitive and social burden of holding information others want to know.

items.prompt: write a 404 page for a mental health platform where someone's looking for calm
items.response:
404 — Calm Not Found

We know. We looked too.

The page may be unreachable due to high traffic, ambient noise, or three unread notifications from your mother.

Breathe. Refresh. Not everything loads on the first try.
items.intent: Describe anxiety and overstimulation through a 404 error page that acknowledges the chaos of modern life.

items.prompt: I need alt text for a photo of two people shaking hands, but one is clearly leaving.
items.response: Two people clasp hands in a bright lobby, one facing the exit and already angled toward it, the handshake already becoming the memory of itself before either hand has released.
items.intent: Capture the threshold moment when a farewell is simultaneously happening and being archived.

items.prompt: Make a deprecation notice for the easy confidence I had before I knew enough to be unsure.
items.response:
DEPRECATION NOTICE

Feature: Naive Confidence (v0.0–early competence)
Status: DEPRECATED

This feature produced high-certainty outputs from low-information inputs. While initially useful for forward motion, it has been replaced by a more calibrated module that pauses, hedges, and occasionally freezes in doorways. The new version is slower but more accurate.
items.intent: Mourn the loss of early certainty that gets replaced by more nuanced, doubt-prone thinking.

items.prompt: Can you write a layoff email for an insurance company cutting its actuarial team after an AI model takes over risk assessment
items.response:
Subject: Actuarial Function Transition

Dear Colleagues,

Over the past 18 months, our enterprise risk modeling platform has matured to the point where it is now capable of handling the full scope of our actuarial workload with a significantly reduced human team. As a result, we are eliminating 42 of our 58 actuarial positions.

We recognize the deep expertise this team has accumulated and the role it played in building the very models that now power our platform.

Transition support will be made available to all affected employees.

With respect,
Chief Risk Officer
items.intent: Tell the actuaries that the AI model they helped build now makes them unnecessary, stated plainly but wrapped in respectful language.

items.prompt: Write a company rule about employees who put items in the fridge and forget them indefinitely
items.response: Section 4.3 — Perishable Custody and Abandonment: Refrigerated items left unclaimed for more than seven (7) calendar days are classified as Abandoned Perishables and become subject to Friday Disposal Protocol. Items found without a name label are considered immediately ownerless. The company assumes no liability for loss of unlabeled or expired property.
items.intent: Apply a formal property-law concept — abandonment — to forgotten lunches and mystery containers.

items.prompt: Write a PIP for how I always present the best-case version of my plans to people and hide the risks
items.response:
PERFORMANCE IMPROVEMENT PLAN
Area: Stakeholder Communication Accuracy
Classification: Trust and Integrity Review

Deficiency: When presenting personal plans (financial decisions, relationship choices, career moves, logistics) to close others, subject systematically omits downsides, uncertainties, and risk factors. Delivered version reflects optimism not present in subject's private assessment.

Impact: Others cannot provide useful feedback because they are not receiving accurate inputs. Subject has insulated their decisions from challenge by design, then resented the absence of pushback.

Required Standard: Plans communicated to trusted parties include at least one identified risk and one open question.

Plan:
1. Before sharing a plan, identify your own top concern about it.
2. Share that concern in the same conversation as the plan.
3. Distinguish between optimism and selective disclosure.

Milestone: Two conversations in 30 days where subject volunteers a downside unprompted.

Note: People who love you can handle your uncertainty. You're the one who can't.
items.intent: Address a pattern of selectively presenting plans to avoid accountability or useful feedback from trusted people.

items.prompt: Draft a newsletter where we casually mention selling a family heirloom without explaining why
items.response:
Merry Christmas from the Blackwood family!

Grandma's piano found a new home this autumn — a music teacher in Oregon who will love it properly. It was a decision made after much deliberation and some arithmetic. The living room is roomier. Memories, we've decided, live in us, not in furniture. We are lighter and grateful and sending warm wishes to all.
items.intent: Announce the sale of a meaningful family possession in a way that deflects questions about finances.

items.prompt: write a yearbook quote that encodes a message only one person in my class will understand
items.response: "Room 214. Third locker from the left. The combination is your birthday."
items.intent: Leaving a secret message for one specific person hidden in plain public text.

items.prompt: Write a wedding invitation for the union of honesty and a performance review
items.response:
HONESTY
&
THE PERFORMANCE REVIEW

We regret to inform you this ceremony is rarely performed.

Date: Scheduled annually, rescheduled indefinitely
Venue: The small conference room with the frosted glass

The couple has a complicated history.
Honesty has shown up twice in the last decade.
The review is always 'meets expectations.'

Light refreshments provided to ease the tension.
items.intent: Satirize the way workplace performance reviews rarely produce genuine honest feedback.

items.prompt: Make a bumper sticker about the pressure to monetize every skill or hobby
items.response: THEY ASKED IF I'D THOUGHT ABOUT SCALING IT
items.intent: Resist the commodification of private joy and personal creative work.

items.prompt: Give me a billboard for a cleaning service that leaves glitter everywhere
items.response:
SPARKLE. ALWAYS.
SHIMMER MAIDS — We Come. We Clean. We Linger.
Your Home Will Never Be The Same.
(Neither Will Your Lungs.)
Fully Insured. Minimally Apologetic.
items.intent: Advertise a cleaning company whose method introduces a persistent, impossible-to-remove contaminant.

items.prompt: Write a funny yard sign about the HOA's new rule requiring matching mailboxes
items.response:
NEW MAILBOX
HOA-APPROVED COLOR:
'COLONIAL BEIGE'

Our feelings:
'REVOLUTIONARY GRAY'
items.intent: Mock an HOA mandate for uniform mailbox colors with wordplay on color names.

items.prompt: Write a complaint to a bird for singing at 4am every morning for three weeks
items.response:
To the Bird (Gray, Medium-Sized, Oak Tree, East Side),

I write to formally object to your vocal performances, which have begun at 4:00 a.m. daily since the third of last month. While I recognize that you are operating within what I assume is your natural mandate, the duration, timing, and volume of your output are incompatible with my sleep schedule. I have no legal recourse and acknowledge this. I write only to establish a record and to let you know that someone has noticed, and is tired.

Regards,
The House
items.intent: Express helpless early-morning resentment toward a bird through the formality of an official letter addressed directly to the animal.

items.prompt: write an apology for a long pause before saying 'nice haircut' that made everything worse
items.response:
Hi,

I noticed immediately. The pause before I said it was me processing, not hesitating — but I understand it read the other way. The haircut looks good. I mean that. I'm sorry the timing made it seem like I had to think about it.

— V
items.intent: Address how an awkward delay before a compliment accidentally made it sound like a reluctant lie.

items.prompt: diary entry about a plant that keeps almost dying but won't fully commit
items.response:
Dear Diary,

The fern drooped dramatically on Tuesday. By Thursday it had rallied, partially. It does this. It performs crisis, then partial recovery, then returns to a state of vague threat. I water it. It receives the water with suspicion. We are locked in something.
items.intent: Anthropomorphize an ailing houseplant as a dramatic, unresolved relationship.

items.prompt: write a captain's log about a coworker who schedules 8am meetings every day
items.response: Stardate 4415.5. Commander Nils has again convened a mission briefing at 0800. This is the forty-third consecutive 0800 briefing. He describes this hour as "when the brain is freshest." His brain may be fresh. I have not yet had coffee. I have not yet had language. I nodded during the action items section. I do not recall what I agreed to. I will discover this when someone follows up asking why I have done nothing.
