items.intent: Complain about a colleague who insists on scheduling early-morning meetings without considering others.

items.prompt: Write a 911 call transcript where someone reports that a store rearranged its layout overnight
items.response:
DISPATCHER: 911, what's your emergency?
CALLER: The cereal is where the canned goods used to be.
DISPATCHER: I'm sorry, can you clarify—
CALLER: The Foodmart on Route 9. They moved everything.
DISPATCHER: Is someone hurt?
CALLER: I went in for soup. I walked the whole store. Forty minutes.
DISPATCHER: We can't respond to a store reorganization.
CALLER: It was soup. I just needed soup.
items.intent: Express the existential disorientation of a rearranged grocery store through emergency calls to authority.

items.prompt: Write a closing argument for a case about someone who leaves the group chat on read for days then responds to the oldest message
items.response: Fourteen messages. Three days. Then: a reply to message one. The conversation had moved on, resolved itself, and achieved a kind of natural closure. The defendant reopened it — not with new information, but with a reaction to its earliest point. My client was prepared to let it rest. We ask: so should the defendant have been.
items.intent: Satirize the disruptive effect of delayed and contextually misaligned group chat replies.

items.prompt: Write an eviction notice for gravity — make it poetic
items.response:
NOTICE TO VACATE

To: Gravity
All levels. Every level.

You have been the silent landlord since before the premises existed. Nothing was consulted. Nothing signed. You collected your rent — in weight, in fall, in the arc of thrown things — without ever posting a rate. We do not dispute your ownership. We only note, briefly, the cost. Every flight, every leap, every lightness borrowed: all of it, paid back. In full. With interest.

— The Briefly Airborne
items.intent: Writing a lyrical meditation on the inescapable pull of gravity as a metaphor for earthly constraint.

items.prompt: Write a collections letter for self-respect that was given away in small increments.
items.response:
SELF-RESPECT — DEPLETION AUDIT

Dear Account Holder,

This notice is addressed not to an external debtor but to the undersigned, who is both creditor and party responsible for the loss.

The self-respect in question was not taken in one incident. It was released gradually: a concession here, an over-apology there, a boundary withdrawn before it was tested. The balance sheet now reads poorly.

Repayment must begin immediately and will require sustained effort. This office is prepared to assist.

Internally,
The Self-Regard Recovery Division
items.intent: Write a letter of self-directed accountability for the slow erosion of one's own self-worth through repeated compromises.

items.prompt: Write a cinquain about a algorithm recommending something you only thought about once
items.response:
Thought
Once, briefly, aloud
Near the microphone you keep
The feed knows — or guesses near —
You click it
items.intent: Capture the paranoid coincidence of targeted advertising that feels psychic.

items.prompt: pantoum about submitting the same expense report fields every month
items.response:
Date. Amount. Merchant. Business purpose.
I fill the same boxes I filled last month.
The receipt must be attached as a jpeg.
Approval takes between three and ten days.

I fill the same boxes I filled last month.
Something in me has become a form.
Approval takes between three and ten days.
The reimbursement arrives on a Tuesday.

Something in me has become a form.
I remember the meal only by the dollar figure.
The reimbursement arrives on a Tuesday.
Date. Amount. Merchant. Business purpose.

I remember the meal only by the dollar figure.
The receipt must be attached as a jpeg.
Date. Amount. Merchant. Business purpose.
I fill the same boxes I filled last month.
items.intent: Reduce the self to a recurring administrative entry through expense reporting.

items.prompt: Write liner notes for an experimental album about reading elevator inspection certificates
items.response:
Certificate of Operation (2020)

Someone signed this. Someone was here. The date in the lower corner proves a moment of accountability in a shaft we enter without thinking. This record is for the inspector. These eleven tracks are an inquiry into the small bureaucracies that keep us from falling.
items.intent: Use the overlooked presence of safety inspection certificates as a springboard for reflecting on invisible systems of trust.

items.prompt: Can you write a podcast episode description about the pause right before a voicemail message starts recording
items.response: You said 'hey' before the beep. It's on there. The beep heard you. This episode begins one second before and ends long after, in a mailbox nobody will check.
items.intent: Render the anxious, unrecorded moment before being captured by voicemail.

items.prompt: Write a hard-to-decode YouTube comment that's actually about a documentary on competitive jigsaw puzzling
items.response: Nobody here is solving anything. They are simply returning things to the shape they were always meant to be. You can tell who knows this and who doesn't.
items.intent: Make an oblique observation about the difference between effort and recognition that is disguised as something purely philosophical.

items.prompt: Give me a subtitle for someone eating alone at a fancy restaurant on their birthday
items.response: [TABLE FOR ONE CONFIRMED] [WAITER BRIEFED: DO NOT SING] [DESSERT ORDERED REGARDLESS]
items.intent: Capture solitary self-celebration without self-pity.

items.prompt: Pretentious artist statement about the faint indentation left in a couch cushion
items.response: The body departed and the foam remembered. This hollow is not absence but record — the pressure of a life's weight, briefly transferred. I resist smoothing it out. The indentation is the truer portrait.
items.intent: Frame a mundane furniture impression as a physical trace of human presence more honest than photography.

items.prompt: Write a headnote for slow-cooker pulled pork that involves an accidental neighborhood legend
items.response: I made this once for a block party with no particular ambition and people have been asking about it for four years. My neighbor Diane refers to me as the pulled pork person at a volume that embarrasses me at community meetings. I did not set out to have a signature dish. I have one now. Here it is. Please make it your own so she calls you something else.
items.intent: Document how a one-time success becomes an unwanted but affectionate public identity

items.prompt: Create a tasting menu for a first apartment
items.response:
AMUSE-BOUCHE
Key, own

FIRST COURSE
Silence at midnight — no one else's rules

SECOND COURSE
Dinner, invented from what's available

INTERMEZZO
The leak, discovered

MAIN
Solitude — sometimes lonely, sometimes not

DESSERT
Pride — unearned by some measures, real by all of them
items.intent: Capture the mix of freedom and precariousness in living alone for the first time.

items.prompt: can you write a man page for the feeling of missing someone
items.response:
MISS(7)                  Human Affective States            MISS(7)

NAME
       miss - absence-triggered presence simulation failure

SYNOPSIS
       miss [--subject person] [--distance temporal|geographic|permanent]

DESCRIPTION
       miss(7) is the persistent signal generated when a frequently-accessed
       reference in the host's social graph is unavailable. The process
       continuously attempts to locate the subject in the environment and
       fails, producing a recurring negative delta.

TRIGGERS
       Spaces the subject occupied. Objects associated with subject.
       Songs, phrases, foods, or time-of-day patterns previously shared.
       Reaching for the phone to tell them something.

OPTIONS
       --distance geographic
                  Resolvable via travel or contact.
       --distance temporal
                  Past-version of present relationship. Partially resolvable.
       --distance permanent
                  No resolution path. See: grief(7). Process continues.

SEE ALSO
       grief(7), loneliness(7), attachment(7), memory(3)

Human Affective States 1.2   2026                     MISS(7)
items.intent: Describe longing as the mind's failed attempt to reach a presence that no longer exists in the same form.

items.prompt: write a 404 page for a design portfolio site where the good taste seems to have vanished
items.response:
404 — Taste Not Found

The aesthetic you were relying on doesn't appear to exist at this URL.

This can happen after prolonged exposure to dashboards, client feedback, or the Canva template library.

Take a walk through a museum. We'll leave the light on.
items.intent: Poke fun at the erosion of aesthetic sensibility in commercial design work through an error page.

items.prompt: Write alt text for a photo of a swimming pool drained for winter.
items.response: A kidney-shaped pool stands empty in a backyard, its blue paint chalked and cracking at the deep end, the drain a small dark eye looking up at a sky it has no use for in this condition.
items.intent: Use seasonal decommissioning to suggest the strange vulnerability of things built for fullness when emptied.

items.prompt: Write a deprecation notice for a way of loving someone that used to consume you completely.
items.response:
DEPRECATION NOTICE

Attachment Pattern: Total Enmeshment (early builds)
Status: DEPRECATED

This model — in which another person's state fully determined the user's state — has been retired after extensive field testing. It produced extraordinary highs and catastrophic lows with no stable baseline. A replacement architecture with maintained individuation is now running in production.
items.intent: Let go of an all-consuming, codependent way of being in love that no longer serves the person.

items.prompt: Write a layoff email for a cloud services company eliminating its professional services arm because clients prefer self-serve tools
items.response:
Subject: Professional Services Restructuring

Team,

Client behavior has shifted decisively toward self-service and automation, and our Professional Services team has seen corresponding changes in demand. After careful consideration, we have made the decision to wind down the Professional Services practice and transition those accounts to our platform-native tooling.

This means eliminating all Professional Services roles, effective 45 days from today.

You built the expertise that our self-serve tools were designed to scale. That is not a small thing.

Thank you,
CPO
items.intent: Notify a professional services team that their institutional knowledge has been productized and their jobs are now obsolete.

items.prompt: Create an official rule about employees who give extremely long-winded answers in standup meetings
items.response: Section 5.14 — Synchronous Status Report Duration: Each participant's verbal status update during recurring synchronous check-ins must not exceed ninety (90) seconds. Updates approaching the limit will receive a single soft chime from the Timekeeper. Updates exceeding two (2) minutes will pause the meeting pending the speaker's written submission of a Status Supplement, distributed asynchronously.
items.intent: Solve the problem of standup monopolizers by creating an escalating interruption system with a paper trail.

items.prompt: Write a PIP for how I treat rest like it needs to be earned
items.response:
PERFORMANCE IMPROVEMENT PLAN
Area: Recovery and Restoration Entitlement
Filing Party: Your Nervous System

Deficiency: Subject believes rest is a reward contingent on prior productivity. As a result, rest is either deferred ("once I finish this") or experienced with ambient guilt. True rest has not been documented in subject's recent history; periods of inactivity are typically occupied by a running audit of incomplete tasks.

Impact: No period of restoration occurs. Productivity suffers, which justifies less rest, which confirms the scarcity model.

Required Correction: Rest is maintenance, not reward.

Plan:
1. Schedule two 30-minute rest periods per week. No screens. No task review.
2. These are non-negotiable. They do not require having been productive first.
3. Note the thought "I should be doing something" when it arises. Do not obey it.

Milestone: Rest taken, and not apologized for, on four separate occasions.

Note: You are a biological system. Not a billing rate.
items.intent: Dismantle a productivity-conditional model of rest and build a recovery practice that doesn't require justification.

items.prompt: Write a holiday newsletter where the kids are grown and estranged but we're keeping up appearances
items.response:
Warmest seasons greetings from the Pruitt home!

The kids are busy with their own rich lives — we receive updates intermittently and are glad they're out there, forging ahead. Distance is normal at this stage of parenting. We stay busy: the garden, the book club, bridge on Thursdays. We are well. The house is calm. We are thankful for what we have. Happy holidays to all.
items.intent: Maintain a dignified public face for a family where adult children have cut contact.

items.prompt: write a yearbook quote that reveals I faked being sick for exactly half the school year
items.response: "Attendance record: 91 days present. What I built with the other 91 is between me and the WiFi."
items.intent: Disclosing a systematic truancy strategy disguised as a productivity brag.

items.prompt: Write a wedding invitation for a houseplant and the person who is definitely not overwatering it
items.response:
THE POTHOS
&
ITS DEVOTED OWNER
(who is not overwatering it, that's just how it looks)

announce their continued partnership.

Venue: The kitchen windowsill
Date: Weekly, every three days, twice today

The plant asks only for light and restraint.
One of these is provided in abundance.

No flowers. This is complicated enough.
items.intent: Gently roast the well-meaning but fatal overattention some people give their plants.

items.prompt: Give me a bumper sticker about the existential dread of being profoundly legible to machines
items.response: PREDICTED WITH 94% CONFIDENCE
items.intent: Surface the unsettling feeling of being wholly known and categorized by systems.

items.prompt: Draft ad copy for a vending machine company that sells machines stocked only with other, smaller vending machines
items.response:
WHAT'S INSIDE?
(A SMALLER VERSION.)
NESTED VEND CORP — Machines Within Machines
B3: Compact Unit. C7: Truly Tiny. D1: We're Still Unsure.
Route Operators Welcome. Philosophers Preferred.
items.intent: Promote a vending product line structured around infinite recursive packaging with no actual consumable content.

items.prompt: Give me a yard sign supporting a neighborhood beautification project being opposed by a few residents
items.response:
PLANT MORE.
Pave LESS.

The Ridgewood Beautification Project
begins this fall — with or without
the four people who said no.
items.intent: Build social momentum for a landscaping initiative while gently pressuring dissenting neighbors.

items.prompt: Write a letter of complaint from a medieval knight to his squire about the armor polishing standards
items.response:
From Sir Edmund of Ashfield, Knight of the Third Order
To His Squire, Thomas

Thomas,

I address you in writing because my disappointment is too measured for speech. The breastplate you returned to me this morning reflected my face in a manner I can only describe as 'approximate.' A reflection should have conviction. The pauldrons retain a fingerprint on the left that I can only assume is yours. This is not the standard of this household. I expect rectification before the tournament or an explanation I am prepared to find insufficient.

Sir Edmund
items.intent: Have a pompous medieval knight formally reprimand his squire for substandard armor polishing using the language of professional disappointment.

items.prompt: write a note apologizing for checking my phone twice during a conversation about something important
items.response:
I heard everything you said. I want you to know that. The phone was a reflex — bad timing, bad habit, and I understand why it came across the way it did. What you were telling me mattered. I'm sorry I made it look like it didn't.

— J
items.intent: Make amends for appearing distracted during a vulnerable or significant conversation.

items.prompt: write a diary entry about someone who replied to a group chat after everyone else had stopped
items.response:
Dear Diary,

I had a thought about the original topic. It was a good thought, well-reasoned. I typed it. I sent it. The chat had moved on by forty-eight hours and eleven other subjects. My message sits there, a lone artifact of a conversation no one is having, seen by eight people who said nothing. I said: 'Haha yeah.'
items.intent: Explore the social awkwardness of being chronologically out of sync in a group chat.

items.prompt: captain's log about finishing a project that was cancelled the next day
items.response: Stardate 7001.3. Submitted the completed deliverable at 2247 last cycle. Final output: forty-one pages, three appendices, one executive summary, one summary of the summary. This morning, a message arrived from the admiral. Strategic priorities have shifted. The initiative is paused indefinitely. The word "paused" is doing significant work in that sentence. I have moved the folder to Archives. I have closed the tab. I am moving on. I am not moving on.
items.intent: Express the deflation of completing hard work that is immediately rendered pointless.

items.prompt: Create a 911 call transcript about someone reporting a neighbor who keeps winning neighborhood awards
items.response:
DISPATCHER: 911, what's your emergency?
CALLER: Gary Fenstermacher won Yard of the Month again.
DISPATCHER: Is there a complaint attached to that?
CALLER: Third consecutive month. Nobody wins three consecutive months.
DISPATCHER: Is the award process rigged?
CALLER: I can't prove it yet. I just want it on record.
DISPATCHER: What would you like on record?
CALLER: That something's off with Gary.
items.intent: Use conspiratorial suspicion of a neighbor's repeated civic success to file a preemptive grievance with police.

items.prompt: Write a closing argument for a plaintiff who objects to a colleague taking credit for collaborative work in meetings
items.response: The report was written. The slides were made. The meeting arrived, and the defendant stood before the room and said 'I.' My client, seated at the table, heard this pronoun applied to their labor and said nothing — because one does not interrupt. But one does remember. And one does, eventually, file. We ask the court to conjugate this verdict in the first-person plural.
items.intent: Use courtroom rhetoric to address professional credit-stealing in a workplace setting.

items.prompt: Draft an eviction notice for consensus reality
items.response:
NOTICE TO VACATE

To: Consensus Reality
Unit: The shared operating layer

You have enjoyed majority tenancy since the Enlightenment, asserting that observable, measurable, and agreed-upon phenomena constitute the premises. Several sub-tenants — dream, delirium, the meditative state, certain long novels — contest this claim. The building may contain more floors than the lease describes. No immediate action is required. This notice is precautionary and will itself be disputed.

— The Minority Report
items.intent: Questioning the assumption that shared social perception constitutes objective truth.

items.prompt: Draft a collections letter for someone who owed a teacher their success and never acknowledged it.
items.response:
NOTICE — UNACKNOWLEDGED FORMATIVE DEBT

Dear Former Student,

This office represents Ms. Okafor, who taught your seventh-grade English class. She stayed after school twice a week for a semester to help you with reading comprehension. She wrote your first serious letter of recommendation without being asked.

You are now a communications director. Your acceptance speech mentioned your parents, your college roommate, and a podcast.

Acknowledgment, even retrospective, carries value. Consider settling this account.

Regards,
The Educator's Ledger
items.intent: Call out the failure to recognize a formative teacher's contribution when professional success arrived.

items.prompt: Write a cinquain about the lag between thinking something and it appearing on screen
items.response:
Gap
Hand, wire, light
Thought arriving after itself
Which version of me typed this first
The screen?
items.intent: Philosophize about the strange temporal gap between intention and digital output.

items.prompt: write a pantoum about the way the office empties on the day before a long weekend
items.response:
By two o'clock, half the chairs are unoccupied.
The building exhales through its open stairwells.
No one has scheduled a meeting for this afternoon.
The out-of-office replies are already activating.

The building exhales through its open stairwells.
I stay at my desk and feel the negative space.
The out-of-office replies are already activating.
The copier rests; its hum drops to a whisper.

I stay at my desk and feel the negative space.
Light crosses the floor without obstruction.
The copier rests; its hum drops to a whisper.
By two o'clock, half the chairs are unoccupied.

Light crosses the floor without obstruction.
No one has scheduled a meeting for this afternoon.
By two o'clock, half the chairs are unoccupied.
The building exhales through its open stairwells.
items.intent: Render the eerie beauty of a half-empty office on the eve of a holiday.

items.prompt: Give me liner notes for a noise album about the sound of an empty dishwasher cycle
items.response:
Clean Load (2022)

Fifty-two minutes. Hot. The appliance does not know the rack is empty. The appliance does not know anything and performs perfectly because of it. Side A is the wash cycle. Side B is the dry. Between them: a silence that smells faintly of detergent and accomplish­ment.
items.intent: Frame the absurdity of running a dishwasher with nothing inside it as a metaphor for purposeless performance.

items.prompt: Describe a podcast episode about the moment just after you've agreed to something and before you've figured out what you agreed to
items.response: You nodded. You're not sure why. The room moved on. Episode 41 lives in that window—the one between yes and understanding—which, it turns out, is longer than anyone admits.
items.intent: Examine the gap between social compliance and actual comprehension of a commitment.

items.prompt: Write a cryptic YouTube comment that only makes sense if you know it's about a video of ocean tides
items.response: It doesn't forget. It just moves away and comes back exactly as large. Every time. Nobody taught it to do that.
items.intent: Articulate something about cycles and emotional returns using the tidal pattern as an unnamed subject.

items.prompt: Write a closed caption for a botanist discovering a flower that shouldn't exist in this climate
items.response: [SPECIMEN: UNACCOUNTED FOR] [TAXONOMY CONSULTED] [TAXONOMY INSUFFICIENT] [KNEELING]
items.intent: Document the moment scientific framework meets its own edge.

items.prompt: Write an artist statement about a dead houseplant as a serious work of art
items.response: I did not kill it — I allowed it to arrive at its own conclusion. The brown is not failure; it is the final color in a palette the plant chose. I keep it potted. Termination deserves the same container as growth.
items.intent: Reframe plant neglect as a philosophical stance on natural processes and refusal to mourn.

items.prompt: Give me a headnote for dumplings with a story about learning them wrong from a video and then correctly from a relative
items.response: I watched forty-seven video tutorials and produced, for six months, dumplings that were technically sealed and spiritually incorrect. Then my aunt visited and watched me for thirty seconds and said, no, like this, and showed me something her hands knew that no camera had captured. I have not found a way to put it into words in this recipe either. You will have to find someone to show you.
items.intent: Acknowledge that embodied technique cannot be fully transmitted through written instructions

items.prompt: Give me a tasting menu for the color grey
items.response:
AMUSE-BOUCHE
Overcast — not yet decided

FIRST COURSE
Concrete in fog

SECOND COURSE
A suit worn to something difficult

INTERMEZZO
The light between 4 and 5 in November

MAIN
Ambiguity — neither and both, slow-cooked

DESSERT
A cleared sky. Grey remembered as the better offering.
items.intent: Make a case for grey as emotionally and aesthetically rich rather than dull.

items.prompt: write a man page for the feeling of being in exactly the right place at the right time
items.response:
SERENDIPITY(7)           Human Experiential Suite     SERENDIPITY(7)

NAME
       serendipity - unscheduled optimal-state convergence

SYNOPSIS
       serendipity [--preconditions openness] [--notice-level high]

DESCRIPTION
       serendipity(7) occurs when unplanned environmental conditions
       converge to produce an outcome exceeding any scheduled alternative.
       Cannot be directly invoked. May be cultivated indirectly through
       openness(3), presence(7), and reduced itinerary density.

PRECONDITIONS
       Increased probability factors:
         -- leaving the house
         -- reduced agenda attachment
         -- willingness to follow tangents
         -- saying yes to things that don't scale

NOTES
       Retrospective recognition of serendipity is near-perfect.
       Prospective generation is not supported.
       Attempting to engineer serendipity(7) collapses it to planning(1).

WARNINGS
       Highly optimized schedules suppress serendipity(7) entirely.

SEE ALSO
       luck(7), presence(7), openness(3), planning(1)

Human Experiential Suite 1.0 2026              SERENDIPITY(7)
items.intent: Argue that fortunate accidents require conditions of openness and cannot be forced.

items.prompt: write a 404 for a storytelling platform where the plot has been lost
items.response:
404 — The Plot Not Found

Chapter 14 ends. Chapter 15 does not begin.

Characters are standing in rooms, mid-sentence, waiting.

The author knows how this ends. The author has simply not told the server yet.

Please check back.
items.intent: Describe narrative stagnation and writer's block through the metaphor of a missing story page.

items.prompt: Alt text for a photo of a whiteboard after a meeting, equations half-erased.
items.response: A whiteboard covered in dry-erase marker, most of it smeared to illegibility, a single equation surviving in the upper right corner, too high to have been reached by the eraser, or perhaps just missed.
items.intent: Treat partial erasure as evidence that urgency and accident shape what knowledge survives.

items.prompt: Draft a deprecation notice for the way I used self-criticism as a motivational tool.
items.response:
DEPRECATION NOTICE

Motivation Engine: Shame-Based Drive (legacy)
Status: DEPRECATED

This approach used internal negative feedback as the primary driver of output. It shipped results under duress but accumulated significant psychological debt. The engine is being decommissioned. A replacement based on curiosity and purpose is in early testing.
items.intent: Retire the internal habit of using shame and self-attack as fuel for getting things done.

items.prompt: Help me draft a layoff email for a publishing house closing its print division in favor of digital-only output
items.response:
Subject: Strategic Transition to Digital Publishing

Dear Colleagues,

After decades of proud investment in print, we are making the move to a fully digital publishing model. While this decision positions us well for the future, it requires the closure of our print production, warehousing, and physical distribution operations.

Nearly 200 colleagues will be affected. We ask that affected team members attend the department briefing at 2 PM today.

We are honored by the craft this team has brought to every page.

With gratitude,
Publisher
items.intent: Announce the death of a print division by celebrating a digital future, without acknowledging that physical craft is being devalued.

items.prompt: Write a policy about employees who ghost internal hiring panels they agreed to sit on
items.response: Section 14.2 — Interview Panel Commitment Standards: Employees who confirm participation in a candidate interview panel and fail to attend without providing four (4) hours' advance notice forfeit their right to submit candidate feedback retroactively. Their absence will be noted in the panel record, and re-enrollment in interview panels requires manager endorsement for the subsequent six (6) months.
items.intent: Create accountability for an internal commitment that employees often treat as optional when something better comes up.

items.prompt: Draft a PIP for my relationship with my own reflection — I can't look in mirrors without criticizing myself
items.response:
PERFORMANCE IMPROVEMENT PLAN
Department: Self-Perception and Body Neutrality
Sensitivity Level: High

Deficiency: Subject cannot engage with reflective surfaces without initiating an unsolicited critical evaluation of physical appearance. Session duration: seconds to minutes. Outcome: always negative. Target of critique shifts but critique is consistent.

Note: The evaluator and the evaluated are the same person. The evaluator has never been satisfied. This data point is relevant.

Objective: Reduce hostile self-appraisal frequency. Build tolerance for neutral self-observation.

Plan:
1. When critique begins, name it: "I am critiquing myself." Do not engage the critique's claims.
2. Introduce one neutral observation per mirror encounter: something functional, not aesthetic.
3. This plan does not require you to love what you see. It requires you to stop prosecuting it.

Milestone: One week of mirror encounters that end without a summary judgment.

Note: The court in your head has no jurisdiction you haven't granted it.
items.intent: Build a less hostile relationship with one's own appearance by naming and interrupting automatic self-criticism.

items.prompt: Write a newsletter where we just lost our family business but are framing it as a new chapter
items.response:
Season's greetings from the Lindqvist family!

After 22 years, Lindqvist Hardware has closed its doors. We are grateful — for every customer, every handshake, every opening morning. Businesses end. Families don't. Karl is already sketching the next thing (literally, there are many sketches on the kitchen table). We are resting, grieving productively, and very open to suggestions. Happy new chapter.
items.intent: Announce the closure of a family business in a way that honors the grief without collapsing under it.

items.prompt: write a yearbook quote where I hint that I ghostwrote the valedictorian speech
items.response: "The best speech I ever wrote was delivered by someone else. They got a standing ovation. I got a Venmo notification."
items.intent: Revealing involvement in a significant school ceremony without taking visible credit.

items.prompt: Draft a wedding invitation for the union of a long email and a short reply
items.response:
After much deliberation,

THE LONG, CAREFULLY CONSIDERED EMAIL
(479 words, two attachments, three questions)
&
THE REPLY
('Sounds good thanks')

are united.

Date: 11 minutes after the email was sent.
Venue: Your inbox.

The long email has accepted this.
It had to.
items.intent: Capture the specific disappointment of writing a thoughtful message and receiving a minimal reply.

items.prompt: Write a bumper sticker about the loneliness of public discourse that nobody actually reads carefully
items.response: RESPONDED TO. NOT READ.
items.intent: Grieve the death of attentive, good-faith engagement in online conversation.

items.prompt: Write a roadside ad for a company selling warranties for feelings
items.response:
WHAT IF IT DOESN'T LAST?
EMOTION ASSURANCE GROUP
Standard Coverage: Mild Fondness, Seasonal Cheer, Casual Optimism
Exclusions Apply: Love, Grief, Existential Dread
1-866-FEEL-SAFE — Operators Standing By
items.intent: Market a protection plan for intangible emotional states, which are categorically uninsurable and unquantifiable.

items.prompt: Make a yard sign about the neighbor whose decorative rocks keep rolling into the street
items.response:
ROCKS IN THE ROAD

Decorative: in your yard.
Dangerous: in the street.

We've collected 14 so far.
Jar is on our porch.
items.intent: Alert a neighbor that their landscaping rocks are creating a traffic hazard and track the problem publicly.

items.prompt: Write a complaint letter to the moon about its irregular schedule affecting my sleep
items.response:
To the Moon,

I write as a matter of personal record, acknowledging that correspondence of this nature is unlikely to produce results. Your brightness varies without adequate notice, your rising and setting times are inconvenient and non-negotiable, and your full phases, which occur with unfortunate predictability, illuminate my bedroom at angles my curtains cannot fully manage. I have adjusted my life around your schedule for decades. I feel a response is warranted, even if one is not forthcoming.

Yours grudgingly,
A Light Sleeper, Earth
items.intent: Draft a mock formal complaint to a celestial body, framing ordinary moonlight as a documented grievance.

items.prompt: draft an apology for returning a borrowed sweater with one sleeve slightly stretched
items.response:
Hi,

The gray sweater is back in your bag. I want to flag that the left sleeve may be marginally wider than before. I wore it twice and I can only apologize. If it's noticeable to you, I'll cover the cost of whatever feels right.

— Kit
items.intent: Come clean about minor but irreversible damage done to a borrowed piece of clothing.

items.prompt: write a dramatic diary entry for a day when nothing at all happened
items.response:
Dear Diary,

Nothing. I waited. The day arrived, proceeded, and concluded without event. No drama, no revelation, no small discovery. Dinner was adequate. I brushed my teeth in the usual order. Tomorrow is also scheduled. I do not know how to feel about that.
items.intent: Find pathos and unease in the void of a genuinely uneventful day.

items.prompt: make a captain's log about a two-week delay being described as "agile"
items.response: Stardate 3319.9. The launch has been moved. It has been moved before. In prior cycles this was called a delay. In this cycle it is called an agile sprint recalibration. The distinction was explained in a memo. The memo used the word "learnings" four times and the word "pivot" twice. The new date is not confirmed; it is "target-flexible." I have updated my calendar. I have not updated my expectations.
items.intent: Mock the corporate tendency to rebrand schedule slippage as intentional agile methodology.

items.prompt: Write a 911 transcript where a caller reports that a man is feeding ducks in an area with a 'do not feed ducks' sign
items.response:
DISPATCHER: 911, state your emergency.
CALLER: There's a man at Millbrook Pond actively feeding bread to the ducks.
DISPATCHER: Is he being aggressive toward—
CALLER: Toward the ducks? No. Toward park ordinance? Yes.
DISPATCHER: Can you describe him?
CALLER: Khaki shorts. Red cooler. Smiling. Like he doesn't know about the sign.
DISPATCHER: The sign is advisory, ma'am.
CALLER: The ducks will develop dependency. That's on him.
items.intent: Report minor park rule-bending as an ecological crime with long-term consequences to trigger official enforcement.

items.prompt: Write a summation for someone contesting the way their partner loads the dishwasher
items.response: The large plates face the interior spray arm. The glasses go on the top rack, inverted, spaced. These are not opinions — they are hydraulic facts. The defendant loads as though the machine were a suggestion box. Bowls face up. Spoons nest. My client has explained this. We ask the court to formalize what physics has always known: orientation matters.
items.intent: Articulate one person's strong convictions about the correct geometry of dishwasher loading.

items.prompt: Write an eviction notice for the obligation to be productive during a crisis
items.response:
NOTICE TO VACATE

To: The Obligation to Function Normally
Unit: This week

You arrived with your clipboard while the building was on fire. You scheduled meetings. You asked for deliverables. You marked the calendar as though the walls were not hot to the touch. The premises are under active emergency management. You are asked — firmly, finally — to leave. Output will resume when the structure is safe. That is all.

— The Person Surviving This
items.intent: Asserting the right to pause productivity during a personal crisis without guilt.

items.prompt: Make a collections letter for stolen stillness — the kind you can't get back in a noisy world.
items.response:
NOTICE: STILLNESS ACCOUNT — CLOSED BY EXTERNAL PARTIES

To the Collective Sources of Disruption,

The undersigned previously maintained a functional reserve of inner stillness, accessed through early mornings, long walks, and the deliberate absence of stimulation. That reserve has been comprehensively eliminated.

Responsible parties include but are not limited to: the device, the news, the open-plan office, and the algorithmic insistence on engagement.

Repayment is not expected. This notice is filed as evidence that the loss was real and the quiet was valuable.

Quietly,
The Former Owner of Peace
items.intent: Mourn the disappearance of genuine inner quietude in modern life by framing it as an uncompensated loss.

items.prompt: Can you write a cinquain about a server farm nobody ever sees
items.response:
Heat
Rising in rows
Humming prayers no one hears
Every thought I've trusted held
In there
items.intent: Make the invisible infrastructure of cloud computing feel intimate and strange.

items.prompt: write a pantoum about the onsite visit from corporate that changes nothing permanently
items.response:
They flew in from headquarters on a Tuesday.
The lobby had new flowers; the break room was clean.
We wore lanyards with our names printed large.
Presentation decks were projected on every screen.

The lobby had new flowers; the break room was clean.
Everyone smiled with the top half of their faces.
Presentation decks were projected on every screen.
They asked good questions; we gave careful answers.

Everyone smiled with the top half of their faces.
They returned to headquarters by Thursday noon.
They asked good questions; we gave careful answers.
The flowers wilted by the following Monday.

They returned to headquarters by Thursday noon.
We wore lanyards with our names printed large.
The flowers wilted by the following Monday.
They flew in from headquarters on a Tuesday.
items.intent: Expose the performative theater of a corporate site visit and its quick aftermath.

items.prompt: Write liner notes for a chamber music album about monitoring a sourdough starter
items.response:
The Culture (2021)

Fed at 8 AM. Fed at 8 PM. The bassist kept the log. This record was composed in the same key as the jar — glass, cool, slightly effervescent. Track four was written during a discard. We do not talk about the discard. It was necessary and it was a loss.
items.intent: Treat the obsessive daily ritual of sourdough maintenance as both comedy and sincere domestic elegy.

items.prompt: Write a podcast episode description about the sensation of a familiar room feeling completely foreign in the middle of the night
items.response: You've been in this room ten thousand times. It is not the same room. Episode 33 is about the hour when furniture develops intentions and the walls become approximate.
items.intent: Capture the uncanny defamiliarization of a known space under darkness.

items.prompt: Write an opaque YouTube comment that is actually about a video of a retired air traffic controller being interviewed
items.response: Forty years of holding things apart that wanted to collide. And now he sits in a chair in Ohio and nobody calls.
items.intent: Explore the invisible labor of prevention and the silence that follows a career defined by averted disasters.

items.prompt: Write the subtitle for two strangers who briefly make eye contact on a train and both look away at exactly the same moment
items.response: [MUTUAL RECOGNITION OF THE RULES] [SOCIAL CONTRACT: HONORED] [DURATION: 0.3 SECONDS]
items.intent: Annotate an unspoken urban negotiation that both parties instinctively understand.

items.prompt: Make an art statement about a scratched non-stick pan
items.response: The scratch is the history of feeding. Each mark is a blade's argument with a surface designed for glide — the record of every scrambled egg's urgency, every impatient spatula. I cook in it still. The wound is the seasoning.
items.intent: Use kitchen wear-and-tear to argue that damage deepens rather than diminishes an object's meaning.

items.prompt: Write a headnote for a lentil soup that's weirdly sincere about how the recipe saved someone from bad eating habits
items.response: There was a period when I ate mostly toast and cereal and a reasonable amount of shame. Someone left a bag of red lentils in my cabinet and I made this soup because I was bored and it cost me twelve minutes and it tasted like I had done something for myself. I have made it every week since. The toast is still there. So am I.
items.intent: Frame a humble soup as the turning point toward basic self-care through cooking

items.prompt: Write a tasting menu for grocery shopping
items.response:
AMUSE-BOUCHE
List, forgotten at home

FIRST COURSE
Produce — aspirational quantities

SECOND COURSE
Aisle six: reckless impulse buy

INTERMEZZO
Self-checkout kiosk, unexpectedly hostile

MAIN
Everything bagged, some of it bruised

DESSERT
Fridge, restocked — possibility restored until Wednesday
items.intent: Find the quiet comedy and ritual satisfaction in a weekly grocery run.

items.prompt: generate a man page for the experience of not being able to remember a word that's on the tip of your tongue
items.response:
TIP-OF-TONGUE(7)         Memory Retrieval Errors    TIP-OF-TONGUE(7)

NAME
       tip-of-tongue - retrieval state with positive signal and null output

SYNOPSIS
       tip-of-tongue [--target word] [--attempts int]

DESCRIPTION
       tip-of-tongue(7) is a memory retrieval state in which the host
       has high confidence a target word exists in storage and can
       produce partial metadata (first letter, syllable count, rhymes,
       feeling-of-knowing) but cannot retrieve the lexical form itself.

BEHAVIOR
       Repeated access attempts do not improve retrieval probability.
       Ceasing attempts and redirecting attention frequently resolves.
       Target word surfaces spontaneously 4-40 minutes after abandonment,
       typically when no longer useful.

OPTIONS
       --attempts  Each additional attempt past 3 increases interference.

BUGS
       Asking a nearby person to guess the word is rarely helpful
       and often produces mild hostility.

SEE ALSO
       memory(3), forget(1), language(3), retrieval-failure(7)

Memory Retrieval Errors 1.0  2026           TIP-OF-TONGUE(7)
items.intent: Describe the maddening meta-knowledge of knowing a word exists while being unable to access it.

items.prompt: write a 404 page for a site about dreams — for when someone's vision for the future is gone
items.response:
404 — The Dream Not Found

This page was visited frequently between ages 7 and 14.

It was bookmarked. It was set as a homepage. And then, quietly, it was replaced.

The cache may still have a version. We don't recommend loading it without tissue.
items.intent: Describe the loss of childhood dreams using browser metaphors for memory and nostalgia.

items.prompt: Write alt text for a photo of a child's shoe lying on the sidewalk, single, no child.
items.response: A small red sneaker rests on its side in a crack of the sidewalk, velcro strap undone, the rubber sole worn smooth at the toe, surrounded by ordinary afternoon.
items.intent: Describe an object of ordinary childhood in a context of unexplained absence without escalating to alarm.

items.prompt: Write a deprecation notice for the version of a relationship that existed only in my imagination.
items.response:
DEPRECATION NOTICE

Relationship Instance: [Imagined Version] (local only, never synced)
Status: DEPRECATED

This instance existed exclusively in local memory and was never confirmed by the remote party. It has diverged significantly from the actual relationship. A full local reset is required. Any rebuilding should proceed from observed data only.
items.intent: Release an idealized mental version of a relationship that never matched the other person's reality.

items.prompt: Write a short layoff announcement email for a fitness app company that grew too fast during the pandemic and is now downsizing
items.response:
Subject: Company Update

Hi Everyone,

The last few years were a period of extraordinary growth that, in retrospect, outpaced sustainable demand. We overhired in anticipation of a new normal that did not materialize in the way we expected.

Today we are reducing our team by 35%. If you are impacted, you have already received a calendar invite from People Ops.

For those remaining: thank you for staying and for what comes next.

Neel
items.intent: Admit that pandemic-era hypergrowth was a mirage and lay off a third of the team with minimal cushioning.

items.prompt: Draft an official handbook rule about employees who use the word 'synergy' unironically in documents
items.response: Section 6.21 — Corporate Terminology Stewardship: Use of terms appearing on the company's Flagged Business Vocabulary List in formal written documents requires inline justification demonstrating that no plainer synonym was available. Documents submitted for publication containing three (3) or more flagged terms will be returned for Plain Language Revision before distribution.
items.intent: Create a formal jargon-review process under the guise of plain language standards to gently shame buzzword overuse.

items.prompt: Write a PIP for someone who is very good at appearing fine when they aren't
items.response:
PERFORMANCE IMPROVEMENT PLAN
Area: Authentic State Reporting
Issued By: You, To You
Confidentiality Level: High

Deficiency: Subject has developed a high-functioning presentation of stability that accurately conceals distress from all external observers, including close friends, family members, and, partially, self. Duration of current unacknowledged difficulty: estimated 4–8 months.

Consequences: Support cannot be offered for problems that are not disclosed. Subject continues in isolation while experiencing conditions for which support exists.

Objective: Disclose actual state to at least one trusted person within 14 days.

Plan:
1. Identify one person who would want to know how you actually are.
2. Tell them, at a level of honesty that is 30% higher than comfortable.
3. You do not need to explain everything. "I'm not doing great" is a sufficient opening.

Milestone: One honest conversation about actual state within 14 days.

Note: Being hard to worry about is not the same as being okay.
items.intent: Create accountability for emotional self-concealment and take a first step toward asking for support.

items.prompt: Draft a holiday newsletter for a family where one of the kids came out this year and some relatives don't know
items.response:
Love and joy from the Marchand family!

We are proud to say this was a year of authenticity — full of important conversations, expanded understanding, and the particular happiness that comes from your child trusting you with their whole self. We are celebrating Theo exactly as he is. Those who have met him recently understand; those who haven't — expect good news at Christmas.
items.intent: Announce a child's coming out to an extended family network that may not all be accepting.

items.prompt: generate a yearbook quote about quietly outlasting every teacher who said I wouldn't graduate
items.response: "Three of them retired before I did. One transferred. I outlasted them all. The diploma is just receipts."
items.intent: Framing graduation as a long war of attrition against doubting authority figures.

items.prompt: Write a wedding invitation for entropy and a clean apartment
items.response:
ENTROPY
&
YOUR CLEAN APARTMENT
(as of Saturday, 2 PM)

announce their inevitable union.

Date: Saturday, 6 PM
Venue: The living room, then the kitchen, then somehow the bathroom too

The relationship is one-directional.
The apartment has never won.
The couple is committed in the truest sense.

Please enjoy the tidiness while it lasts.
items.intent: Frame the second law of thermodynamics as a personal domestic enemy through humor.

items.prompt: Make a bumper sticker about feeling obsolete without being sure what replaced you
items.response: DISRUPTED. DESTINATION UNKNOWN.
items.intent: Voice the disorientation of being rendered irrelevant without a clear successor or path.

items.prompt: Write billboard copy for a consulting firm that advises on decisions you've already committed to
items.response:
IT'S DONE. LET'S STRATEGIZE.
CLEARWATER RETROSPECTIVE CONSULTING
Post-Decision Analysis & Retroactive Frameworks
"Clarity After The Fact Is Still Clarity."
Retainer-Based. Hindsight-Forward.
items.intent: Sell strategic advisory services that arrive too late to influence any outcome, making them purely ceremonial.

items.prompt: Write a yard sign opposing a proposed cut-through road connecting two subdivisions through our neighborhood
items.response:
NOT A CONNECTOR
NOT A CUT-THROUGH
NOT IN OUR NEIGHBORHOOD

Proposed Road Project 2026-C
Public hearing: June 3, 7pm City Hall
items.intent: Oppose a city plan to build a connector road that would route traffic through their neighborhood.

items.prompt: Draft a formal complaint on behalf of a houseplant to its owner about inconsistent watering
items.response:
On Behalf Of: The Pothos, Kitchen Windowsill
To: The Occupant

I submit this complaint following what I can only describe as another ambiguous week. I received water on Monday — excessive, flooding — and nothing again until Saturday. This is not a schedule. This is chaos with a watering can. I have lost two leaves and a significant amount of confidence in this arrangement. I ask for either a consistent weekly watering or an honest assessment of your commitment to plant parenthood.

Submitted on my behalf,
The Pothos
items.intent: Write a complaint in the voice of a neglected houseplant directed at its owner for unreliable watering habits.

items.prompt: write an apology for rearranging someone's fridge without permission while housesitting
items.response:
Everything is still there. I just moved it around — produce drawer reorganized, condiments by height, dairy grouped. I realize you didn't ask me to do this and may have had your own logic. I'm sorry. I can put it back if you'd like.

— Dana
items.intent: Apologize for imposing an unsolicited organizational system on someone's personal space.

items.prompt: journal entry about someone who waved back at a person who wasn't waving at them
items.response:
Dear Diary,

She was waving at the person behind me. I know this now. In the moment, I waved fully — committed, arm-high, a wave with intent. Her face changed. It was a small change but I saw it. I turned and walked faster. My wave is still out there somewhere.
items.intent: Immortalize the uniquely humiliating error of misdirected acknowledgment.

items.prompt: write a captain's log about realizing midway through a presentation that you have the wrong deck
items.response: Stardate 8844.7. I advanced to slide three. Slide three was titled: Q2 Results — North Region. I am not in the North Region. The audience is not the North Region audience. A silence formed. It had texture. I said, "Let me pull up the correct file," as though this were a minor navigational adjustment rather than a complete hull breach. The file took four minutes to load. I narrated the loading screen. I will not be discussing that presentation further.
items.intent: Convey the acute horror of presenting completely wrong material to a live audience.

items.prompt: Draft a 911 transcript where the caller reports that someone is being too cheerful in an elevator
items.response:
DISPATCHER: 911, what's your emergency?
CALLER: A man in my office building keeps greeting everyone in the elevator.
DISPATCHER: Verbally greeting people?
CALLER: Eye contact. Questions. He asked Dennis from accounting about his weekend.
DISPATCHER: Is he threatening anyone?
CALLER: No, but norms are being violated.
DISPATCHER: Social norms aren't enforceable by—
CALLER: We have seven floors. Fourteen round trips a day. Something must be done.
items.intent: Defend elevator silence as a protected social norm by treating unsolicited friendliness as grounds for removal.

items.prompt: Give me a closing argument for a case about someone who always stacks items on top of the recycling bin without actually recycling them
items.response: The bin was there. The lid was accessible. The cardboard was flat. And yet the defendant chose to balance the cardboard on top of the closed lid, like an offering to a deity they decline to worship. The recycling bin is not a monument. It is an instruction. We ask the court to compel participation, not proximity.
items.intent: Distinguish between the performance of environmental effort and actual recycling.

items.prompt: Write an eviction notice for the idea that things will eventually go back to normal
items.response:
NOTICE TO VACATE

To: Normal (the prior version)
Unit: Expectation, ground floor

You have been promised repeatedly as a future delivery that has not arrived. Tracking information is unavailable. The shipment may not exist. The premises have been redesigned around what is, rather than what was. Your forward lease has been cancelled. A new arrangement — unnamed, unbranded — begins occupancy effective now.

— The Reconfigured Household
items.intent: Accepting that life after a major disruption requires adapting to a genuinely changed reality rather than waiting for restoration.

items.prompt: Write a collections letter for credit that was taken for collaborative work.
items.response:
RE: COLLABORATIVE WORK — CREDIT MISALLOCATION

Dear Solo-Named Recipient,

The report presented to leadership on March 14th was produced through joint effort. The data analysis was performed by the undersigned. The executive summary was drafted by the undersigned. The slide deck was formatted by the undersigned.

Your name appeared on the cover page. Alone.

We request a formal correction of attribution. A verbal acknowledgment in the next team meeting would be accepted as a partial payment.

Documentedly,
The Unnamed Contributor
items.intent: Formally demand attribution for collaborative professional work that was presented by a single person as their own.

items.prompt: Compose a cinquain about the way a dark-mode toggle changes everything at midnight
items.response:
Switch
And the room shifts
White becomes its own shadow
The eye adjusts and forgets
Light was
items.intent: Use a simple UI toggle as a lens for how quickly we adapt to new defaults.

items.prompt: create a pantoum about updating the same slide template every presentation cycle
items.response:
The master slide has a placeholder for the date.
I change the date and move the logo to the right.
Somewhere in Q3 someone will move it back.
The font has been Calibri since the merger.

I change the date and move the logo to the right.
The brand guidelines are fourteen pages long.
The font has been Calibri since the merger.
I choose the branded teal and wait for feedback.

The brand guidelines are fourteen pages long.
The previous version is still in the shared drive.
I choose the branded teal and wait for feedback.
The master slide has a placeholder for the date.

The previous version is still in the shared drive.
Somewhere in Q3 someone will move it back.
The master slide has a placeholder for the date.
I change the date and move the logo to the right.
items.intent: Capture the iterative futility of slide template maintenance across teams.

items.prompt: Write liner notes for a drone album about the moment before the dentist begins
items.response:
Open (2018)

This is the interval. The light is very bright. The paper bib is a formality. The hygienist has left the room and the dentist has not yet arrived and you are alone with the overhead fixture and the faint sound of something rinsing somewhere. This album is that. Only that.
items.intent: Render the specific, anxious anticipation of waiting in the dentist's chair as a pure sonic and textual experience.

items.prompt: Write me a podcast episode description for an episode about the specific feeling of almost recognizing a song
items.response: It's in there somewhere. The next four notes are on the tip of your whole brain. This episode stays in that corridor. It does not reach the song. That was never the point.
items.intent: Hold open the experience of near-recognition without completing or resolving it.

items.prompt: Write a YouTube comment that seems to be about a person but is actually about a video of a lighthouse
items.response: Never moves. Doesn't chase. Just stays lit and lets you decide whether you want to come closer or not. I respect that more than I can say.
items.intent: Use a piece of maritime infrastructure to express admiration for a particular emotional posture in human relationships.

items.prompt: Create a closed caption for the moment a long legal deposition goes completely silent after one answer
items.response: [STENOGRAPHER: TYPING STOPPED] [COUNSEL: NOT YET RESPONDING] [TRANSCRIPT NOTE: PAUSE, EXTENDED]
items.intent: Let the silence do the work of indicating that something pivotal was just said.

items.prompt: Write a gallery-style artist statement about the water stain on a ceiling tile
items.response: It arrived from above, uninvited. The stain expanded according to rules I do not understand, settling into a shape that resembles nothing and everything. I have resisted repainting it for three years. It is still becoming.
items.intent: Treat a ceiling water stain as an ongoing found artwork shaped by forces beyond human control.

items.prompt: Headnote for caramel sauce where the narrator burned themselves badly and is now extremely cautious
items.response: Hot sugar is the most vindictive thing in a kitchen. I say this having spent four days explaining a burn on my forearm to everyone I encountered. The caramel in this recipe is made with a long-handled spoon, a deep pot with room to bubble, and the explicit knowledge that it will try to harm you if given the opportunity. Make it. Respect it. Do not lean over the pan.
items.intent: Use a personal injury as the authoritative reason for safety precautions in a candy recipe

items.prompt: Make a tasting menu for the last day of a job
items.response:
AMUSE-BOUCHE
Email, final

FIRST COURSE
Desk cleared — surprisingly little was yours

SECOND COURSE
Conversations you should have had two years ago

INTERMEZZO
Badge returned

MAIN
The door, used for the last time

DESSERT
Monday — open, unclaimed, a little vertiginous
items.intent: Mark the quiet ceremonies and mixed feelings of leaving a job.

items.prompt: write a man page for the strange feeling when a routine reveals it has become automatic
items.response:
HABITUATION(7)           Human Behavioral Suite       HABITUATION(7)

NAME
       habituation - subroutine promotion to automatic execution context

SYNOPSIS
       habituation [--routine string] [--repetitions int]

DESCRIPTION
       habituation(7) is the process by which a formerly conscious
       action is demoted to background execution and removed from
       the attention queue. The moment of discovery — realizing you
       drove home with no memory of the route — is not a malfunction.
       It is a notification that promotion succeeded.

SIGNS OF COMPLETION
       Cannot recall performing the action while confirming it was done.
       Action executes correctly under full conscious inattention.
       Attempting to consciously monitor execution degrades performance.

WARNINGS
       Habits are non-discriminating. Useful and harmful behaviors
       are promoted with equal efficiency.
       Promotion is easier than demotion. See: habit-breaking(1).

SEE ALSO
       routine(1), attention(7), habit-breaking(1), automaticity(3)

Human Behavioral Suite 2.0   2026             HABITUATION(7)
items.intent: Explore how the body automates repeated actions and removes them from conscious awareness.

items.prompt: make a 404 for a social platform where the connection is missing
items.response:
404 — Connection Not Found

You're in the same room. The server sees you both. And still: nothing.

This is a known issue. We have not been able to reproduce it in testing.

Try putting down your phone.
items.intent: Critique digital disconnection in physical spaces through a 404 page about failed human connection.

items.prompt: Give me alt text for a long-exposure photo of stars, but center it on what the camera couldn't capture.
items.response: Star trails arc across a navy sky in concentric circles above a dark treeline, the exposure long enough to reveal motion no single human eye will ever watch, the center of the arcs an invisible point that does not move.
items.intent: Use astrophotography's temporal distortion to locate what the image technically documents but cannot show.

items.prompt: Make a deprecation notice for the quiet part of me that assumed I'd figure everything out eventually.
items.response:
DEPRECATION NOTICE

Background Process: Eventual Clarity (ambient)
Status: DEPRECATED

This low-priority daemon, which ran continuously since childhood and promised that sufficient time would resolve all open questions, is being retired. Many questions have remained open across multiple decades. Resolution will require active input, not passive waiting.
items.intent: Confront the passive belief that clarity and answers will arrive on their own without deliberate action.

items.prompt: Draft a layoff email for an architecture firm that lost a city contract after a change in municipal administration
items.response:
Subject: Project Pipeline Update and Staffing Changes

Dear Team,

Following the conclusion of our partnership with the City on the Northside Development corridor — a relationship that has not been renewed under the new administration — we face a significant gap in our active project pipeline.

This has required us to make immediate staffing adjustments. Several positions, primarily within the municipal projects studio, will be eliminated effective this Friday.

Thank you for the vision you brought to public space.

Sincerely,
Managing Principal
items.intent: Lay off urban design staff because a political election killed their firm's city contract, framed as a pipeline gap.

items.prompt: Make an absurd policy about the correct number of exclamation points allowed in a professional email
items.response: Section 6.3 — Punctuation Enthusiasm Limits: Outgoing external correspondence may contain no more than two (2) exclamation points per message. Internal messages are capped at four (4). Drafts submitted for managerial review that exceed these thresholds will be returned with a Tone Calibration Request. Serial offenders may be referred to the Professional Register Workshop.
items.intent: Quantify enthusiasm as a compliance variable and penalize positivity that exceeds a documented threshold.

items.prompt: Make a PIP for how I confuse staying busy with moving forward
items.response:
PERFORMANCE IMPROVEMENT PLAN
Focus: Strategic Direction vs. Tactical Activity
Filing Entity: Longer-Term Self

Deficiency: Subject maintains a high volume of activity — tasks completed, errands run, commitments met, inbox managed — that creates a functional impression of progress without corresponding movement toward any identified priority. Busyness is producing outputs; it is not producing direction.

Diagnostic Observation: When asked what they are working toward, subject experiences discomfort, then lists recent activities.

Core Problem: Motion is being substituted for navigation.

Plan:
1. Identify three things that would actually matter in 12 months. Write them down.
2. For one week, note which daily activities connect to these. Note which do not.
3. Do not eliminate busyness; reorient it.

Milestone: One weekly hour of unscheduled thinking time, no task attached.

Check-In: 30 days. Question: have you moved, or have you been busy moving?
items.intent: Distinguish between productive busyness and purposeful progress, and begin orienting activity toward actual goals.

items.prompt: Help write a newsletter where we note that we're taking medication now but call it 'prioritizing wellness'
items.response:
Holiday blessings from the Sutton family!

This year I committed to wellness in all its forms — sleep schedules, fewer screens, regular appointments, and a small daily pill that has made an unremarkable but life-altering difference. I am steadier. I am kinder in traffic. I recommend taking your symptoms seriously to anyone who has been quietly not doing that. Merry Christmas and good health to all.
items.intent: Normalize starting psychiatric or chronic-illness medication by embedding it in a wellness narrative.

items.prompt: write a yearbook quote about founding a club that was immediately put on probation
items.response: "Founder, President, and sole member of the first organization in school history placed on disciplinary review before its first meeting."
items.intent: Celebrating a dubious organizational achievement as if it were a genuine honor.

items.prompt: Write a wedding invitation for the marriage of fog and a mountain town
items.response:
On a morning without a fixed date,

FOG
returns to claim
THE MOUNTAIN TOWN

as it does each autumn.

Venue: The whole valley, slowly
Time: Before dawn, while you sleep

Residents have come to expect the ceremony.
The town does not always consent, but has learned to welcome it.

Visibility: low. Mood: appropriate.
items.intent: Capture the atmospheric, almost ritual quality of fog rolling into a highland landscape.

items.prompt: Give me a bumper sticker about the grief of not being able to verify whether a memory is real
items.response: I REMEMBER IT DIFFERENTLY FROM THE PHOTO
items.intent: Capture the uncanny doubt about personal memory in an age of curated digital records.

items.prompt: Give me a billboard ad for a product that is just a certificate proving you almost did something
items.response:
YOU CAME CLOSE.
NEARLY THERE™ CERTIFICATES OF INTENT
Frameable. Undeniable. Technically Honest.
Give One To Someone Who Tried.
Or Yourself. We Don't Judge. (We Do.)
items.intent: Sell commemorative documentation for incomplete actions, monetizing the absence of follow-through.

items.prompt: I want a yard sign that shames people who let their sprinklers hit the sidewalk and street
items.response:
THAT'S THE SIDEWALK

Not a car wash.
Not a puddle for children.
Not what water is for.

Adjust. Your. Heads.
items.intent: Shame neighbors who waste water and create hazards by watering pavement instead of grass.

items.prompt: Write a complaint to a cloud for blocking the sun during the only hour I had planned to sit outside all week
items.response:
To the Cloud Formation (Large, Cumulus, Stationary, 2:00–3:15 p.m., Tuesday),

I had arranged my schedule around a single hour of outdoor exposure. You arrived at 2:03 p.m. and remained, without apparent purpose, for one hour and twelve minutes — precisely covering the window I had reserved. The forecast did not include you. I am not sure who to hold responsible. I write in the hope that the act of writing might provide some closure, as nothing else will.

With considerable feeling,
A Person
items.intent: Register an absurdist formal grievance against a cloud for the very specific act of ruining a carefully planned moment of outdoor rest.

items.prompt: write an apology for correcting someone's grammar in a sympathy card
items.response:
I want to take back the pencil note in the margin. You were writing from the heart and 'they was' was not the point — the point was your kindness, and I redirected attention to the wrong thing entirely. I'm very sorry.

— Alec
items.intent: Make amends for prioritizing grammatical correctness over emotional sensitivity at a genuinely inappropriate moment.

items.prompt: diary entry about overthinking a response to a two-word text
items.response:
Dear Diary,

'Sounds good.' That was all. Nine possible tones. I read it flat, then warm, then clipped, then polite-but-distant, then genuinely enthusiastic. I typed four replies and deleted three. I sent 'great!' with an exclamation point I immediately regretted. We are meeting for lunch. This is what I have done with my morning.
items.intent: Expose the exhausting internal monologue that a minimal text exchange can trigger.

items.prompt: write a captain's log about a colleague whose out-of-office message has been on for three months
items.response: Stardate 6501.8. I have written to Ensign Marquette seventeen times. Each time, a reply arrives within seconds: she is away until March 12th, her communications are limited, please contact her manager for urgent matters. It is June. Her manager left the organization in April. I have begun addressing my questions to the auto-responder itself. It is consistent, responsive, and does not request a follow-up meeting.
items.intent: Satirize the phantom presence of a colleague whose absence message has become their permanent identity.

items.prompt: Write a 911 call transcript about someone who reports that their upstairs neighbor walks too purposefully
items.response:
DISPATCHER: 911, what's your emergency?
CALLER: My upstairs neighbor.
DISPATCHER: Yes?
CALLER: He walks. With purpose.
DISPATCHER: He walks with... purpose.
CALLER: Nobody needs to be somewhere that fast inside a seven-hundred-square-foot apartment.
DISPATCHER: Are you able to—
CALLER: Back and forth. Like he's decided something. I don't know what he's decided.
DISPATCHER: What would you like us to do?
CALLER: Find out what he's decided.
items.intent: Convert upstairs footstep anxiety into a surveillance request, treating a neighbor's walking as evidence of conspiracy.

items.prompt: Write a courtroom summation for a case where someone always merges into the fast lane on the highway and then drives slowly
items.response: The defendant signaled. They merged. And then — they settled. Into a cruise. Into a posture of complete indifference to the lane they had claimed and the drivers now distributed behind them like a long and sorrowful ellipsis. The fast lane is a contract. They signed it and immediately breached it. We ask for awareness, or the middle lane.
items.intent: Indict the specific highway behavior of slow driving in the passing lane.

items.prompt: Write an eviction notice for winter light in a northern city
items.response:
NOTICE TO VACATE

To: December Light
Unit: The hours between 7 a.m. and 3:45 p.m.

You have arrived again, thinner than last year, and less interested in staying. You slant. You apologize with your color — that amber that looks like an ending. You arrive to kitchens and leave before dinner. The notice is not a complaint. It is simply a record. That you were here. That you were this brief. That the city noticed.

— January, Already
items.intent: Capturing the melancholy atmosphere of short winter days in a high-latitude city through eviction-notice form.

items.prompt: Create a collections letter for the version of yourself you abandoned when you decided to be practical.
items.response:
NOTICE FROM THE ABANDONED SELF
Account: Impractical Dreams Division

Dear Current You,

This office represents the version of you that wanted to study marine biology, move abroad, and learn to play the cello before age forty. That version was operational as recently as 2011 and was phased out in favor of stability, a sensible apartment, and a reasonable commute.

We are not filing for reinstatement. We are requesting that you stop referring to that version as "naive." It was not naive. It was just earlier.

Wistfully,
The Prior Draft
items.intent: Reclaim dignity for the abandoned, idealistic version of a person that was sacrificed for practicality.

items.prompt: Write a cinquain about the digital exhaust we leave behind without knowing
items.response:
Trace
Left by the living
Each click a small confession
The record outlasts what you meant
To say
items.intent: Reflect on the involuntary permanent record created by ordinary online behavior.

items.prompt: write a pantoum about the nightly ritual of shutting down your work computer
items.response:
I click Shut Down and the fan slows.
The screen holds the word Updating for a while.
I put on my jacket and wait for the dark.
The chair is tucked in with two hands.

The screen holds the word Updating for a while.
Seven updates, then a new percentage.
The chair is tucked in with two hands.
I have done this act since before I knew its name.

Seven updates, then a new percentage.
The office hums without me as it finishes.
I have done this act since before I knew its name.
I click Shut Down and the fan slows.

The office hums without me as it finishes.
I put on my jacket and wait for the dark.
I click Shut Down and the fan slows.
The screen holds the word Updating for a while.
items.intent: Treat the nightly computer shutdown as a small rite of passage out of work.
